Visited this place on a Saturday evening toward the end of summer. The town itself was hopping with people on a beautiful end-of-summer evening. Surprised to find this place mostly empty. Anyway, I tried two of their beers. The first one, SMASH, was a very unique IPA. It was served from a 16 oz. can. Very reasonably priced too.  The second one was good, but nothing to talk about. Ordered an appetizer (one of their poutines) which was excellent.  The bartender was very friendly although a bit distracted despite the lack of people. Overall a very good experience. Still don't know why the place was empty.  There was a live band scheduled to play and I would've thought free live music would bring in people. I'm obviously wrong.

They were out of both beers I tried to order with no replacements tapped. The host was the best part of our experience. Menu and draft layout slightly confusing and all of the pages of the draft list we're completely stained with old sauces and dressings. The atmosphere and decor were pretty cool though.

Great little find.
Enjoyed our time with our new friends.
Will go back .
Friendly. Great live music on weekends.  They serve their own beer and local PA spirits.  Food is pretty good also.

My first time there and I won’t be back. There so many other much nicer places to go. Ordered the fried Mac and cheese, one bite it tasted like curdled milk. They checked and turns out it was the sour cream and gave me a drink on the house. The place didn’t even feel clean. Thanks, but no thanks!
Beer was very good. Looking forward to going back in the near future and spending more time.

This is a handy little place off the beaten path in West Chester, and at the hour of day we visited, it was fairly quiet. That said, I'm wondering what the reason was for lukewarm stew and potato soup, as well as poutine. All seemed to have been sitting under the warmer for a bit. Three out of five guests had less than optimal experiences in that regard.

Poutine was over spiced with crab boil, but house chips were delightful.

The boxcar burger was billed as coming with mushrooms, which would have been great, but in fact, it was like a double-decker burger because the mushroom was a whole portabello!  One or the other would have been great, but together, it was too much food.

We tried three different beers, none were especially memorable.
Great beer. Live music. Haven't tried the food.
I like the look inside, but the drinks are so so

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: I came here for the first time a couple weeks ago. It wasn’t crowded just a few people sitting at the bar. When we sit at the bar a notice a man sitting at the bar with his lab top. I come to realize he is either the owner or manager, because all I hear is him discussing the employees hours so everyone at the bar can hear. He would call over all the employees and talk about the hours. He was also talking about cutting a bartenders hours or make a major change and you could tell it upset her! It was extremely comfortable for anyone that was in there. I get that maybe he has to make changes but you don’t do that in front of customers! One of the employees was looking for something so they came out to ask him where to find it and he stormed in the back and make it seem like they were all idiots. It bothers me just knowing the employees are being treated that way. Food was good and our service was good. But unfortunately we will never go back. It was the most awkward situation to be in. I couldn’t help but feel bad for the employees.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: Call this constructive criticism because I think that they can do better.  The beer selection was really good but they fell down on the food.  They were understaffed.  The waitress was very late getting to us after we were seated.  I didn't care for my beer and requested a different one. That change took 20 minutes and my new beer arrived near the end of the meal.  The menu was very limited and they were out of the vegetarian burger that I requested even though it was only 6 pm.  So I ordered a pork sandwich.  There was hardly any meat on the bun and only a small amount of other things.  They have potential but until the restaurant gets its act together I can only recommend the bar.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: I was asked by the owner how could they make it better. taco should be seasoned better. they were bland. Also, the menu does not make any sense. it is great that we can put mac and cheese on our nachos, but, I'm expecting a little more traditional foods for an Ale house. and, if you are focusing on Mexican style food, make it good. season it. Finally, deem the lights a bit- it feels like a dining hall instead of a bar/ tavern/ ale house. Would LOVE to check it out again with friend. Good luck! it's wonderful to see new local businesses.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: Very disappointed.  Was looking forward to Tueaday taco night local in the hood spot.  Decided to sit outside since it was so lovely out, waiter said no problem be right out. Noted only about 8 patrons at the bar and 1 table w 4 people. We got the menus ourselves got a table outside and were the ONLY customers. We waited about 10 mins no one ever came out to offer water, drinks, specials ...you get the idea. Sadly we were hungry and did not wait any longer. Should be no stars so disappointed we didnt even get to try the beers :(

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: So this is a Round Guys Brewing (Lansdale, PA) extension brewpub. This was a "stop in to check it out visit" with a friend so I only had two of the beers on the tap list. Both were good, I would say above average compared to a few other breweries in the area. I didn't get any food this time, but a couple next to me were raving about some of the non traditional egg rolls they ordered. There were plenty of dishes that are gluten free and vegetarian friendly if you're into that. There's also plenty of NON vegetarian and gf stuff too. They also serve cocktails and wine and a mead... For the non beer drinkers. I'll definitely be heading back with a group of friends in the near future. Service was great and the atmosphere is pretty cool.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: Food was luke warm at best.  Served the Mac & cheese after I already finished my sandwich which I stalled eating for as long as possible. My wife's tacos were soggy.  Definitely would not recommend eating here at all.  Maybe the GAH should just stick with serving their beer.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: Great for beer, food needs some work. Good ambiance and wait staff!

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: Review of beer alone - not food.  Beer was mediocre .  First thing the bartender did when insat down was show me their list of fine cocktails - not the beer list.  I think they know the beers are meh (at least when I was there).  Bonus star because they have a parking lot though.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text:
1st time...Won't be back. Place literally had less than 10 people. Staff never came to even take our order. Waited...waited...waited...got
tired of waiting.
Went somewhere else, had a nice time there.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: I also hope this can be taken as constructive...thats why I am taking the time tobwrite so much. I really want something to last here!  I really wanted to like this new spot.  We had a friendly waitress (although she disappeared for a while when we wanted the check) who suggested a beer flight.  I found all of the beers very bitter- even the pale ale.   But that may have to do with my own taste buds. So, not taking a star for that.  The chili fries we ordered as well as the enchiladas were extremely spicy and there was no warning about that. I like spicy food but the enchiladas were filled w red pepper flakes...so much so it was hard to eat without a glass of milk.  I think there should at least be a warning on the menu about which foods are spicy. The lighting in the dining room w the bar was very bright...too bright for a bar atmosphere...and hurt my eyes. I probably just sound like a complainer, but I really think dimming the lights would help the atmosphere and customers with sensitive eyes/eye issues.  Lastly, and perhaps most disappointing, I tried to play a song on the jukebox (thought that was the point of having one)  since all that was playing over the speakers was TV commercials, and it seemed to be an issue with the staff and was turned down immediately. Then the staff was instructed to pick a play list. It's not like I picked an offensive song in any manner... Not sure what the issue was other than that someone on the staff hates Stevie Nicks?  After our visit, we walked to another local bar...also with a jukebox and the customers were all having fun picking songs from a wide variety of artists/ genres and everyone was having fun and even the staff didn't turn off the terrible Josh Groban song someone chose...we all just groaned together until it finished.  That's the fun of letting your customers pick music.  It mixes things up. Anyway, I hope this is helpful. I want this place to do well. To be specific, we visited on a Saturday night and the enchiladas were ordered on a different day a couple of weeks ago. So IDK if they have been toned down w the red pepper flakes. Veggie burger was very good!

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: Taco tortillas way too thick, need a redo on the recipe. Overall food not up to par, and staffing seemed inconsistent. Needs improvement.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text:
Enchilada had a string of hair embedded in it. When alerted, the restaurant acted as if it was no big deal.

Edit to Reply to Owner -
This occurred last night - 10/08/18.

business_name: Glenside Ale House by Round Guys Brewing Company
review_text: Not bad

This used to be my favorite place in Mansfield. I was willing to overlook the petty 20 cent credit card fee because everything else was good. However, someone got the brilliant idea to set it up like McDonald's. Walk in, place a beer and food order at a counter, have a seat and wait. Nope!  I want to sit at the bar, try some beers, relax, and eventually order dinner. I won't be back until they fix this.

Camping nearby and googled breweries.  Even though this establishment had a poor rating, we decided to try it anyway. Their website said they were open til 10:00 pm.  Well after driving for 30 minutes they were closed. And it was 5:30 pm. One would think with all the bad reviews, they would try to improve.  I guess not.
Save yourself the trouble and go to the Wellsboro House in Wellsboro.  Excellent.

Not the best experience. Ordered dinner with a friend for his birthday and left hungry because our meals were completely forgotten about. To make matters worse, they did absolutely nothing to rectify the situation. I won’t be going back. Beer was consistent and beer.

Pretty bummed with the service and especially the food. Typically, a brewery restaurant blossoms in the details and quality. We all know servers make mistakes (they work extremely hard and always take great pride in providing an awesome customer experience). The server was not clear on what was on the happy hour menu (food, drinks or both?). As customers, we assumed given the server’s statement "All items with the stars next to the menu items are considered happy hour” (I’m paraphrasing). However, after we ate and received the check, we noticed the items we ordered weren’t considered happy hour. Totally fine with us that there was a miscommunication and lack of design accessibility aspects, however, after speaking to the manager about the situation, we noticed the manager and other servers were irritated while they were fixing our check.  It made us feel extremely uncomfortable and disappointed for them to not take our constructive feedback and learn from their customers. Due to this experience we won’t be coming back - it’s unfortunate we really enjoyed the atmosphere and drinks.
Prices on the food are way higher than what it merits.
Do not go here! The service is the worst! Sat at the bar for 20min cash in hand with my girlfriend and didn't even get acknowledged while people right next to us kept walking up and getting served.
Charged me my tip twice. Tried for weeks to see the manager. Nothing but excuses.

Come here every Monday and the last time was different they have a promotion fifty cent wings then tonight they started charging for sauce and charged for cheese on my burger and didn't explain the any extra charges they lost a guest on Monday nights. If your black don't go.

i am sorry to write this. i was given a mixed 6 pack from a person whom i did a favor for. it was the worst beer i ever had. i encourage your business and i would def stop in to try your other brews. best of luck!
Good not great beers.  Nice layout for meeting people

I'm giving 4 stars because the beer is stellar, the ambiance is chill, they've got board games but the food is very disappointing.

If the beer wasn't so awesome I would have been really disappointed.  The beer is great, really top notch.

The food is microwave pizza and pretzels type stuff. Even the smoked wings we're obviously old and reheated. They didn't even toss the wings in the sauce, they served plain, smoked, warm wings, with sauce on the side. This was at 6:15pm by the way.  Their sign said they start serving wings at 5pm...

The beer is really top notch.  I've traded and collected thousands of world class beers over the last few years and this place makes world class beers.  I had the blood orange ipa, the Oakbrook ipa, and the white Russian.  All of which were amazing. The white Russian was amazing.

But the food.  Seriously, the food. Don't come here expecting a good meal. My opinion, if you're not going to do something well then don't do it at all.  They'd be better off offering bags of snacks and the occasional food truck.

The restaurant is really nice and clean. I wanted to like this place, but after 4 visits I’m done.
The wonton nachos are very good, but I didn't like a lot of the menu items. Sandwiches and burgers were soggy n gross from the pools of grease they were sitting in

Not a bad place. Staff was friendly. Beers were pretty good. I had the burger of the week. Nothing special. Honestly just get a regular cheeseburger and call it a day. Also recommend if you get a burger order it the level of cooked above what you actually want. I ordered mine medium well. It was medium at best. I'll give them another try at some point when I'm out that way again to see if it was just an off day.

The beer was great.  The atmosphere nice, food tasty.  Service.........terrible.  Our server was quite obviously not interested in being there.  In general, the staff appeared to be more interested in socializing with each other and less interested in taking care of customers.  The owner did stop by our table to introduce himself.  He seems like a very nice man.  We will give it another try in a few weeks to seem if they have able to work the bugs out.

The food and service were fine. Our only complaint was it was so loud we couldn’t even talk to each other at the table. The TV sound was set so high, it was deafening. Closed captioning would have been a good solution.
Nothing to write home about we found the smallest martini in the world. Food  left terrible grease taste that stayed . The beer is is nothing special rather have a cold corona.

Would give zero if I could. Bartender paid absolutely no attention to us and flirted with ONE guy the whole time. Not to mention the awful attitude she had overall. Karen maybe? Red hair. Will not return. Will tell people to stay away.

My husband and I were there Saturday night. Still upset about how long it took to get service at the bar. The bartender with reddish curly hair & was more concerned flirting with the men at the end of the bar then servicing other customers. I never leave reviews on anything. But I will not be back in to dented keg.

DO NOT EVER EAT HERE!!! THEY SERVED MICROWAVED FROZEN CHICKEN THAT WAS ALMOST BLACK INSIDE. THE BURGER IS A CRIME AGAINST HUMANITY. COMPLAINED ABOUT EVERYTHING RECEIVED NO DISCOUNT. I've worked in restaurants for 25 years and this one of the worst I've ever seen. Read my other reviews I'm not that guy to trash places but this place is a joke. I wish you could give zero stars.

As a craft beer enthusist I was very excited to hear that a new brewery was coming into the area. However, I'm disappointed to see that this business does not brew their own beer as they purchase the wort from another company and only ferment on-site.
Good but very very loud

It's okay.   Nothing bad.  The brew pub fad is waning for me.  The beers are fine, but to serve them in plastic cups is a loss.  Last time we met friends there, someone mused that the place reminded them of a college fraternity basement bar.

It would be nice to have beer from a glass rather than a plastic cup but other than that the atmosphere is really comfortable and friendly.  The variety of food trucks never disappoints.  A really, really nice addition to the EC area.

Ok first off food Saturday night was very good , this so far was the only time there . Second I'm not much of a beer drinker but every once in a while I will try a beer . The beer was not really cold to start by the time I finished it it was warm . I don't know about you but warm beer is not that good . The biggest reason I gave a 3 was your tables and bar stools. . For a person with a bad back to sit at those high tables on very uncomfortable stools by the time I left I could hardly walk . It would be nice if you had a few lower tables and a few regular chairs with some padding on them . I do use a cane so those high stools are very hard to sit on .
There are other things I'd like to try like the lobster rolls but I only have to get it to go .
Good place to check out. Lost a star because they use plastic cups. They could also use a couple more beer options but it's new so I'm sure those will come with time. Overall great experience.
Recently attended a fundraiser for the Ellwood City Festival fireworks..so great to have a first class place to support our comunity in such a cool atmosphere.

Hibachi was good.  Not a fan of $5.50 beer in a 10-12oz plastic cup. Original bartender was good... she took a break and second bartender walked by my empty cup and money at least 4 times. She then used my same cup with a different beer before going on her cell phone. Doubt I’ll be back.
This location just opened. Beer is good! I like the Blood orange IPA. Food trucks provide your meals so check Koehler's web page for who is scheduled.
Cool atmosphere, good beer .. needs heat turned on,  otherwise a nice experience
Great location and atmosphere, most of the beers are good but a few are just meh

We want to come again tomorrow but don’t know which food truck is in??
We had a good time last time, good Indian food. Looking forward to have Glas instead of plastic cups.
Could be a nice place.  Music WAY TOO Loud.  Only stayed for one drink.  Music and loud conversation will drive away older customers.
Friendly atmosphere, live music, good food from meal trucks.
New brewery in the area. Experienced.  Growing. Good brew. Nice roomy bar. Food trucks every night.
Been here plenty of times to enjoy the food trucks and the atmosphere.
Roomy , yet comfortable environment  to sit in. Different food trucks each night. Friendly  employees.
Food trucks are the big draw.  Could use more tables and chairs.
Fun place....great ambiance
The Mexican food truck has amazing food
Wings are 1.00 each. Weren't any better than the usual bar wings🍗
I like the idea here but the food trucks are very pricey and I haven't really gotten anything that I would go back for
Cash only??????? Not happy

The brisket sandwich was huge, but a little one-dimensional. The balance of flavor was completely saved by the pickle. Their ESB Amber Ale was very good, although something else would have paired better with the sandwich.

I thought the food was very good. I felt that they were ill prepared for the Post Championship parade by not having sufficient staff to cover the crowd. Why generally think it's important for Hospitality folks to spend time with customers the cashier spent an inordinate amount of time with those ordering and purchasing food having idle Chit Chat while the line continue to grow call generally think it's important for hospitality folks to spend time with customers the cashier spent in the Norton in the amount of time with those ordering a purchasing food happy I delete it chat while the line continue to grow I truly believe that the management needs to better prepare for large event crowds. I also think of the crowd was indicative of the food and the drinks it would have been much more fun experience for me heading that way in an hour and 15 minutes for my food after I ordered it

Food has been good since management has changed. New management has significantly improved the overall experience from when the place first opened.

That being said, the membrane needs to be taken off the ribs. I shouldn't need to struggle with a knife to pull apart ribs.

For a Saturday night this place was not busy. This is not a small neighborhood bar. Rather it is a large sprawling restaurant that looks like it could easilyaccomodate more than 100 patrons. I wonder if the limited parking nearby may discourage customers. That combined with the negative reviews was concerning.
But my partner and I had a good experience. Service was good. We had seafood pizza which was quite good. They had a good selection of beers on tap.
Restrooms are unisex but in a European style with toilets contained within fully enclosed closets.
.

Honestly, we gave the Brewerytown Taproom a try not expecting much, but we were glad we did. I was expecting a decent beer selection, which they had, but I was surprised by it's large, clean space and quality of food. Our service was fantastic, however to be fair, my husband and I were the only table there. Given the space I could see servers being overwhelmed at times. Nonetheless, the food was impressive and we'll be back on that merit alone.

It's a good for what it is. A comfortsble spot for a craft brew paired with elevated pub fare.

Beer was excellent and isn't that why you go to a brew house? Food was good. The brick oven pizza was burned the 1st time around but 2nd time is the charm. Delicious. Friends had parts of the or orders mixed up so there are definitely kinks to work out. Would I go back... definitely. Waitstaff more than willing to make the experience enjoyable.
Was there for a private event. Food was ok. Wings were dry n under flavored. Not enough bar staff at start of event. Good space but better food when there not as a special event.

Pretty cool place in an otherwise desolate neighborhood.
Staff is hot and cold depending on what mood they happen to be in. Food is good but kitchen staff seem a little scattered.
Beer is great! Always great choices!

The first couple visits were great, I was pumped that a spot like this opened up in the neighborhood, i had been here over a dozen times ...then it all went down hill really fast. The last time i was in there with my friends the bar tender ignored us half the time, i literally had to wave her down like I would a cab (mind you, i have tipped this same bar tender very well in the past and was always polite). Fyi, they like skipping songs you play on the jukebox, even when half of their staff including a manager was singing along. There are way better spots in the neighborhood to get a drink and with much better service!
Great food, LOVE the caprese salad. Drinks are strong and delicious. Since it's never crowded when I go, bartenders are always friendly and talkative which is nice.

I went here to pick up a uber eats order that couldn't be filled because they were closing but it looks like a nice place to eat and chill. It's huge and appears comfortable. The two staff I encountered for my order pick up were friendly too.

Very strange service in beer garden— no servers for the tables, just the bar tender. Seemed odd to only have one person taking care of a bar plus an entire room of tables during a football game. She did a decent job, but we technically were not even supposed to get table service. Food was ok, but seemed a bit expensive.  Not sure how long this place will last , as it was pretty empty for a sports bar during an Eagles game.

Good food. Terrible bartender (the one with black short hair). I enjoy the food, however I cringe at the thought of the female bartender that works there mixing drinks. She is an inept bartender and can’t mix a drink to save her life – her long islands are laughable at best (I hope she has a day job). I almost don’t want to leave her a tip but decide to leave something very nominal for the disgraceful drinks. However, I don’t even bother with the drinks anymore and I’ve advised friends in the neighborhood to do the same on the nights she bartends. Disgraceful. Food only!!
Wasn't overly impressed. The idea is there for sure, just need to pull it together. Pretzel was good, tots not so much.

Great food love the 5 beer samples for 12 dollars....
The rib sauce really make u wanna slap ur momma and I was there with her and my sister and daughter best time ever
Good food, good service, but currently in a transitional period so the vibe isn't there. I'll be back once they reopen in March.

The restaurant is smack dab in the middle of a residential neighborhood but often has the door open so anyone who lives around here has to suffer. I wish the owner of the establishment understood that the restaurant isn't located downtown. Leaving your door open so that live bands and loud music being played throughout the neighborhood is very disruptive.
Really neat industrial feel inside the Crafthouse.  Flying Fish has some great beers but the surrounding area is still pretty sketchy.
Nice place great atmosphere! Gourmet food and great craft beers.

I'm hopeful that the recent changes will be for the better. As of now, drinks are over priced but food is still worth the $. Hopefully it can integrate better into the neighborhood with out it's big name partners.
Disappointing every time. Slow service, slow food even when restaurant is empty. Forgettable menu. Two stars for good beer. Go to 2637 or Crime and Punishment instead.

Went in for the first time since the staff change.  This crew is definitely friendlier. Also their menu has changed which is for the better.  Alot more general bar foods.  I ordered the full rack of ribs which we nicely cooked.  Not falling off the bone and a little too over sauced but they were good. I also had the cheesesteak spring rolls which weren't as good and for $12 I expected more than 4 of them or at least better quality.  All in all its a great location and worth a try if you havnt been here before.

This restaurant is such a disappointment!!! It has changed hands and names 3 times since its opening and has gotten progressively worse each time.

Some of the food is halfway decent but the wait staff and management are some of the worst in Philadelphia. My wife and I waited over 2 hours to get our food one night. The "manager" ended up giving us $75 in credit and a six pack of Pabst so we would come back. (Jon Taffer would have a field day with this guy)

I swore after that night I would never go back, but last night we decided to give it one last shot. And boy did they live up to the same expectation. The creepy manager with the mustache couldn't decide where to seat us for the Villanova game and after a while just told us to seat ourselves. Severely creeped out my wife. Our waitress was very nice but I could tell she hadn't been trained and dint really know what to do. For example she took our menus away after we ordered apps and drinks even though we were going to order dinner. And it took her a very long time to come get our second round of drinks.

The wings are actually very good here. That being said everything else was terrible. Their brisket is trash. My wife got the pulled pork sandwich and couldn't even finish half of it because it was so bad. I got a chicken sandwich that was just a huge dry-ass fried chicken breast and coleslaw with sun dried tomatoes!? Their tots are good, but who could mess up tots?

Long story short. Try and find another restaurant if you are in Brewerytown and looking for somewhere to grab a beer and a bite.

I would recommend 2637Brew, Crime and Punishment, or anywhere on Fairmount Ave before this place.

The beer selection is nothing to write home about. Half the beers on the menu where not even available!
Then they screwed up my order which the staff apologized for but when they tried to fix it the food still was not anything like the menu description. There are plenty of better options in Brewerytown.

Great beer selection.

Environment could be good, but the staff made us feel very unwelcome. Granted, it was an empty bar on a Saturday night, but they went through the closing routine (flipping the chairs upside down on tables all around us) hours before closing time, then proceeded to turn the music volume louder and louder where our conversation was deduced to yelling at one another in what seemed to be an attempt at flushing us out.

But good beer.
it was my first visit and i enjoyed the serve and food the wings and that special sauce was delicious i will go back
Food and beer were good.  Service staff was very disorganized.
Great food and beer, definitely suits a bigger group with the open atmosphere and long picnic tables.

Food service sucked. Waited over an hour plus a lot of food was being delivered to the wrong tables. Bartenders were serving food, getting supplies when they ran out which caused long long lines for drinks.

Food was delicious beer is flying fish which for me is serviceable but not amazing
Pretty remote location but huge and well done
Would be great for groups
Service is haphazard and it's unclear if there is table service or at the bar. Beers are standard, nothing really special for the location. Food portions are substantial.
Good beer selection. Food is ok.
Nice atmophere, fun employees

I hate to leave bad review during COVID times but to be honest the food was pretty bad. The wings were very overcooked and the other batch was soggy. Other food didn’t even come out.

The beers were very good though and the atmosphere was nice. Maybe it was just an off day for the food and I hope to come back and have a better experience with the food.
Great live music, tasty empanadas and Mexican street corn, good beer. A really fun outing
Nice place. Very nice beer offerings.
Awesome service,and they all work together great staff,

Loved the beer and good food.  Stumbled upon this and will definitely return.  Very impressed with the menu and everything we tried we loved! Nice surprise while there they had live music! Definitely worth a stop!

The noise level was pretty intense.  When considering this rating, please bear in mind that there was a very large group, and I suspect that may have affected the quality of the food and service.  The. Beer Mac 'n cheese with pulled pork was delicious, very creamy cheese sauce.  The burger had the same cheese sauce.  The porter cooked short rib quesadilla was also nice.  The calamari was a bit too greasy, but the apple-radish slaw was very refreshing.  The nachos were okay; we topped them with chili, but we think the pulled pork would have been better.  Sadly, because of the large previously mentioned party, most of our food was lukewarm and the appetizers came out with the mains.  Service was not as attentive, as it should have been.  It's possible we should have ordered the pulled pork sandwich or one of the flatbreads.

NOTE:  02/12/2019 - The owner responded to this review, acknowledging the issues.  We appreciate that, and will certainly give Kennett Brewing Company a second chance.
Beers pretty good actually. Flat bread was nice . Small place. Sunday night had a live band on that was pretty good

The location is really cool! The beers on tap were really good and the staff was very knowledgeable and friendly. The only downside is that the space doesn’t seem to be utilized well. They have a lot of tables for 8 and bar space, but not so many spaces for smaller groups.

Okay brews, but make sure you check for that auto included 20% gratuity on all tabs (not disclosed) the blank gratuity line on the tab does not say additional gratuity. Don't give more unless worthy.

You know what... reconsidering, just go to victory down the road.  They don't play games for more tip, and have quite a good product despite their bigger scale. Plus much better pricing for what you get.
Food was delicious, but it is a limited menu since everything is fresh. The beer was excellent!  It's a downstairs brew pub, so atmosphere is so-so, but it makes up for it in taste and service.
Food looked good, if a bit pricey, and the jazz band and cozy small town bar feel were nice

Eight beers on tap, with a good variety for different tastes. Wait staff was knowledgeable of the various types yet slightly disorganized. Had two types of flatbread between us and wings for appetizer. Would order both again. Skip the dessert.four stars for the food with a minus one for the dessert.
Have gotten to enjoy this place more. Good beers, usually good music, and the service has been better of late. Definitely a great place to go in kennett.
First time there. The wait staff were friendly but inefficient. Food was okay. Atmosphere was good. Not too loud
Service was slow, server was rude, beer not that good.  Will not go back.

I rarely have the urge to review a restaurant moments after leaving. However I almost had to leave because of a continuous strange fowl smell my wife said was seafood, I think not. We did not automatically get menus. When we arrived we were told to sit at table 81, the pointed to where that was at, how rude. Our straws for our drinks were dripping wet with what I assume was water but who knows.
On the good side the waitress was pleasant, the food was good and we did not have too long of a wait. I most likely will not return, that smell was too much....I still feel sick!

Over the weekend I contacted the manager Danielle and she took the time to track down the offending sickening smell. However the pointing to the table where we will sit is their policy because of Covid and social distancing yet the waitress was standing about 18" away when she took our drink order. It is absolut BS that one of their staff can't walk 6ft ahead and show you to your table like every other popular restaurant in the area.
So this review went from 2 starts to 3 because of Danielle's detective work.
Working hard to get back into the swing and even with a limited menu it's worth a stop... The hummus platter needs a lot of work but our Burger was superb and the voodoo Pizza is very flavorful
Food was ok but service was terrible. Worst I’ve ever experienced. Can’t wait to never come here again!
Disappointing

Good beer with a wide variety to choose from. I liked their IPAS and the one Night Howler beer that was a Stout. However the food was less than satisfying. The pizza was bad with a watery sauce that my beloved 6 yr old couldn't stomach after a bite. The fries were way over salted.  Though my wife and daughter would never come back. The good beer they serve def gets recommended by me! The staff was friendly and attentive as well, Cheers!

I'll start by saying the beer is very good. However,  the batch I received of Super Villain IPA, although 90% great, the last sips were undrinkable. I suppose the beer is not filtered a whole lot if at all because the bottom is a pool of unpalatable sediment. Not sure if this is intentional but it's very jarring.

The bar tenders chatted it up with others but never came back to check on me after placing a glass of room temperature water down. One of them said the restaurant closed at 10:00 P.M but their website said they didn't close until 12:00 P.M. I'm annoyed because I really love pizza and was looking forward to eating at this spot. Maybe if I wasn't Afro Latina they would have showed more love 💕. Better luck next time.
Wow! Beer is sooo bad. Bought a flight and didn't finish it. Watery, no flavor.
Rye beer was one of the worst beers I've ever had.
Terrible service every time
Pizza wasn't very good.
Good

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Ordered shrimp tacos. Only came with 2 tacos with maybe 3 shrimp on each one. No veggies. Was a much smaller portion than most restaurants. Most restaurants offer 3 tacos for a few dollars less. ABC only offers 2 tacos with a tiny side of rice for a few dollars more than it's competitors.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: BOGUS!!  Stay away!  If you sub onions rings  for fries they charge you for the full price and you don't the fries.  Shared a beef on weck with my wife, and 2 house made sodas and it was 40 bucks!!  I know I got jacked so no hate mail.  Oh and we did get sweet potato fries as well.  NEVER AGAIN.  Go ANYWHERE else and get 2 entries cheaper and better.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: The food is good and usually pretty fast. The reason for this negative review is for the fact that me and my wife took our dog and in laws out to eat and sat at the beer garden on a Saturday evening and got takeout containers to eat. We loved the food so much we came back today and we're told it was a half hour + wait even though there were multiple picnic tables open and no one waiting before us and on top of that was told we have to be seated by a waiter. Don't know if we will go back as we got settled in and we're kicked out right after walking around the town today.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Food is good. Beverages were good also. My only negative is how seating inside is done. I get that you're busy and I heard short staffed but, when you say first available for 2 that includes the bar. The times given out weren't very accurate also. First got there and was told 30 minutes. Ended up waiting an hour and a half.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Seemingly among the best of limited quality options in Gettysburg. Excellent beer garden. Beer is good, not great.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Super crowded!  Good beer and good food.  Stop in, but beware that dinner time could be an hour wait even the place is huge.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Decent food, decent service, friendly waitress and staff. The drinks were also decent, even though several had already sold out for take out 6 packs. We had a good time and would come again

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Delicious food! Every item on the menu was tempting to order. Great service also. Highly recommend to anyone visiting Gettysburg.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Really good Root Beer Floats but still a bit greasy with their fish n chips. 100% Authentic Ice Cream and Root Beer!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Great food good service nice atmosphere. And great company my daughter and her hubby.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Very good. Look forward to coming back to sit out on the patio during better weather.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Wasn't impressed with the beer. Friends didn't care for their drinks or food. Meatloaf was good though. I wouldn't recommend.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text:
Granted, I know this is all during COVID and the restaurant has to operate under strict state guidelines, however....

- unable to make reservations
- put our name on the list for indoor seating, were told it would be an hour wait
- had to wait outside in the cold, we were not allowed to buy a drink and start a tab to wait until a table was ready.
- we saw many tables open outside. But since we put our table down for indoor and waited for 40 minutes so far, we decided to continue to wait.
- after an hour or a little over, we were finally called to get seated at a table.
- THEY SAT US OUTSIDE!!!!!!! We waited for an hour to sit outside? During that entire hour, there were many tables open outside and several groups who arrived after us were seated before us out there.
- there were no apologies.
- We were all hungry so we thought whatever we will just sit out here and eat. After all, we saw a fire pit they could start or a standing outdoor heater.
- The heater was out of propane - when we asked, the waiter had a "too bad" attitude, not attentive to suggest "hey we should tell our manager so we can replace it, people are out here and freezing and this table was screwed over". There was nothing. And he said he had to ask his manager if they could start the fire (I could tell it was previously lit so there should have been no issue). Our waiter never mentioned anything else to us and the fire was not started.
- when he finally got to us to start taking our orders, he took ages to get our drinks to us (we were freezing and needed some whiskey to heat us up).
- food also took forever. I ordered a medium burger, it was surely well done. Everyone else seemed to enjoy their order. I ordered an old fashion and it was the worst old fashion I'd ever had. I then ordered a Irish coffee to help with the cold, it came out lukewarm... When I asked our waiter to heat it up, he did so reluctantly.

I know the waiters do not have full control over how/when we are seated, how the bars and kitchen handle food and drink, but we received NO apology. We were being treated like a burden. This was the biggest issue.

This restaurant is run poorly and seems to have inexperienced staff working. All we needed was an apology and some effort. This is why I am writing this review today.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Foods always good. Not much variety with the beers if you don't like happy beers.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Terrible Food and Lousy Service. Gave this place a try today based on the reviews. Not sure what we are missing but we had a terrible experience. The “stacked high” beef sandwich was tiny, cold, tasteless and overpriced. The waitress suggested trying the onion rings instead of fries so I did. She neglected to mention a $4 up charge! The beer cheese soup came out Luke warm and was more like cheese dip, not soup. Our waitress was useless, neglected to bring silverware or napkins, never refilled drinks and the had to be hunted down after waiting 20 minutes for the bill. Never again for us. We wasted $35. Lesson learned.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: We and our friends (party of 4) dined at your restaurant on Thursday,  Oct. 8, 2020.  It was a very pleasant experience.   Didn't even wait 5 minutes to eat inside. The waitress was great and very accommodating.   Our food was hot and delicious.  Fish & Chips was a good portion and very tasty.   The hamburger was delicious and so was the mushroom quesadilla.   Overall excellent meal.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: I mean...most of the beer is good, but I just had one of the worst beers I've ever had in my life there. Service is great, so I can't complain there!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Great food.. wonderful prices.. make sure to get the elephant ear..

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Went there for a tasting and got food to go. Got home the food was good the beer was excellent picked up a six pack and a growler

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: This is a great place, and their beers are fantastic. Our service was great as well, but the menu selections leave alot to be desired. Hoping that's just in response to Covid.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Took almost 2 hours to get food and not that good, while we were waiting for the food nobody came over to let us know what was going on or when the food would be out. The food was not worth the price or wait, very disappointed piss poor service.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Cozy pub with good service and food selection.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: The beer was good, but the food wasn't worth the wait

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Food was cold and had no flavor at all. When I told the witness it was cold she said I can microwave it.  I know one thing reheating food in the microwave is a big no no.  It breaks done the protein.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Food was good and the waitress was friendly and informative.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text:
Our server was great.. He kept apologising for the kitchens mistakes.
But the kitchen didn't read my order right. Not the first time or the second.
My lettuce was brown.
Our order was slow. My husband's food was good. Not great.
We are tourists. So I might give them a second chance if we lived here.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: It’s o.k. Beer menu is diverse, tasty brews ready to be had.  Food comes out to order.  Some help with the staff communication would help.  Inquired about a missing ingredient in the sand which.  Server informed me how I was incorrect on the ingredients.  Otherwise a standard bar food location.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Our food was good, service very good!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Great food nice friendly staff.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text:
We visited during the COVID-19 pandemic and for the most part, the staff seemed to be following the social distancing regulations. However, we did see one waitress wearing just a face shield, no mask, so she is still very much at risk of transmitting the virus with her mouth not at all covered, but luckily she wasn't our waitress.  Does make you wonder about what PPE the kitchen staff is wearing though....

I had one of the dinner specials, pulled pork tacos (pictured). I have never had anything like their honey jalapeño sauce, really elevated the tacos.  If you like IPAs, definitely go for the Outta Focus Double IPA. So good that I had to get a sixer to go!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Good food, good service

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Beer is great and food is good but the recent service has been absolutely horrendous. Servers are nasty and drag their feet to set tables.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Good vegan options

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Fish Tacos were so spicey my wife couldn't finish them. Was never offered any form of reimbursement for food that was inedible. I had the Mac & Cheese and it was tasty but not very cheesy. The Scotch Ale was delicious.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text:
Today is Monday March 9th i took my son to work an had my neice with me so decided to eat our waitress Mandy was about the worst waitress I have in a long time. She offered us nothing not even a straw had to ask for one an still never offered the rest of the table one had to ask again for a straw. My son let her know we needed to go containers she got him one. My neice had to ask again. We had a to go order also so we had a bag an three other containers she never offered a bag for those to help carry them out. Needless to say I did not tip her. I eat there often an have ALWAYS left a nice tip. She did not earn one
Its a nice place to go an has good food bit that waitress was really lousy

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Well walked in they were dead slow had to wait around 10 minutes  for the hostess to stop talking to a waitress just to seat us. Only two other couples eating here but there were at least 12 tables with dishes everywhere. The entire time I was there tables were not cleared or cleaned and the two waitresses just stood around talking my food was also horrible my wife did however enjoy her meal even tho they got it wrong

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: We were seated as soon as we walked in the. Then we sat for 15 minutes as all the employees stared at us but no one would take are order. So we left and went somewhere else

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text:
Party of 14...so not a small check.  Gave me FOUR tortilla chips for my chili.  Tasted good.  Asked for a few more chips since they didn't have crackers.  Wanted to charge me for a side of chips!  Cheap!!

Edited - they apologized with no excuses...willing to try them again :)

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: The hostess states "we only have low tables" with attitude,  get to the table and wait and wait then order our drinks and wait and wait she comes back and says the bar is backlogged. And yet a table sat more than 5 minutes after us gets their drinks before us.  The server does not bring the appetizer to us. Just hangs out in the other room with other servers.  When our food finally came out it again was not our server.  When we got the food I ordered chips got frozen fries.  The burger was over cooked in sections and undercooked in other sections. Waited for her to come back so we could get another rootbeer.  Looked over the bill saw we were over charged.  This is over priced pub food.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: We had high hopes for this place but unfortunately were disappointed.  Large beer (and non alcoholic) section which was good, but nachos were lack luster to say the least. The “spicy” cheese on top was clearly the type from out of a can, globbed on top of the chips. Nachos would be a lot better if they used shredded cheese and melted it. Fruit flies were literally everywhere over the bar. One waitress had a tap splash all over the bar back and bucket of fruit under the taps and never bothered to try to clean it, just walked away and left the area.  I’m sure that doesn’t help with the fly problem, amongst other things. Wish I could say it was a good time, but.....

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Waited over 45 minutes for our food after ordering. Only explanation our server could give us was the kitchen was really busy and backed up. At that point, somebody should’ve been making rounds trying to smooth things over with us and the other tables waiting an excessively long time for food, but zero attempt to make it right. I won’t be back.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: The hostess was not welcoming at all.  It was our first time there.  We didn't have our menus opened before waitress asked for our drink order.  I asked for time to look at the menu. I thought she would at least suggest some local brews but she mentioned NOTHING about alcohol which I found odd for a brewing company.  Ordered the tater tots for an appetizer.  Waited too long for appetizer that was brought by another waitress.  No plates for our appetizers.  I ordered stromboli which was very greasy and overbaked on the bottom.  I couldn't cut through it because the crust was tough and it slid off my plate. Husband had large portion of fish but it was very greasy.  There was a lot of wait staff but they were neglecting customers.  The atmosphere was really nice.  The tables were not on top of each other which I liked. Food was ok.  Service was not acceptable. I'll give it one more try but I was disappointed tonight.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: I’ve been here several times over the last five years. The two stars are weighing in with my previous visits because this time around the food was almost inedible. Very disappointed and I think my rancid fish tacos have effectively made this restaurant my last choice when I visit Gettysburg. Top it off with the aloof and then rude staff and I’m done.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Went there 1 time wasnt very impressed. Homemade Soda and the meal I got was $15.00 and wasn't much to it.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: We came in for just drinks. The waitress was so rude. When we said we didn't want to eat, just wanted drinks, the waitress ripped the menus from the table. We had 1 drink each, between 3 people and the waitress was no where to be found for our check until we got up to ask for our check from someone else. Then she wanted to act nice at the end so she could have a tip. Another waitress had a boyfriend who came in that was kissing her and touching her while she was on duty. Very unprofessional. As we were leaving, I told the other workers to have a good night and they couldn't be bothered to say thank you or even respond because they were too involved in a personal conversation that should be kept out of the work place or at least away from their customers. Will not go back!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Food is not great....had a french dip called something else very bland no cheese no grilled onions very boring sandwich for $14...i can make better at home...might want to revise your menu

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Food was cold and not that good for the price. Service was terrible. Our server was only at our table to take our order and get our check. Not for refills or anything. Highly dissatisfied.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Server seemed a little grouchy when first went in.Told us they open at 11 which it was 11 and door was open to go in.Fish and chips were average at best.Just went there over the weekend(9-22-18) .Host seemed a little confused on seating had to check and see if waitress and waiters were there so we could be seated where we  wanted to sit. Took at least 10 minutes for waitress to come over to our table.After we were waited on everything went  really good

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Waited almost an hour for salad with salmon and burger. Kept giving excuses. Mgmt did not offer anything no appetizer or taking off a beer. Stopped on way to DC. Beer was ok but not great.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Atmosphere was the only thing it had going for it.  Out of four of us  ordering, only one person really liked their meal.  Advertises a full bar but waitress said they could not make a mint mojito.  Waitress did not even question why I only took several bites of my meal and left the rest of it on the plate.  Over all very disappointing!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Could be nice. Service wasn't great and one of it beers tasted off, but they refused to offer to take it back. Disappointed.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Daughter order Mac and cheese with the chicken . Chicken very dry and not much cheese on mac

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: No one asked if I needed anything or if I was ok or how's the food waitress only had 4 tables I was one of those tables visiting might have spent more if I was cared about probably will not go back

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Terrible service, marginal food, served cold. Waitress was definitely not interested in timeliness or urgency in anything. Most items ordered took a reminder from us to get them served after a long wait. This has been twice now that I have had similar experiences at the same establishment.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Beer is awful. Food is expensive and boring. They to ages to serve. Plenty of other places. Just not this one.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: I give 3 stars only because the service was bad. Food...excellent. Beer...obviously excellent. Service was poor. Maybe an off night.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: My family and I stopped in. Our second time there. My son ordered a burger with lettuce. When our food came out his lettuce was moldy. I brought it to our waitresses attention who caught an attitude right away trying to make excuses on moldy lettuce. My hand touched her and she caught a super attitude saying” don’t touch me” when all I was doing was showing her the lettuce. And on top of that I didn’t realize I touched her by accident till after she got an attitude. Then she proceeded to chat with someone in a gray shirt which never came over to our table. The food was good other than the moldy lettuce.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Seen one franchise seen them all. Boring

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: The restaurant is new, large, fresh and clean. The food unfortunately was disappointing, and not inexpensive for bar food.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: The beer cheese soup is phenomenal, but everything else from beer to food is mediocre.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: I wouldn't recommend this place to anyone. The food was just OK, but the service was atrocious. Our server Eddie was basically useless.  This isn't the first time this place has been a let down.  My mistake for trying it again.  Do yourself a favor and go elsewhere.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Rather expensive for food that didn't have much flavor. Great beer selection though!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Good food but we wanted to eat outside and they were hesitant to give us napkins for some odd reason

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Hours changed for the winter. We were at the hospital late for two nights. The first, we were turned away because winter hours closed earlier than advertised on the web. Second night, got there at 9:30 pm and found they closed the kitchen at 10:00 pm. We had to order appetizers, soup (chili), and dessert at the same time before the kitchen closed. Everything was crammed together. Nachos were terrible with drizzled canned cheese being the best part. Chili was worse. Fish was all right but mile-high meatloaf was tasteless and bland. Iced tea tasted burnt. Coffee was lukewarm. Csbernet Sauvignon was good. No close access to handicapped or regular parking, so, long walk on a cold and icy night with risk of falling on the ice. Definitely, off our list regardless of the good wait staff.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Tried this place for the 3rd and final time. Small portions, warm beer, mediocre service, high prices and disappointment. I won't be back. Do yourself a favor and skip this place.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: You're better off going to McDonald's; the food was utterly trash😷

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Had to try it out

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: This is the worst experience I have ever had in the past 2 years. The waitress complained about having to work... she picked her job. We waited and hour and 15 mins for food. French fries were cold, the ceaser dressing had no flavor, we had to ask waitress for the food, after that the service got worse. I orders and appetizer, only 2 parties ate it but all 3 got charged, response from waitress "no one told me differently".  Exactly my point, no one told her to split it but she charged someone who didn't eat any of it. It was all about the waitress and not the customer. I won't ever come back.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Long wait on a Saturday night but worth it. Good food, good beer, good service.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Food was okay for a chain restaurant. Word to management....servers are a dime a dozen. You really need to clean house. We were there for bike week and decided to try this place. Not once did our server check back on our table to see if we needed anything or if our food was acceptable. We had to get up and find our server to get drink refills and the check. There are way too many eating establishments to choose from to not have good customer service.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Service is slow and not much individual attention is paid to the customer.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Food was cold and service matched. Not a place we would ever go again. Never offered refills on a free refill soda.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Service was horrible. Decent beer if you can actually get served.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Sucked, start to finish.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Ordered fish and chips. Fish was dripping in oil.

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Service was good, but my park chop was over-burnt, hard and dry, charcoal like!

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text: Slow slow slow, forgetfull service

business_name: Appalachian Brewing Company of Gettysburg - Battlefield
review_text:
(Translated by Google) The food is good. Of the beers, the mountain camp tastes best of German Pilsner.
You can taste the different beers beforehand.

(Original)
Das Essen ist gut. Von den Bieren schmeckt das Mountain Lager am ehesten nach deutschem Pilsner.
Man kann die verschiedenen Biere vorher kosten.

Friendly staff who were knowledgeable and happy to engage with us as we sampled drinks and threw some axes. Interesting flavors of mead (while I'm not wild about mead in general I did find a few that I liked). The axe-throwing was quite fun and the staff member running it was helpful and quite nice. Definitely worth a trip to check out the axe-throwing and sampling the mead.
Staff very friendly just not my kind of place. If you're Viking you'll love it.

A Mead shop with a great ambience. Good selection of high quality Meads.
Flights are $12 and you get a generous fill in all 4 glasses.
Axe throwing is a lot of fun and costs $10.
Did not see on site parking but found nearby street parking.
Had a great first visit and will be back.

The Mead was excellent! Place was kind of empty, but we were there early on a Saturday, so kind of expected. It gave us some time to throw axes without a wait. Instructor was great, walked us through everything and explained himself very well. I would go back.

I went here on a date with my boyfriend. The mead was delicious!!! Didn't throw the axes but it was fun to watch. The atmosphere was super cool. A little hard to find, but that just makes it more of a hidden gem.

Sometimes it's fun to find something different.

A friend had brought a couple bottles of mead over to our last party. We were downtown and decided to find the place. We walked into a party! It just happened to be their 1yr anniversary. They had door prizes, a band going, ace throwing, mead tastings.  What a fun time.

The place is rustic. I think designed that way. You walk into what looks like a warehouse. It opens up to a bar with mixed tables and high tops. When it was busy it was hard to get a drink and the payment process was really slow. For only a year they still have some kinks to work out but overall it was a fun place with a totally different drink than you would regularly find out
Fun and different evening
Cool place beer wasnt my style but they have axe throwing

They need to have more than just mead to appeal to a broader audience. My mother wanted something more dry (since she is a gin drinker mostly) but all they had was sweet mead. Added to this the entrance to the mead hall was a bit lame. They are totally indoors. No outdoor seating and they don't have a building to themselves. Signage outside is poor and you have to go through a lobby to get to it. Small unimpressive place upon entrance.

The axe throwing is a ton of fun though and the long table in the back is ideal for playing Magic the Gathering since the mead hall sets an ideal atmosphere for such a fantasy card game.

The pretzel saved the review. Went there for our 6 year anniversary. We don't get out a lot due to kids so we were excited to go there. The mead was ok it reminded me more of a cider though. They were out of a few meeds and yowlers. They gave us additional samples to make up the groupon difference. We also bought a ax session for each of us and added our names to the list. There was almost no one there when we signed up but some how we watched group after group that came in after us get to go up and throw. We could not wait any longer and had to get a refund. Would of been nice to try. Owner said we could go next and for no cost when we explained issue but time is of the essenance when you have a sitter. They need to get things better organized. The place it self was nice and staff friendly. The pretzel was off the hook and boosted review. May give it a chance again if we can get out again.
Very new place, under construction was cold, but the meads are pretty good . New places like this are a nice thing to see. I recommend checking in on them as they will likely improve even more.
Cool atmosphere nice earthly feel
Excellent customer service.  Many different varieties of mead,  one is sure to suit you.

The food is excellent, but our service tonight from server Andrew M was really bad. We watched as he hovered over another table instead of ours the whole night. We had a broken glass, no big deal but he didn’t care. Wish the server was there to do his job and not to socialize while dismissing our table. Coaching badly needed.
The food here is good especially the burgers. The decor leaves something to be desired.
Was ok. Pretzel was awesome! Service excellent and food was O.K.

Was looking forward to a bite after a hike.  Walked in and was quickly seated, there was only one other table. Proceeded to wait 10 minutes for one of the two waitress to stop by for drink order. Tried to wave one down, got eye contact but no attention.
Walked out after 18 minutes of being seated with them waking back and forth with no service.
Sad, really want to try this place.

I was here today with a friend of mine and it was a disappointing experience. Truthfully the food sucked, it was cold and literally no flavor. To add, it also took 45 minutes to come out to us. I don’t know what was going on in the kitchen, but the place was so empty for the food to take that long. This place used to be good and it just seems to keep going downhill. I won’t be back.

This is an odd place, the beer is good - the Jolly Scott was a very smooth red. But there are things that threw the entire independent brewing company thing out of wack with me.

The restaurant/bar is so brightly lite, that you it feels like you are in a children's restaurant. The bar area itself was tiny with only four high tops and six bar seats. We were sat in the restaurant area, without even being asked our preference - which really would have been in the bar because that was empty enough and we came to have a couple hours out.

The food is okay, nothing really special, thought the portions were a little small and didn't play up to the Appalachian part of the restaurant. Expected it to be a little more rural heavy - homely type stuff.

In all wasn't a horrible experience... But, here is what lost ABC a star in my eyes. We got to the end of the meal, the server came over and asked if we would like dessert, we declined, as we mused over what we were going to do next that evening, she came back with the check!

The check?

Whoa... We hadn't indicated we were leaving or requested it, in fact I was thinking about having one more beer and my friend was thinking of a coffee. Maybe then needed to flip the table, but the place was mostly empty by now (We arrived around 7.15pm and it was now 8pm on a Friday night - not a hopeful sign), I looked at the entrance, maybe there was a sudden crowd - nope nothing.

It felt like the server was saying, you ate, now get out.

I have never been given a check by Wait staff without requesting it first. Maybe the staff wanted to leave early? I found it a very unfriendly gesture.

Will we be back? Unsure...

Slow Service, Cold food, Small Portions, High prices. My “Mile high Meatloaf” was small, cold, burnt and dry. I left hungry. Our other meals were also small and cold. I understand now why it was the only eatery not crowded after Ship’s graduation. Sorry but this was one of the worst meals I ever paid for. We will not be back.

NOT HAPPY WITH SHIP.. got seated waited for waitress to take drink order seemed as though no one wanted to even look at us as waitresses pasted are table plenty of times we ended up just leaving after 10 to 15min of waiting and nothing hope your other breweries are not like the one in ship. First time I ever been there not a good experience

Service is always lacking here. They always seem short staffed...can never get a refill and food takes 45 + minutes to get served...very obviously having sat under a warmer for some time. Food would be great if it was delivered in a timely manner. Manager is willing to do anything to  make it up...just wish he didnt have to.
Bloody $15 wings. Bartender took way too long to greet

Staff completely untrained and incapable of running a restaurant. 2 people completely overwhelmed while others standing around. Had to ask for a waitress, recieved the wrong drinks, waited over 30 minutes for some pretzels. Said pretzels cost nearly 9 dollars. Only upside is the food was good and it is nice inside.
Food was good, but service was a little slow. I definitely plan on revisiting in the future and updating my review, as they did open semi recently.
Too long of wait for no so busy of a night. Poor service quality. Felt rushed to eat.
Food was good, temp in restraint necessitated keeping my coat on
The food was good although the menu was limited. The seating seemed a little tight causing the waitresses to walk into seated customers chairs.

Very disappointed in this place. If you have a gluten allergy do not I repeat do not eat at this restaurant. They are not gluten friendly as they say they are. Food is prepared on Steam service as well as fried in the same fryers. That is not gluten friendly. The only reason they got one star is because you had to.
The Oktoberfest menu wasn't near or even similar to Germany/Bavaria
My burger wasn't the greatest, but overall it is a good place to eat. Service was prompt and friendly.
Foods not really great anymore .
It took 2 hours to get our food and when the food arrived it was cold
Was going to eat here until I was told that police were not welcomed. While I’m not an officer myself, I can’t believe that they wouldn’t serve someone due to their job. Terrible business model.

Food is amazing, beer is decent for a small brewery. They have a huge selection of barbecue sauces, all of which are amazing. Beer is appropriately priced for its quality, the food is very pricy, but also very high quality. Great atmosphere, sometimes a wait.
They have no idea what they are doing. After my third time, I have given up on prospect of this place getting its act together. It is a poorly run business.

Food and beer were fine, but I left a cash tip well over what it should have been for a $14 bill and when I got my credit card statement, they added an additional tip. Make sure you check your charges from this place. They have a dishonest person working there.
How can anyone leave a review when the place isn’t even open yet...seems like there a fake hype happening here!!!!
Sad to say we made the trip and we couldn't get seated. It's outside only and seating is very limited.
Would like to try this place. Havnt been there yet..

Jake boy are you close down for good if so that's 👍
..

Our first impression of the resturant had us extremely excited to eat here. It is well put together and plenty of open seating with space between. we were told to order from the table or we could order from the counter.  After looking at the menu we decided to order from the counter as we had questions with it being our first time here.  This is when the nightmare began. The girl upfront was one of the rudest, disrespectful, tasteless people I've ever encountered in my entire life. I simply asked if a side of fries
could be upgraded to another side and was told no we make everything to order in the rudest tone accompanying an eye roll. Both of us ordering looked at each other and wanted to cancel our our but e
The card already went through. When asking for a receipt we were snapped at for asking for paper.  This is a business trip and we would never bring any other colleagues to this establishment.  Even if the food is amazing unfortunately we won't we wont be back because of the watriess from....

I was very excited to order from here. I love BBQ brisket. But unfortunately not from this place. The Brisket was so tough I couldn’t even pull it apart with my hands let alone chew it. There ambiance is cool, but there food fell short for me. If your not slow cooking your brisket then You shouldn’t be selling it....

Bad Fathers Day Experience. It seemed like the perfect plan. We ordered our dinner for a 5:30 pickup before lunch. Uncle B's is one of our favorite places and we were happy to see them serving again. The food was normal Uncle B's BBQ which is very good for this area. The service, unfortunately, was much different. Our email confirmation said our meal would be ready at 5:30. We planned our afternoon around the pickup and I expected to have dinner shortly thereafter at home. We waited in a line of vehicles for an hour and 10 minutes before our food was delivered. But what's really frustrating was knowing they allowed themselves to be overbooked. I understand a restaurant only has so much capacity so you're online reservation system should cut people off from selecting a time where there are already too many reservations. You run a restaurant, this is not rocket science. Not only was the weight frustrating but there was nothing done to ease the suffering - two strikes. When I asked one person who pulled out with his food 10 minutes before ours was ready what time he placed his order,, he said 4:30. He was in line behind us and ordered after us, he had more food and still got his order before us. What was the logic in prioritizing orders? Maybe it was alphabetical... fortunately I am going to the Carolinas a couple of times this summer so I will get my barbecue fix elsewhere. It will be a while before I return.

Not the type of Father’s Day dinner I would expect. My wife asked me what I wanted and We decided on Uncle B’s since we tried there before and enjoyed it at their old place. 1st issue: Placed my order online at 430 to be ready by 630. Show up at 620 to a huge line for curbside... told to be patient because of the Father’s Day rush which was to be expected. Sitting in line with no updates until I called at 7 to find out the status of my order which was then brought out right as I asked. Others who came after me in line got there food way before us. If I wanted my food at 7 I would have asked for it to be ready at 7. It’s not like I just called right before I placed the order hours before. 2nd issue: the insanely small sizing of sides. Who gives a little sauce cup for sides and charges $3 for that?? Seriously small portions for the pricing. Also they were out of bbq beans, but they did refund those and gave us two more sauce cups of other sides as a sorry were out which was nice and unexpected. Food was eh, wings were very good but everything else wasn’t that great. The pricing across the board for what you get is just way high now and not sure I’ll be trying it again.

There was a long piece of plastic in the smoked chicken container. Obviosly, did not eat it. The KC BBQ sauce just tastes like you're eating straight ketchup. The ribs were overcooked. Penne for mac n cheese, really?! Tiny portions of sauce that are nowhere near enough for the meal. Also, for what I got the prices are laughable. Never again.

So disappointed!!! Service was extremely rude and couldn’t believe how they acted! After opening for quarantine they must have changed the staff and it went down hill and it is unacceptable. If they continue to treat their customers like this they’ll lose not only my business but all their business. This is a warning for those who are emerging from their homes to go to a nice restaurant for the first time in months, don’t come here!!!

Ordered curbside pick up for 2 people and had to sit in the line outside for over an hour. Never again. Not even close to worth it, and management was not too concerned. Used to like this place when they were small. Unlikely to return now. Too bad.
Great food poor service
