Very disappointing. Ordered a chicken Caesar salad and pulled pork sandwich. The waitress brought our plates outside, on plastic plates, and then said the silverware was inside, also plastic. The pork on the sandwich was cold and not very good, the chicken on the salad was very dry and the salad was swimming in a very sweet dressing that tasted nothing like Caesar dressing. Their sign said beer was $4, but they charged $6.80 on the receipt. We didn't even want to finish the food despite being overcharged.

Made a Catering order 4 days in advance. I pick up the order get home to look at what I paid good money for and the brisket was pulled apart sitting in tons of oil and fat.  So  basically it was pulled beef and after you drained to juices out i was left with half a pan of meat. Total rip off. Everything else was sub par. I don't recommended eating at this place.  I am not a Chef and I can make better food than this place.

those were the worst pork ribs I've tasted absolutely no taste they seemed boiled that's not barbecue and the sides I did not even want to try them because they looked over cooked, mushy. Total waste of money.
Just paid $9 for a couple pieces of brisket on a bun.  Unlikely to visit again at these prices.  Cost doesn't justify the quality, but it wasn't bad.
Got some burnt ends.  They were ok.
Visited here a few times while on way to a WBS Pens game. Great beers and food. Need to find an excuse to visit again ! Awesome atmosphere and very courteous staff.

Went for dinner here tonight with  my wife and another couple.  Food was very good and the beer was even better.  Had a great time although the only concerning observation was the cooks not wearing any masks at all?????
Bruh someone come take my order.

Worth the trip. Food was good, had one of their huge mac creations and smoked wings. Had the porter to drink which is a style I always appreciate as well as the lager and a Czech pils. Staff was great, plenty of parking and easy to find. Would definitely go back.

Good food, great beer, server Amber was awesome.  Wish they had more tables for old couples like us.  We can't the high tables, so they put us at a table for 6 and told us we may have strangers sitting with us.  We weren't very happy as we were celebrating, but Amber was so wonderful she turned to the whole evening around.  😀
Brisket was good. Beans had no taste at all.  Went to ladies room and they threw my takeout away. Normally we have very good experiences here and love the beer!  A little disappointed tonight.
Food was good , service was good but nothing really wowed us. Definitely need more seating ,we where put at a picnic table .
They have the best smoked wings in the area. Great home-brewed beer too.

Beer was ok. Fried pickles were good and so were the wings. The reason for the 2 stars is they had a special chicken and sausage risotto. It was disgusting it was more like a jambalaya one bite was taken from it and at first was only offered 5$ off the dish. Our waitress who was super nice but slow ended up taking it off the bill as we had asked to only pay for the side of mac and cheese from the entree because that was the only good part of the meal. 46$ later and definitely not worth the money.

EDIT: why would I pay full price for something that was offered as risotto and more like a jambalaya that tasted like it came out of a box of zatarans...the part that got me was "oh I only have the authority to take 5$ off the bill" why on earth would we still pay for something we didnt like and that she had to "talk to the cooks about it" the mac and cheese that CAME AS A SIDE INCLUDED IN SAID ENTREE. We wanted to pay the 4.50 for. Any decent restaurant would have taken the whole meal off or would have offered something different
I think 2 stars is fitting.
Namaste. ✌
Server was really nice.  Food was not that great.  They were out of a lot of beer selections.  Won't be going back.
Good food if you can get in. Staff is not properly trained. Waited 20 minutes and they let people that come in behind you seat first. Even though they had the same size party and came after us

I have my own smoker and smoke my own ribs and pork and have been to Memphis in may so I have an idea what BBQ should be. I like to taste the meat,  their pulled pork is smothered in sauce.  They have great atmosphere good beer.  BBQ is ok at best, steak flatbread amounts to nothing more than blue cheese pizza with a little meat.  My wife loves going there but this past week she got the wings and they were way over cooked and she said that we can just go down the hill to pasquale's.  Never a disappointing meal there.
Tge food was delicious. The service was decent. It's a nice little place
Food is excellent, drinks are great, band was really good. But $1 just for a side of bbq sauce is a little absurd
Beer is always tasty. I go there because I love their food from the pit. Smoked wing,pastrami, and pastrami mac and cheese are amazing!!!!! Try the blackberry "Down Da Line".
Benny s brew was really good.loved the French onion soup ..the corned beef sandwich was wonderful.homemade French fries!!I would highly recommend this place.i tasted a dark beer which was fantastic..

Took forever to get seated on a Friday night but we hung out on the back untill we were seated. Good beer selection and the food was pretty good. The waitress messed up and only put in half our orders but the rest of the food came out about 5-10 mins later when the rest of our party was almost done eating. No discounts given which is fine just could have been dealt with better for their error

My husband and I went to Benny's for a date night on a Tuesday evening, and ended up leaving. We were waiting for 15 minutes before anyone acknowledged us. We had to let the hostess know that we were standing there and not one person working there even welcomed us. We then asked how long the wait would be, and the hostess said that there were a few people ahead of us, a couple of tables were leaving. We asked more than 3 times what the wait time would be, and we never received a definitive answer. In the middle of hostessing, she was cleaning tables and serving people their food. I never seen a place disregard their paying customers like that. We will not be coming back to this establishment ever again. Understaffed?
Excellent food and beer. Will definitely be going back soon.

Waited 10 plus minutes with a relatively empty bar to get served.  Had dinner at the old man's place /Marty's.  Then stopped here for drinks after.  Bartenders were rude.  One completely ignored us.  The other finally came over after 10 minutes.  Ordered a sampler and had some questions as we were tossed up on our last 2 selections.  Asked the bartender what he recommended.  I quote his response was "oh I don't know any of them will do" and named off every beer I didn't select yet.  Great service.  Maybe it was because we were dressed nicely, thought we were preps.  Maybe its because we were not familiar faces.  Maybe it was because they were tired or having a bad night.  Don't matter to me as they chose to work in a customer service oriented environment.  Treat people with kindness and go out of your way or get the hell out of that line of business.  Very disgusted as I heard good things about this place and marty's is so good.  But after talking to more and more people everyone compliments their food but none that I spoke to complimented their beer or service.
Great beer as always, but original printed food menu needs replacing. Really looks worn and dirty. Go to a weekly new printed whole menu like the special one you do now or get a new designed one.
Good beer. Food not so much.
The beer was really good, any food from the barbeque is very smokey though. Service was great, wait was a bit long but not terrible. Overall pretty good.
Bingo nite was a fun time! Garlic parm wings were too salty!
Great food and beer. Worst service I've ever had. There's other places around that all 3 are great.

The atmosphere was typical of a microbrewery. The food was great with average portions. The beers that were available were also very good. My complaints would 1st, be the service. We arrived and the place was packed. We were told there was a 30-40 min wait, which was accurate. Once seated our server wasn't great. She seemed to be serving at least 5 tables. It took a while for the initial visit after several "I'll be right theres". Then it took 2 more attempts until we got the water that was ordered. My last complaint...There were beers listed on the wall. 2 different lists (one was maybe the "seasonal" list). There were also beers listed on the main menu, and finally on a beer menu. None of them seemed to jive. I ordered (in January) a beer listed as seasonal noting is was available IN Jan, that wasn't available. Once I was told what was available I was happy with 4 of my 5 beer choices. I'm not a bitter beer drinker and it was nice that the "ibu's" were listed.
Overall id go there again hoping for better service.

Very good food and service. Glad to see something like this in the Hanover area.

Only thing I didn't like was the very small and crowded parking lot.
Great staff. Food is great. Recommend the wings
Good beer and good food.
Terrific entertainment and great food too.  Enjoyed Benny's!
Good food great service place for all the family...
Good food at a reasonable price.  Atmosphere was nice too
The food and beer were both very good.
Had a beer, it was good. Didn't try food because it was way too busy to get a table.
Beautiful new building, good solid beers, great food, amazing bartenders

Pretty good food, service was good.
was there on a Friday night, live music was decent, but was too loud to carry on a conversation.
The mors I  come here the more I like if.
Great beer and food, so many choices
tastes like 90% of the stuff comes from frozen.
Good place,packed with me good beer and food
If you go here all the time it starts getting old quick
Good food and lots of it.
Wait was crazy long and food was just ok.
Very good food and beers.
Terrible... Just terrible... Waited 25 mins for a seat at the bar... Prices are insanely high. $10 for a corn dog.. Seriously????!!!!!!
Love the food
Great atmosphere!! Delicious food!!
The Tanya Harding Belgian Triple is delicious.  I had the noodle bowl with pork belly and it was delicious.   Many people were enjoying their pizzas. I may try one next time
Service sucks... food overpriced and marginal.
Service was just ok. Food a little disappointing

Social distancing was within tolerance.  Seating was personal. G. M. Stopped by to check on us. My salmon was very good. Vegetables need a little work. Brews are always good. Can't wait to eat inside.

Looked nice when coming in until got to our table, noticed the table had sticky areas that definitely had not been cleaned, besides that... Has lots of room area. Meal portion is big. I had a chicken  Caesar salad but I I don't  understand why there was lemon flavour on the chicken & Cesar salad. Was a bit over powering, even for someone like myself that enjoys lemon flavour.
Decent food and fun open atmosphere
Ate in for the first time in months.  Wait staff was masked up so were the patrons upon entering.  The tables were well spaced.  The service was good so was the food.

Extremely disappointed in the amount of food provided during the “Tacos & Enchiladas” special they are running. I doubled my margarita order, and with tip it came to $60 for pickup. First, I only got 3 enchiladas (4 are advertised). Second, I did not get the side of refried beans (as advertised). Thirdly, the amount of fillings you get for the DIY taco are very sparse - filled maybe 2 of 4 small tacos. Finally, my margaritas came in plastic drink cups with openings for straws - very difficult to transport home. I used to love going to McKenzie’s for happy hour but I will not be ordering more takeout during the social distancing. By the time I get home to realize what was missing, it’s not worth driving back.

Been here at least twice and both the food and service have been good each time. There's a good variety of beverages and I like that on Sundays they serve brunch.

Today I ordered one of the specialty coffees and it was hot and tasty. For $5, it hit the spot.

I tried the Bavarian pretzel appetizer and I must say that I was pleased and disappointed. The pretzel is huge - 5 of us split it easily. The cheese sauce was delicious but the stone ground mustard wasn't as good as it looked. Others liked it, but I preferred the cheese sauce and that was a little disappointing.

The kale and cauliflower mac and cheese was good, but nothing special. Others in the group loved the Mediterranean Salad, burgers, and items from the kids menu.

We will continue to come here. It's good and convenient when we're in town.

I rate this a 3, not because it's bad. On the contrary, it's good food and a nice place. I just never had anything here that makes it stand out from the rest that would make me want to push the rating to a 4. Been here multiple times and will return. On a 10 star scale it would be a high 6, almost 7. The outside dining area is nice during good weather.

Good place for a big crowd.  Good beer collection.  Eclectic menu, ranging from Italian fares to Asian influenced tuna salad.  Sliders and burgers are decent.  Salads are huge.  Soups are quite flavorful.  Good service.  Wait staff was attentive when we stopped there.

Being better than average gets McKenzie it's 4th star. Of course it has the expected good selection of beers. The food is better than expected. It has to be. The place is really large. They need volume. But the service was timely, competent, and not intrusive. Can't really complain about anything except don't expect the friendly intimacy implied by the name "pub." Think big restaurant with a big bar serving decent beer and food. Prices are a little bit higher than chain restaurants, but it's a bit better. I'll go back.

This place is a nice place to go hang out with buddies / team / party. But I'd say it suits non-vegetarians better and evenings would be spent much more merrily than noons.
Vegetarians might feel place okay or good(ish)...

Great place to host private event. The food was good and venue was good. Staff is friendly and supportive.

The event manager Keil (I may be spelling the name incorrect) was great in dealing with some minor billing discrepancies and very professional.

Only complaint I have is the Wifi and network in the basement venue was too bad. We could not play our songs shortlisted which was a bummer.

Beer had a weird after taste and the service was slow. To the point we had to go to the bar and refill our own drinks. Sad thing is we were only 1 of the 4 tables that had people there. The server knew she screwed the pooch and pull the old Its my birthday and I'm not suppose to be working today scam which my wife fell for.

I will give McKenzie’s 3 stars because the food was excellent. The fried cheese curds could have been a meal on their own. Outstanding! However, the incredible burger and appetizers were overshadowed by the very poor service. I was seated nearly 20 min before I even had my drink order take.  I saw my server three times in the two hours I was there. She didn’t ask if I wanted a refill until I was ready for my check. Customer service training is in need
Place is just OK beers are hit or miss. I do enjoy the Key West Tuna Salad. Service is good. Very generic Brewery.

Food was supbar with small portions and sloppy presentation. Server did not check on us once, food runners brought the food one plate at a time and didn’t know where it was to go. Had a large party. The only reason for the 3 stars is the location and options for alfresco dining. This is more of a bar than a restaurant, heavy focus on drinks and beer. I will not be returning to McKenzies much better food for the price right down the road.
A nice place to hang out with friends. I eat outside when conditions allow. Can be very noisy at times. Food is ok

Terrible experience today, the service was terrible, the food was inedible and made my husband sick, the temperature in the restaurant was hot and the music was terrible. We spoke with the manager and they were not receptive to the feedback or making it right for my family. We will not go back and I wouldn't recommend it to anyone.
The food and service overall was good. I do not recommend the Caesar dressing it's very pungent.

Food was great, service was a joke. Had to flag down another server to get drink refills. The when the server came back to the table, he had a noted attitude. I asked for a printed receipt,  he huffed and wasnt happy about it. Go for the food not the service
Good salad choices, some interesting stuff. My companions said the salads are great, I had the blackened shrimp kale bowl, and it was great.
Great food, Ok service.... They need more gluten free options, There isn’t much but salad and salmon which doesn’t suit everyone 😏 Maybe gf pizza dough or buns for sandwiches.

The wings used to be crispier,  maybe I got the wrong flavor (I picked bbq).  The service was kind of slow.  The waiter had to go check on our appetizers, unprompted, to see what was taking so long.    The food was ok.  I like salt,  but the shepherd's pie was almost too salty even for me.   We did see a rainbow.

There food is much better than the beer.  The IPAs almost ok.  When they opened years ago they were pretty good but the just have not kept up with the craft beer revolution and the elevation of well hopped beer.
Nice atmosphere. Food was ok. Poke was so bitter I couldn't finish it. 3 stars because mom liked it.
very good place, beers and a very extensive menu. good for sitting outside
Good good food and service.Nice atmosphere.
We've been there 3 times and each time the service was extremely slow!  The food was good, hence the 3 visits, but my husband declared we won't be going back a 4th time.
Comfortable, casual dining. Varied menu. Reasonable prices.

I don't even want to give it 1 star. Food was good,  but the service was HORRIBLE! The waitress needs to find another line of work! Drinks were late,  food order was so messed up and the last person received their foods when the other 5 were halfway through their order. Then waited 45 minutes for boxes and for someone to take our payments. They did try to rectify it by removing things off of our bill but in my opinion if that's what you needed to do to make a customer happy then I'll eat somewhere else that I'm happy to pay for the service and dinner experience. Oh,  and thanks for ruining the very special occasion we were there for. Never ever again.

Wellll...came here for my sisters birthday and will never come back. The hostess was miserable and didn’t seem like he even wanted to be there. Our waitress was terrible. Very rude, took about 10 minutes to get us waters when there were about 15 ppl in the whole restaurant. We had the nachos appetizer. I’m not sure how you can mess up nachos but they were awful, so plain and bland with no flavor. Our entrees all had seafood in them which I️ highly suggest not getting. The shrimp I️had was so undercooked and chewy I️ couldn’t even take a second bite. Not one of us finished our meals and we didn’t even bother taking home the leftovers. Back to our waitress, we asked for our check and a good 15-20 minutes later we got I️t. Not to mention there was only about 20 ppl in the whole restaurant so there is no reason for the lack luster customer service. I️ will never go back and will tell everyone I️ know to not dine there. Save your money and go somewhere else!

I have had a consistently bad experience the last few times I've been to McKenzie's. I tried to give them the benefit of the doubt but it's just been so awful. It starts with the host or hostess (yes - different people, same attitude) being completely disgusted to seat me the last 3 times I've visited. As if I'm inconveniencing them by just existing let alone trying to eat at their restaurant. I'm sorry for my patronage. The service is slow - today it took over 30 minutes for a member of the waitstaff to come over to the table to even say hello/take a food order. They are consistently leaving key ingredients off of my food - like bacon on the cobb salad. Not extra ingredients - one the meal is supposed to come with. What the heck happened, McKenzies? You used to be a favorite lunchtime and happy hour spot because of the casual atmosphere and deck in the summer. You've changed - I miss the old McKenzies :(

Came in as soon as they opened the doors. Large party of lacrosse players and families. Was had about 25-30 of use. Manager REFUSED to see us next to each other. Would only push tables together for groups of 10. So we decided to put the kids at one table and the parents at another. Only problem the manager then wanted to sit us on different sides of the dining room. The place was empty. It had just opened. Jen kept insisting that he was attempting to accommodate us and even went as far to say he had been doing this job for 7 years.  Needless to say our group left. While all the servers that had no one to wait on just watched us leave. Unfortunate for them and us as we decided to go else where.

Bottom line, manager was unwilling to keep our business.

McKenzies Malvern. I've been to a few of these restaurants throughout the PA area and this one comes up short.  Like most of them, the food and drink selection is good.  Probably why we keep going back. But the service at this one will probably prevent us from returning.  Website hours say 2am. Waitress says midnight. Cool, I get it. Business is slow and you want to go home.  But being made to feel unwelcome at 9:45 is a bit much. That was just the first time so hey chalk it up to someone having a bad day. Happens a second time and I'm starting to wonder if its us.  But were not college kids being unruly. Just a few couples out without the kids. Here we are for the third time and boom...happens again. This time I'm told that when ppl close their bill, mgmt is hesitant to let them reopen. But the porch was closing (10pm hour) and I don't know about you but it is standard to close so the exiting waiter can receive proper tip.  3 strikes and your out. I'm really hoping this had nothing to do with the race card because again, we are nothing but well paying professional adults enjoying a night cap but being made to feel like we are unwelcome well before closing time means I get the picture and will be sure to let everyone know.
Service and food were great?
Sausage was quite good! Beer, too.
Great food, great beer.. IPA's.
No service waited over 10 minutes told the hostess still no one came over. We walked out.
Served me cold soup after I waited fifteen minutes. No manager said sorry. No one said by when I left. 🖐 I’ll take my money somewhere that wants it !!!
Food was good, beer was good, service was good
The service tonight was terribly slow. Lots of people waiting 40+ minutes for food.
New servers were not trained..food overcooked

We were there on a Sunday and they were out of pork and sweet potato fries:(
Brisket was good. Many sauces available.
Turkey was good.
Mountain of very good fries.
Wait for food was way too long.

Food we did get was 4 1/2 stars, food they were out of and wait time was 1 1/2. I would give it another try.
The food was decent.  The meats were better than the sides.

Food was ok but way too pricey for me. A pulled pork sandwich, a burger and 2 sides with 1 drink were $40+.

Nothing to check.....if it's supposed to be cheaper than $40+ the girl at the counter needs to check her math! I wish I had my receipt, but it's ok..... won't miss much by not returning! ✌out

So I've  never had an issue before coming here. I ordered  2 dozen smoked wings and was told it will take about 30 minutes or so. Ok cool, I get there, there's one guy working the counter, taking orders over the phone, in person  & taking care of guests dining outside. As he gets to me he tells that  it will be 4 wings short can we replace those with something.  I say ok fries. I get back to the vehicle  and about 10/15 minutes  he says I know I told you 4 wings  short but it's actually 8 wings short. So my daughter  opted for a little boy/ guy grilled cheese...my only suggestion  is, the guy needs some help & get the wings which is a popular item counts sorted. Just my suggestion. Kerin not Karen
You dont expect much when you arrive but this place is great! Good food and friendly staff.
Great food and better service! I would give a 5th star had I had a chance to choose which sauce I wanted on my ribs. But they were still good, and beer choices are solid.

Only tried the Pulled pork so far but it was delicious!!!  Little weird how it was set up...We walked in to an empty restaurant besides the staff and not one person greeted us, told us it was ok to sit anywhere, showed us where to order, we were pretty much on our own.  Once we ordered a server brought the food out and was then very attentive.   There was girl who came in, had to be either an employee or a daughter of an employee or owner since she had free roam behind the counter who thought it was ok to talk very loudly to our server about her adventures from the night before...Would have been one thing if it was discussed in a place other than in front of the table we were occupying at the time :/
Small town BBQ with great flavor at a reasonable price. Homemade sauces, chicken, beef and pork we're all done well. A hidden gem off the beaten path.

Never knew this place was here. A little off the beaten path and must not do much advertising. We found this place by accident while looking on Penn-Live for something else. Do not let outside deter you... there is outside seating for nice weather. We had the beef brisket and you can choose your own sauce. The Golden Carolina sauce was my favorite. There are several others for those that like a hotter taste. Meat was tender with the exception of some of the edges that were done a little too hard. Macaroni and cheese was good for a side but Cole slaw not so much. Finding a place locally that can make a tasty BBQ like down South was a real treat. They also give 25 percent discount for military,police, fire and EMS.
Great food! A little pricey, but the value is there. Very friendly staff.

Had dinner here tonight, first visit in a few months. They now have table service which is sub-par at best. They still have self serve fountain drinks; self serve, that is, if they give you a cup.  Food was average. My Carolina BBQ sandwich was watery and my daughter’s little boy grilled cheese was so chocked full of grease she couldn’t eat it.  My advice Official BBQ, go back to what you once were and you will earn a few more stars.

Friendly service, very overpriced... I got a large fry and to add brisket, chili, and cheese it’s an extra $16!!!

The food was good close to Carolina bbq however due to the unadvertised price to add to my lg fry I wouldn’t come back. I did not like there were no prices posted for the extra I was thinking maybe like an extra $5 or $6 not $16.

That fry would of costed over $22! My bill over $70 for 3 people and this didn’t even include drinks. My suggestion advertise all pricing do not just nickel and dime customers. Close to average $24 per person in my party with no drink.

Good bbq, wasn’t impressed by sauces, and poor display of letting people know up charges.

When the place first opened they were very good.  Then they stopped caring a little and things were frequently not in stock but the ribs were still good when they had them.  Today we got 2 racks of ribs that are the driest and toughest pork I have ever eaten and had to settle for other sides because they didn't have any mac and cheese.  I don't even know how it is possible to make ribs that tough.  We will not be coming back.
I relied on other reviews when choosing to come here.. mistake.  Paid $40 for the worst brisket sandwich and mac and cheese.  Very disappointed.
Good service even better food

The food is pretty decent here. My family ordered a little bit of everything and it all tasted great. I would definitely go here again.

It would be great if the restaurant posted their catering/large/sharing portions more prominently. We ordered food for seven people and the given menu only had individual meals.
Food was great. The guy could have been a little more friendly. Apparently, they stay super busy. Be prepared to wait.

The food is pretty darn good. The guy behind the cash register was knowledgeable and very friendly. We LOVE the food, here! I would give it 5-stars but there is only one small detail that keeps them from the "best" ranking. It takes a very long time to receive your order. I do understand, however, that this contributes to the freshness of your food. Your best-bet is likely to call-in about 45 minutes ahead and submit your order. That way you will not have to use all of your cell phone battery entertaining yourself while you wait :)
Not a lotta food for a 35 buck sampler platter 😑

Based on the quality of the Brisket and fries, I would have given this 4 solid stars.  However the price is what drove this down. I got a Brisket Sandwich, regular fries and cup of water and it cost over $19.  If you go here make sure you pre-qualify with Quicken Loans.  I understand food costs have gone up, but I could have had a sit down steak dinner for probably a few dollars less.  "Official" Ripoff

First impressions of this place werent the best. As it's looks dirty, and run down. Not sure if they are remodeling but half of building is tarped off. If you know what to look for it's obvious this place could be much much cleaner and more sanitary. This place is like a mission BBQ without the franchise money. They came across as struggling for income or poor management on several occasions. They do provide great portion sizes besides on sides which is the cheaper item to give you more of. But they do give you alot of fries. The staff seemed very nice. The food items are limited to what's made daily. Another reason poorly managed or short on income. If you can't provide half your menu on a regular business day something isn't right. Especially with sides. There was a list of items they were out of. And after we ordered our 4th and 5th choices of food the foundation system was also empty of it's choices with no stock to resupply. Once the food came I have to say I was very impressed. I enjoyed the main courses we all did. The fries were good and fried in clean oil. The burgers were cooked to proper tempature. The pulled pork was great and the BBQ sauces brought it home. The pork ribs weren't as good as the beef ribs would have been I'm sure but they were seasoned very well and a very good value. The rub on the chicken wings was very good. Almost in my top 10 which is very good as I've eaten thousands of types from all over the world. But to each his own I'm sure you will absolutely love these wings and the flavor they come with. Overall with better management and a cleaner appearance this place could be better the mission bbq in my opinion in many ways.

Tastey barbecue with a family-eatery feel. Took about 20 minutes to get the food that we ordered, but once we got it, the portions were large and the range of barbecue sauces offered was excellent. The barbecue sauce made by officials was by far the best and they gave us a large basket of fries which easily fed our table of 4 to split. I would try a few other places in town before going back, but I would make my way back there.

We had turkey and pork BBQ sandwiches. Good smokey taste but not overpowering and the portions were substantial on the regular sandwich. We were split on favorite sauce. Bother traditional and Carolina sauces were great. Good tang and the right amount of spice.  Regular size fries feed 2-3 and they were good and crispy. Great value.  Would go back.
Great food and friendly service. The portions are pretty small though so be prepared for that. I got a half rack of ribs and while amazing tasting, it just wasn't enough.

There has been a new owner for awhile and their food is still good but it's just not the same. I loved their black label burger - but now the brisket isn't in the burger paddy but on the burger paddy and it just isn't the same.

Edit (6/12/2018): Official has been sold and is under new management, and while it's still good, it's simply not to the same level of quality it once was.

Please be careful. Eating at this establishment will ruin your ability to eat at other BBQ joints. The staff are always kind and if there's a problem with your order they will make it right. If I'm being honest, I would say that the ribs aren't the best (they aren't bad, just not great), but everything else is the best I've ever had.
The brisket is good but the turkey is better and you need to get the sweet potato fries.  The fries are awesome!

Here is my official review of Official BBQ. I visited this establishment for the first time last night and was truly disappointed with the product. I ordered a brisket sandwich, large mac & cheese and an order of fries. Let's start off with the brisket sandwich. The portion size was on the smaller side, the meat was very fatty however it did have good flavor and was very tender. Then bun matched the portion as it was a little bigger then a dinner roll. Next item was the mac and cheese which are like a soup. When I mean soup I mean soup. I observed the gentleman making the mac and cheese and it seemed a little unorthodox on how they prepared it. He took cooked noodles and ladled a scoop of melted cheese on to them, stirred it and then placed it in the container.  I took two bites out of this Mac and cheese (soup) and threw it away as it was very gummy and did not have much flavor at all.  The french fries that we got were overcooked and again had no seasoning on it so they were very Bland. Then it comes down to the price, two meals came out to $50. All this money for what! We both threw away the Mac & cheese soups! Picked through the order of fries to get the best ones and had to pick the fat off the brisket. A buddy referred me to this restaurant and unfortunately I will not be back. As a side note when I went to pick up the food the parking lot was empty so there should not have been an issue related to them being busy and producing subpar food.

Delicious BBQ place. The pork sandwich with the Carolina BBQ sauce was delicious. Some of the best  fries I've ever had. The potato salad left a bit to be desired. Overall, I highly recommend Official.

Monumental waste of time. Employees don't care. Guests two back from me with more complex meals than a single burger served first, because they were regulars it friends? Waited 15 minutes to be told one single 'fresh burgers take longer'... Meanwhile they were just starting to cook it as I walked up for a refund. Guests walking back and forth from behind counter. Never will go back here. Shame.
The Mac & cheese is incredible! I did find myself wishing for a broader range of BBQ sauces. But other than that, I was impressed. I'll be back for sure

After 25 years in Atlanta, I get serious cravings for good southern-style BBQ! This vinegar-based BBQ was not what I'm used to but it was flavorful. I tried the pulled pork sandwich which was piled high with savory 'que, but the bottom of the bun was soggy and hard to hold.
The mac & cheese did not disappoint and even the small size was a good portion! Almost as important as the food is the iced tea which was right on point.
Two suggestions: post descriptions of the various sauces that are available so patrons don't need to ask, and put some better glides on the chair legs!
Very good bbq and their burgers are delicious. Skip the mac and cheese!
Not terribly clean. Food adequate.  Service slow.

I have been purchasing pizza from the Brewing Co since they opened in the Farmer's Market-The pizza has always been delicious! Today, I tried the new Taproom for lunch. I brought one of my coaches for a tennis meeting and we were both very disappointed. The service was nonexistent. I waited forever for my iced tea (after a reminder)and then again for a spoon and sweetener. I was asked if I wanted a receipt and I said that I did. It never came. It required us getting up and interrupting our waiter while on his cell to get he receipt after waiting for over 10 minutes at the table.  While we were eating, two managers/owners (?) literally stood in front of our booth and discussed  the bathroom and flooring issues. Really? There were only 2 other customers in the restaurant. They couldn't go anywhere else? It was so incredibly inconsiderate and inappropriate. To be that clueless was beyond bothersome and unprofessional.  Customers don't particularly want to hear people discussing issues and business matters when they are trying to eat lunch. We couldn't even complete our own meeting. I was intending to bring the family over the weekend, but have changed my mind!

So I love this place and their pizza. But last night they randomly put a 15% gratuity on my check (party of 2) and of course I didn’t expect that so I tipped on the total anyway. Got home and realized why the bill was so high. Definitely my mistake but I bet I’m one of MANY people making it. This feels kind of shady. I’m all for servers making money and I always tip 20% or more. But it really feels like they tricked me into tipping 35%. And that’s not cool. Especially when I had to seat myself and order from a window (ie they didn’t really “serve” me much at all). Just a warning to everyone else to watch out for an auto tip at this place.

Probably the worst dining experience I've had in Philadelphia in over a decade. The server was borderline aggressive in her service. It was like she was trying to get us out the door as quickly as possible. At one point I even turned around to see if there was a wait for seats . . . Nope. Plenty of empty tables.
The calamari was the saltiest thing I've ever eaten. Which is a shame because they were cooked well and otherwise would have been perfectly fine, if someone hadn't dumped a full cup of salt on them when they came out of the fryer. Ordered a pizza for take out and it was wrong. Server made a half-hearted offer to correct it, but by that time I was so ready to get out that I just took it as is. Bill came out at full cost. Ended up giving the pizza to someone out front because I couldn't stomach the thought of eating anymore of their food (especially after my companion told me they had gotten violently ill after their last visit).
Unfortunately, I'll never go back. This place used to be really good and the go-to in the neighborhood. No more. Hope they can turn it around.

The staff was very friendly and helpful.  The beers were all very tasty.  We did not have any of the food.  They also offered a beer discount for my American Homebrewers Association membership!  Cheers!
Really good pizza and beer. BUT NO AIR CONDITIONING IN A 95 DEGREE HEAT WAVE.
Website states food is available but currently the kitchen is closed. Bartender said we could bring in food but was really iffy on local restaurants in the area . Beer was ok.

I visited with some friends and we had a horrible time. The acoustics inside are terrible. Maybe thirty people inside, and my friends and I couldn't hear each other from a foot away. Upon approaching the bar, the bartender ignored us for over ten minutes. After ordering our food, I watched people that arrived after us get half our order before we got any food. And when our order number did finally get called, I actually had to confront another customer who tried to take our food. A redeeming quality, the beer was great.

Rather disappointed. I sat at the bar for 20 minutes and neither bartender acknowledged me dispit it not being busy at all. It was 9:30 on a Friday night. Only reason one finally asked if I wanted anything was because she saw me grab my twenty (that had been sitting out since I sat down) and put it away. At that point I decided not to waste my money and just told her I didn’t want anything at that point. In all both women walked pass me at least four times, ignored eye contact, and served people that arrived after me multiple times.... Also the owner might like to know that the bartenders saw me drink from my friends glasses several times but never waited on me let alone check my ID. The little bit of beer I tasted was good though but not worth the drive and frustration caused from poor service.

Very rarely do I ever feel the need to complain about a restaurant. As someone who has spent their fair share of time running ranch and wearing the ugliest pair of anti-slip shoes on the market, I felt the need to put my two cents in. For starters, we enter the restaurant on a relatively slow Sunday afternoon. The decor looks nice, and the atmosphere appears hip and cozy. There’s two bartenders and about 5 customers (my party included). We belly up to the bar on flat, metal barstools straight from the ‘Uncomfortable and Useless Furniture’ catalog of 1981. I should also mention there’s no place to hang your coat so you’re either holding it, wearing it, or sitting on it. We ask the bartender for an ice water to start. To which she replies, “Our water is self serve, it’s on the wall over there.” Oh....how convenient. The “self serve water station” is a tap drilled into a barrel lid with a stack of dixie cups next to it. I feel my hatred for hipster culture grow at the very sight of it. After getting my own lukewarm water I take a sip and immediately realize it’s the exact same water I taste when I accidentally open my mouth in the shower of my crummy studio apartment 2 blocks over. While sipping my Chlorine Colada I went over the menu which was drawn on the trendy chalkboard wall with trendy chalk by trendy people. Meaning that if you are at the other end of the bar you have to walk down to look at the menu, I can’t make this stuff up. The menu did look great, and as a fat Pennsylvanian I was eyeballing the six dollar pirogies. During the somewhat crawling wait time I got bored of my iceless tap water and asked the bartender the infamous question; “Yall got Coke or Pepsi.?” The bartender then informed me that they have a “bottled cola.” What she brought me was a real cane sugar cola that was actually quite delicious and a great change of pace. However, at $2.50 a pop, you’re not going to be enjoying too many. Out of the corner of my eye I see the chefs placing dishes in the pick up window, I’m waiting with bated breath. It’s been an hour since my last meal and I just can’t wait any longer, this is America. I’m waiting for the server to run it on over when all of the sudden DJ spatulas picks up a mic and says over the loud speakers, “ORDERNUMBAHTWENYFIVEPIEROGISBREKFASTSANDWICHANDCHICKENTACOS.”

I’m hurt. I’m actually offended. We’re in a restaurant paying top dollar for food and beverages, getting our own water, and now we’re expected to get our own food. Is this a brewery or a Sheetz whose father didn’t love him enough? I walk up to the pickup window and there it is...
There it is....
The reason this place gets one star...

Three
Mini
Pirogies...

Six
Freaking
Dollars....

I kid you not, there is a plate of cold, wet lettuce, and nestled between the leafy greens are three, cute little baby pirogies. I didn’t know wether to eat them or tuck them in and read them a story. I reluctantly take them back to the bar, plop down, and eat them within seconds. But wait a second. What? These things don’t even taste good? Yes believe it or not when you put hot food on cold lettuce it tends to cool it down substantially. Just like whenever you try to be unconventional and season pirogies with saw dust and pencil eraser shavings, they don’t taste very good. I’m furious.

We ask for the check and it gets delivered.....in.....a novel. Yeah, those little leather wallet things were just too mainstream. The bill is $9.01 and there’s a tip box. I truly feel bad for these poor bartenders. I can imagine alot of people feel the same way I do at this establishment. If I have to get my own water, and my own food, why should I tip you? Believe me, I know it’s a terrible thing to say, but honestly. Six dollars for three mini pirogies and I have to get them myself. Why should I, as a customer feel obligated to tip? Regardless, I really hope they get hourly as well, and furthermore, I really hope the Voodoo brewery can turn itself around. It’s in a great location and we need more young places around. Best of luck in the future.
I DONT LIKE THEIR STAFF THE WAY THEY TALK TO US IS JUST LIKE THEY DONT HAVE MANNERS.
it was great, not sure why the service was horrible today

This former train station is now home to Saucony Creek Brewery. Great beer and drinks go with their great food. Indoor and outdoor seating available.  Enjoy your drinks or meal on the spacious patio that includes a fire pit and music. Private off street parking adjacent to the patio. Casual attire and moderate prices.

Extremely pricy with extremely small portions

I wanted to love this place. The gentleman that greeted us was incredibly friendly. We loved him, and wish we had gotten his name. Loved the concept of the venue. It’s gorgeous! Drinks were amazing. Great beer list. But the food sadly was subpar. While the owner has responded to other reviewers that they use fresh, local ingredients, source from farms and such, I’m afraid to say that perhaps it’s the cooks whom are inexperienced then. Our pork belly kabobs were terribly overcooked. The flatbread’s bbq sauce wasn’t flavorful, and I didn’t even see any chicken on the slice I got. Mac n cheese bites were flavorless. The street corn was cold upon reaching our table. The ear of corn was really small (and growing up in the Midwest I know what a decent size should be). The flavor of cheese and spices was great, but would have been a lot better had the corn not been cold like I said. Overall, I was incredibly underwhelmed with our small plates. Perhaps the entrees are better? I really enjoyed the aesthetic, just wished the food was better. And coming from someone that knows her way around a kitchen and has a sophisticated palette, this fell flat.

At this point, I’ve been sitting here for over two hours, I’ve seen my waitress 4 times - once for her to tell us she was bringing water, two times to get adult beverages and get a food order, and now my whole table can’t wait to leave and wants to cancel our food orders we put in an hour ago. Seems like the staff needs some coaching and they could be new which is ok but they need to figure it out. I would’ve had 3 beverages by now and eaten dinner anywhere else I’ve been during COVID. I know times are tough in COVID but this is actually ridiculous. Im a very understanding and kind person but I’m also looking at plenty of other tables who have been here longer without food too. I think folks are just hungry at this point. People are talking about getting fast food on the way home.

The band is kicking tonight though, kudos to the entertainment!

We were served on the 18th of September at 5:45 pm. The host at the door was very friendly and sat us promptly. Upon being seated it took us 20min for a staff member, Marrisa (Melissa?), to come to our table to ask for drinks. Her first response was to apologize that she was seated with three tables simultaneously and was overwhelmed.  Having worked in the service industry, I understood and said no problem. We placed our orders at this time, approximately 6pm.

Fast forward 30 more minutes, our drinks were refreshed and our appetizers came out. Great. A little lengthy but given the forewarning I understood completely.

Here is where the service took a turn for the worst. The waitress, who was overwhelmed to begin with, frequented our table every 20 to 30 minutes, promising our food to come out shortly. We waited and watched as others were served and the kitchen door turned ever so frequently.

Check in 5:35 pm, now approaching 815pm and 4 drinks later, agitation and annoyance had set in. Our food finally arrives close to 8:30pm. I promplty asked for this to go as I had only budgeted 2.5 hours of time to be in here total with prior obligations. The bill was a thick $86 for 4 drinks, an app consisting of 4 meatballs and food that was 2 and a half hours late. The waitress apologized and said they were not prepared for the crowd. Panning my head around and given the current pandemic restrictions, there were 4 other groups in the etablishment, i scratched my head confusingly.  I tipped her fairly as clearly this was not her fault.

This was a Friday night, unacceptable for an establishment that, although has good beer, charges $20 for a piece of bread with grilled chicken on it.

I hope some remedy is in order. This is piss poor service and overpriced food.

I wish they offered beer flights. Wait time was too long to get served, but I attribute that to the place just opening. Will definitely be back.

Really like this place. The beers are great. I particularly like some of the sours they've been making. The food is very good too. The fries are curly. Seriously, what sandwich accompaniment is better than a seasoned curly fry?

I went to this place to find a good vegetarian meal and beer, which I certainly did. The brewery is located in a bit sketchy area, but most of the areas look sketchy in Philadelphia, so don't allow prejudice to keep you away. Next to the colonial houses, there will be urban & modern (trailer-like) architectural masterpieces. There is nothing else next to the brewery. But once you enter the place you will be surprised with the interior decor, beer selection & food.
The interior is very interesting with mirrors in the old frames hanging from the walls, long bar and few tables here and there.
Make sure to try all of their beer types ($3 for 4 oz). For those who prefer mild and/or wheat beer .- definitely go for their Dr. Double Wheater. They also have interesting stouts and IPA(s).
What I loved the most is their vegetarian burger and veggie-ribs! The ribs just blue my mind away - they tasted so real that, as a vegetarian, I was hesitant to eat them.
Staff is professional - no empty conversations. Bar tender asks for the order and brings you the food. Unless you are a hot chick or a handsome single guy.
If you are a beer lover - stop by and have a drink. If you are a vegetarian, although the food is delicious - there are many other places in Philly offering great food.

Cute little place with great decor and it's very open and welcoming. It was very much a hipster place though. The food was mediocre both times I went but it is a brewery so I'm impressed they even had food. The beer was decently priced but out of all of their selections there's only one I actually like and of course it was out the last time I went. The first time I went the guy actually let me sample some of the beers instead of making me just pay out right for them so I could actually find one I liked. This time the guy made me pay for each one and every single one was pretty gross so I feel like I wasted money. To be fair I don't really care for beer so I'm not the best judge but they don't exactly serve liquor. My partner enjoyed the beer he had and he even liked mine. So I guess that's a good sign haha. That's the main reason I gave it 4 stars. He said the beer was good and I enjoyed the layout of the place and the decor.
23 Code violations 10 serious.  Great place to go for some droppings    If the owner replies LICENSE AND INSPECTIONS WILL BE YOUR NEXT VISIT

So much better than I expected.  Beer variety was distinct and good.  Food was high quality and unexpected ( smoked ketchup!).  Departing the bar around dusk to the sounds of band practices in adjacent places makes one recognize that this venue is in its golden age.  Just go here and enjoy!

Enjoyed my visit early on 2/25/17.  They had 8 beers on tap and 2 wines (a red and white).  I don't think they serve liquor.  The beers I tried were good.  They serve them in a variety of sized pours (I think 4 oz, 10 oz and 16 oz).  They will not serve more than one 4 oz pour at a time to a person, so getting a standard type of flight of samples is not possible.  I'm hoping that rule changes, but if you are with a group of people, then everyone can get a 4 oz of a different type and share.  I really liked the Camellia Sinensis (IPA).  All the tables were high tops with stools for sitting.  So, coming here with an infant and a 2 year old was very tricky.  It is not so much a kid friendly kind of place (high tops and no high chairs).  The menu was brief, but adequate for a brewery.  The servers and workers were pleasant and seemed to take a lot of pride in the food and beer.  I'm hoping in the summer they will use their sidewalks for some regular tables, chairs with backs and obtain some high chairs.  I'll definitely be back (sans children).
