I've been to Roy Pitz several other times in the past without problem. It's a standard large bar with good drink and foot specials. You can usually find a table and have relatively decent service.

But I'm writing this review today because my last experience was... weird. I went in with 8 people on a Saturday at 4 oclock and the hostess asked me "Well how long do you think you guys are staying? Will you be out by 6?" I said, "I don't know, do you have an event or something and need us out by 6." She replied "no but we have a lot of reservations." Mind you, the bar was mostly empty at this time, with probably 10-15 people in it total. She reluctantly sat the 8 of us at a circle table with 5 chairs and said we could pull up extras. All the while, there are long bench style tables that probably fit about 15 people, completely open.

When 6 oclock approaches, the hostess comes back to us and asks us if anyone's leaving or willing to give up their chairs. There is still plenty of tables open without any line or wait at the door and we're still actively eating and drinking. By chance, some friends were planning on leaving and she hurriedly took away their chairs.

In short, we should have asked to speak to the manager because the idea of seating 8 people at a table that's far too small for them, then rushing them out of their chairs all for "upcoming reservations" is insane. But we didn't, in an attempt to enjoy ourselves and be flexible. So here I am, writing this review.

This place has a young crowd, good food, and historical architecture. The Irish Prig BBQ sauce on their wings (which they give a lot of) is one of the best BBQ sauces I've had. I will never be back, however.

Their beer is run-of-the-mill brewpub style beer. They do have good sours, which is hard to come by. But nothing warrants a growler fill or anything.

Where they really fall short is their seating. The large "picnic style" seating is aweful. Nobody wants to sit
2 inches next to a stranger, as their mess spills over into your area. I asked for a table, but got a bus stop style bench seat instead. There is plenty of room for 2 people seasting if you cut the tables up. I find it extremely disgusting to sit within a dirty napkins distance to someone I do not know, as their conversations, loud chewing, and mess spills over into my partie's area. I understand it is nice for large parties, but it's not for small parties (2 people), who mostly utilize these seats. It is not a beer hall. Just get tables. I guarantee you'd get more happy customers. I will not be one of those though.

I've been buying the 50 wing dinner for some time now, at $36 per order, and they are quite good.  However, I ordered them again this evening, only to find that they have upped the price to $49!!!!
Penn Brew has been my favorite place in the area for wings/cheesesteaks...but no more.  Terrible customer-relations.
EXPENSIVE. Just raised all prices 1.00 again today. Good food but not worth the constant up in price.

Updated review: They have their first brew on tap now. It's a ginger pale ale and it's delicious. Also recommend their house muscles. Nice ambiance and a fun and friendly staff. Enjoy myself every time I can make it here. Looking forward to when they expand their hours

This place has a unique menu and the food tastes good,  however they don't do any of the classic bar foods and the food is overpriced.  A lot of the food is locally sourced and the drinks are great.  $5 for potato wedges which were the equivalent of 1 potato deep fried and served with mayo.

Our friend, Bill, took us to lunch in Bangor and to be truthful I wasn't expecting much. I was proven wrong. Everything and everyone at Bangor Trust Brewing was great. I had the house mussels which were very tasty. I used their freshly made bread to sop up the beer and chorizo sauce. My husband had the lasagna that was also excellent. Our waiter, Cory, helped us with beer and hard cider selection. Leo came out of the kitchen to introduce himself and tell about upcoming events. We will definitely go back with friends.

Not great beer (I was told, I didn’t have any), limited menu, service was incredibly rude when my mother asked about gluten free for me. I understand that some people are ignorant and think it’s a fad but I have celiac and get very sick when I have gluten. Many bakeries and restaurants offer gluten free so this isn’t a ridiculous question. We’re very disappointed in the attitude from the service and definitely won’t be back.
Hip place to hangout n have a beer,  wine from local places.. Cool vibe.. Had a live band the night we went,  which I enjoy!
Food was good. Service was good. Will definitely go back.  Loved the games.

Flatbread pizzas were very tasty. We had the 3 wild mushroom and cantelope/prosciutto. The wedgies were good with the roasted red pepper and garlic sauces.
Beer wasn't to my husband's liking.
The building and decor was nice.
Music, old classic rock, not from radio station that I ever heard, was a nice surprise.
Service people were pleasant.
I would give it 3.5 stars, 4- if the brewed beer was better.
Great to see another great place growing in Bangor. Excellent differa pizza's cold beer and great owners that always  support the community!

Meh. Really wanted this place to be a keeper.  The food was good but the service unprepared. I was there for all you can eat mussel night and after 2 hours, I sampled one batch. Sorry to say I won't be back unless they hire more staff and find their rhythm in the kitchen.
Good beer and good food. Well worth stopping in. All natural ingredients from local providers
Very friendly people,  nice concise variety of brews, next time we are going to check out the food!
The server was so sweet, food was nothing to brag about and they ran put of some sort of sausage as well as straws. I also didnt enjoy eatting out of a paper plate.
The beer and food are solid. Love these guys
Nice place.  Beer needs to mature

Food is always top notch.
Disappointed they got rid of the Frites, was our favorite item there.
Good place with decent brews
Authentic and cool
Before you go  call ahead for the hours! Hours posted on Google are incorrect! Hours posted on the door are also incorrect! Arrived around 1:30 on Friday and they were closed.
I came here and ordered the seafood and got terrible food poisoning. I woke up and threw up for an entire day because of their rotten food. You couldn’t pay me to eat here again, disgusting !

Racist establishment. Servers were very incompetent or very prejudice to customers of color. I wish I didn’t have to put any stars. The server never came back to clean off our table. Several of our orders were wrong or never came out. He charge the wrong amount to each cards. The population was PRIVILEGED and snarky. Thumbs down for this place. I will never go back. Relax it isn’t even truly the “mainline” it’s ARDMORE.

The food and drinks were mediocre for the price. I got bourbon blossom and I could barely taste the bourbon. I got Morocco Salmon which was ok but the side dishes were mediocre.  Also the waiters kept on wanting me and my company to leave even tho it was no where closing time. Rude!
Giant restaurant but at 5:30 you have to hover and stalk people to get a table which might work for some but not a family. Not a way to run a restaurant.
An excellent selection of craft brerws.  I enjoyed a couple of glasses of their Saison.  Also, the was a good three piece band playing at the time with no cover charge.
Good beer and a great atmosphere!

We only went to meet some friends. The live music was louder than a nightclub from my college days. The drinks were mediocre. The food was alright. The waiter majorly screwed up our bill and was irritated that we caught the mistake. They snuck in a $7 cover charge at the end as well. Won't be back.

The football match was more of a priority than the customers. We were not greeted when we walked in, had to go find someone and ask when we'd get a table. The server was nice, but there was absolutely no service. It was just a place for people to sit and watch the game. Check if there's a Liverpool game on before you go, because unless you're looking to watch the game, you won't be eating.

This place is terrible. Only 2 tables of guests were dining at the Saturday 6pm. We were first driven by Groupon voucher bought in advance our Philly trip. When we came back from the aquarium in a chilly evening, attempting to get food and back to hotel. The server first refused to serve us with takeout order, which this rule wasn't mentioned in the voucher. As I checked with him my understanding is correct, I asked him to talk to the owner standing aside, the answer was still no. I then asked the owner directly, he arrogantly refused the take out order and insisted this policy. After that, he continued humiliating us by saying that we customer are only trying to get things CHEAP. OMG, I thought you have the right to utilize Groupon to do expend your opportunity, why you want to fool customer? And anyone who will refuse a tired family with toddler kid in a cold weather for serving food, and they have the voucher? Without humanity I suggested you stay away from this restaurant.

I don't think they do "brunch".

Terrible service from what I was told was the owner and served raw pork. What the actual hell.

I saw something circulating on social media about a "scotch egg festival" and thought, "sounds like a fun brunch"

We get there at 1pm, 3 hours after opening , expecting it to be busy, and there's 2 guys at the bar, 8 or 10 women at a ticketed wine tasting, and what I was later told was the owner who was hosting them. There were no other customers or servers.

One of the women comes up to us, introduces herself by saying, "this is akward, I'm a friend of the owners... I think I have the right menus... And you can sit anywhere" Perplexed, we choose seats and she leaves us with a variety of menus and goes back to her wine tasting.

About 10 minutes later, a family with small children walk in, and the owner walks over and asks if we know what we want. Now, at this point... We haven't even been given water. I explain we're there for the scotch eggs and request the water and he walks away, quickly coming back with 2 small menus with the options... And no water... And leaves us to go back to the wine tasting.

At this point, we're at a loss as to stay or go. He comes back over after pouring another round and we explain we'd like to try one of each and he nods, and walks back to ticketed event.

We are now 20 minutes into waiting for water when a server walks in in flurry. She begins setting out silverware, and gets us the waters and we believe actually takes our order? We're not really sure because 15 minutes after that, she comes back to let us know they're out of one of the options and lets us choose another. At this point another 4 top has come in and sits next to us and I just want to lean over and tell them to run.

A full(I am NOT exaggerating) 50 minutes after being seated we receive the 5 scotch eggs we ordered and beging to tuck in. I'll be honest... The cooked ones were quite good... But after we tried the completely RAW one, we lost our appetite.

Knowing that we would not see the server again(no one had been by to check on us, or refill our drinks) I walked up to the bar and requested the check.

While settling up, we explained that one of the eggs had been served raw and to be careful, because we knew the 4 top next to us had also ordered the scotch eggs. She apologized profusely and explained she would have taken it off our bill if she had known, but at this point, I just wanted to get gone ASAP.

I see they do a lot of fun events and this was our first time checking out the place before committing to a trivia night or drag show.

I have never been made to feel so unwelcome in such a sad, empty restaurant. A simple explanation from the owner when we were first seated that they were waiting for a server to come in because of a call out, or comping us even a cup of tea for an almost hour long wait would have gone along way to making the trip and meal a little bit more palatable.

Needless to say... There's other places I can see drag and play trivia. And so can you 😣

Even though they may be short staffed right now, the food is still delicious and the atmosphere is still fun. I've tried Thursdays prime rib special cooked medium and it was well seasoned and cooked properly. Their "Zingers" or house made chicken strips are crunchy, juicy, and delicious with whatever sauce you choose. (I chose honey bbq). Their Alfredo with broccoli and blackened chicken is delicious. They gave me shells instead of fettuccine noodles and It was even better than what the regular noodles are. If you're looking for their Mac and cheese, it's good but if you don't like chives or a Parmesan taste you won't like their MAC. Overall, a great restaurant besides the wait time. Amazing servers and support staff (the little who are there). Once they have a full staff I believe that place can be 5 starts no doubt.
Nice atmosphere.. similar to other locations...seemed a little short staffed..long wait for order.  Maybe due t covid-19. Food excellent.
Great food and service...pricing is ok.
1st time here.  Food was good.  Service was good.  Bathrooms were dirty and the floor was sticky.  If the Bathrooms wouldn't have been dirty I would have given a higher rating.

The food was good for sport bar fare.  Covid has led the establishment to cut back on servers so service is a little lacking.  Overall we enjoyed our meal (been about  year since we last dined out) but felt lost in this restaurant with it's light clientele  in attendance.
Great food and service...pricing is ok.

Thought Miller’s Ale House took pride serving those who serve the country. Not only were 8 present who serve this country but only got discount for one military member. The server took no pleasure to correct the mistake. Also the server was hardly there to refill and check if everything was ok. It wasn’t busy as we were the first customers to come in to Miller’s Ale House at 11:30am. We had the stack of plates and multiple empty cups to show that we were not being attended frequently.
Was told the wait would be 25 minutes. Been sitting for well over an hour. Will not clear tables that are empty. And also a -5 stars for following wolf's guidelines. Would never recommend.
The people who work there are very nice and the food is good and very good prices
Can never get in. Wait is too long. Not well organized and staff not friendly
Ordered a steak cook medium  came out like shoe leather. Replaced it took it, off the bill but it came out like shoe leather again
I ordered the Prime burger and it tasted pretty good but there was only 2 tiny shavings of prime rib on it.  I was pretty disappointed.

The service was terrible. We where the first once in and our food didn't come out till very late. We keep stacking our plates and cups on the next table cause they never got retrieved. The new lasagna had no flavor and our military discount was not provided to group.
Great place, service was great, I enjoyed myself and the service that we had from Joey he was great 👍👍👍👍

I used to love the guys here but lately it is lacking.  I got a steak asked for med well and it was a hockey puck. Waste of money.  Got a Ceasar salad and lettuce was brown.  Not good considering they aren't serving inside.

Disappointed..... food over cooked,   took forever to get food, there was a kid 7yo is there that was soooooo loud couldn't believe parents or restaurant staff asked her be quieter   ( no haters  im use to kids in restaurants this kid was yelling at tv the entire time ) her siblings were appropriate

Didn't really enjoy this place. Margaritas tasted like pure salt and then in turn when I got my full rack of ribs they tasted like salt.. I love salt but this was too much salt.. Water was cold which was a good thing
Non the less I ate all my food but could get down that Margarita sorry 🤷🏽‍♂️
Food was delicious and service was good. Staff was friendly
Flatbread was decent and tasty.
Service was good. I had a southwest chicken eggroll app. Would recommend. LBH I was there for the beer buckets

Did curbside pickup
Food was good
Great atmosphere especially if you want to watch the game. Food was pretty good aswell.
Just OK food order was all wrong. Missing stuff on my burger and Mac and cheese was cold.

This was the first time I tried a Millers Ale House. I had a flat bread and Osso Buco. They were both brought to me close to the same time. My Osso Buco was ice cold so I had to send it back. I didn't care for the flat bread as it lack in the combinations of flavors and the flat bread was a bit stale.

The Osos buco was reheated, the manager brought it out and said they added fresh vegetables and extra gravy. It need the extra gravy as now the internal meat was chewy and dry.

I first ordered a small Perrier water, only to get no beverage offer or even water until the very end of my meal. I was very disappointed that one one followed up to see how my meal was. I did eat it all as I wasn't going to leave it after paying for it.

The experience wouldn't have been so bad if the server  would have been more attentive, or manager would have followed up on me, and realize I had no beverage.

I had few interactions with the server when taking my order, when I had my issue and bringing the check, nothing in between.

UPDATE

I reluctantly went back in to Miller's Ale House After talking to the manager. I have had great food since returning. My server, who I ask for always, Heather is phenomenal. I also have to do a shout out to Katrina who is at the carside hostedd area. She is so amazingly nice which makes me want ti return. I have been in five or more times since my first experience and have had a very enjoyable experience.

Service and hospitality is awesome, and the food is good. It works well for me when I travel to the area.

This restaurant serves typical Americana food like burgers, other sandwiches, wings and fries. It's definitely not a steak house. I only gave it three stars because the menu is pretty unremarkable and the food is just okay.  I ordered the salmon and the temp of the fish in the middle was not warm which was concerning and my salad was old. That being said the staff was remarkable and very accommodating, friendly and on the ball.

I came in for Flat bread pizza, of which isn't on the limited menu due to covid. Disappointed, had the Cobb salad which was good, but wasn't what I went there for. Must say the egg rolls where delicious.
The cheesesteak and chicken sandwich was so salty. The fries were undercooked, and the zingers tasted like nothing. Very disappointed. I expected better.
It was good food.went at lunch. I would go again.
The food is extremely salty. I couldn't eat much of it. I had the cheesesteak and mashed potatoes and my daughter's grilled chicken and fries were incredible for a child.
Service was good. Food was also good as usual
Great margaritas,  good service, food was decent

2 meals had to be sent back. Zingers are the only good thing here. Left hungry after having fajitas...not a lot of protein to eat. Disappointing. 2 stars because our other party members had a good meal. Probably won't be back
Food was very dry and bland. Ordered the mahi tacos and ended up in the bathroom all night. Save yourself the money and dont dine here.

Saw on the menu a creamy shells & cheese. What was brought to me was no taste kids Mac & cheese. Disappointment. Steak ordered medium &  was blood red. Had to send back for further cooking. We did have tasty pot stickers.

I ate here Wednesday night with my Mom. We were sitting for about 15 minutes in the booth until my mom asked a server passing by to please get us drinks since no one had greeted us yet. Service is terribly slow, food was mediocre could be seasoned better. Please work on training your servers to be more aware of surroundings, be approachable and attentive
A nice sports bar, with amazing food and customer services.
Had the pork shank (their specialty) and part of it was cold. Small portions of the sides. My server, Ashley, was polite and friendly.
Service could have been a lot better.
The zingers were the worst things I’ve ever experienced.
The waitress was very nice the food was excellent but the bathroom was disgusting. They may be cleaning the tables but not the bathroom, the women's bathroom I mean.

Ordered a zinger mountain melt, southwest rolls and onion rings through DoorDash. The food was disgusting. Taste like it was taken out of the freezer and into a microwave. My stomach literally hurts rn from the bite I took.
I liked this place the service was awesome and the wings were very good

Brought the family out for my husband's birthday dinner.  Service was a little slow but we aren't complainers.  Appetizers came out, pretty good.  Food came out, my son wasn't a fan of the burger, roll was too soggy.   But the killer was our dirty plates sat on our table all night.  Even when i decided to stack them up in the center of the table about 7 high they were still there when we left.  I work in the food service industry and it's hard  work so I still left 15%.  But when the restaurant is empty there is no excuse for a messy table.  I would have left 25%.
Decent food, good drinks, friendly staff
Nice place great food
It was ok.

I would love to give them a good review but unfortunately I can't.  Me and my fiance went to eat here and we walked in, it wasn't busy at all but no one came to greet us or seat us. We stood there for 5 minutes and nothing. Bad impression and I will not go back... They lost good money... We took our business else where...
Not very friendly m

Food is okay. The General Manager needs to learn better guest service skills. Before eating lunch I went and used there bathroom. While in the restroom I slipped on some kinda of fluid on the ground. In return ripped my shirt. I went and told the manager what had happened and he blew it off like it was nothing. Now I have a ripped Hugo Boss shirt. Thank you Ale House... The Florida store's are so much better.
not bad

Went here last night for the first time. I like the atmosphere. The wood tones make it feel warmer and cozier than say Big Woody's.

The service was just shy of stellar. The waitress was cheerful and attentive. The only issue was she never asked if we needed drink refills even though my glass was empty and sitting right on the edge of the table.

I only wish I could say as much for the food. My wife and I both had the French onion soup and we both thought there was too much sherry. It overpowered all the other flavors . My wife also had the calamari appetizer and enjoyed it. She liked the Thai chili dipping sauce. I had the 'Cue Bacon Cheeseburger, no cheese. The burger was a bit greasy and unformed. It was as if they grabbed a handful of ground chuck and put it on the grill without actually forming it into a patty. It was falling apart even before I picked it up off the plate. The fries were also very heavily salted. My daughter ordered the Zingers with honey barbecue sauce. She didn't like them. To be fair I think it was her being very picky because they're not prepared the way you would think; either being chicken tenders with sauce on the side or slathered in barbecue sauce. The sauce is more of a syrupy glaze than an actual barbecue sauce. When we told the waitress our daughter didn't care for them, they took them back, comped them off the bill and the manager came over to discuss why they were sent back .I did appreciate that level of customer service

Because of the atmosphere and the great customer service, we will likely be back to watch a game and have drinks. We may even try other items of the menu.

Food prices are good. Large selection of beer.  Decent atmosphere. Waitress was just ok.  I felt the food we had was very bland. (French onion soup, mozzarella sticks, shrimp)
Would certainly go back though and try something else.

Ill jump straight to it. I visited 05/27/19 at night. The steak I ordered was bleeding all over the plate and shoukdve been medium rare. I had a puddle of blood on my plate when the waiter came and said "yea that's not medium rare." She took the steak back, but the rest of my plate was saturated with blood. The steaks are seasoned at all so A1 is sadly needed.
Steak must've been cooked in a high flame because the outside was charred, but the inside was still beating. I'd imagine the experience would've been good if the steak is on its own plate....
In the other hand the drinks were good and the loaded fries (that were on another plate)

This is the second Miller's we have visited.  It was a crowded Saturday night.  Waited ~35 minutes for a table for 7.  Took about 20 minutes from order to receiving apps.  It was another 25 minutes after the apps that we got our meals.  Prime Burger was decent but sloppy to eat.  Overall it was an almost 2 hour experience.

Ordered a steak, baked potato and mixed vegetables. After a 45 min wait (lunch break) my "medium-well" steak comes out  charred and tough.  As I was famished I kept my vegetables and potato. Ate those while they re-cooked the steak. Long and short of it they took the steak off my bill and  apologized for the inconvenience. What I did eat tasted just fine. Maybe another time ?

Had chicken nachos chips were too salty and the lime crema left a bitter taste in my mouth.  I also had the French onion soup which was excellent.  My husband had the fried hot chicken breast sandwich.  He said it was very good too.  However the French fries served with it were also very salty.  Somebody in the kitchen had a very heavy hand.  Service was excellent as was the atmosphere.
Love the pork shank but mashed pot are never hot order the prime rib broth with it.Gracy is too thick and salty and I love salt.
On par with a Chili's, a bit better than an Applebee's.  I went several times before rating.  Nothing special and nothing bad either.
Food and service were pretty good. Nothing to stands out though. I would go again.

Super Bowl Sunday was my first time there. The place to be to watch college football and NFL. They have a ton of tvs. Food was above average. Wings were decent. Service was very good. Our server was very attentive and checked in regularly. Prices were what you would expect. Will definitely go back during football season.

Was not very impressed I will admit. For a Wednesday evening the service was much slower than anticipated. The staff was nice, but we rarely saw our server through the night. I had heard great things about the food, but was not super impressed with that either. I ordered the fajitas- the grilled chicken was very tasty but the portion size of the sides (salsa, lettuce, etc) was smaller considering the price of the meal. I did enjoy the atmosphere, I thought the venue was spacious, warm and entertaining. I would be willing to give it another try but perhaps order something else and come on a different night.

Long wait, was told tables needed to be bussed.  Went to bathroom and walked past 3 clean and ready tables.

Ordered some food.  Long wait.  Ok, they are busy. When food comes out it is ice cold.

Waitress walked past our empty drink repeatedly.

Garlic toast was orange and flavorless.  Not sure where the orange color comes into garlic toast.

Really poor experience.
It's a good place and food is ok. It gets packed so expect a wait.

Came in on a Sunday at 11:30.  We were greeted and sat at a booth, which was fine with us.  We ordered four beers, which took about 15 minutes to get.  Our appetizer, the pretzel, took 30 minutes to get to our table.  While waiting for the rest of our food, my daughter and I used the restroom, and it was visibly unclean.  The toilet was gross and the floor was sticky and dirty. The floor under our table was clearly not cleaned and sticky as well. Our waitresses was great, that was the highlight of the experience.  We don't be back anytime soon.
Filet Mignon was the worst ever. Brought out twice and cooked wrong twice. Couldn't even chew it. Won't be going back.
Very clean and the food was cooked perfectly

Service wasn't great, sent back my medium rear burger since it was medium well.  Bar server didn't bring any napkins with dinner or ketchup for the fries until we asked for them.  Same server was drinking an ice coffee while she was waiting on bar customers.  One and done for us.  PS they did not charge us for the burger and the manager did come over to address the problem.

Ate there for lunch today and I literally got KICKED by a waitress. I was walking back from the bathroom and a server who was standing against the wall put her leg out and kicked me right in the knee! I was shocked I just stared at her. Thank god I didn’t fall because I’m pregnant! The staff has always been the down fall of this place the atmosphere and food is good but every issue we have is with the immature staff
Greeter had a attitude when we walked in. Ate lunch at this place. Server Ariel was nice and I got the fish and chips. Fish was very greasy  and fries were cold and old when I got my plate.

Food was average but the wait was extra long. I understand that it was a Friday night but when we walked in at 7:00, they told us the wait was 30-40 minutes. We wound up being seated 57 minutes later.
They have a cool "app" that shows you where you are in the queue but it is not very accurate.
This place closes at 1:25am on Saturday, not 2am according to their staff. Poor customer service and work ethic.

Terrible customer service from management . Restaurant manager harassed us because our card wasn’t working and we were trying to pay online . Was told to not comeback . No problem . Crazy to see such a popular place managed by someone with no customer service . Definitely don’t recommend . Would give 0 stars if possible but food was good at least . Sad to see an old man harass a young woman like that .

Placed and order for take out for my boyfriend and I. We got the Bavarian pretzel, "south west chicken egg rolls", and 2 classic burgers. Pretzel hard as a rock, Burger, burnt, dry as a bone, no condiments put on it either, and as for the southwest chicken egg rolls it had 2 pieces of chicken in it! Should've been called corn and bean egg rolls. Horrible.  $50 which is absolutely ridiculous for burgers and fries, not worth it at all. McDonald's has better burgers save your money.
Food was good . Service was good
Very slow. Food mediocre at best. Limp fries. Draughty and cold in seats close to the door.
Food was decent, service was ok,sat at bar fit lunch and bartender was extremely busy. Orders came out wrong but were quickly fixed.
Service good, fish and chips good, ribs dry and not fall of the bone
Food suck people was nice tho

Food was boring and unimpressive for the price. The mashed potatoes were kinda gross, the broccoli overcooked and my filet tasted unseasoned. The service was adequate but I wouldn't be interested in returning.

The atmosphere and the beer selection is pretty good... But food takes so long to come out. Just waited 30 to 40 minutes on a Tuesday night. Had to get my food to go cuz it took so long. Will likely choose another venue in the future.
Service was slow. Had the Mahi Mahi that I have always enjoyed at other Miller's locations but here it was dry and not seasoned very well!
Mediocre. Got a steak and a beer. Fine for the price. Service could use improvement.
The food was awful.

We have dined at Millers on 3 different occasions in 2019;  first time in March, after shopping, we headed down, not sure what to expect so we split a burger, (real good), fried shrimp plate and a couple of beers. All hot and tasty; the server , Tony, was above par; moving fast!
Then we went back around 7/4 for Tuesday's steak special and Ariel served us with reserve and professionalism.
Steak & side was great, beer cold and we even signed up for rewards on recommendation of two hosts manning the podium who were personable, funny & engaging.
Just back from a 2 pm steak special today with server, Brett who doesn't care what he serves; I ordered the sirloin and caesar salad and the petite plate of romaine was grossly WILTED & disgusting. I asked him to replace it and he did, mentioning it was BIGGER; but still the greens were not fresh!
I started to tell him a story about salad greens in restaurants and he picked up our plates and walked off showing his dismissive attitude.
My observations are this place operates according to manager on shift with grave inconsistencies;  appealing prices with extensive beer/bar selections; the cleanliness is average and food fluctuates with cook on duty.

Discrimination against people of color/appearence. If you are not white, you can only have one beer.  I asked the manager what system they had to prevent discrimination like this and the manager said there is no system, the decision to serve guest beer is solely a personal decision. I was denied a beer, because I am Hispanic and didn't look like the clean cut guest they are use to. Came here on 11/3/2020 to get to-go food. Ordered Beer battered fish and chips sub onion rings for the coleslaw (still wanted fries also), house salad with balsamic vinegar dressing add chicken, cup of beer ale soup, cup of chili, and a stout to drink while I wait.

Pros: soup and chili were pretty good.

Cons:
1. The beer was room temp and the bartender (who was extremely nice Kim?) poured it out of a empty/brand new keg so it was over 3/4 head and she waited for it to go down and served it.
2. It took over 30 min for the food (other people were sat and received full family meals before our to-go food came out).
3. We drove to our hotel (Wyndham <2 min, shares parking lot), and opened the food up to find it was all cold.
4. They left out the fries and didn't give me the coleslaw that would have normally came with it. I got the (cold and chewy) onion rings which I was charged an additional $4 for. The full cost of side of onion rings is $4...
5.  Wrong salad dressing, poppy seed vs balsamic vinegar?
6. Chicken for salad was cold and rubbery
7. The beer battered fish was cooked in really old oil, the breading was burnt, the inner fish was rubbery and cold. I couldn't even bite through it.

At this point I couldn't not complain for this $50 meal... look at my Google review history I only write very few and only when it's really bad... I called the restaurant and asked for the manager, waited over 10 min on the phone and decided to walk back to the restaurant. To set the scene there were 5 total people in the restaurant... i explained to the bartender why i was upset and brought the food with me to show) while I'm still on the phone the manager finally comes out from the back to comp my food. I hang up (20 min) since I can talk to the manager in person, she doesn't say a word to me just starts typing on the register. She hands me a receipt and says ok I comped the fish and chips and the added chicken. I politely tell her thats unacceptable and re-explain points 1-6 from above along with mentioning the phone call. Her initial reaction was, "Why didn't you mention any of this when you were here?" I simply reply that it was to-go food and I apologize for not thoroughly checking each item for temperature and accuracy. How dare I assume that would be responsibility of the restaurant? I told her all I'm willing to pay for is the soups since they tasted pretty good. I worked in the service industry and at this point I would have comped the entire meal, but she proceeds to charge me for the soups and the tip, which... ok I said I'd pay for. She hands me the receipt and says, "Here, good-bye" and leaves to the back.

Needless to say I'll NEVER go back, and I hope people read how bad this experience was. I can't imagine it's  always this bad since they are in business but wow, that manager is the representative of their corporate office? Might want to put a now hiring sign up or start boarding up the windows (and not because of the social unrest looming with the election today).

Settled on chicken wings which ended up being 10 very small chicken wings that were overdone in the fryer and nearly devoid of sauce, not the worst set of wings I've ever eaten but pretty close. Hot mess of a restaurant, but the wait staff did a truly great job considering all the problems they faced throughout the evening.

Food was good. Service was absolutely terrible. Took forever. We were served our food and the server never returned. Had to flag down the hostess for another beer. Went to the bar to pay and they didn't even offer a discount.  Stick with the ABC in downtown Gettysburg. Way better!!

Yes, my account is a made up name.  This review is unfortunately real.  I ate at this location for ABC a few times after it opened and loved it.  The food and beers were amazing.  Tonight (12/13/18) I tried my favorite dish at ABC again and brought some family who were visiting.  The perogies and brats were unrecognizable compared to past visits to this location.  The potatoes were mashed lumpy which I am ok with but, the large lumps still felt hard and uncooked toward the middle.  Perogies were ok but did not seem like I remember them. The brats were so-so but looked like they were steamed or something as there was no char or browning on the outside of the sausages.  I am not hard to please with food, also I was not the only one who was unhappy as three people at our table ate about half of their meals and sent them back without asking for boxes or doggie bags.  I did not ask to speak with any staff this evening as that is not my way.  I hope that my words will be read by someone who cares about offering good food and hospitality and has the ability to bring this place back to where it once was.

If I could give 0 stars I would. We waited 2 minutes for the hostesses to decide where to sit us in a nearly empty restaurant. Then 6 minutes for a "waitress" to finally great us. She took the drink order and brought back drinks...said she'd be right back. Waited a bit and she returned to get our orders. She seemed irritated as we ordered appetizers. She  placed our orders and never saw her again. 4 additional people refilled drinks or brought our food. No app plates or napkins to be had. Chili was not even luke warm and was microwaved in the same bowl that left the table. Plus we had to flag down the manager just to get a check. As we were sat next to the wait station we got to watch as the manager struggled to get our check. Then asked not only was it a single check or separate but couldn't figure out who ordered what. We paid as a group and left. We were very upset by our experience and would never go back.

Really good beer & burgers. However, poster with confederate flag in background gave me pause. I was meeting friends (we were all from out of towns) so decided to stay. I won't go back because of the poster.

Ridiculous long wait for cold food (suppose to be hot), too many choices of your own beverages, even soda that did not taste good.  Large group told at the end can't pay separately when we specially twice we wanted split checks. Worst was a person who is vegan and ordered a vegan item with CHICKEN in it but then told sometimes foods get mixed.  Fortunately he's still alive. Would never recommend this pub to anyone.

My husband and I did not feel very welcome. When a bar table became available, we sat ourselves, assuming it was like most other restaurants and bar seating was first come first serve. That wasn't the case when we were there because there was only one bartender (on a Saturday night at 8:00) and she couldn't handle the bar and all the table alone. Totally understandable it is a lot. But, she and the hostess for the night really embarrassed us for doing so. They were talking loudly and pointing at us in front of everyone. When she returned, the bartender said, "I'll take you this time but this isn't how we normally do things." We won't return.

Please retrain your waitstaff! We have visited an ABC for +10yr. It's great beer and food! However, our last visit there were 4 tables and 2 waitresses. We sat for 15 minutes without an acknowledgement from the staff. We walked out; I doubt they saw.

This was my first time here. I went with my family and parents. My father ordered the beef tips which looked horrible. He sent them back and asked for a burger instead. Rather than put a rush on his order he just sent back another ticket. After everyone was done eating we asked to cancel the order. The waiter asked which meal we wanted to pay for after receiving neither. The manager then came over and tried to explain to us that there staff is trained to require you to pay for food you didn’t receive or food that is sent back because it is disgusting. I haven’t even mentioned that my medium burger was well done and the bun was wet or that my mother’s coffee was cold or that my wife’s nachos were stale. I would not recommend this place to anyone. After to talking to the manager we were left to wait at the table for another 15 minutes before I went to the kitchen to find our waiter to get the check who told me it was out of his hands the manager was handling it now. So thanks for a great evening. At least I learned of a restaurant in Gettysburg to never recommend.

We stood for 30 mins for a table for two. The place was run by kids. We got no explanation as table after table left. There was no manager to be found in the building. We knew that even if we got a table it would be forever getting food so we left. We won't be back.
Got chicken and waffles! The gravy and chicken on top of the waffle tasted like it had sat for 10 hours and it burnt. Was the worst thing I’ve ever eaten.

Food was delicous , but the server and Manager, Deb were Terrible which led to my review. Actually the attention we received from the flies in the restaurant was far better then the attention we received from our server. When I went to pay the bill with the restaurant's gift card I was told by Deb the Manager the restaurant had been having a problem with their gift cards for more then a week and they couldn't accept it. Do yourself a favor, if you are in Gettysburg visit the sister location on Steinwehr Avenue.

Save your money! They have terrible beer, mediocre food and not enough staff.  Basically they are Denny's (except way more expensive) with alcohol. Save yourself the headache and heartburn and go to Fourscore downtown Gettysburg.

Food was decent but the service was out of this world atrocious. Our waitress was just plain mean. Might just be an isolated server, but it really turned out entire group off of ever going back to the place.

Asked for a sugared rim on the Holiday Beer and was told "No, the brewer wants you to experience the taste as they created it." The beer tasted terrible. It had a skunky back taste and very little of the cinnamon and clove flavor. The sugared rim would have helped to hide the sour after taste 😉

I've been coming here for years, and I haven't had a bad experience until recently. The customer service was terrible, and the menus had black mold growing inside of the plastic. I won't be returning to this location.
We came in and sat at the bar without being acknowledged for over 10 minutes. The bar tender was back there the entire time
The manager needs to learn customer service skills and how to make a Moscow mule.
Very mediocre food and atmosphere.
Food was great but service was poor
Worst meal i have ever consumed i had better food in the service
Food was great except it was ice cold when it was brought out. Apparently our server was also tending bar. Got busy at the bar and neglected to bring our food to us.
Stinks.  Service was horrible, food was worse.
Very poor service. The waitress was not very accommodating. It took awhile to be served

I have been here several times. The food and beer is pretty good, but if you go there any time before 5:00 PM, it is pretty dead inside. The last time I went (about a week ago) we got there around noon and there were only 3 other tables in use…. So not busy at all. Despite the few tables to wait on, service was INCREADIBLY slow and our waitress was pretty bad.

We were not given a wine/cocktail list, and the menu simply stated “wide variety of wines” (or something to that degree.) After being seated, our waitress took drink orders. My friend needed a minute so the waitress took my beer order and said she would be right back to take the other order.

It took about 15 minutes to get the beer…..

The waitress dropped it off, then asked if we wanted an appetizer, which we placed, but then she promptly walked off before we could even request a drink for my friend.

Another 15 minutes pass…….

Our appetizer came out and it was dropped off as our waitress walked by, again didn’t even give us enough time to order another drink. So we had to flag down our waitress in order to get a drink. She came over and this is exactly how the conversation went:

Friend: “Do you have a Riesling wine?”
Waitress: “Nope.”
And our waitress walked off.

That was all we got from her.

I walked to the bar to see if I could get a wine list or something but there was no one there. I waited for about 5 minutes, but no one showed up so I went back to my seat.

Finally, the waitress came back to get a food order. We place it and I quickly asked what kinds of wine they had so my friend could get her drink. This is how that conversation went:

Waitress: “Don’t you have a wine list?”
Me: “No.”
Waitress: “It is on the table.”
Friend: “We don’t have one.”
Waitress: “It’s right…. (sees we do not have a wine list) Oh, well it should have been right here. You should have been given a wine list. Sorry about that! Well, we have….. uh…. We have…… (proceeds to swing her arms and snap her fingers as she tries to think) Gosh we have so many. I’m sure if you just name something we have it. We have all house wines.” (remember, they did not have a Riesling and that is a house wine)
Friend: “Do you have a list that you can bring over?”
Waitress: “No we don’t have any wine lists.” (hum…. Shouldn’t we have had one on the table?)
Friend: “Then do you have any sweeter wines? Something like Rieslings or Moscatos?”
Waitress: “Oh yes, we have sweet wines. Do you like red or white?”
Friend: “White”
Waitress: “Ok.”
And she walks off…. Doesn’t give any choices, does not list what they have, she just walked away to get a glass of unknown wine. She comes back with a glass and sets it down and she says “this is the sweetest wine we have. If you don’t like it just let me know.”

If she took a minute to talk to us about the wines they have, we could have told her we do not want the sweetest, but perhaps an in-between. She didn’t do that and just got a glass of whatever. And let me tell you, it was sweet and not good. We dare not say anything because of our experience thus far and just drank it.

After that, our waitress disappeared. I needed another beer, or a water, or ANYTHING to drink. I went back to the bar, but again, the bar tender had also disappeared and I couldn’t even ask for water.

About 25 minutes later, she stops by our table, saw our empty glasses and asked, “Do you even want another drink?”

Do we *even* want another drink? Really?? What waitress askes that kind of question?

We ordered water because we were thirsty and then got the check so we could leave ASAP.

Very disappointing.
First visit was great. Good sevice, drink, food and service. Next night: not so great. I strongly discourage ordering their Nachos (no real cheese, poor sauce, wilted lettuce, tasteless chips).
Food was delicious.. service was horrible.

I’m confident this place only had the volume it does due to the location(walking distance from the Wyndham and Courtyard Marriot). While the foods pretty good and reasonably priced, their staff and seating practices leave a lot to be desired. I went twice due to the ease of not having to drive anywhere. The first time our table wasn’t even clean when we were sat and the waiter seemed annoyed that we complained about that. We gave it another shot and it was even worse. On a Sunday of a long holiday weekend, everyone who came in was told it was a 30-45 min wait when they only had half the tables filled. They also ran out of burgers before 630pm apparently but neglected to tell anyone that til after they wait the 30 mins for a table. The hostess also scolded 2 large parties for basically doing nothing but walking in the door saying that they would have to be on one bill before even greeting them. She also refused to take their cell phone # to call them so they could wait with their kids at the lobby of the hotel that’s 20 yard away because “they don’t do that and don’t have a phone”. This place was a massive let down, Wuxi h was incredibly unfortunate given it’s a brewery and has the potential to be so much better. They should also try and staff and prepare a little better for holiday weekends and maybe even coordinate with hotels when they are sold out or have conferences going on so they know if they might be getting a lot of business instead of having the host basically tell them to go somewhere else.

The service was terrible. It took over 45 min just to get our appetizers. The waitress was very inattentive. Someone spilled their drink and we had to clean it up ourselves while the waitress watched without offering to help or even get more napkins.
My daughters salad had slimey parts that were very obvious and yet it eas served anyway.
Bad nachos , ok chili, very overcooked dry burger , and place was kinda dirty , good wait staff.
Service was not good...waitress needs to be trained or retrained.
Food quality has gone from great to horrible.   Won't be back
The attitude of management and staff is horrible
Disappointing meal. Service was slow. Overall grade C+
The food was good, but the service was very bad!
I do not understand how you do not take reservations yet you can stand in there for over 20 minutes to get seated while seeing many open tables.

Brewery machine is broken it produces hard brownish substance that causes headache and lightheadiness vandalism on your vehicle will follow, now I have to put my account on credit watch, as I was exiting my car was obstructed by construction vehicles I also was followed, dangerous place avoid at all cost.
Service was good but the food was horrible. Brussels sprouts swimming in grease.

Never got seated.... We got put on a list and told they would call in about 20 minutes... We waited for over 30 minutes. When I went back to the checkin to see how much longer I noticed that we had been skipped over several times. People that arrived after us got seated. Maybe another day.

Common sense has left the building. I refuse to believe I cannot place an order for a beer without placing my food order at the very same moment. In fact I know this isn’t true as I am able to get a drink at other businesses without a food order. Would be nice to be told at the door.

I would never have this place again the food was bad drinks were bad I love supporting people but there is a line between acceptable and jsut bad and it would of been just a bit better if they would at least put a good description of the food instead of jsut adding stuff without description sorry never again

Awesome food. Went here on my wife's treat after a long afternoon at Banana Joe's, Going Bananas or something like that.  The food and beer were great. Will have to come back for some more deliciousness.

My husband and I love the food here and we always have a great experience with the staff! However, I am disappointed at how crowded and difficult it is to get a table within the last 2-3 years. There have been multiple occasions where we have decided to eat elsewhere upon seeing how packed and crowded it is and being unable to make reservations. We don’t even try eating here anymore due to the fact that we know it will be packed and are unable to make a reservation. I hope that the new expansion helps with this problem and that they begin to take reservations! I considered having a party of 15-20 people here for a birthday celebration, but was told by the manager that they are unable to take reservations. Such a shame as they lost business due to having to book a space elsewhere!
Always busy here. Beer is always great and food is always great too. Definitely recommend.

Very pricey the chicken and waffles were so dry and chewy, no coleslaw just super hot pickles the fish tacos were tasteless, add two glasses of wine for the hefty price of $47 so loud you had to yell to be heard! We had to walk around for a 1/2 hr. to finally get a table. Never again so disappointed!
Good beer food is pretty good but a bit pricey.
Good beer, interesting and  tasty food options.
Slow service.

We called ahead and made reservations after we asked if the patio area was dog friendly and we were told yes. When we got there,  there was music blaring from a speaker attached to a laptop at a table at the patio.  We were seated and the hostess apologized.  Our waitress came over and we told her it was so loud we had to shout at each just to hear what the other was saying.  Our dog was terrified and was cowering under the table. Our waitress tried to take our order but we said  we didn't know if we were going to stay because of the loud music. At no time did anybody offer to turn anything down. When we made the reservations no one told us that the loud music would be playing so obnoxiously. I understand background music or if there's live music, but this was just as speaker and a laptop hooked up at a table. We left without ordering because it would not have been a pleasant dining experience. We couldn't hear one another and our dog was frightened. This definitely wasn't a dog friendly environment, as we were told, or suitable for a dining experience.
Outstanding food and exceptional service

I do love the food and drinks at this place, but I feel like I have to say something here. A few days ago was the THIRD time that this happened - after drinking a few margaritas, I had an excruciating pain in my stomach. It did not go away all night and I woke up several times to vomit - also had diarrhea all night. Three other people in my group experienced the same symptoms. The ones who only drank beer did not. This happened when I was there a few weeks ago, and before that, some time last fall.

Edit: In response to the owner's response, I'd like to point out that this happened to four of us that day. Should we all see doctors? We drink margaritas at other restaurants and are fine. I pinpointed it to the margaritas because it was the only thing we had consumed when the pain started as we were still waiting for food. Also, I see another review below saying someone got sick so this isn't the first time you've had this complaint. But reading your responses to other reviews, it seems that your restaurant is perfect and nothing is ever your fault. That's a great way to improve upon criticism!

We recently went to Estrella and had a horrible experience. First of all, we wanted to go to East Branch Brewing and the check in area (which was directly in front of the East Branch Brewing Door) had no signs that said it was for actually for Estrella. We ended up waiting 45 minutes for a table and by the time we were seated and realized that it was not in fact the intended restaurant we were too hungry to wait another minute for food so we thought, lets give this place a try! Boy, do I wish we didn't. The service was slow, the food took another 40 minutes to arrive (and we got basic things), AND half of the food was COLD! To top it all off my husband and I felt sick the entire evening and next day from the food. The taste of the food was not horrible but very mediocre. I will never go back here and strongly recommend that when they have 2 different restaurants literally on top of each other, they make an effort to clarify which restaurant is which. At least say something like "welcome to Estrella!" We got none of that.

I hadn't been to Estrella since the pandemic started. The food was phenomenal. My issue is with the drink service. I felt as though I was being baby-sat the entire time. I ordered a pitcher of margaritas and it was only delivered after my friend finished his drink. My friend is a bartender at a local brewery and said this was not actual PA state law. Just seems a bit overkill.

Update: we have been back and the quality seems to be inconsistent....

We had to go to check it out the first night! The atmosphere is really nice in there, but gets very loud. We got Pop's Nachos which were so good and unique! And I am not a steak person at all, but it was so delicious! We also got the grilled corn which was yummy. Also, we had the guacamole, which was good, but $11 for a small amount. We had the brisket sandwich, which was good. The dessert (the fried dough one) was kinda undercooked and bland. Other than that I would def come back!

Great Food. Poor Customer Service. In my experience, I ordered about $50.00 worth of food everytime. The first time the grilled cheese was burnt, I let them know and they said they would replace it and they did. Great. A few orders later I got home to notice I had no cheese for my brisket fries. I called and told them. Ashley seemed to have an attitude sighed and said “we’re about to close, do you want to come back and get it?” I did drive back in the rain and get it. No apology. The next time I let Ashley know the brisket for my pit master was extremely greasy to the point where it was barely edible. Literally it was submerged in grease. Not like it is usually. I called to simply let them know so it wouldn’t happen again. Ashley got irate and snippy and asked “who is this, Johnathan?! You seem to always have a problem with everything you order!” I calmly let her know it not a problem, just thought you would want to hear feedback from a customer who is a big fan of your food. I have yet to order again. Sadly, because I love the food, but I don’t think that’s a proper way to speak to a person who is patronizing your business. I’m sure Ashley doesn’t own the business but management should pay close attention to who they let operate the phone. They went from my first choice to my last resort for brisket. Treat all people with respect please.

Will not go back, my husband ordered the 3 meat Bbq platter and it was not good.
The wings were ok but the pork was left over from who knows where and It was So dry it was like plastic, clearly not fresh the brisket seemed to be scraps from other plates not finished or left over from the two days prior
Served with a teaspoon of Bbq sauce. Service was slow but friendly.
It’s a shame how the cook can send out food just to see if it comes back. But I guess minimum wage gets you minimum help. Many other restaurants with much better bbq and solid portions not scraps.
Completely disappointed and will not recommend this place to anyone.
I've loved it before, but the last two times I've gone, the quality seems to be diminishing :( it makes me so sad because I was in love with the food!

I went and I got the ribs... They were not good at all. My wife on on the other hand had the tacos and she said they were really good... My review is more for the ribs that k the overall restaurant... I will give them one more shot..
Really want to like this place but I have had 2 bad experiences.  The brisket tacos were inedible.  The place looks great and the outdoor space is nice.
Really want to like this place but I have had 2 bad experiences.  The brisket tacos were inedible.  The place looks great and the outdoor space is nice.

Very olly tacos, as I was eating oil was dripping from the taco. Salsas were horrible one was spicy but none had any flavor. The inside is too noisy, the outside is great in the summer but other than the drinks I didn't find a reason to go back. Service was fine and quick.
Nice patio! Food ok. Mixed drinks are not good & the wine comes in a water glass!! Get some wine glasses & "real" alcohol!!😕🍷

Called to place an order to go and was told to use the website for ordering. Chose my order and put in my card information and it would not let me continue. So I put my card info I n some third party ordering site and I didn't even get to place an order. Ill probably never attempt to order food there again. Its a shame I drive past the place multiple times daily.

The kids were fine, we parents we're upset about not being treated fairly and basically ignored or brushed off when we asked questions.

I was super excited to try this place and I was not impressed. The margaritas were super sweet and the service was lacking. The guacamole was good, but the chips were saturated in grease. We were going to try the tacos but decided to just pay the bill and go to another local restaurant after it took our waitress 30 min to return to take our order. It was also very difficult to differentiate between staff and patrons since there is no uniform at all which made it hard to get someone’s attention to order.

Have eaten here with my family a few times (swept up with the new place in town hype), and have been underwhelmed at best. Very easy to rack up a high bill with not many items, and the staff kind of runs around like chickens with their heads cut off (forgetting drinks, waiting for things for an insanely long time, not knowledgeable about menu). Food is fine, nothing amazing. Atmosphere is typical to any new trendy "instagram aesthetic" restaurant you find in every city. Hard pass for me.

Had an unfortunate experience here. We got seated right away but after 15 minutes of waiting for a server to come to our table we had to leave. Although it was a Friday night it didnt appear to be too busy. I was looking forward to one of their specials which I'm sure was delicious but was denied the opportunity.
Food was marginal at best. Overpriced drinks. Kids running around unsupervised. You can do better.
Bar was packed, service was slow.  Food ok.  Taco are better down the street at bottle room.

Had an unfortunate experience here. We got seated right away but after 15 minutes of waiting for a server to come to our table we had to leave. Although it was a Friday night it didnt appear to be too busy. I was looking forward to one of their specials which I'm sure was delicious but was denied the opportunity.

Mediocre tacos for white people. Overpriced food that's excused with the description of "Austin-style," not to mention the god awful customer service. Just another step towards the gentrification of Downingtown. If you want something more authentic and worth your time + hard earned cash, there's a better place just a few steps away.

Visited here to have an xmas lunch with my co-workers. I dont know what the deal was, but our hipster waiter seemed really lazy. He did this thing where he didn't want to walk around the table to serve us so he would stand at the head and lean over and hand it to us. I dunno... it was weird. I had a chicken sandwich. It was ok. Kinda bland. It was a cool hip place, but in my mind it was just another of of these trendy restaurants that kind of all get lumped together.
Like going to an Italian restaurant asking for spaghetti and getting ramen noodles with ketchup. About as Mexican as president President Trump.

Great atmosphere, the service was really poor. Forgot beverages/wrong beverages, minimal check ins. Still new with room to improve and I'll be coming back again to check in. For a taco place, I would like to see more taco options added to the menu

Service was ok but the atmosphere and food was mediocre for the price tag.  Less of a Mexican restaurant and more of a food truck vibe.  Wasn’t offered napkins or water.  But when it came, the water was room temperature and served in what I could only imagine doubled as a shot glass.  The margaritas were weak and overpriced and the food wasn’t flavorful.  Felt a little disappointed and cheated as we paid the bill and left.

Great service, food was mediocre.  Beer cheese was not tasty at all, brisket taco was dripping grease and margaritas are very syrupy, mix was not flavorful.  Chicken thigh taco and pork belly taco were good.  Not sure I would give this place a second glance.

I really wanted to love this place, but sadly I was disappointed. The food was good, but the poor service killed the whole experience. I am hoping it is just new restaurant bugs that need to be worked out. Maybe I will sit inside next time I go, as it seemed more organized in there.
Thought it was overprice, and really just overall disappointed. Kinda surprising after a few folks told me how good they thought it was, but that was not my impression.

Avoid this place like the plague! Mexican it is not. Jalapeno's and red onions on every thing do not make it Mex. Greasy tacos.  8 in party, two violently ill within hours! Cramped seating, small tables . Small portions, expensive!

I was stationed in Corpus Christi, TX for 5 years.  No.  I've never had a taco like this.  They were cold when they got to us, my potatoes were burnt/charred, the eggs were dry, and I couldn't finish my second taco because the burnt taste from the potatoes was so bad.  I got the pico de gallo to go with my tacos.  There were no jalepenos, barely any onions or cilantro, zero flavor and tasted carbonated(not sure how to explain that).  My kids wanted guacamole. Which also had no flavor and they barely touched it.  Our waitress was incredibly slow, and forgot the plates, napkins, silverware, and drink refills we asked for(we were one of maybe 4 tables inside).  We got them from another waitress.  The beer was good, and so were the chips and salsa.  We appreciate the gentleman that came to our table and apologized for our experience.
