Such a dissapointment! My husband and I live walking distance and really wanted to like this place but it was a total disappointment.  We had the guacamole, which I believe was $12, fairly small and not particularly good.  We also ordered the frito pie which was literally some of the worst food I have ever "eaten".  I put eat in quotes because it was actually inedible.  Also the Frito pie was slid at us by the food runner (?)....I hope it was just a very off day and hope to return sometime to confirm as it is usually very busy
Tacos were good but after that nothing special. Nachos were bad and very small and small pitcher of margaritas for 25 bucks?? Overpriced for what u get. Most likely won't be back.
Not impressed! How does a Taco place not have Beef Taco’s? Service is ok but disorganized. Serving trays look like metal prison trays
There was nothing on the menu for someone who can't eat super spicy. To be fair my son (45) totally enjoyed it and the brewery beer.
Mad weak. Not worth it unless your hammered

Beer always delicious,  great friendly service :) Disappointed with the nachos and pint of bacon last night. Cold store bought cheap chips, salty chicken and hardly any cheese, was cold when we got it.  $8.99 for 5 slices of bacon in a glass :(
WHAT HAPPENED RB?!?!
Didn't have any food. I enjoy craft beer and am enthusiastic about trying new ones. Walked away very disappointed in the quality and taste. Hope they can figure it out and make it work.
I liked it better when they emphasized Irish dishes, rather than everyday bar food of sandwiches and fries.

Reclamation was ok...the beer wasn't memorable, really, but the atmosphere and food were ok. The ladies tending the bar were friendly. However, I can't recommend anybody to visit
Reclamation because of the owner's anti-LGBT stance. If we'd known they wanted to discriminate against workers and residents of Butler, we would not have visited. We will not be back.

The menu changed and the food now is typical bar food nothing to get excited about. Beer is good. Service ok but slow.  Really disappointed about the menu changes. Won’t be going back because of menu changes. Food a 1 star service 3.
Accidentally forgot a mask was treated very rudely. Ended up leaving with my husband can’t believe they treat health care professionals that way.

Entered the restaurant and we were told to seat ourselves. We sat down at a table and the wait staff rudely insulted us for sitting at a dirty table, wiped the table off and then never returned. We left after this rude encounter. Used to enjoy this establishment but now we are extremely disappointed and will be taking our patronage elsewhere.

Stopped in for a beer on a Saturday night and enjoyed the atmosphere and the beer. Service was quick and friendly. It wasn’t overly busy but there were a couple of bigger groups contributing to a louder noise level. Definitely would come back here, only thing I didn’t enjoy on this trip was the beer selection. Kolsch, Lager, Amber ales and no IPAs which seems odd with the current trends in the beer world.
Don't go here if you are over 40. You will not get seated!

I was ignored and not given a menu then after asking for a menu wasn't asked if I wanted a drink or anything for after being ignored for 15 or 20 minutes I left... they were not busy and the bartender was too busy flirting with the guys at the other end of the bar.

Poor service,
Waited lengthy time for drinks and additional staff had to come to replenish our them.
Wait staff had rushed/hurried attitude.  We took additional time to choose our menue items and this seemed to.irritate wait staff.
The Burger was good. There was not much on the menu. The hubby was disappointed that everything was made with citrus!
Stay away if you don't usually drink kraft beer.

My coworkers and I are regulars mostly because of location, which is central and accessable. We have been going there almost every week since it opened.

It is modern and clean. Miller's probably doesn't stand out for anything specific, but they are good enough in every category. They don't disappoint.

The one area I would hope they most improve is service. It is hit or miss depending on your server, where there seems to be a lot of turn over.

The loaded cheese fries appetizer is enough fora meal on it's own. You don't need to order anything else for dinner.😁😆 Everything is pretty good. It's not spectacular, but it's very good bar food. Great addition to the neighborhood. Two adults drinking 3 beers and three kids (3 kids meals) came to $83 plus tip. Very good for what it is. We'll definitely go back.

I ordered a combination dish yesterday when I went there with my son's and whoever the manager was that night refused to allow me to swap the sausage for chicken as I do not like sausage at all. He/she (manager) will not allow me to simply swap it out as they wanted to charge me an additional $5 for the chicken even though it was a swap for the sausage and would not take anything off for the sausage when I stated forget about the chicken then! this is the third negative experience I had is this Miller's the first one my son slammed his head on the patio shutter that was open because of the way the seats were arranged too close to the opening before the covid hit! Second time was during the quarantine when I tried to place a pickup order online. That night The mail Manager got nasty with me when I was talking on the phone with my mask on outside of the restaurant to pick it up as my pickup time went past almost a half an hour and I called asking what was going on. I told the manager how I felt and told him to cancel the order and they still charged me I had to fight it on my credit card!
Good food. Friendly people.  Zingers are very good.
Good service but in 4 soft food because of dental surgery so only had soup & mashed potatoes

Service was nice and quick. But staff wasn't trained correctly om their menue items and had bo idea what was on the loaded baked rock, I mean potato. The food was okay. I wouldn't rave or tell people to go there specifically but if thr other places like that around there are busy its not the worst.

My wife and I went to Miller's last night (Horsham rd.)  for my birthday.  The atmosphere was nice and my wings appetizer was very good.  This is where it goes downhill.  I got the Prime Rib special with french fries, both were barely warm.  The Prime Rib looked dry, was tough and I wanted some salt.  It took over 15 minutes to get our waitress' (Ty) attention, which by that time I was done eating.  Ty never came back to see if we needed anything or ask how the meal was.  We were planning on desserts, but didn't want to wait a 1/2 hour.  We were very disappointed as this was my birthday and the first time dining out in months.
Good food, waitress couldve been more attentive to actually ask how the meal was. Funny thing how quick she was to come back to collect her tip though.
Good food and a large half outdoor bar.

We love Millers.  This was an off day for them.. We order from Millers a lot and do curbside. Usually pretty good but this time... not so much. Food cold. Appetizers we tried different ones like loaded ff and that was cold and flavorless.  It had chicken pieces on it i think which had no flavor and very dry. Tried pizza, also cold. We reheated in oven just nothing to write home about.  Curbside was very slow.. which i think was why things were cold. They didn't notice we were there for 15 min.. thought they were making food as we arrived obviously I was wrong. We will try again. Fingers crossed.
The sevice was good and the food was delicious.

This was my first time at Miller’s. My sons high chair had a piece of broccoli and other food stuck to the inside of it. Clearly it had not been sanitized properly after being used. I had to eat my salad with my fingers because I never received silverware or napkins. Very disappointing!

Not first first time here , I first time the food was great, second not so much tasted like they forgot to change the frying oil, and last night 03/05/21 was bad , my Mac and cheese was greesy and no cheese, cheesecake lack of cheese and salt and dried out, and my fish taco were blend , eat able but nothing to rave about.
Way to loud for seniors. Qwill not return.

Friday night at 6:10 pm I placed an order online, I received confirmation via text and email. 10 minutes later, at 6:20pm on a friday, I get another text saying "Hi, your order from Miller's Ale House has been canceled because the store is now closed." So naturally I call them, no pickup, just ringing. By the 5th time I got through someone transferred me and put me on hold again, I listened to music for a few minutes, then the call dropped me. Called back several times and no pickup.. could not reach the restaurant to save my life. Mind you my card was already charged.
I did not enjoy this restaurant. I found the burgers plain and the service poor. I do not plan to go again.
Food was good, waiter wasn't the best. Overall enjoyed the
Improper use of masks. Employer should provide proper masks that fit securely.. manager said they did provide each employee only one company mask. Not going back.

Got the mountain of zingers, it's amazing.
I've been to many before, and this one didn't disappoint.
Great food and service even during these times.

Food is good. Both times I have been there on my lunch break, I've had to ask them to change my order to "to go." The time it takes for food after being seated has taken more than 30 mins. If you have an hour lunch, you'll be fine. Anything less, you may want to look elsewhere unless they improve their kitchen times.
Great food and staff

We tried out Miller's twice, both times with mixed experiences. The bar is great and the staff is friendly, but the food was anywhere from average to just bad. One of our burgers came out cold, the salad was swimming in dressing, and the dry rub wings were slimy. Would only go back for the bar.

Being a chain restaurant, you get the typical stuff. Nothing really special. Typical beer selections and what not. Staff was fast and friendly. However, the bar area is a good place to watch games and has great vibe on the weekend. Wouldn’t come here specifically for food.
Found a UFO(unidentifiable food object)in my Blackened Chicken Breast Salad. I chewed on some that just wasn't edible food. Hard as a stone.
Our server was wonderful; her name is Sunshine! But the food is way over seasoned. I got ribs and mashed potatoes on Valentine's day and spent the rest of the evening very sick.

Awful customer service. Was told 5 minute wait five other families were seated who arrived after us. Including a party of 8 when we had 7. When questioned it was told they were cleaning a table for us. 20 min later told they were cleaning a table for us. All the while my husband was waiting patiently in a neck brace. We left and went to the bull across the street. Fantastic service and food as always!

Miller's Ale House does not care about YOU.

Its clear from the parking lot that this is a quick chain operation.  The building itself has absolutely no character, cheap stone and stucco, and no real details - mean this is a money making only operation.
40 some beers on tap.  Hard to understand the draft menu but i guess its like 20 standards and then 20 "others".  Basic liquor - to make you standard drinks quickly.
The food.  Well I ordered wings and i got "Zingers".  I said it was okay.  Then I realized I got RANCH not blue cheese.  Told the waiter - this might not be my order.  But he assured me it was "and the system is difficult".
The nachos were insane.  But only CHICKEN.  no other options for the nacho toppings.
Then the person next to me had the same problem.
Then the person at the end of the aisle got the wrong salad, and had ordered 2 meals and only got 1.
I got no comp or apologies for the wrong food, wrong sauce, etc.
Tianna the bartender was great, helpful, nice to talk to - I highly recommend her.
There is not a seat in the place that does not have a view of like 47 televisions.

But it is clear to me, that this is a chain that is all about serving high profit beer and quick fried food.
Totally honest - go some place else.  You can get the same food items, better, other places.

Wait staff was not very good.   We waited over an hour for our food to be delivered.    When it was delivered, it was on scolding hot plates indicating it was either heated up or sitting under the lamps for too long.   Food was dried out, but I was too tired or hungry to send it back.  Besides the fact that our waiter kept disappearing so I couldn’t complain if I wanted to.    On top of that the air conditioning was running on a winter  evening making it extremely cold in the restaurant.  After asking three different servers, the heat finally came on as we were leaving.   Needless to say I will not be going back!
Does anyone know if they have any type of allergen menu particularly a gluten free menu? I heard they are not very gluten friendly or knowledgeable. Help Please

It was ok overall. We had lunch on a Sunday, wasn't too busy when we arrived. Our server seemed to be in a hurry, the few interactions we had with her. Food was ok. I ordered a chicken sandwich with cheese and bacon, I believe it was called a chicken Club. I don't plan that on going back. Nothing was that 'good' that it deserved a second chance.
Does anyone know if they have any type of allergen menu particularly a gluten free menu? I heard they are not very gluten friendly or knowledgeable. Help Please

Meh... It's like Applebee's with more beers on tap and maybe better TV screens. The food and the beer selection was just ok for an "ale house." The Pour House around the corner is so much better and only a little more expensive. The Metropolitan Diner has higher quality food for about the same price and is only about a mile away. My salmon dish was about 20% salmon and 60% coleslaw and tartar sauce. Broccoli side came in a separate dish for some reason and was super overcooked to the point it was falling apart when forked. Caesar side was yellowish romaine leaves with little dressing. The roll-up garage doors, and antique wood walls were a nice touch but not enough to earn an extra star. I had hopes this place would be great. Oh well.

It was ok overall. We had lunch on a Sunday, wasn't too busy when we arrived. Our server seemed to be in a hurry, the few interactions we had with her. Food was ok. I ordered a chicken sandwich with cheese and bacon, I believe it was called a chicken Club. I don't plan that on going back. Nothing was that 'good' that it deserved a second chance.
very food and service
Bar food friendly staff
Meh.

Same as most millers, sportbar with american apps and decent beer selection. Went there during some college games, two main complaints: 1. Took forever to get a drink. My friend literally snap chatted a video of me waving for several minutes trying to get a beer at the bar. It wasnt that busy and most of the bartenders were chating in the corner. That was disapointing.  2. They need a better list of the draft beers. There were several(5) missing from the list that you could see the handles to. I had to ask several questions to know what wasnt on the list and the bartenders had to look it up, inconvenient for both of us.

Food was not hot, order was prepared wrong with one of our guess order causing long delay. Waiter was not as friendly with our table as observed while serving other tables. Manager did comp order that was delayed but we will not be returning to that location.

First time here and told over an hour wait...plenty of people maybe over 20 waiting and just inside the door an empty table for four...totally clean/looked unused all night!
Not my idea of good service...no tables w/ a wait should ever be empty!  So went to Iron Hill, called ahead and sat down upon arrival! Winning!
Good food
Great food and service.

We stopped for lunch and boy was it LOUD. The acoustics were awful! Personally, I think Millers is a bit too expensive for the quality and taste of the food. Service was good. Place was clean, but did I mention LOUD?
Need to get better service
Very slow, food was cold, staff was friendly
Great food
Food was a little salty for my taste
Meh: Chain fare, lackluster service, mediocre food. Not likely to return, only went here because the person I dined with chose to. Slip the chains and eat at a real restaurant instead.
Hands down the worst service iv ever had.

Rude and unknowledgeable staff, did not know what a stout beer was when I asked if they had one on tap and we were told our drink voucher was not good even before presenting it. Food was poor at best, chips were stale, and staff was rude when asking for a refund...said the chips were opened that morning. Supposedly voided the original purchase and overcharged us when re-ringing the purchase.

The woman that "helped" us during our tasting  was rude and not hospitable. Sandy? Short
Curly black hair. She gave no explanation of the wines. She was very short and abrupt with us and made us feel very unwelcome. The "bar" had black table cloths that were dirty and she kept spilling wine as she poured.  There were flies all over the floor by the windows. She doesn't seem to actually care about the winery that she works at.

That being said, it's a beautiful building and other people had food and although we were so put off by the employee,  we didn't stay and eat but the food did look decent.

There is an event here today so it is busy however, the service was lousy. They are clearly understaffed. After standing at the bar table for 11 minutes someone finally said ' hello'. It was another few minutes before anyone actually took our beer order. Good booze, good atmosphere, sub-par service.
No customer service. Waited for about 20 minutes before I had to ask if we could order food and drink.

Took forever to get waited on. We paid to taste and it took ten minutes between each taste. People cutting in front of us that got waited on first  while we stood there waiting. Granted it was busy but atleast staff accordingly if you know there's a tour coming through. Our money is just as good as anyone else's. We still bought wine when we left.

Not terrible i suppose,  but I don't get the Attraction!  Maybe If you go in early enough that you arrive before all the "FLIES" do everyday you'll have better luck at even finding a spot to park, let alone Finding it possible to walk in the joint n making it in past the the entrance  Sooooo if your checking it out be prepared for a small parking lot with no where to park an then accompanied with a small inside with no where to sit, being well over occupancy.

You get 2 stars for being a rebel and going against Wolf. The place is clean and staff is on par. I have stopped at this place a half dozen times and cannot figure for the life of me what they are thinking when it comes to their beer. If you only have two beers to offer then offer other local brews! If they have 6 beers listed they are normally out of 3-4 of them. It's been the same story since day one. Come on guys, it's not your first week anymore. All this being said, support your local establishments. Just please figure this out.
Fine dining it is not. Classy? No way! What they've got that makes it worth going: beer. Is the beer good? Meh. One star for the beer which is the only redeemable thing about the place.

Never been to a brewery with so few beers on tap. Every time I've been there's like 4 maybe 5. Beer and food is just ok. You won't complain but you won't be impressed either. Would be 2 stars but the place is clean and the staff is very nice

Absolutely no COVID restrictions, employees not wearing masks when carrying or even preparing food, no social distancing, nothing! This place is a complete disgrace to public safety and clearly does not care about the health of their patrons or employees.

Also you don't get to pretend you didn't just say this. How unprofessional.

They have an amber and a red!! The blood orange wheat is good also!
IPA's, stout, kolsch, pale ale, sour and a dark are on the menu also!
Food served here too!
Indoor and outdoor seating.
The staff are friendly and attentive!

What an awful place to go. Packed full of people, not socially distanced, full bar of people without masks, and none of the servers were wearing masks. Disgusting. People like this is the reason we're still in this pandemic. On top of it all the food was terrible. I zero stars was an option you would get it.

I tried placing an order online through a third party delivery service, DoorDash.  Working with the delivery service was easy and painless, unfortunately it appears as if The Borough Brewhouse is not yet operating in the present century.

Their posted online ordering selections were completely incorrect with almost none of the listed items available.  Once the service contacted me to find substitutions I figured it was merely of minor hiccup in the process.

They then called back and informed me that since it was 4:35 the new items were not available either since the restaurant would not start serving dinner until 5pm.   None of this was indicated on the order site.

When I suggested the simple solution of just holding the order until 5pm and then fulfilling it they called me back and cancelled the entire order.

If you are going to allow online ordering make sure your menu is current, your service times are properly noted and if these are not being properly attended to be accommodating to customers who are inconvenienced by your lack of basic technical ability and online marketing skills.

We have eaten at McKenzie many times but this was the worst ever.   We were invited to dine with friends so we did not make a big deal about our dinner quality,  Ordered the Chicken + Cashew Stir Fry.  Two I" pieces of chicken, tiny bits of broccoli,  tons of snow peas and the rest of the items on the menu were not found. The beer was delicious but dinner terrible. The waitress never came to the table to ask about the meal, that tells me something.

Outdoor dining was lovely.  Buddha bowl was delicious.  The fish and chips was a bit heavy. Overall I would go back, but i wouldn't order the fish.  Our server was sweet and helpful with food recommendations.

I would give it a 5 all but for the weird dinner roll experience and a really bad side salad. The crab brie bisque is stellar. I went with the spinach and 🍄 mushroom Calzone, which was so very good, I can't even describe it. My mother got chicken parm and asked for a small salad.  It came out as a handful of mixed greens. Nothing even on top. We were given 2 dinner rolls that were rock hard on top, impossible to cut with even a steak knife,  yet soaking wet and gooey on the bottom.  The butter was odd tasting.  Our server apologized,  so no worries. Brought out 2 more and they were fine... Just weird. Nice staff and clientele.

This was our first and last visit.  We ordered a double bourbon on the rocks @$14.75 and a glass of house Chardonnay @$7.50.  Really!
It likely would not have hurt so bad if the entre was not so pitiful.  We ordered the Fish and Chips (should be called chips and fish).  The plate was large and full of fries but the fish was 4 very small, very thin pieces of over battered over cooked fish.  We had asked if they recommended it and were told it was great.   It was not!

Worst and slowest service ever!! Would not recommend going for there food. The alcoholic drinks were good, but I was so disappointed by the horrible service I wouldnt go back. Some of our entrees where okay and a few werent even edible.
It was ok...not healthy food at all but soooo expensive. And very little food...didnt care for this place...all the drinks were sooo watered down...and did I mention expensive

Very disappointed,  meals took 45 min. Order was incomplete,  meal came with 2 sides, only received  one. Very poor service. We like to eat out to support  local restaurants,  but the food and service is below  normal standards
Outstanding job adjusting to the pandemic restrictions to accommodate the seated outdoor guests. Modified menu quality was good.  With time the accommodating  wrinkles will become smooth.

Very disappointed,  meals took 45 min. Order was incomplete,  meal came with 2 sides, only received  one. Very poor service. We like to eat out to support  local restaurants,  but the food and service is below  normal standards
Outstanding job adjusting to the pandemic restrictions to accommodate the seated outdoor guests. Modified menu quality was good.  With time the accommodating  wrinkles will become smooth.
Worst experience. I would like to give 0. Worst food taste. They missed an item from my to go order. Worst service. Dont waste your money and time.

Unfortunately although the food was satisfactory and the waitress was attentive and professional I overheard another staff using profanity in the restaurant and the waiter spilled ketchup over my plate and didn't bother to exchange it! Please do better...I won't be back there for a while!

My wife and I got the salmon and vegetables with rice. The salmon itself was super  DUPPER salty but the waitress was just ok about the recognition. Not to the fact that when I told her about the saltiness of the salmon that she did anything about it.  She just said she would say something about it. This isn't a bad review that I'm leaving to sure you. Dont want to run the Livelihood of someone else. The atmosphere was simi good. Great beer. We Wouldn't eat hear again though. As a Marylander I felt like eating the salmon was like eating crabs times 3 as far as taste when it comes to seasoning. If you want good beer and bar food then yes... family dinner Food .. No!

Had a large party and was seated in the bar area. May have liked it better if we had been seated in the dining room because bar area was very loud. Food was okay. Roast beef sandwich was tasty but the roast beef did not fill the whole roll. Very small portion of chicken parm. Homemade chips were delicious!

We used to like coming here for a bit of lunch, but after today we wont be coming back. I have never been treated so rudely at an establishment before. After waiting 20 minutes for our drinks, our appetizer came out first (before our beer at a BREW HOUSE) and was dropped without a word. We were given no utensils, napkins or small plates. We had to ask for utensils to eat with. Finally after asking again for drinks, our drinks came. Our food also took longer than expected. We were very frustrated. Our server barely returned to check on us.

We also ordered food to go. Our server brought our tab, on a small paper and slammed it on our table, with no little booklet to keep our card or cash in to hand back to her. She brought our change back WITHOUT the food to go (which we paid for on the tab). We started to head out to find a manager and found her mingling with other servers at a table. We politely asked about our food of which she seemed annoyed, asked "what food?" and rolled her eyes.

Very annoyed at the whole experience.

Just stopped in for a quick beer. Sat at the bar, we were served quickly, bill came right after we asked for it. Staff was friendly and helpful.  Beer was good, selection did not seem as big as the last time I was here.

Happy hour prices are pretty good,  we used to go here for drinks and it's a nice clean bar. Couple of bad experiences later and we don't go here anymore. Kept giving it a chance but some of the bartenders are just grumpy and difficult. This is strictly experiences at the bar, nothing to do with the restaurant part.
Service was excellent, beer is good.. tried all of them, food also excellent. Almost didn't go based on some of the neg reviews... glad we went

Menu is a blend of high end bar food and comfort food, ecclectic enough that anyone can find something they'll like. There are weekly specials as well as seasonal items. Portions are generous. The food rates a solid good, not great, the quality might me a victim of the large menu. Bar area is well separated from the restaurant area, basically a sport bar, lots of screens. Considering we visited on a saturday night, the place was not crowded, but weather / sports events will cause that to fluctuate, and parking may be a problem. There is a huge covered deck, which was not open for our visit.

Good Food
Good Brew
Great Service

Enjoyed the food especially the potato chips.
Service was a tad slow. But I will be back.

Good service, tasty tacos!  Craft beer made on site.    Recommend for tavern food and More!  I tried the chilli with chorizo.  Very Good.  Just wish they brought out some fresh bread to go with it instead of a hamburger role.
Service is always polite and friendly. Food and drinks are fresh and correct. Clean, comfortable atmosphere.
Food was quite good, even though made a mistake with my order. Place also looked amazing.
Spacious Bar area, good variety of food selections. Waitress was great!

Have been patronizing since opening. This past year we’ve noticed a decline in the quality of food & beer.  Most beer choices  have a citrus flavor.  Food is inconsistent, it’s either delicious or under cooked and tasteless.  Service has been mediocre with new hires.  Training & systems need attention
Great lunch, i had driven past many times snd i am glad we went today.

McKenzies McSucks.  Visiting Microbreweries and Brew Pubs is a hobby I've had for over 30 years now, and this was a genuine disappointment.  The beer lacked any real body and dominant flavor indicative of the local area.  They food was average at best and very bland.  I tried a Bloody Mary to try and save the experience figuring any establishment that advertises  "Bloody Mary Bar" should be rocking this tasty breakfast treat.  The drink served tasted of simple tomato juice, and they don't even carry horseradish...

This place may work for others, but true microbrew enthusiasts will be disapointed.
Brunch is just OK. Not as many options as other places. Some of the food was cold or Luke warm. No egg whites available and you cant order from a menu during brunch.

Had high expectations but did not deliver. Food was so so and atmosphere was ok. Wedge salad was a disaster. Whatever you do, do not order the Brewhouse Sausage. It looks and tastes like something Dr. Frankenstein made.

Very disappointing.  I stopped in for a late lunch. I was seated at a table in the bar area.  I was the only table in the section. The waitress left me with my menu and then disappeared for aat lrast ten minutes.  When she finally returned and toom my order shr thrn again disappeared.  It took almost 45 minutes for my burger to come out and it came out in correctly, so by the time I finally saw a person (NOT my waitress) and was able to get the bacon fot my burger, the burger was cold.  The burger was on a brioche roll, which was at best "day old" and at worst stale.

I will not return.

I usually drink the brew, Saison Vatour at this brewery. That is my favorite brew here.  They have several other kinds of brew which I have tasted in the past.
The server Penny was very kind and helpful.
Eggs were overcooked, toast was rock solid, and they forgot to bring out my bacon. Waitress was nice though.
The menu has been expanded from what I remembered, I was pleasantly surprised
We do music trivia night at McKenzie's!  Always great and friendly service...solid pub fare...great music trivia fun...laughs!
Usually very good. This time really dry chicken sandwich and the roll on the roast beef sandwich was stale.

They advertised they'd be open until 2 am on New Year's Eve, but instead closed at 11:30 and ruined the night for multiple groups of people. This is not a company that cares about the customer at all; save your money and go elsewhere.
Friendly. Good food. Nice atmosphere.
food not. so much beer not so much new brewer

Went there and had a terrible time. Then I received an email from the GM asking how he could make it right and he was sorry. I said "thank and I'd like to try again" to only be met with silence. So not only do they provide bad service and are a restaurant that servers beer just like everybody else, they are also liars.
Great food and beer! Originally was not happy due to an unresolved issue, but Billy O’Neill took initiative and reached out to help.
The beer menu was larger than the food menu
Just got a variety pack for Father’s Day don’t know how it happens but some cans are only half filled an taste horrible I checked for leaks couldn’t find a single one an the box didn’t have no damage

Although Victory is famous for their brews, I have to say that every time I have ordered food, I have been very disappointed.  The service is always slow, the dining room is extremely noisy, and the quality of food is poor.  Really poor.  I now they can do better!

Great locals at the bar, friendly. Staff, bartenders couldn't have cared less for the customers. Very standoffish. Signed up for a tour that no one showed up to lead, so we just left. Disappointing experience.
the beer is good the food is very poor if you want to get get beers go for it but if you want to eat food  I do not recommend
the beer is good the food is very poor if you want to get get beers go for it but if you want to eat food  I do not recommend

Disappointed with my recent trip to Victory. My wife and I used to visit frequently (once a month). We were visiting for the first time in about a year, and all of the food was bad. We’re not food snobs, but the nachos were dry and lacking flavor (and cheese) and the margherrita pizza tasted like frozen pizza. Also, it was a busy Friday night and our server wasn’t around for 15 minutes then came back smelling like smoke. The beer was solid as usual. I don’t write reviews often, but I’m writing this in hopes that Victory can get back to the quality that it used to be.

It's really just ok. Nothing special about the food or beer. They had a terrible DJ hosting quizzo and he was loud, lame, and annoying. Ruined the vibe. If you're in town, theres no harm in checking it out. But nothing about this place wowed me in any way.

Beer was ok, food was not.  I ordered the chicken schnitzel with bacon spaetzle.  First, it was not a chicken schnitzel (even though I asked the server) it was a chicken breast, fried and not pounded flat.  It was dry and tasteless.  The bacon spaetzle was deep fried (who does that?) and tasted like uncooked pasta.  Was expecting better.  I asked for info before I ordered this dish and wait person basically lied to me.

Unfortunately we did not have any beers because we had a tasting elsewhere so we stuck around for just dinner.  Perhaps the food would have tasted good if we had had beers.  The burgers were tasteless with normal toppings and nothing really fancy even though the menu indicated such.

The burgers were ordered medium and they came medium well.  It has the feeling of a chain restaurant even though it's a local place.

The short rib poutine was tasty yet super salty.  I should skip checking this place out unless you just want beer.

The fries were good.
Brew Works have always done beer quite well. Food is quite good as is service. I love their consistency..

My coworker and I decided to go here on our lunch break. We were seated at 11:45 in a row of booths. The waiter kept going to the other 3 booths, but never stopped at ours. After about 7-8 minutes the host walks by. I let him know that the waiter still had not been over to even get our drink order. The host said he could get our drinks but they were very busy and only had two waiters working. He brings our drinks, says something to the waiter pointing in our direction, and seats another table behind us. My coworker and I then notice the waiter goes over and takes that tables drink order, brings their drinks, and then takes their food order. This was all without stopping at our table even once! Well needless to say we couldn’t stay any longer (it had been close to 20 minutes) since we were on a lunch break. What was supposed to be a nice lunch on a Saturday work day ended up turning into scarfing down fast food.

**Update: The manager made sure to reach out to me very quickly to apologize and make up for the bad experience we had. The manager had great customer service.**

Nice spot...Very spacious, so they can accommodate larger crowds. The beer is always excellent, though the food menu could you a bit a shake-up more often. Overall, a quality spot with good food and excellent beer.

We chose this location for our company Christmas dinner and the service was sub par. Our drinks were lacking in alcohol, ALL the food was cold when it was served at our table. One of our party received a raw steak after ordering medium well. Another of our party was served raw ribs that were too spicy to be consumed, even if they had been returned, and when mentioned that they were too spicy, the "manager" continued to question it like if we were unknowning of what SPICY meant. We were also charged for the food they had to correct. All in all, the experience was terrible. The server was no where to be found for long periods of time, even though they weren't even busy, and when asked for a take out box for one of the other people in our party, she brought the smallest one they had available and our coworker couldn't even fit her food in the box. An order of fish and chips had random beans in it. We were informed by the "manager" that they would be taking $75.00 off our bill, but all he did was removed the food we were unable to eat and still charged us for the replacements. The experience was a really negative one and we will not be recommending this place to anyone that we may know.

Went there last night for dinner.  Got the Whiskey burger which came out room temperature, well done instead of medium and missing the fried onions and sauce that the menu described. Sat at the bar which was half full and the bartenders service was terrible. Had to flag the barback multiple times to get the bartender to pay attention for drinks/condiments/etc.
Lots of other good food and beer options out there so would not recommend.

Beyond disappointing. We made reservations with a table of 8 and it took over 30 minutes to get the first round of beers. 45 minutes later the half the food came out and all of it was cold. Another 30 minutes and the rest of the food came out. Burgers were all cooked to well done despite being ordered medium. Multiple meals had issues with missing toppings for burgers and sandwiches. All the fries were cold. It took so long to get beers we stopped ordering. The place wasn’t busy and not even half full. Spent over two hours and a half hours and only was able to try two beers and a bunch of cold mediocre food. Would not recommend

The food and beer was great. The reason I give this place a 4 stars is because we got our 4 appetizers before we got our beer. It took about 10-15 mins to get our first round of beers. I expected my first beer to be delivered before my apps but either way, still good.

Poor service,they weren't busy. ordered brat sandwich,out of Cole slaw, forgot mustard,did I say poor service. Had to keep asking for drinks. When we finally received food it was cold and rolls were soggy because they were sitting too long. Go somewhere else..
Beautiful place. Took my wife here two times and loved the scenic view. The food is a little pricey but great beer and great burgers.

Not great food or beer. The event that made this meal memorable was when the server arrived with our food. There was a large group of us and many plates came out at once. He lifted the bun on my sandwich to see what it was. With his hand, right in front of me. Gross.

I used to love coming here for a good brew and a meal. The last two times I ate here was not the same. Staff was great. Food was not. Salad was drenched in dressing. Poutine was bland...old meat and the gravy was watery/oily from the cheese. We should have taken the trip to the bethlehem brew works.
don't worry if it's busy, it is worth the wait. the service was very helpful and observant. prices were fair.

First time here yesterday and last. Made reservations and food was still late (over an hour). We asked to speak to the manager,  they informed us that 2 dinners were messed up and had to be redone (that's understandable) but inform the party waiting for the food on what is happening, so they don't think you've forgotten them. The waiter was super nice!!!! 2 meals were not good, can tell it was just sitting up there, before being sent to us!  Couldn't do a drink for me, then were out a a beer I choose.
They comped some tickets, mine was $3 off,  great!!! Not going back!

Good Allentown staple bar/restaurant/venue. Decent food, albeit on the slightly pricey side unless you get there for happy hour or various specials. Staff is nice and helpful more often than not. Wide beer selection. Can get busy, leading to a diminished experience during hockey games and other events at nearby venues, so be sure to check on area events, the live Google timeline, or call ahead if you're planning for a group or are trying to find a quick bite to eat.

Had to wait 15 min. for packaged cans to go at 1:00 pm on a slow Sunday. My first and last time here. Bartender didn't inspire me to spend another dollar. Entrance lobby of establishment had a small gauntlet of street people saying "get me something".

My family and I came for dinner before a hockey game. Our waitress, Madeline took forever to get our food and drinks, forget our silverware, didn't give is straws, forgot to take our menus and never checked if we needed anything. It was horrible service

We were very disappointed tonight with our experience.  We had been here a couple of years ago as a family and loved it so we didn't think twice about suggesting we go as a group of 13 with my daughter's college room mates and their parents.  We had a reservation for 13 at 6pm and when we arrived we were seated promptly.  That was the best thing that happened tonight!  We waited about 20 minutes before we finally were able to put in food and drinks orders, the drinks took a good 15 to 20 minutes to arrive.  The server didn't remove any empties over the entire time we were there and when she brought something to the table, it seemed she could only handle a single task at one time.  No checking if we needed anything else, if everything was ok etc.  They obviously missed out on a number of drinks sales, because we never saw our server to order more!  Finally after waiting for our food for a full hour after ordering, we hailed our server down to see what was going on and that was when she told us that they were out of fish and chips (a signature dish!!!!????!?) and that they were holding all the food until the person who ordered the fish and chips could select something else.  We were still waiting for appetizers and soups and they hadn't appeared yet.  Lo and behold, one of the first things to appear was the replacement order for the fish and chips!!  WTH?  My soup came about 5 minutes after the salad I ordered and once the food started coming it took about 15 minutes for it all to arrive.  That is, all but one of the appetizers, which we finally got when we asked for the check.  The food itself was hit or miss.  I enjoyed my soup and salad (but had to ask for a dressing after it had been delivered undressed) but those getting the fried chicken dish were disappointed with the small portion, strange combination of sides and general presentation.  One of our party with allergies had asked for the sweet potatoes to be done without mushrooms yet they still came with mushrooms mixed in and had to wait for another 5 or 10 minutes until her plate was re-prepared without the mushrooms.  We finally go out of there just after 8pm.....not soon enough unfortunately.

Despite any claims to the contrary, and not that we were looking for comps, we were still charged full price for our meals.  I don't want money off my visit.  I want a better quality visit, at least to meet a minimum reasonable standard which we had seen on a previous visit.

A group of us decided to go to Brew Works after opening day inservice nearby. Upon entering the hostess greeted us by saying "you must be teachers too." As our group ordered meals the server came back multiple times first telling us the restaurant was out of arugula, then they were out of the soy sauce that typically goes on the brussel sprouts. The restaurant was also out of 2 specials on the menu. This would typically be understandable but it was a Tuesday afternoon and the restaurant was not busy. When we received our bill one member in our group had an additional upcharge for a side of macaroni and cheese which she was not originally told while ordering. The restaurant did rectify this by comping the item for the guest.
When we asked for boxes and our bill it took the server some time to figure out which bill belonged to which guest and she came back several times as she forgot to bring boxes.
It was overall just a disappointing visit.
Good beer selection
The service is very nice the craft beer is off the wall and the people who work there are knowledgeable and lively I like this place.
Great few and drinks I really like it here but like the original one in Bethlehem a bit better, may just be a personal choice
Really enjoyed the great food and great brew.. Staff + food+brewery+ Allentown= satisfied appitite,quenched thirst and respect

Very disappointed when I got takeout and opened the box of what was supposed to be shepherd's pie and it looked like a thin layer of vomit.  No meat or veggies in it at all. When I called to tell them they said it doesn't travel well and they are too busy to receive a picture of it. They offered a gift card for the amount but honestly it really grossed me out and I can't believe anybody would've put that in a container to give to a customer...

Just has the worse meal ever the service was so bad. We didn't get the starter we ordered and the fries were burnt. Had to find the server to order the dessert which was also wrong when it came and the correct desert was thrown at us. Dont go here.
Service was horrible. It's a shame usually pretty good. Tonight it was worse then a dive diner.

I am currently sitting in this establishment and i have waited an hour and a half for my meal and everyone around me has been served prior to my food arriving, even though they arrived hours after me. The food is cold, ironic as i was told everything is running slow in the kitchen. On top of that, one of the others in my party did not receive their order at all. Also, the waiters are difficult to get a hold of and the manager was also very unhelpful. Overall, not a great experience and if i could give this place a lower rating i definitely would.  As a result of this poor, lengthy experience i had to cancel the rest of my evening plans. Highly dissatisfied.
The atmosphere is awesome. The food is good, the service is great, but it's a little expensive. All and all I'd say it's worth checking out. Even with the high prices I've gone several times.

Multi level (4 floors) for a variety of social gathering possibilities. Good bar food and always great beer. A little disappointed they didn't have their award winning ESB available like the Bethlehem location.
The service was friendly and promote. The menu is varied. We had 3 different beers and dishes all were average tasting.

We were there for a bridal shower and for what the girls paid for the food and the hall was not worth it.  We had fruit, rolls/danishes, scrambled eggs, bacon, hash browns potatoes, mimosas, coffee and juice. They ran out of food while people were still in line and took forever to replenish. They did not have anymore potatoes.  Mimosas tasted watered down.  There was no salt or pepper, we had to request this and by the time we got it we were almost done eating.  This was our 2nd time using the banquet facility and the first time was not any better.  Would not recommend this place for banquets. We did have dinner here once and that was good.
This has been a favorite everytime I'm in Allentown. Can't beat the brews he but was disappointed with the lack of smoke flavor in the ribs definitely different from a few years back.
This has been a favorite everytime I'm in Allentown. Can't beat the brews he but was disappointed with the lack of smoke flavor in the ribs definitely different from a few years back.
They have tasty vegetarian and pescetarian dishes but are more known for their beer and  meat dishes.
They have tasty vegetarian and pescetarian dishes but are more known for their beer and  meat dishes.
Just aweful. We just left, wasn't informed of happy hour, absolutely AWEFUL service from our waitress, the food runner is the only redemption and reason for the 1 star.
A good place to grab a beer before a phantoms game. Trivia night is a good time too, I've won a couple of growlers and money towards the tab.
Nasty food :(  I won't go back to this restaurant again.
Very good food service was great will return!

If there was a no star rating I would choose it. We went with four a few weeks ago at 8 pm on a Saturday thinking it would be the same quality we have grown to know about the Bethlehem brew works. Boy were we wrong. The place was empty and it took the four of us 3 hours to eat dinner. Food items came out cold and incorrectly. Sour cream came on the veggie burger when mayo was asked. When we asked to have the bun replaced it took 20 minutes and came back ice cold. We were not asked for drink or beer refills once. To top it off there was some weird adult costume event it the basement attracting some shady characters.
The appetizer fills you. No room for anything else.
The wait staff was friendly but were impatient with the bus staff. There was no wait to be seated so the place ran smoothly
Attended a wedding here.  Nice place.
I love local breweries and Fegley's has some of the best local beer and food.
The pretzel bites with cheese sauce are my favorite appetizer!! And the service is usually very good.
Better customer service !
Keep those $4.00 Long Islands comin'
Good food good beer
Keep those $4.00 Long Islands comin'
Under staffed and over priced
Good people and a fun spot!  I’ve been there a few times since opening and every time is better than before.  They’re getting it dialed.

Decent brew and guest brews. I found the bartender an older gentlemen to be rude and unfriendly.  First visit, not sure they put their best foot forward.

Went back same bartender. Much more cordial and friendly.  Still could be more engaged with the customer's , would find he makes more if he does.

New place so its was busy but...waited a good 15 min before someone even asked if anyone helped us. Watched another couple leave before they were helped. Beer I asked for was tapped, no biggie...food came out cold. When server came to ask everything was she was immediately interrupted but front of the house hostess about other people coming in and we couldn't even get a chance to ask for water. Overall, meh...other brewerys in the valley with better selection and better food.

Although the staff was friendly they new almost nothing about the beer they were serving.  I was served a beer from Sly Fox that was 3/4 head and when I questioned "what's that" I was told " that happens when you tap a new keg"  The wood fired pizza was under cooked with some nearly raw dough in the middle.  I will find it difficult to ever return.
Great place for brand new beers and fresh food. Usually pretty crowded, so make sure you are prepared for a wait.
Cool place with good food.  It's going to be beautiful with better weather

Went for lunch as my first visit. First they brought me the wrong order, then i found a 6 inch piece of hair in my sandwich. No one i observed making food had a hair net on. I ask for another sandwich to go since i dont have enough time to wait for another and i also lost my appetite.  They bring me a sandwich on a plate and make me box it up. When she brings the check its a 9 dollar tab, i give her a 20, she comes back with two 5s. You expect a 60% tip after my horrible experience? Needless to say she didnt get a tip. When i get back to work which is only 2 seconds away the sandwich they gave me was ice cold, they didnt even bother making me a hot one to go. I will not be going back.

Went for lunch as my first visit. First they brought me the wrong order, then i found a 6 inch piece of hair in my sandwich. No one i observed making food had a hair net on. I ask for another sandwich to go since i dont have enough time to wait for another and i also lost my appetite.  They bring me a sandwich on a plate and make me box it up. When she brings the check its a 9 dollar tab, i give her a 20, she comes back with two 5s. You expect a 60% tip after my horrible experience? Needless to say she didnt get a tip. When i get back to work which is only 2 seconds away the sandwich they gave me was ice cold, they didnt even bother making me a hot one to go. I will not be going back.
The beers weren’t bad but I was a little upset that out of the 12 taps they have only 3 beers were their own.
The beers weren’t bad but I was a little upset that out of the 12 taps they have only 3 beers were their own.

Visited today late afternoon. I was all prepared to leave a glowing review based on the beer & food. I think the Untappd ratings are a bit unfair for this brewery - I tried Embers Remain and Argyle Sweater and thought both were above average for their respective beer styles.

However, the service really turned me off. It was okay at first, food came out quick, but I felt I sat there 10-15 minutes after finishing before my server asked if I was ready for the check, and then another 10-15 to actually get the check. It was getting closer to dinner time, so I suppose it was becoming busy, but still... unacceptably long wait time.

If you're visiting Lititz and you enjoy beer, definitely worth a stop, but wouldn't call this a "must-do".

Food was good, but a bit pricey. Service was decent. Covid guidelines were well followed, which was appreciated, seeing as Lititz has been very lacking in that department. All in all, good beer, good food, good experience.
Great beer, great food! My husband and I always love our experience here. I just wish they’d mix up the menu with some different items more. It’d be exciting to see some new stuff!

We really enjoyed the food here.  The beer was OK.  I ordered the burger which was topped with pulled pork and cole slaw and it was awesome.  My wife got the fish and chips and they were awesome too.  The tartar sauce was amazing with it.  The house made chips were so good, we ordered a batch to go!  We also ordered a flight of 8 beers.  They were good and I like the opportunity to try many different 5oz beers and splitting it with my wife was nice.

Foods great but hopefully your not thirsty because they only come around once in a blue moon and they take forever to bring you your check I could see if they are busy but it’s 4 o’clock and this is the second time I was here and it was like this. But food is amazing
Good beers and food was better than expected!
Good food and beverage, friendly staff, and nice atmosphere. Try the Rockfish taco!
Everything seems to be fine, but there are some staff that needs to observe but still good customer service!!
Food was good, beer was very good, service totally sucked

My boyfriend witnessed one of the cooks leaving the restroom without washing his hands and followed this man (by chance) to the restaurant, where the employee went directly to the kitchen and proceeded to cook.

Was looking forward to trying this because I've heard good things about the chain. Was incredibly disappointed. Was directed to the bar (which was dirty) instead of being sat at a table. No big deal. Bartender asks for my drink order and if I want a food menu, to which I say yes. She then fills a soda glass halfway, leaves it behind the bar, then walks away. A couple of minutes later she comes back, fills the glass the rest of the way and asks for a 2nd time if I wanted a menu, so I say yes for a second time. I looked through the menu and decided what I want but the bartender never came back to take my order. So I left money on the bar for my undrinkably flat soda and left. I won't be returning.

Great atmosphere and service. Nice temperature inside and the building is beautiful. The food was really bad though. I ordered the most expensive thing on the menu which was steak tips. They were way overdone and very chewy with charred vegetables that were soupy and overdone. My girlfriend got a turkey burger with pineapple on it. It was decent aside from a very dry bun that basically fell apart while she was eating it. The fries she got were dry and maybe sitting for a while before they were served? Pretty bad food. Love the beers they have though.

This location is really circling the drain. Service was poor, food was burnt beyond recognition and nobody really seemed to care about fixing the issues at all when asked. I couldn't even get the bartender to come swipe my card after 20 minutes of trying to get her attention. And yes, I was still charged for the burnt food after bringing it up. Also, bring your own paper products for the ladies room, because they are probably out. Truly terrible experience, and I will not ever go back.
Great food, brewhouse atmosphere, nice experience but it gets slammed so it took about a half hour to get our food order so don't go on a Saturday night if you have anywhere to be.

Went tonight with friends. It was their first time. We always enjoyed ABC Lititz and thought we would share the experience. What a disappointment. When we arrived at 7 pm there was a sign on the door saying they kitchen closed at 8 pm. They never posted that on their website.

Waitress was horrible, forgot one of our meals. Had to ask for refills. She didn’t know the menu or the beers. They were out of mountain lager. We were rushed out.

Won’t be back. Place is going down hill.

Similar experience as the previous person. Came here for the first time with my family, miserable women working.

My daughter's food was scolding hot, my wife's French dip meal was totally cold including fries and au jus.

That's after waiting 45 min for food. Barely saw our waitress .. and she didn't bother to ask how everything was like she already knew it was going to be bad. This reflects poor management. Not a comp meal or apology... Nothing, repeats with other reviews

I like this place but they can't get my sweet potatoes fries right.  I got an order a few months ago and they were extremely burnt.  I decided to go and order again today.  Guess what, got them the same way  - burnt.  They bought me another order and only about 12 fries were edible without a burnt aftertaste.  I'll come here again for a beer but not for the food.

I like this place but they can't get my sweet potatoes fries right.  I got an order a few months ago and they were extremely burnt.  I decided to go and order again today.  Guess what, got them the same way  - burnt.  They bought me another order and only about 12 fries were edible without a burnt aftertaste.  I'll come here again for a beer but not for the food.
I changed the rating to 3. Good food and good service but overly priced for my liking. Not a place for someone who wants a cheap meal. They also charge you for soda refills..

I'm sorry   but i just don't like it here. I have been there 3 times to give it a fair chance but I just can't give it more then two stars.Food always taste   like cheap generic food service food( just my opinion). Service always seem slow as molasses even when the place is half empty. 2 out of three times i had stomach pains by the time I got home.

We went to Appalachian Brewing Company tonight for my dad’s bday because abt 2 months ago we went and he really liked the food. Tonight, on the other hand, was a complete disaster from start to finish. The hostess couldn’t seat us when we arrived, even though they had a table ready, because they didn’t have enough menus. Our waiter told us that the burgers had been coming out very undercooked all night, so we all ordered them one degree more than we typically would. From the time we got seated, it took 1 hour and 10 minutes to get the 4 burgers we ordered. At about 1 hour the manager came over to tell us our ticket was up next and apologized for the wait, informing us that they had 3 new line cooks and the dining room pretty much all got seated at the same time. Our burgers were beyond overcooked!!!! I’m not exaggerating, one side of the burger was so charred that when I banged it on the side of my plate to show the manager it actually made the same noise as a utensil on a plate. She was so mortified she gave us the entire meal for free. Great, free inedible food .... no offer to remake the food, no offer for dessert, no offer for a voucher to come back another night and give them another chance.

Do yourself a favor, go somewhere else!!!!
Tried multiple times.  Terrible service for a large party, cold food, and plastic from the cole slaw bag in a sandwich.

Great root beer & varied menu. Beef on weck, Malt Haus salad with blackened chicken, and grilled Caesar salad were fantastic!
However- takeout accuracy is terrible. We have ordered several times and been incorrectly up-charged for items not included in the order or not received our entire order. Disappointing because the food is great!

Edited: Yet another attempt at takeout, tonight the kitchen was too backed up to accommodate any additional orders. Get it together.

However- takeout accuracy is terrible. We have ordered several times and been incorrectly up-charged for items not included in the order or not received our entire order. Disappointing because the food is great!

Edited: Yet another attempt at takeout, tonight the kitchen was too backed up to accommodate any additional orders. Get it together.
Chicken ordered was not done. Pink and blood in middle.  Probably will not be back
Solid beers, but only so so food. Avoid the curry chicken and the tacos, and stick with their burgers.
Took two hours to eat and food was bad but the service was great
Young women working up front with the host (possibly a manager) was very unfriendly with a horrible attitude.
Need pub snacks, pistachio nuts or something.
Highly recommend beer. Great fruity sours. Food on Sunday very limited brunch (breakfast) to 3pm. Then only apps no sandwiches or burgers. Housemade Seasoned tortilla chips are good

This place is really big! Decor is trendy and nice to look at. It’s in the strip district so parking was tough to find on a Saturday night. The beer though was pretty excellent. I highly recommend the raspberry whipper if you like sours as much as I do.

Been a few times, had some hiccups with each visit. Latest trip there were 4 of us they only brought out 3 of our entrees. We thought the other one was coming but the wait staff never said anything or checked. Waited ten minutes and had to walk over to check with our server who said oh it must have been brought down stairs. Okay, not a big deal but it was a hot dish that they completely forgot about then offered no apology and served it to me cold. Tough to go back after that level of customer service

I’ve been quite a few times since opening. It’s great space with okay drinks, but definitely not managed in the best fashion. The staff are constantly trying to enforce all of these odd rules (last call at 10:30pm on a weekend, no splitting checks for more than four people, no taking your drink from the bar to outside, etc) and aren’t too friendly about it. The food is eh as well compared to all the other great places in the Strip. The Lawrenceville location is much better.

The burger description was short and not clear. It was not what i expected. The burger was very very sweet and difficult to eat. Fries were good. My husbands beans were very salty. He ordered the can bird. The description says half of a bird it was a wing, small leg, and part of a small thigh. Not enough chicken but more than enough beans. They are expensive. I ordered a rose and its 12 for 4 oz.

I love the Cinderlands in Lawrenceville so I thought I would love the one in the strip, but that was not the case. It was barely reminiscent of the Lawrenceville location. The downstairs is just a giant room and it was so loud I could barely hear the person right next to me. The service was pretty rough, we only saw our server maybe twice the  whole time we were there. It was also hard to find something on the menu because everything was pretty outrageously priced. We split the beer can chicken, which I would describe as 'okay'. Certainly not worth $20.00 and will certainly not get it again. The saving grace was the beer I had, but I'm not sure that is enough to make me want to go back to this location. Overall, very disappointing.

The beers were good but pricey. The food was decent and the service was friendly. Had the pirogies that probably had more onion then pirogies (still decent) and the German Reuben, which looked to be described as something special but really wasn’t (still not bad). Fair place but probably not worth another trip.

I wanted to like this place more because the staff were very nice and accommodating, but it could have been that my group were the only ones in there (which struck me as odd since we went during the lunch rush hour). I had the brisket nachos. From the menu description, I was expecting shredded brisket (which was my fault because I THOUGHT that's what it said, however it does just say "slow-roasted beef brisket), but I was also expecting the salsa and sour cream on top. Unfortunately, the nachos came out as if they were microwaved. The cheese had that glossy, microwave coating and the tortilla chips were toasted (just under burnt). The flavor was okay but it left much to be desired. Another in my party had the Rachel sandwich and said it, too, was okay. They used turkey lunch meat instead of fresh. The drinks were probably the best part. We also got a round of the pretzel sticks, which weren't bad, but the Pub cheese seemed like it came straight out of the fridge. It wasn't creamy/smooth like other Pub cheeses I've had. Like I said, the service was nice and everyone was so welcoming, but the food left so much to be desired. This was a one-and-done for me.
Not bad selection to choose from and atmosphere inside definitely different. I'd be willing to make a second stop.
The beer is good. But needs more lunch options and price deals for people that work in downtown.
Always a solid pub atmosphere.
Waited outside over 5 minutes and were not acknowledged by any server. Lofted, not impressed.
Wings were freezer burnt.  Menu needs work. Beer was good.
Food is pretty sub par.  Chili is dry and flavorless.
It was small, but just as loyd as the full size brewery.
Beer list is good!
Fortunately 1 stout on tap! Attended an event so can't review food options
Horrible

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Only received 4 stars because of the weird hours. I guess they don't want to be a late night hang out bar?? I don't know. Otherwise, the food is great, the beer is great and it is incredibly well done on the inside. Never expected it to look like that the first time I walked in. I guess the outside could be nicer. Anyway, food and beer is most important. Great, innovative place in an area that desperately needs great, innovative places. Well done.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: I loved this place!!  The beer, food and staff were great. Both the burgers and polish food were fantastic. The environment was laid back and relaxed.  The beer selection was good as well.  We will be returning in the future to try more of the menu.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Good food, solid beer, very tasty tradtional Polish recipes. Worth a stop if you're in Lehighton

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text:
Beer was good but I am sitting here writing this because I ordered food 40 minutes ago and they still haven't managed to produce my hot dog. 😑

Update: got my food 50 min in. It was in fact, delicious. Shouldn't take close to an hour to make 2 hot dogs.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Best spot to eat in the area.  Authentic Polish food plus more.  Craft beers that change weekly.  Good prices.  Worth every penny

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text:
Food wise, we got the polish platter and everything was very good!
The house beer was Okay, we did really like the blueberry shandy!
Great local place for a drink and polish food!

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Ordered a cheeseburger and an order of fries with cheese on the side.  I asked for lettuce and tomato and they forgot it. The bun had no condiments on it, and one slice of white american cheese.  The fries were better than the burger. Beer selection wasn't good and pricy. Won't be going back again.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Really great lunch stop! Very good service and excellent food!

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: The beer was less than average.  The pale ale, for example, tasted a bit smokey for some reason.  I also tried the Radical Wine Meathead.  It started out tasting like Manischewitz concord wine, but got a bit better toward the end of the glass.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text:
Food and beer were decent. However the waitress was extremely inattentive.

Not a very pleasant dinning experience at all.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: The food had a good taste, but once I realized that the cooks DO NOT wear gloves or hair nets, I was disgusted, especially after I watched the one older, blonde cook pick popcorn right from the maker and started eating it and then went back to the kitchen to cook.  It's a shame, because I had a really sweet server!

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text:
Service was good, cheesesteak with their "special“ chilli sauce was alright. The sauce had a curry taste to it and it didn't fit on a cheesesteak. The potato pancakes were excellent!

Worth a try for sure.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Such a disappointment. Most of the food was very bland.  Halupki was soggy. Kielbasa was overcooked.  The woman who took our order was nice and the place was clean but she went right from handling money to wiping down tables and right back to handling food with her bare hands without ever washing them.  We will not be going back.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Food and beer was ok.  Service was pretty terrible.  Waitress messed our orders up, had no clue how to split a check and never checked on us outside.  Wouldn't come back

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: My experience was not what i expected. The place looks nice , The waitresses seemed like they enjoyed their job. That was all I found to be good. The bar counter was so sticky I never touched it. . I only had a half hour so I got the fish and chips . Simple food and very good when done right.  I got the fried seafood with fries. The worst fish and chips I ever had. The shrimp were so small you felt like you were eating a grease ball of bread. The long stick fish,which looks like it was right out of high school was a step above the shrimp. The sole fish which i was hoping would be the savior ,was the worst piece of fried fish i every ate. NO flavor in the breading and oil burnt to a crisp . Beer Batter? No flavor beer batter. The only good thing i ate was the cole slaw. The icing on the cake of this experience was I paid $20 with the tip. I would have enjoy a whopper more and it would have been 1/3 the price. I wasn't in the mood to complain .It seems like every time you go out to eat ,you are accepting mediocrity and paying a high price for it.  If you want to drink a beer and feel ill ,go get some grease at Red Castle. Never Again. OH yah . Change the fry oil once in a while!!Go to Red Robin and try their fish and chips you just may learn something. Thats not saying much. Believe me this place aint making it on Diners Drive in and Dives! I cant believe all the good reviews. I guess people are use to eating crap these days. Or maybe they drink so much beer they can't taste the food.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Great food, friendly service, and distinctive beer/wine. I'll be back tomorrow!

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: I usually stop here for a taste from the old coutry. The food is heavy and large just like my german relatives.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: I've been there several times. I was there tonight. I ordered two king salads to go with dressing on the side for tomorrow and an extra dressing for each. Salads came out with dressing on them. I asked the bartender to take them back and bring me two with dressing on the side. She then brought out two new salads, no extra dressing. She went back and came out with one extra dressing. I asked for one extra dressing for each salad. She said I could only have one. I offered to pay for the extra dressing but the bartender said she was told I could not have anymore. I was very taken back by this. Was the cook mad because I sent two salads back because she made two mistakes and I sent them back? She probably doesn't care but I will never go back again. That was just not customer friendly in my book! I hope the owner reads this😔

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: We showed up at 7:50 p.m. and they state that they close at 8 p.m. Despite their published hours, they denied us service, even though there were at least a dozen people eating and drinking. We only wanted take out. Poor service. Stay away.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Great the Hunter's stew now in the winter!!

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Very good

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: They may be open till 10pm but don't expect to get any decent food after 8pm when they switch to a garbage "late night" menu Thurs - Sat. Plus your pilsner sucks.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: Mediocre execution.

business_name: Red Castle Brewery, Brewpub & Wine Bar
review_text: We came for the pierogies; we'll be back for a beer flight.

Very disappointed! We walked in at 7:30 on a Saturday night with two other friends to celebrate my birthday. We were rudely greeted by the "manager" on duty who instantly said he was closing in ten minutes. When I clarified that they were supposed to be open until 9, he said that he hadn't had anyone for the last two hours (dinner time) and that he was closing early, even though 4 of us just walked in and there were 3 people there from the adjacent bar. They walked out because it was be incredibly awkward and he was very rude. I honestly don't know how this guy became manager. I worked in food service for 8 years. I would have been bummed but understanding if he said it nicely, but he was incredibly rude. I commented on the rudeness and he continued to argue with me about his decision. I am so disappointed. I made my birthday plans to go there because I love mead and tested one of their meads before and really enjoyed it. I'm not sure how I feel about returning if he is working.
Good Spirits... John Took Good Care Of Us!

It's a brewery with a tap-room. The beer is clean and within guidelines, and there's ample parking. Did not get to try the vittles-truck, but it's on the list for next time. Love the idea of a different food truck every weekend.

It's a brewery with a tap-room. The beer is clean and within guidelines, and there's ample parking. Did not get to try the vittles-truck, but it's on the list for next time. Love the idea of a different food truck every weekend.
GMO bioanalysis.   Very odd; seems they love pesticides and toxic chemicals!   Round it up!!!

This place was great! Everyone there was really enjoying themselves. It is a family establishment, there were two large families with younger kids when we were there. The hot and honey wings were really good, the hand cut fries were fantastic. The wife's chicken sandwich was perfectly cooked, however the menu said didn't specify it came with a dry slaw and it should have been more like a dressing. The beer was right up any NE IPA lover's style. They didn't have any stout's, porter's, or any west coast style IPAs. Maybe next time we go they'll have more of a variety! Fingers crossed cause this place is worth going back to!

Had high hopes, but was disappointed. Mac and cheese was tasteless and noodles were too hard. Had a falafel salad that had a raw potato fry in it and the falafel was mushy and not crispy. On top of that the service was not great. Had reservations, yet waited 15 mins for a table after i got there. On top of everything the beers were not good. Bathroom was nice.
Wanted  to order food like usual on Friday night from these guys but got turned away not a good thing to do to loyal customers

Atmosphere and staff were awesome however menu very limited and out of things that were on the limited menu. Cuban sandwich was tasty but not authentic. Pizza was fair. Soggy crust in the middle. Good craft beer selection.

Cool atmosphere,  food very good but not much to choose from, the pizza dough is great, toppings a little strange ex. Duck, bacon jam & apples w/blue cheese, strange but its actually very good. Prices a little high but not outrageous.
Please change the name of your Wild Mushroom Pizza into a Mushroom Pizza, there was not even one single  wild mushroom on it! In overall good experience:)
Please change the name of your Wild Mushroom Pizza into a Mushroom Pizza, there was not even one single  wild mushroom on it! In overall good experience:)
Beer was amazing. Food was very good, but a bit over-priced. Service was ok. Worth the drive for the beer though.
Managing the new COVID rules nicely. Good was great. Only reason not a 4⭐ was dishes were wet, one with some stuck on food, and  they were out of the cider of the week at lunchtime on a Saturday

Met a friend for lunch and a beer. Food was good... I had the Cuban sandwich (they could be a little more generous with the meat) and the brussels sprouts appy (very tasty- large portion for an appy!). My only complaint was that my stout was served ice cold... I get that here in America everyone thinks that all beer is supposed to be served "on ice" cold... but letting it warm up opened up the flavor so much more. If I want tasteless and hurt your teeth cold, I'll order ice water. C'mon Vault- show some respect for your efforts and serve your beer at it's proper temperature.

UPDATED: I had to edit my previous positive rating to 1-star. The food, drink, and atmosphere was great while dining. However, we paid by credit card and left a cash tip...and the server (or someone else) wrote in a big tip for themselves after we left. I checked against my credit card statement, and attempted to call the Vaults manager to resolve. After a week, I still haven't gotten a call back.
