Another Cheers bar.  Pizza is great.
Food was good.
Got a beer and pizza here. Overall was nice.
The food was delicious, and the beers were tasty.

The ambiance is fun! We have enjoyed the artisan pizzas in the past. Parents tend to bring their kids during Friday happy hour. Staff is friendly. We like the appetizers but the entrees tend to be real pub grub. One exception was the crab cake sandwiches that were nicely done. Finally,  the beer is fantastic. Wish I lived closer!
Always an enjoyable experience. The only thing I wish was that they had a kids menu!

Came in for the chicken sandwich, paid $13 and got a baby sized sandwich. If I’m paying $13 I’m not expecting a club sandwich with potato chips. We got the deli for that. Description made it seem like a gourmet size sandwich and it wasn’t. If the sandwich was $7-8 then okay. However if you are coming for the brewery then this place is legit

Solid beers.  Comfortable atmosphere with low noise and light levels.
Pizza red sauce was a tad on the sweet side for my tastes.
Always a great experience when we are there.

Food is delicious and they always have some cool new beers. Also a very cool building- it’s an old bank vault. My complaint is more the staff, in my several experiences there they’ve been cold or dismissive (hosts and servers- bartenders have been nice)

This restaurant/bar really reminds me of my old stomping grounds in Haddon Township NJ and especially a restaurant I used to frequent called Keg and Kitchen.

This restaurant is located in an old bank building, hence the name. It definitely has an interesting interior, there is also outdoor seating and more traditional seating outside the vault area.

I hear it gets pretty busy at prime bar hours, however we came here around 5 pm on a Thursday evening and there was ample seating available both indoors and out. I would think it's best to come here with a group of people as they had big platters for $17-$20 that could feed probably 4-5 people and the food looked really good. They also had a really nice selection of seasonal craft beers.

We got the mushroom pizza and waffle grilled cheese. The crust of the mushroom pizza was soft on the inside and crispy on the outside, just right. Keg and Kitchen makes the same pizza, mushrooms, arugula, and truffle oil, but K&K's tastes a lot better for some reason. I couldn't really put my finger on it. I think it might be the truffle oil but I'm uncertain.

The waffle grilled cheese was served with a quarter bowl of tomato dipping soup. It was really good. The only thing missing was the crispy toasted flavor.

The food was good, but not amazing. Drinks and atmosphere were great. Four stars.
Always great for good and drinks
Good food, decent beer and poor service.
Food was good. Nice service. Nice selection of drinks

Went in on Friday last week around 9:45 pm. Was informed by the host stand that they could not seat us and the kitchen was not making anymore food for the night. I asked if we could just sit even outside and come back up to the bar for drinks and she informed me no again. She then said if you want to drink just walk over and stand by the bar. 1 hour later a couple next to us standing at the high top received food. They came in after us. Strange experience.
The beer is great, food was good, burger was a little pricey for just burger and chips (and no fries or pickle).
Good food and beverage selection .
I went there yesterday and an hour later I got food poisoning...... after 24 hours I still have food poisoning, im not pleased with this place at all.
Too bad the beer selection is only IPAs, I miss Belgian blonde. The duck and apple pizza is great as always.
Great atmosphere, good food.
Great soup. Good food. Too much dressing on salad.
Great staff great food!
Delicious food and great service.  Can't walt to go back.

We were not impressed with our experience at all. Waitress was not attentive or friendly, and hardly communicated with us at all. One of us didn't get our drink until the food came after asking about it twice and letting a different waitress know we didn't get it. One of our orders was not correct and had to be sent back. Our waitress didn't even realize this happened after she didn't come back until we we're pratically done with the food. We even had to ask someone to take our check and card so we could pay and get out of there. We certainly will not be back.
Good food.  But, its crowded as heck with four bar stools and a bunch of tables you have to put your name in for.  They have a whole room downstairs... why dont you open that up?

Underwhelming and disappointing experience today for lunch.  Four of us celebrating my friend's birthday arrived for lunch and won't be back.  The waitress was not attentive, took drink orders and did not visit our table until it was time to deliver the bill.  Raspberry lemonade took two waitresses and three conversations for it to be delivered well after the beer arrived; I even joked that they must be growing the raspberries.  Food arrived incorrectly with one plate needing to be sent back.  Once our meal was finished all we wanted to do was leave and even needed to flag someone down to take our money so that we could pay.  My last experience was similar to this one... I gave you another shot and was disappointed again... no amount of conversation or compensation would have/will change my mind today - I won't be back.
Great beer and food. Interesting building and nice outdoor seating.

After reading most of the reviews it had to be an off night. Pretzel braid was good, brussel sprouts were inedible! I mean solid rock, parboil people parboil! Marguerita pizza ok. The crab cake sliders another thing inedible, tasted like the crab was months old needed to be thrown out! Only good thing the chicken sandwich was the best thing ordered all night, however won’t be back.
Great beer and food. Interesting building and nice outdoor seating.

After reading most of the reviews it had to be an off night. Pretzel braid was good, brussel sprouts were inedible! I mean solid rock, parboil people parboil! Marguerita pizza ok. The crab cake sliders another thing inedible, tasted like the crab was months old needed to be thrown out! Only good thing the chicken sandwich was the best thing ordered all night, however won’t be back.
Cute place, good service, good food.

Overall meh. Beer is ok, food is eh. Prices are a bit high for portions and quality of food. Service is ok, but not deserving to start tipping suggestions at 18% Been here twice, won't be a third time
Food was okay, service was just okay.  Disappointed,  heard good things about the place.
Going to go back for a birthday
Good beer and decent atmosphere
The place was nice and the food was okay parking is horrible no place to park limited spots ...
The place was nice and the food was okay parking is horrible no place to park limited spots ...
Get brown sugar buffalo pizza
Beer. Beer. Beer. Beer. Beer. And food.

Very nice brewery with great beer/drinks but unfortunately we will not be back because it is not family friendly.  We had a group of 8 people including my 2 kids, we arrived around 6pm and ordered grub hub delivery from a local restaurant.  We were finishing up dinner around 8:15 when the bartender came up to our table to let us know the brewery has a “no kids after 8PM policy.”  I couldn’t believe this- my kids were very well behaved and we were planning on leaving shortly after we were done eating.  Broken goblet is a great brewery but we will go somewhere family friendly from now on
So brewery is ok, but in order to buy beer you have to buy food from food truck othere wise you not get served beer me and my family already eaten before we arrive and force to get food.
Good beers with a cool space and excellent service from the bar staff

The food was okay. I got the pot stickers and flatbread. Pot stickers were good but the flatbread definitely stayed in the oven too long. Drinks were decent. The reason why this restaurant is receiving two stars from me is because the customer service was beyond poor. A group of 9 of us gathered for a co worker outing and we felt rushed to eat and leave as soon as we were done. The most bizarre thing of all was the puzzling deposit that is required for reservations. Never heard of this in my life and I dine everywhere. To sum up it was $10 per person that someone is required to put down if there is a no show. We were asked to pay $120 for the 9 of us just for a reservation!  You can get back the deposit when you arrive but the kicker is that it does not get reimbursed to you through cash or card. Instead you get a gift card which should not be the case after someone has to put out $10 bucks a head. So essentially you are paying for your meal plus the ability to dine there. Will not be returning.

Restaurant was empty, the hostess who was quite rude asked us for a reservation, we had none and should have left at that point.  She seated us at a bar table that was taken over by a party that was seated/standing at the bar.  We were ignored for most of the meal, steak was raw, mussels were filled with shell and sand, with the exception of the fries everything was cold.  Moving to the area and was hoping to find a good place to eat and drink, this won't be it.  Hostess is the 1st and last person you see and should be the happiest, yours is cranky and miserable.

Felt kind of a rip off : payed $20 for a 0.75l bottle of their craft beer but enjoyed the taste.
Next time I should more clearly ask about the price tag before ordering

We tried to go here with friends of ours (2 couples, 2 toddlers) on Sunday evening, and we were turned away at the door. We were told that they were booked solid and if we didn't have a reservation we couldn't get in. *Updated to address owner's response*
The one-star was, in particular, because as we were leaving, I heard the  group behind us asked if they had a reservation, then start to be told about wait times when they said "no". This left us with the impression that our patronage was just not wanted. Apologies, I should have noted that at the start.
Beer was decent, food was good, bartender was horrible. She should really reconsider any customer service related jobs.

Excessive wait time during non peak hour weeknight for takeout order.

Pizza was burnt and missing bbq sauce.

Buffalo wings were dry and flavorless.

Tex-Mex egg rolls were the only edible part of the order probably because it’s relatively difficult to ruin fried foods.

I have frequented the west Chester and Ardmore iron hill and can say with confidence the media location is by far the worst. The lack of quality control is deleterious to the brand.

Iron Hill is a staple in Media. Overall good food and a really good selection of craft beers. They even recommend beers to accompany meal selections.  The food can be hit or miss at times, but it's more of a hit than a miss. The Pub burger is my favorite!

I tend to avoid this place, because the prices dont match the food quality, but a co-worker wanted to go here for lunch.  So I though shucks lets try it out. (again) $14.50 for a medium burger... it was borderline burnt, and same with the coworkers. Wait time was longer than it should have been also. Pub burger more like burnt halfway to hell burger. Not going to be coming back.
onion soup bland cheese big glob management try it yourself food overpriced this 2nd one we have tried wont go back i guess brewery is good

Our server Kyla was really sweet and was attentive in her service. Both, the wheat beer and January beers, were really good and sweet, not bitter like most other places.
However the vegan garden burger disappointed. No sauce, or seasonings made the burger really dry and flavorless. The buttered spatzle on the side was a unique item and I don't know what's the ideal taste, but it was bland for me. The fries side is a much better option.
Kudos for the decor, beer, service and having vegetarian options. Taste could improve.
Nice restaurant but they don't accept Postmates
Third time here, the whole experience  is better with more people. That darn covid thing!
I found the price on beer ludicrous. Ask before you buy! The rueben sandwich was fine, above average in my experience. Probably wont come back

Disappointed in the food. I know that's not necessarily why you go to a beer hall, but our meals that weren't sandwiches were like bad airline food. Dry, gummy, flavorless. May go again to try something new, but I'd be hard-pressed to find a reason to risk it.
Pierogi was ok. The cabbage with it was pretty good. Excellent cheese with the pretzel. Festbier was meh.
Really good custom service! The waitress was very attentive with our group. The food was good and the beer was great. Also the live music was adorable and nice to listen to.
Food and service was excellent. They were forced to do outdoor dining & the patio was freezing. The heaters were not sufficient and our meals cooled off quickly.
Service was nice food to expensive no lunch buffet = empty dining room 5 people in a hour
Atmosphere was amazing as was the service! Food wasn’t bad, like to try something different next time.

Good food, atmosphere, and drinks. Food and drinks were reasonably priced. Waiter was busy and didn’t check in very often, but once items were ordered they were quick to arrive. Music was loud but mostly created a good vibe.

Really great beer, but the food was disappointing, very bland. The saving grace was the potato pancakes but that was because they were filling. Don't bother with the sauerkraut balls or the perogies. The Octoberfest was very nice.

Nice staff, great space, some traditional German food. I am a native German ( Grew up in Germany) and was hoping for more traditional German foods and drinks. My son and husband enjoyed the Schweinehaxe and French Fries, my daughter enjoyed the Salmon. I had the Jägerschnitzel which was ok. I was looking forward to a good traditional German potato salad but it was a big disappointment. It didn't tasted like a German Potato Salad at all. Sugar does not belong in a potato salad! German Potato salads are not sweet! The apple strudel itself was nice, the vanilla sauce way to sweet.

Good food and good beer. Fun atmosphere with a polka band playing your requests. It is a little nosey so talking is minimum. Appetizers are a good size and made to be shared. Most entrees taste good but aren't truly authentic. If you do get dessert, I highly recommend the apple strudel, of course!
Not crowded when we arrived at 4pm, great beer, great food. Then again, I'm from German heritage, so I enjoy German food.
The blackforest cake was really really good

We came to celebrate my mother's 80th birthday. Compliments by my son who wanted to take his grandma out. So of course I had to go with him. The food is amazing The atmosphere was nice too. We came during the time of when restaurants are only allowed to be 25% filled because of a coronavirus. I look forward to when all restaurants can be open at full capacity. If you have never been to Hofbfrauhaus, I recommend you try it. German food and recipes along with names that you're guessing you're saying it correctly lol. Afterwards it's a great way to go down to the river within walking down a few steps. Very peaceful atmosphere. Enjoy your time and your food if you ever get a chance to go there.

Tasty German food, nice & helpful staff, pretty good deal for your money.  Atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming.  Lunch was not overly busy, the our waiter made great suggestions, and I left full w/leftovers (my friend had the lunch buffet and was very satisfied).  Never been a huge beer fan, but I enjoyed the beer I had.  Ask for Joe... He's great!!

Great food and great beer as we have come to expect from all the other Hofbrauhaus locations we have enjoyed.  Our server was very attentive and helpful. The only complaint we had was that we felt rushed out.  We had to request a second round and the server acted surprised. We had planned on staying for a while and enjoying the crowd with more drinks but the server brought the check and didn’t give us the option.  We try to be mindful of a restaurants need to turn tables so we would have left or moved to the bar if there was a wait or no empty tables but that didn’t appear to be the problem.
good food, good bier

I came here twice. First time was a few months ago with my classmates, second time with my husband. With my classmates, we hung out in the main hall filled with German music and dancing. With my husband, we sat in the quiet hall for a late-night meal. I had a good experience both times.

The main hall is very loud and that's the place for parties, group meals, happy hour and bar socials, dancing and sing-a-long. The quiet/chill hall is for those that want regular quiet dinning. The food is good! The appetizers are large! The waitresses wear tight German costumes... God bless them it looks uncomfortable! But they are all very friendly.

Good overall food. They make those own beer and from what I have heard from others it's all pretty good. The food is a little expensive but the portions are also on the large size so it balances out. You sit in a giant cafeteria at picnic tables so the room can be loud if it's crowded. They have a stage for live music and an outdoor patio for summer sitting. Overall a nice place. Unfortunately the is rarely any street parking so you have to pay to park in the garage.

The food is good, but some dishes aren't quite worth the price.  I usually go here at least once a year.  This time I had the schweinebraten and the pumpkin dunkel beer.  The pork was OK, but the potato dumpling was really good ; a salty crust and a dense texture.  The beer is always good, but I don't think it is mindblowingly good.  The food hall gets loud, so keep that in mind.
Good food and good beer. Pricy.
Very good food, reasonable prices and lovely views of the river

Ordered happy hour drinks, went to a table, then went back to the bar to order food. Made eye contact with the bartender for about 4 minutes straight as she explained to the only other customer that they needed an ID to order drinks. Eventually they left, then I was told that I couldn't order happy hour specials because happy hour ended 10 seconds ago... The customer who's been standing there doesn't care if "the computer won't let you."
Their previous many was better. It was special and really thematic. This one looks poor and not special anymore...

Good food, good beer, terrible service.  Lauren refused to combine our checks, rang our food in incorrectly, didn't check on us once, and was extremely quick to cash us out after ignoring us the majority of the evening.  Didn't give us a pen to sign our credit card slips, either...
Good food
It's a fun experience. The food and entertainment are good.

Friendly service, decent enough beer for a chain restaurant. Now I'm not really a german food expert... But the sauerkraut was kind of creamy?  Is that a thing? Wasn't terrible, not great, but not sure I would eat that specific menu item again.  Rest of our meal was decent. Probably wouldn't go out of my way to eat here again.

All of the food here when I go with friends and/or family is always cooked hot and heavy, though the service can occasionally be slow (kitchen-wise). The outdoor seating is always desirable for us, and the biergarten gifts you with a great view of the river side trails and river itself, and most of the outdoor seating is shaded. They have an inside and outside bar, though you should choose the outside as the inside of this location can get very loud. They also have a great weekly College Night special we exploit now and again where liters of their house brews were 6$ and their giant pretzels were 5$; cannot pass up.

It was a busy Saturday but food was mediocre and too salty.  Staff was impatient and flustered and even the performer seemed to be phoning it in.  The evening was saved by great friends, good beer and one of the best outdoor areas in the city.
Surprisingly large venue with OK cuisine, the beer is more interesting than the fare in my opinion... nice meeting place for a group or party
arrived at six, it was not packed. service is consistently above par. the bill was appropriate for the quality. feels like a place in los angeles i loved.
Like this place. Strange things happen "we don't have any booths available" then we walk by three clean empty booths. Good German food and beer.

What a cool atmosphere!!
I am so happy they have a Gluten Free menu
Some of the worst customer service I’ve ever received. Hosts, managers and bouncers are extremely rude and curt towards customers.
Expensive. But not bad. Definitely a tourist trap, but the staff is kind to locals
Awesome German food experience.
Always the same menu they never change anything

(Translated by Google) 괞 cold
It's a little more than a restaurant in Manhattan, but lol.

(Original)
괞찬음
맨하탄에서 가는 식당보다는 조금 들하지만 ㅋㅋㅋ
Good atmosphere and kind staff. Wonderful selection of beer and bar foods. Highly recommend their chicken and waffles. Also are able to brew your own beer.

Stumbled on this by accident. Was looking for Homebrew supplies.
Glad we found it.
Anchor Porter and brisket Pittsburgh Salad are excellent.
Thanks for the White wheat and lunch.
Food is good, beer selection decent. Prices reasonable.
Great local sports bar. Warm and not too loud with a relaxed atmosphere and good food.
New menu sucks. It's basically pierogis. Beer still great.

My friend and I both noticed a mouse run from the kitchen to under the beer fridge. We pointed this out to a server and she mentioned it to the manager. Nothing was further said to us about it. The response was basically "meh, deal with it". I used to enjoy the Houghs experience but it makes me sad  that our observation did not warrant an apology or explanation from the manager. Think wisely about your food safety before you choose Houghs.
Nice Beer and Food Selection. Try the Sunday Brunch.
A very interesting place!
What a gem`

5 meals all with tasteless chicken that had been boiled. Hate to leave a bad review . It’s unfortunately our first and last visit . Not Japanese cuisine. We found huge chucks of raw cabbage 3x1 inch raw pieces. One bad dish would be passable but all 5 dishes awful . We are always visiting town and like the bakery next door . We left our food and went next door for a hot dog n pie. We wondered why they charge the meal and tip before serving . I’m all for support of small businesses. Specially since the pandemic has made it hard to survive . I hope the owner takes my review into consideration. Not the best way to represent genuine Japanese food .

This is an unbiased honest review. They have a sign that says "Leave a 5 star review and get a discount" when you are there. So this is why they have so many 5 star reviews. The food is just okay, the outside atmosphere is just okay. There aren't many options for dinning in the area so you may have to SETTLE for this restaurant. Will you be disappointed, maybe yes maybe no but I promise you will not go there more than once. This place is nothing worthy of talking about to anyone.

The staff was and the guy who we believe was the owner was nice.  The ramen we got was awful though.  I see a lot of good reviews for the other food and sake, which I wish I tried instead of the ramen.

If I can give less stars I would have.  Took 40 min to get 4 rolls, cost $60 and was mostly rice.  VERY DISAPPOINTING.
When I said about the time (40 minutes) the man said “We can give you your money back and you can eat somewhere else.” Didn’t apologize or anything. Roll have NO FLAVOR.  Very disappointed.

Don't typically leave reviews of places I don't enjoy, but after reading owners responses to others and clear inability to accept criticism,  I felt compelled.

We had Tonkatsu ramen and okonomiyaki.  Truthfully, Okonomiyaki wasn't bad. The ramen on the other hand, was not good, it was very bland and i was very disappointed.

I lived in Japan for many years, and my wife is Japanese, before owner assumes their Japanese culture superiority over us.  Good luck.
Sake brewery without sake... "Sushi" with one ingredient and upcharge for adding cucumber with the fish... "Ramen" with one spoonful of meat... what a joke.
Nice beer selection,  awesome buck a shuck Sundays. Rolls are a little heavy on the rice but still tasty

Terrible terrible terrible. There is nothing Japanese about this restaurant. Food is tasteless and soggy, the ambience is beyond terrible. The only positive note was the staff (not a single Japanese, unusual for a Japanese restaurant ) nice but good service would not be enough to bring me back to this restaurant. If it stays open. It really looked now like it is on its way to closing soon.

This was the first time I have eaten at a restaurant so highly rated (4.6) on Google and been totally disappointed. Honestly, I wish I would have read the reviews in depth prior to visiting, particularly the negative reviews that garnered rude responses directly from the owner. That kind of unprofessionalism says a lot about this establishment. The food itself was adequately made, just not the flavor profile I was anticipating. Very bland. The decor was an odd "outdoors with a flair of Japanese" that really didn't add much to the experience. Didn't try the sake so I can't give an honest review of that aspect, but it seemed to be very popular among the others dining. Before the owner jumps down my throat: NO I have not had ramen in Japan. I have, however, eaten ramen at many places (NYC, Philly, Dover, etc) so take my review as you will. Also beware of the "convience fee" attached for using a credit card. Apparently "everybody" is doing this now.

Very, very, very disappointed. The outside was filthy, entrance was filthy and could not find a clean table to sit at. You get the menus at the counter, go pick out a table to sit at and go back to the counter to order. Very bizarre!!  The music was extremely loud and annoying.  We walked out. Do not waste your money or time.

A surprising little delight in the delaware watergap.  I have never really sought high caliber cuisine in the commonwealth because usually it falls short.   These guys did not fall short.  Their curry appetizer was lovely,  and their ramen was comparable with NY or Chicago on our visit.   I was impressed.  They do not appear to be brewing saki yet, but i certainly hope they follow through and start soon.  Definitely worth a stop.  They serve local beer and wine as well.   The interior is charming, with a strong Appalachian Trail motif.
Food was gross, atmosphere was bad, building was dirty
Disappointing neither Japanese nor anything else. Should ditch the attempt to do japanese food and go with bar food eith an Asian twist.

Drove 30min to come here,  not worth the drive at all. Had the tonkatsu ramen and pork gyoza, the gyoza were fine but nothing special and the ramen broth had almost no flavor and overall was just very bland. Would not reccomend. Changed the review to one star because of the owners response, very rude and cannot accept criticism. Only insults me because of my bad experience at their restaurant

Takoyaki and okonomiyaki were decent, but disappointed with the ramen. Seems like they are just starting out, and are still working on the aesthetics of the place.

EDIT: the tonkatsu broth didn't hit the right note for me - it was thin and didn't have flavor depth. the noodle consistency was good.

WOULDNT EVEN GIVE 1 STAR BUT REVIEW WOULDNY SUBMIT WITHOUT AT LEAST 1 STAR.
OWNER COMPLETELY OFF HIS ROCKER YELLING AT 2 FEMALE  CUSTOMERS ABOUT HOW HE WORKS 12 HOURS A DAY & DIDNT NEED CUSTOMERS WHO COMPLAINED ABOUT THE FOOD ONLY HAVING 1 SHRIMP.  WE ALSO HEARD HIM THREATEN TO KICK A LADIES SERVICE DOG HE ALLOWED TO BE IN HIS RESTAURANT.  SOMETHING WAS QRONG WITH THAT GUY.
WE WERE A PARTY OF 6 WHO WENT THERE TO ENJOY OUR  VACATION NOT HEAR THIS OWNER RANTING & RAVING ABOUT HIS CUSTOMERS.
PLUS THE BATHROOM WAS NASTY WITH USED TOILET PAPER ALL OVER THE FLOOR. WE WILL NEVER GO  BACK OR RECOMMEND THAT PLACE.
I ordered miso ramen, it was not hot enough. Noodle needs to be cooked more.
Really !!!!  What horrendous food.  Please guys, ,  it is not that difficult to make decent food.  First and last time visiting that place.  Even beer needs good food with it!!!
Definitely some interesting brew tastes.  Smokey and sour were some unusual choices.  Nice place, nice staff.  We didn't eat but menu seemed like a good mix of traditional bar food.
Good food great BEER. Decent prices. Seen on a first date!!!
Great place to eat... service is a bit slow at times but in their defence their usually pretty busy every time I've been there.  Beer is great!
Service was slow. 2 of our orders were not cooked right and food was warm not hot. The food was plane tasting. It took about an hour to get food.

if there was a 2 and a half, that's where I would be here, but I do not want that to be a bad thing -  it's a nice place!

THE FOOD - What I am extremely happy with is that there were some non-meat items on the menu. We do not eat chopped up animals so I ordered the Black Bean Veggie burger (burger and fries, $10) and my wife had the Jailhouse Caprese (essentially a mozerella grilled cheese of sorts, with tomato on a ciabata w/ fries - $7) - we split the sandwiches so we each had half of both. While the sandwiches weren't bad, the fries were the best part of the meal and they were "ok" .

THE DRINKS - I had the best draft I have ever had ( Best Blonde Lager - Helles -$5 for 16oz) ... hands down - FANTASTIC ! My wife on the other had a mixed drink, some sort of "punch", it was the least expensive drink on the cocktails and wine menu (though thats not why she chose it, it sounded appealing to her) and it was WEAK. $9 for a mixed drink in Chambersburg is quite high. We brought that up at the end of the meal and the server expressed that it was because of the high quality ingredients used (which I get), but my feeling is that if you pay a premium for an alcoholic beverage, that alcoholic beverage should pack some sort of punch. My wife almost never drinks. She drank 2 of these and normally after 2 drinks she would be something to laugh at for a couple of hours. I tasted the drink... uh- thanks for the high quality sprite I guess??? I didn't taste anything worth $9. so ehhhh, IDK

THE FACILITY, DECOR, SERVICE, ETC. - All that was good! Great atmosphere! They had a good live band going ( NAS Trio - a nice 3 man band with a good sound ) The place looks fantastic. 2 rooms of seating with dining in both and a bar. They also had an outside dining area that was rather small but nice to see. The service was good also so no complaints there.

* This is a nice place ! All in all I don't want it to sound like a bad review... It is a brewery after all and the beer I had (3 of them actually! ;-) was TOP NOTCH) - The vegan offerings were an exciting thing to see! The service and atmosphere were great, but unfortunately we went there for everything they had to offer. The food, and the other drinks were a let down. If all I had done was had a beer, and watched the band, it would have been a five star review! I think my story of this place is one of a "great place that fell short".
Good food, busy place!

This would have been a five star experience except for the Voodoo pourer. Zero personality and poor communication; we almost left before having what we wanted because the guy was unable or unwilling to properly explain the beer sizes (do you want 12oz or 4oz of a 12% beer - pretty important info.) The food was fantastic and the beers are solid. They just need to get that one guy in a position that suits him; not in front of customers. (Updated: I re--did the review with 4-stars but Google reverted it to two. Trying to update again.)

This is a pretty cool spot to chill with a beer, and I bet it'd be great during the summer when they open the doors next to the creek. They are taking precautions due to the pandemic, such as a wearing masks, tables are 6 feet apart, etc. The service can be kind of slow when during their peak hours, but the food (provided by the Blonde Bistro) and beer are pretty good and the industrial vibe is very cool.

Axemann Brewery is in a slightly strange location. It feels like you're driving forever on a twisting winding road, but then come across an industrial park with a brewery there.

We were there on a Sunday, which meant the selection of food was limited. We went for the breakfast burger, which was an interesting take on a pub classic, and enjoyed it.

The selection of beers isn't huge but the beers are good. I noticed a few taste characteristics on Untappd suggesting the beers were "thin" and "watery". If you taste them and think that, let them warm up a little and try again. I found the first taste of a few beers was slightly disappointing but once they warmed up a bit the flavors really came out and I enjoyed them much more.

It's good to see a range of different types of beer rather than endless variations of IPAs. It would be nice to have a wider range of food on Sundays but it's obviously easier to say that than to arrange the staff to make it happen.

They offer most of their beers to go and you can buy anything from a six-pack (including a mixed six, if that's your thing) to a case or larger.

We'll be back.

Good beer here! This is a nice large place tucked into a good location. Easy to maintain distance, I imagine this place would be super-cool if C19 weren't an issue. I liked the food-truck vibe of the Blonde Bistro and the pizza was really tasty!

I know these are covid times and the service industry is suffering, but that shouldn't mean there should be any cutting corners on service itself. This place is known for its beers. They weren't that special. The food came in to go boxes. The food was overpriced. It was self service as in you pick up your box of food. There's no napkins or plates. They didn't let us know they'd call us to pick up the food. I will say the server was attentive but he only serves the drinks. I know some of these methods are to stop the spread but please understand the paying customers needs. There's a dollar charge for a sandwich even if you order food

The brewery is a great place. The facility is amazing and the beer and staff are off to a great start.  The Blond Bistro must get 1000% better or call it a day.  I unfortunately don’t see it happening. We were there 2 times now and both times waited 1 1/2 hours for our food and I only got a pizza. When I got it, it was 3/4 baked.  I support the local business 100% but she is not cut out for that at all.  Even her last place was the same.  Adding the food trucks was a great idea.  It will keep people coming back.  Best of luck, we will always be back and recommend you to everyone that comes to our very busy Air BnB, but for now I will only recommend food trucks.
Excellent selection of beer on tap. Service was also good.

We came here twice on our trip home from Florida. The first trip on Thursday was a good experience thanks to the waitress downstairs. The second, however, was a major disappointment. I understand that covid rules are in place, but I feel the way my mom was treated was absolutely uncalled for. We were seated upstairs and she walked up the huge flight of stairs with her mask on and was winded at the top, due to asthma. Upon reaching the top floor we were approached by a dark haired guy that manned the door the first trip. He began to ask her questions and she was struggling to catch her breath and pulled her mask away from her face to breathe. Not off, just away from her face. He proceeded to say put your mask back on, very rudely. This was the first strike. She should not be talked to as a child when she has a medical condition. Then we were sat and the waitress approached and we asked for water for my mom and she said "if I can remember, I have 30 people on this patio waiting for a water". She laughed it off, but this is absolutely unprofessional, especially considering you are serving alcohol. We all wanted to leave at that time, but we stuck it out. The food was good, but service was awful. Like I said, I understand times are different, but medical conditions should be considered, kindness should be shown rather than demeaning comments, and you should really rethink the sandwich rule when people are ordering food. Why the mandated $1 sandwich (that we were told not to eat) when we were ordering food from the bistro. Just seems like a waste of food to me. We won't be returning until covid clears. The food and beer wasn't worth the hassel or the poor treatment.

The beer was pretty good,  but not exceptional,  however the space is very nice.  They have a lot of room to spread out during these continuing pandemic days,  plus a pleasant outside seating area that overlooks a stream (note,  dogs are allowed outside,  at least while we were there). Hope they do well,  I'll probably go back.
Great building. Nice beer selection. Highly recommend the pizza. Very good. Will for sure make another stop here.
Very solid beers, great food menu and outstanding venue.

The beer at the brewery as well as the atmosphere is 5 stars.

My one star is all due to Blonde Bistro. My group and I ordered from the restaurant and waited over an hour for 6 overpriced pasta dishes that were not very big. But the icing on the cake is due to the owner of the Blonde Bistro. She was so over the top rude and loud in the kitchen, it made you uncomfortable to be seated close by. Then, later that night, she walked by our table and made some weird, extremely rude comments about us! We didn't even say a word to her!

Idk why Axeman let the Blonde Bistro move into the restaurant portion, but do yourself a favor and just eat at the food trucks that regularly go there.
Very rude and was treated like an inconvenience.  Never return
Cool environment and very industrial style, didn’t get a chance to taste the blonde bistros food, but the beer needs a lot of work, still new, but def need to work on that for a place that size.
Too busy very slow with orders 😶
Beer was great but service could be more prompt. Overall a fun experience.
Servers, beer, and the environment was great! Their downfall currently is Blonde Bistro. Very slow and unorganized. So 4 Stars until they fix that service.

business_name: Tony Joe's House of Great Eat's
review_text: Smell in the dining room was beyond unpleasant. Service was good. Food disappointing

Still a great beer and food experience. The food well prepared and beer...well, what can I say. I drink a different style each glass. Cheeseburger and the fish & chips. Perfect as expected. We took train from Philly just for this!

Our entire experience was horrible. First the hostess had to "encourage" the waitress to visit our table twice before she finally came to take our drink order, this same waitress readily served a table across from us when they were seated. We placed an appetizer order and our meal order at the same time, the meals were delivered before the appetizer, we informed the person who delivered our meals that we hadn't received our appetizers, only to be told the waitress forgot to enter the order.  My wife and I  both ordered the fish and chips, neither of us liked the meal. Needless to say, we won't be returning.

The hostess and waitress were extremely friendly and polite.  The overall atmosphere was quiet and clean.  Unfortunately, the food was just terrible.  The Irish Menu offered a Rueben, and it was coated with tons of butter, one small spoonful of sauerkraut, and a pretty bone dry.  The Irish Menu also offered whisky glazed short ribs that were described as a as a craft demi whiskey glaze, but was really gravy.  The side of potatoes were cold.  Another member of our party ordered a burger medium and came out well done, and the power bowl, well it was less than desirable.  Can't say I would return.
I have been a patron for awhile but this was a bad evening at the Harrisburg restaurant

Went in after the outdoor show in Harrisburg and was greeted by a rather unfriendly woman. She shouts at us as we were coming in the door "45 minute wait". I look around only to see like 15 empty tables. Yeah not waiting 45 minutes with a ton of tables and listening to Oscar the grouch greet people like that.

The waitress was nice but didn't know anything about the food on the menu and seemed like she was on too much valium. The food was bland. We had to wait a while to get our beers, napkins and sides. The restaurant was fairly empty so I'm not sure why everything took so long. We ordered a dessert and waited, and waited. We cancelled the dessert and waited for our check . A waste if time and money.

So I'm not usually a review person unless service is spectacularly good or bad.

TLDR the service is terrible, the manager doesn't care, and the tater tots aren't as advertised. If you want to wait 2 hours to eat then this place is fine. If you want reasonable service go elsewhere.

I'm currently sitting at the table 2 hours after we got here shaking my head in disbelief. It took awhile to get seated but we got drinks at the bar so it was what it was. Our appetizers took a half hour to come out and then they came out wrong. The manager had no great response besides "well the kitchen is backed up and I wouldn't know if the original apps were wrong unless we made new ones". Not a great response after we've been waiting for a ridiculously long time. They then offered to comp dessert that we never intended to originally order with promises that our food would be right out. 20 minutes later they claimed that they were just waiting on fresh french fries. Another 10 minutes later the food finally came out. While my table mates said their food was fine mine came out completely lukewarm. Definitely not worth waiting for almost an hour. The food is fine. Not great. Not terrible. I do not recommend the tater tots as they have neither the cheese nor bacon that is advertised. We are now waiting for our desserts 15 minutes after the restaurant supposedly closed. Time to see how long we will be waiting this time. One of my table mates is a waiter and she's fairly certain the restaurant forgot to put our order in until we complained. It was not a great experience and we will certainly never be back.
Beer is horrible. Tried all of them over two nights this past weekend and they were horrendous. Owner and her husband were unreasonable when I spoke to them. Avoid this place! Go to Evergrain

Once we were seated it was fine, but apparently they'll make you wait on a bench until a server can take your order, even if the place is half empty.  That's not very hospitable at all.  We'd just drove 2 hours and at least wanted something to drink but we couldn't be seated.  The way she told us this it seemed like the hostess didn't really want us there either.  Some welcome.  It was kind of tough to stay at that point and if we weren't sick of driving we would have moved on.

Crazy slow service, beer was way expensive, meal came out cold. Our waiter was nice, but that didn’t make up for the mediocre meal. I probably won’t come again. - I originally rated this two stars, but after thinking about how bland the food was I switched it to one.

Waited 25 minutes for a table, which is not bad. They were really busy, so we thought okay let's wait. Waited 1  hour and 30 minutes, only to be told our order had been lost., Sorry fold never got to try the food. Just really sad that they did not have the Waite staff to handle the load. Won't be back

It looks nice and the food tasted good, but as we were leaving we saw a mouse. When brought to the server’s attention he chuckled and said they’re throughout the building....they might want to get that checked out. We will NOT be going back.

We waited for nearly 1.5 hours for a table for 6 plus a toddler. Then the food took over an hour to arrive. Luckily the toddler's food arrived first. It was very hot! When we got ours it was warm or cool, not hot.  Service was horrible. Food was ok. Although they discounted our bill, it didn't make up for such a long wait. The kitchen clearly cannot handle the volume of orders they have. They should know what to expect on busy weekends! Smaller tables were served long before us even though we were seated first. Probably will never return.

We had not been at ABC for a few years as we had been disappointed by the service and food. We gave it another try yesterday since we had a gift card. Food came out completely cold, we did not receive parts of the meal, and our server was inattentive.

Service was very poor. Had to wait 40 mins to get a table, then 25 mins to get our appetizers, then another 45 mins for our dinner. Although not said, it appeared the kitchen lost our ticket. The table behind us, seated after us got their food before us.  Manager tried to make it right by free dessert but would not recommend this restaurant.

Ovetall takeaway: This place is overhyped and overpriced. If you're looking for good food worth paying for, go elsewhere. We paid $45 for meals we could do better at home.

This review applies only to the Mechanicsburg, PA location. Here's our story in brief. We arrived at 5:30 PM on a Friday (literally yesterday as I'm writing this) to an active but not crowded restaurant. Service was decent, not excellent but adequate. I wasn't impressed but I honestly had no complaints either about the waitstaff. They were prompt, our orders were correct, and we were checked on 2 or 3 times to ensure we were fine. A decent effort.

The food on the other hand? Decidely not. Which is disappointing because their menu makes many of their dishes sound appealing. But the execution is definitely lacking. My wife got their Rockfish Tacos while I got their Bourbon Beef Tips. Neither of us came away impressed or even pleased. The fish in the tacos was dry from being over-fried and none of the cabbage bed was seasoned. No flavor or complexity at all except the sauces served on the side. My beef tips were okay. That seems odd to complain about, but bear in mind this is a $20 plate. I'm not paying $20 to go "eh, it's okay" and think that my wife could do this better. They were tender and cooked to medium as specified, served with onions and peppers, and that's about all I can say about it. It was edible and as advertised on the menu - except for having any taste beyond grilled meat.The rest of the plate was a mess. Next to the tips was a literal single icecream scoop of mashed potatoes, like the way they serve them in school cafeterias, and with about as much quality too. Chalky, barely warm, and with a strange lemony flavor mixed in - noticeable though not overpowering. Next to that was steamed broccoli, also without any seasoning, as if someone microwaved a Birdseye Steamer bag and then dumped a portion of it on my plate. Practically everything we ate I felt I could buy at the store and we could do better at home. It's embarrassing, really.

This is one meal we regret having. A complete waste of our time and money. We won't be returning. I gave it two stars for the service and because our appetizer, the Tater Tots, was fairly good and probably the best thing we ate there. A severe disappointment.
1st time in 7yrs and it was almost as awesome as the last time we were there .Very happy to see NEPA drinking food on the menu you can never go wrong with perogi .

BEWARE IF YOU HAVE FOOD ALLERGIES! Went to have a nice dinner with my boyfriend and his parents 11/30 at 4 PM. Food was great, and our waitress was wonderful, but then we ordered dessert. We ordered an elephant ear to share which was supposed to come topped with vanilla ice cream per the menu. A few bites in, we realized that the ice cream, while it looked like vanilla, actually tasted very similar to peanut butter. My boyfriend has a very severe peanut allergy which we made very clear when we sat down and double checked to make sure they didn’t fry their food in peanut oil. We called the waitress over ( I forget her name, but bless her heart) to clarify why exactly it tasted like peanut butter. Turns out some new employee in the back decided it was okay to substitute their “monkey butter” ice cream when they ran out of vanilla. NOT OKAY ESPECIALLY WHEN PEOPLE CAN DIE FROM PEANUTS!! The waitress came back and immediately apologized, went to get the manager, who proceeded to avoid all eye contact with us until I physically stopped him as he walked by.  Without talking to us he comped the dessert and my boyfriend’s dinner. How about you comp the EPIPEN AND ER VISIT that resulted?! When I stopped him he apologized and said that he has a cat allergy so he “understands”.  Really?!? HE DID NOT grasp the severity of what a food allergy can do besides making you a little sneezy and itchy- ANAPHYLAXIS and DEATH, for starters. PLEASE for the safety of others (and to avoid a lawsuit from potentially killing someone) TRAIN YOUR STAFF HOW TO BE SAFE WITH FOOD AND FOOD ALLERGIES!! You are playing with LIVES when the kitchen staff do not communicate food swaps!

Our decided to try some place new so this was on the list. The menu didn't really have much on it to choose from which shocked me. I ended up ordering the shrimp and scallions flatbread. It came out and it was literally like 4 pieces of shrimp and scallions. The shrimp and scallions were even cold. Definately not worth $13.

Let me start by saying the food was incredible but the service was terrible! We waited a hour and 20 mins for our appetizer and a hour and 35 mins for our meal. They did make it right and adjusted our bill which was nice. Regardless of the service I will definitely give it another chance based on the food.
Always friendly and personable. Good beer. Good food. Usually on my way to somewhere.
Food was reasonable,  served quickly.   Dont know Harrisburg well enough to know if this is above average or not.  Good brew pub fare.
The food was good, the craft soda was excellent, but the service was subpar. It took over an hour and a half to get food, and it was not busy in there.

Sitting here waiting for perhaps the worst waiter I've ever had here. One meal was great, one was cold. Slow service. Don't blame the bar for being slow when we can watch you not doing your job. And of course, you didn't provide your name, but it is on the bill,  Sweeny. Your service sucks.
Good food, especially the pub soup . Loved the atmosphere.  We found the wait staff to be efficient and courteous.
Good beer, food took 2 hours to get it all.  Some was cold food. 1 hour wait. Couldn't handle the busy croud.  Wish it was a better experiance.
Nice atmosphere, decent food, great live music venue.
Beer is alright.

I tried to make a reservation for this weekend. My neice is dancing in Irish fest, and we would like to support her and a local bussiness. I was transfered after inquiring and then told by the hostess that they are not taking reservations. Ok, I understand this is the policy. I inquired as to why, and wasnt given an answer. I inquired about an average wait time, as I have children to feed and need to make an informed decision. I again recieved no answer other than I dont know we will be busy. The only reason I am writing this is because the hostess than hung up on me! I have worked in the restaurant biz for 20 years and frankly I would be appalled and embarrassed to treat a potential guest that way! We have never visited this establishment, but if that was our first impression you can forget it.
The root beer is a little overrated, but the servers are nice and attentive.
Was little overpriced. I liked the menu but wasn't alot of options for those of us vegetarian or People don t eat swine.
There was lots of walking around by the waitress; with very little attention given to the costumers. I was with a party of seven, it appears strange everyone's food came out at different...
More a fan of the Abbey bar but I only go here for music. And their sound system is weak.
Food was ok, service was good. They even offer a cheese and cracker plate for kids!
It was not to much quantity for an all you can eat. Only one and one-quarter peace of fish.
Meh... Cold food, cafeteria quality. If I was really hungry and there was nothing better I would return. Tried it 3 times now.
Wedding and reception venue. Well done.
Good food and drink.  Slow lunch service.
More bling than produced.
Food was not hot, service was ok

The WORST service we ever had. The place was completely empty and wouldn’t seat us. We are here for a basketball tournament and we are taking our service elsewhere. Don’t eat or drink here. This place is pathetic!!!!
You can not make reservations, with the link provided, nor can you get someone to pick up a phone (or call back, if you leave a message) at any location. What is up with that?
Beer was okay. Bartender was the worse ever. I will never go back nor will I ever spend any money on any Appalachian beer or product again.
Service was good, food was okay, the beer was awful.
The beers were fair, I wouldn't go back.
Service is horrible and take forever

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: The restaurant is attached to the cutest little shop and has a super open layout. I ordered the beef brisket with two side and my husband got the barbecue pulled pork sandwich. They also have a sauce tray on the table with 6 different types of sauces you can use for your food. The brisket was very tender and flavorful. The macaroni was delicious to, but I think it would have been even better if they had served it to me hot instead of room temperature. This was alright with me though, because I was giving it to my toddler, but as soon as they improve on the food temperature, they will get that last star from me.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: After driving by for years, I finally got a chance to eat here. I have high expectations for a BBQ joint, and this place did not disappoint! The brisket was absolutely awesome. And while fried chicken doesn't have to be a staple of a BBQ place, theirs was very good. Their side staples of mac and cheese and coleslaw were also very good. The corn bread topped it off quite nicely. And the icing on the cake was, the have a bar! I hope to go back soon to try more.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Great place to have lunch or dinner, if you find yourself around the area. Very nice burger and a good choice of beers. Service was excellent.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text:
Oct 2019
I'm being generous giving it 3 stars.  Service was way off, as the waitress really lacked any kind of system.  The BBQ chicken salad had decent pulled chicken but the salad was very lackluster.  The brisket was decent but was a little dry, and the smoked sausage had this odd, dense consistency and it was rather forgettable.  Overall it's a very typical Pennsylvania BBQ experience.  It's just not done right like it is in Memphis, Kansas City, or Dallas.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text:
Fantastic Service and Food! The meat loaf, the brisket and the pulled pork were delicious.

Below isn't a photo of the food but a gratuitous photo of the sunset view from the parking lot.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Not the same as before. Definitely miss Plain and Fancy and the days when things were served family style. Food did not taste the same

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Enjoyable restaurant, nice staff. Food was delicious but a lot!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Terrible experience again! Live in area. Not many options. We arrive to be told a wait, which is fine. After waiting we are seated with over half the tables empty. Only 2 servers on this shift. I ask about the Brisket and at 5:00pm on a sunday they are out. The server was a bit rude about it. We get their cornbread which is delicious and asked for more, her response was she could but i have to charge you. This establishment is constantly under staffed, food runs out, this is not first time, and probably should not have that response about some cornbread. Will not be back

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Great BBQ! Awesome decor!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Not bad. I expected a bit more, but kinda got the school cafeteria vibe once we were seated. The food was okay, but not great. For a smokehouse,  I guess i just expected a bit better fare. It wasn't bad, but I don't know if I would go back. If you're just stopping in for a burger, you'll probably be pretty happy with the place. I was just expecting more.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: food was pretty good, i wish the service had been the same. we were told we couldn’t be seated because there were too many of us, but the young man hostess said if we split up we could be served. when two of the other workers came out, they said it would take an hour and a half for us to be seated because they were “extremely busy and not equipped to handle such a big group” even though it was only a party of 6 and a party of 7 separately (there had been a party of 6 that had walked out as we were waiting). we said we would wait anyways because the food looked so good and the reviews were decent. after the three of them started to walk back into the kitchen, we overheard the two ladies yelling at the young man for accepting us. soon after, one of the ladies came out and said that actually they had one table ready and it would only take about five minutes to wait. at that point we realized it was because they didn’t want to serve us personally, whether because we were speaking a different language or because we had kids, at that point we weren’t sure. when our tables were ready, the younger lady who was the rudest told us that the setting was extremely quiet, basically telling us to be quiet because she expected us to be loud. when we walked into the actual restaurant,  more than half of the tables were empty and there were SEVERAL kids seated with their families all who were talking at a loud volume. the waiter at my table was nice enough. overall, the experience was awful and simply humiliating. will NEVER return.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Delicious food, friendly server.  Cute store if you're looking for touristy momentos.  If you're wanting something a little more homemade & locally made you have very limited choices.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Good food, good prices! Friendly staff. Great Hotel on the same property! Amish view inn is onr of the best hotels anywhere!!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Pulled pork and coke slaw were good. Small portions. Mashed potatoes were so obviously instant. Avoid them. Bartender with blue-green hair was great. Good mixology, handled a full bar of people eating and made drinks for restaurant as well.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Main courses and sauces were awesome. Sides were good. The adjoining store is a nice place to wait for a table. Great wait staff

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Went on Thanksgiving with family. Wait staff was friendly and attentive. Food was OK. The best part was the fried chicken.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Food was terrible and crazy overpriced. Vegetarian vegetable soup had clearly been reheated several times and had that old soup taste. Side of vegetables tastes like microwaved frozen veggies with no seasoning. Macaroni and cheese was so dry no one could eat it. The corn fritters were fine. French fries were good. That was my meal with a drink. $17 for that. Kids didn't eat their meals either. Waitress never cared to ask why we didn't eat our food. My husband said his ribs were good. Bill about $100 for 2 adults and 2 kids and we left hungry.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Excellent service and good food.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Smokehouse is a solid visit with a good selection of BBQ staples for a decent price. Nestled in the heart of Amish country, you really can't ask for much more than Smokehouse offers. The barbecue is tender and tasty, the sides are quite good, and the PA dutch staple shoo fly pie is delicious. A mostly open dining area provides a laid back country atmosphere, and where else can you pick up a jar of jam and straw hat or a bonnet at the general store on your way out?

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: For being a BBQ restaurant, they definitely missed the mark. My husband ordered the smokehouse stacker and when he got it it was dry and cold through. The waiter seemed like he didn't care to do anything about it but did get us a new one. They "explained" that putting the cole slaw on the sandwich makes the beef cold... RIGHT. The second sandwich was warm at best, and still dry and chewy. The fried chicken was great. Sides were good, but too small. $14 for each plate was definitely not worth it. Waiter acted like he didn't want to be doing his job. We won't go back.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Not trying to be overly harsh but this was the worst bbq I’ve had, and the portions were small relative to the price. I wouldn’t recommend this place. To be honest I’m surprised the other recent reviews are so positive.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text:
Beautiful building, friendly wait staff. Food delivered to the table quickly.

Complementary cornbread: A+. So darn good

Wings: A+, juicy, delicious flavor (was not offered celery+ ranch or blue cheese, but didn’t need, fortunately)
Ribs: A+: nice smoky finish. Pretty juicy! They come to your table with no sauce, but there’s a variety of sauces at your table. I was skeptical at first, but they honestly don’t need BBQ at all, stand alone are delicious. I still added BBQ because it’s SOO GOOD
Mac n cheese: C+  a little light on flavor pretty much devoid of melted cheese. Ok.
Stuffing: B-, delicious flavor, very dry. Was not offered gravy.

Will return and probably avoid the Mac n cheese.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: I ordered a brisket sandwich and it was tough. The brisket was not tender and the fat was not rendeed down. At all. I've smoked a number of briskets and I can tell you that this was the worst brisket that I've ever had. Brisket should be soft and can be cut with a fork. This brisket had to be cut with a knife. I was not impressed. The pork that my brother got was good. The sauces were good and the service was good. Other than that I thought the food was mediocre at best.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: We enjoyed our time!  Best BBQ in the area!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Great service, good strong drinks, quality meat, but skip the desserts.  Pineapple turnover was dry and pineapple looked like a dried fruit.  Peach cobbler wasnt any better, looked like they used canned peaches..... maybe it wasnt but could of fooled me.  Portions are perfect and prices were on point.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Not bad, but not great! The hostess made us wait while she sifted through menus for 3 minutes in front of us, not very attentive.  The Bacon appetizer and burnt ends were great!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Very disappointed. Look at the menu online and then get there and it's not the same menu. Use to love the buttered noodles but don't have them anymore. Still listed on the website menu though. VEEY DISAPPOINTED. won't be going back

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Disappointed and well as alot of folks I have spoken to in the change of restaurant and menu.  Miss the old Plain and Fancy Restaurant.  Bring back the old restaurant and menu.  A lot of better restaurants in the vicinity.  Sadly, very disappointed.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Had family style dinner and food was delicious. Fried chicken was spot on!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Food was good but can't accommodate large family... couldn't sit together and have same waitress after 4...if you are family of 4 you'll enjoy.  Unfortunately we won't be able to go back.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Nothing Special, Not A Fan of this Restaurant.  Seating was a joke. We arrived before our friends, but weren't allowed to sit at a table until they arrived. I get that full party seating is wise for a weekend, but on a THURSDAY night when NO ONE was there?  Use some common sense Smokehouse! They need a total Gift Shop makeover; nothing but manufactured "gifts" aka China Junk.  They could make more money and have a better selection with local handcrafted items, which are prevalent in Amish Country. Sad non-use of local talent. I gave them 2 Stars because food was Ok.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text:
First, we waited close to an hour to be seated. There were only 5 groups seated in front of us. At took another 40 minutes to get our food. The restaurant was at only 20% occupancy! They were obivously understaffed. I was looking forward to good BBQ. Was it worth the wait? No! The food was dry with no taste! I won't talk about the dessert, I don't want to pile on!

The prices would've been reasonable though!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: It was just ok. Friday night at 6pm & place was pretty empty but we still had to wait for a table. The sides are very small & the fried chicken definitely wasn't fresh & it was over cooked. Mac n cheese sounded good but was dry. Probably wouldn't eat there again given the many other options in the area.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: I would be happier if I have a zero. Plain and Fancy looks likes plain and disgusting one of the manger or lady with a white apron was disrespectful rolling her eyes at our party upstairs and one of her workers food was okay but not the type of style Amish eats it looks like a plain food. The style of the restaurant did not even look like Amish style it looked like a new themed. They took the food away to fast while my party was still hungry and dessert was so bad honestly. Good N Plenty is better compared to this unprofessional manger and  not a good feeling for people. So don’t expect us to come back to this place my party was not full at all and not happy!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Waited half hour for table. When we were seated, the room was half empty. Serves was very slow. The food quality and quantity didn't match the pricing. We will not go back.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Very tasty and large selection of food and sauces. They also had a good selection of beer. The price is ok for what you get, maybe a buck or two on the high side. Not a bad choice of a restaurant at all. Oh and free wifi too!

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Good food.  Service was friendly.  Would eat here again.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: overpriced, below average food quality, small portions, pass this one up

review_text: It was ok but I've had much better bbq

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: We have been here three times and each time we went pork was extremely dry and un edible. Mac and cheese is also dry and has no flavor. Chicken wings are very good and they have lots of drink items.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Good affordable food, nice atmosphere. Entrance is a gift shop.Excellent place to browse prior to dining.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: We Had lunch with family here and the pulled pork was done well, coleslaw was great. Unfortunately the chicken and the brisket my son in law had were dry and somewhat overcooked. Overall, we would eat here again.

business_name: Smokehouse BBQ & Brews at Plain & Fancy Farm
review_text: Smoked turkey was great the brisket was ehh.

review_text: Food is fair to average at best.  Atmosphere in this place is kind of weird...its like an Amish gift shop with a "kind of a bar" in the back.  Lights are bright...no music...its just an odd setting.

We recieved only excuses, never an apology. Food was warm at best, although the Burnt Ends were quite flavorful and the sauces were good.

review_text: We arrived approximately around 7:55pm on a Saturday to grab some late night dinner, the hostess and the manager were talking for about 6 minutes to tell us that they were closed. I said “I thought it closes at 9?” And she said they stop seating at 8.... it was 8:01 when they finally spoke to us.  We were turned away. And missed an opportunity to eat somewhere else. Terrible customer service.

The reviews are wrong, if you eat here you will regret it!
