Use to love this place but now not so much. Went on a Tuesday night and the bartender was in a mood. Didn't even ask how our meal was and it was not that busy. Took for ever to get a refill at I was seated at the bar.will never go back. Molly pitcher much better.

Came  in for a beer flight and a Jacketed Potato with beer cheese. The potatoes were amazing and could be shared as an appetizer for two people. Definitely want to come back for dinner here in the future.

My flight included their Excalibur Imperial Stout and Mathilde's Last Waltz Belgian Blonde, but the other two beers in my flight were local (an IPA from Back Porch Brewing, which is nearby, and a Pilsner from Harrisburg's Boneshire Brew Works). Wish I could've tried more of their in-house beers, but those were the only two. They did offer the Stout on Nitro, as well as a huge seven-page menu of wine, seltzers, and cocktails.

Everything looked clean and orderly. Staff were wearing masks.

So i just had a few bites of the steak and crab Chesapeake and i must say it was the salty est thing iv ever tasted the past 2 times i came here with my family the food was not up to par of the reputation of this place i dont know whats wrong but i will continue to patronage the local business because the staff is really just nice and attentive i hope it gets better

We were visiting friends tonight from the Pittsburgh area. They were excited to share their favorite place with us. We were patiently Waiting on this holiday weekend not expecting to get a table any time soon. Much to our surprise, the waitress told us we would be seated in 5 minutes. While waiting to be seated with our group of 6, a loud group of 4 came in behind us. Being at a bar, we weren’t offended and were chatting friendly with the group. It was obvious that an uptight couple at the bar were complaining to bartender and making assumptions that our group was part of the other. The bartender was rude to us and called us out in front of the entire bar when we were doing nothing but waiting.  The bartender “privately” spoke to our waitress who then returned to our group 20 minutes later only to tell us that they suddenly had a bunch of previous reservations she didn’t know about. It was extremely obvious that we weren’t wanted because they associated us with a loud group. It was embarrassing and A very disappointing experience.

I have been at this place numerous occasions and really enjoyed the atmosphere and The food. We had a group of friends visiting tonight from the Pittsburgh area and couldn’t wait to take them to our favorite Carlisle bar. We were patiently waiting on this holiday weekend with our group of 6, when a loud group of 4 came in behind us being obnoxious. It’s a bar, so we weren’t offended and were chatting with the group as we were all waiting. It was obvious that an uptight couple at the bar were complaining to the bartender about the whole group of 10 as we were all waiting right behind her. The bar tender was rude to us  then spoke to our waitress who had told us previously we would have a table in less than 5 min. She came back over 20 min later saying that there were a ton of reservations that she didn’t know about (when there were a ton of empty tables)and she didn’t really know what to do. We just left knowing that we weren’t wanted since they associated us with the loud group. We are loyal customers and wanted to take our 4 friends there and since we were incorrectly associated with another group of 4, we were basically turned away. It was embarrassing and disappointing. I’m sad that we won’t return and will not recommend this place anymore.

Food was good and beer menu is extensive. However the service was INSANELY slow. Took an hour and a half to get our dinner. Also, they completely forgot my friends’ appetizer and then had it on the bill. Luckily my friend caught it. It wasn’t even that busy so I don’t see an excuse for the long wait for our food and completely forgetting a dish.

Do not recommend this place. Service is horrible food is terrible. I ordered a "prime rib" I recieved a rib eye steak cooked on a flat grill. No flavor super chewey and not PRIME RIB.!!! Supposed to come with baked potato... instead of another option I was given instant mashed potatoes. Everything came out cold  just a bad experience over all. Go SOMEWHERE ELSE . 👎👎👎👎👎👎

Great food, great drinks, but the waitressing service is something to be desired, mediocre if anything. The waitress we had wasn’t attentive at all. As a matter of fact, I was never was once asked if I wanted refill on my drink, literally sitting there with an empty glass for almost 20 minutes, until I actually had to call her to the table. Even then it took a good while to get a beer. But I still left a 15% tip because I know how tough that career field can be with money.

UPDATE!!!!  Came back May 31 with a dozen guests for dinner...you guys knocked it out of the park!  Everyone was very happy with what they received, service was good too! (The Seafood Medley is OUTSTANDING! Some of our guests got the lamb and said it was terrific!  Thanks very much, see you again sometime!

Came in for dinner this week...Food was a bit disappointing.  Ordered teriyaki salmon with a side of asparagus.  Salmon was undercooked, (sent it back, came back better.) The asparagus was cold in the middle, (frozen?)  Husband loves Shepherd's pie but wasn't too thrilled about the one he got, not bad but not what he expected.  I am planning on bringing a group here in May.  Always liked MCP, so I'm hoping the kitchen will get up on their game by then.  Thanks!
This is a great place to have a friends or family meal.  They are so accommodating and friendly.  We've been coming here for celebrations for years!

A wonderful pub in downtown Carlisle that brews its own beers on the premises. The beers vary in quality. I did a sampler of 5-oz. glasses of all 4 of their beers. Really liked the Belgian blonde beer, but didn't care for the cask-style red ale. The other 2 were decent.

I only had one food item and it was outstanding! The Black and Bleu Chicken Salad had moist, tasty,; lightly blackened chicken breast slices, with sauteed onions, mushrooms, and green peppers; cherry tomatoes and blue cheese crumbles; and a nice house vinaigrette on a bed of lettuce (not too much ... just the right amount of greens). Delicious!

Service was prompt and friendly. The place had a welcoming feel to it. And to someone used to DC restaurant prices, it was surprisingly inexpensive. I don't think I saw a single entree over $20 on the menu!

I dined alone this time, but will return with friends next time I'm in the vicinity.

I could see the cooks preparing food as they wiped their noses played with their beards and texted on their dirty cell phones. Should have to wear gloves to prepare food and change them often. Need to work on cleanliness.
I gave a 4 star rating ...because of the great customer service and the choices of beers... Best for in the area..

We had an unusually long wait to be acknowledged, and served which soured our experience. Our entire party left feeling ignored and awkward. The bartender was inattentive, and borderline rude (bizarre). The reviews brought us in so I hope it was just an off night.
It's a watering hole that serves food, expect the food and service to be of that same vein: sometimes ok, sometimes not so ok.

I have given this place very good reviews in the past, however, it's time to let you know NOT TO ORDER ANY BURGER. They are frozen,  pre-formed patties and nearly tasteless. A restaurant  of this quality should be serving fresh ground beef. 'Nuff ssid.

I was very unpleased by the service we received. It took over an hour and a half for us to get our food and when we got it the chicken wings were cold and we had to wait 25 minutes to get our drinks.  Overall very unpleased and at the end of it they didn't even discount us on anyting.

Went there for a celebratory dinner /drink/date woth my fiance.  The gentalman seating us was very nice and helpful at the end of our meal he was the one who came and cleared our plates away. Food was 50 /50. Fiancé burger and fries were good. I had the fish chicken shrimp combo basket with french fries .....mine was on the greasy side. Service...meh...jami seemed distracted for lack of a better word.she seemed uninterested in us and was much more interested in the table beside us that had 5 or 6 people. The drink prices seemed a little high i was hoping to have more then one but with the prices and the time it took for our server to check back with us i couldnt afford it time or cash. Not sure if ill go back again , its not on my top 5 omg places but i always wouldnt tell ppl to not try it.

My prime rib was not the usual good meal. This particular cut was full of grissle and was very tough.
Not our best meal there.
Good food at a high price, staff treated me awful because I wasn't one of their regulars. To me it's not worth it to go back again.
Not children friendly
Manager is incredibly rude.

We went here twice in the last 3 months.  The first visit was okay.  However, our most recent visit we received poor service.  It wasn't crowded, yet you would have thought that it was with how long it took for drinks and food to arrive to our table.  We had to ask for refills multiple times.  We had to ask for silverware and napkins.  We also reminded the waitress about things we had already asked for that she forgot.  Once our main items arrived, half of the food was not warm.  One member of our party did not receive their main encore without asking for where it was.  We find this sad considering our first visit was okay.

Beer was great! Atmosphere is super! The customer service was also superb. Food could be better though. The wings were not very flavorful and the fish was pretty gross. That’s ok though cause we were really there for the beer!
Great food. Good portions. Absolutely try the peanut butter pie. Amazing good. Service good and clean place.

Went here once and it was really good. Tried to go back for my wife’s birthday and was denied service because I would not put a mask I. I informed them I could not do so that I had a medical reason and was then asked what my reasoning was. I informed them that I did not have to provide them with that information and that it was actually illegal for them to require me to provide that but they still wouldn’t budge. I asked to speak to the owner and was told they would be over shortly. After waiting about 10 minutes the owner never showed up and I was told the owner couldn’t speak. The worst part is we were specifically trying to support local business and ended up going to carabas who seated us no problem.

We will probably try it again, but on this first visit, the service was slow and the food overpriced considering 3 of 5 meals were just okay. The soda is homemade, so if you care, stick with beer or tap water. The pretzel was amazing, and the sauces were all delicious, but I expected far more flavor for the price with the burger and bratwurst. I was also disappointed that "German potato salad" was simply a sliced red potato. Since it took us about 45 minutes from getting seated to getting our food, we didn't hang around for dessert.

Arrived and told wait would be 25 mins. Ended up being over 1 hr. Service was slow. Ordered food items were missing when brought out. Food was over fried.  Waitress came back with our change and it was short! Very disappointed in our experience tonight...would not ever recommend or return!

As someone who's an often critic for Google who each review has over 300 views on each post. I will say this it's a little bit on the expensive side but that's most breweries but you get what you pay for. Their burgers are 8 0zs and fresh and great tasting. The service well I didnt come here for it that's why I came when it was dead just to try the food .The next time I come it's going to be for the service side of things but even though it was dead I was well taken care of.  I didnt see one person standing around and that's good. As for the food for what I had it was great definitely a place to go if you had never been here before.

3 out of 4 of our party’s food was excellent and the service was great. Prices are not too bad either. However one of our party’s food was not cooked thoroughly and the burger was cold in the middle. Otherwise a good experience, wish I could’ve given a better review.

Will not be going back anytime soon.  Ordered a hamburger medium and specifically asked it not be overdone and a “hockey puck”.  Well, it came back black and charcoal looking.  Also, had a flatbread so dry we could not eat it. Had about 1/4 of the entire flatbread before giving up because it was so dry.  When brought to the manager’s attention by the server for a refund the response was “they already ate part of it so no refund”.  It’s crazy to me that a manager of a business would not be willing to take off one item from the bill.  I didn’t even ask about my burger being refunded and they knew it was bad.   Bottom line: food is bad and the manager does not care!

We have been going to ABC for years and we've never had a problem. Tonight we ordered two beers, nachos and two burgers. We received our beer and nachos and after 45 mins of waiting for our burgers we left. We didnt see our waitress nor our dinner. The sad thing is the last time we were in there we gave the same waitress we had tonight a 20 dollar tip for the shear facts of the covid 19 struggles for waitresses. We have always liked ABC but after tonight we will NOT be going back.

The food is very good. Had fish & chips and the the fish batter was a little too greasy but taste was good. Busy for a Friday night but waaaayyyy too many kids, young kids. Not appropriate to bring young kids to a brewery on a Friday night. Waitress was nice though.
Great service, great food!

The food was bland and over done. I ordered the fish and chips. The restaurant was very clean. Too bad the patrons were waking around without masks. I witnessed a staff member even dropped their mask at a table.

I would be sympathetic to their petition if they staff and management would have at pretended to obey the guidelines.

The only way I can describe this place is... Generic.  You won't be disappointed but you won't be surprised.   Food is middle of the road.  Time between ordering and getting food is really good but the food tasted generic.  The beers have a lot of potential and they are SO close to being really good but again they are generic.  Again you won't be disappointed but not blown away.   As my wife puts it " ... It's a safe place to eat while traveling...."

Disappointing. I just walked in to pick up my take out order and the server at the counter said "What's up" and walked away.  We typically don't dine in because wait staff is rude. We do order take out often due to the convenience but i would say more often than not the order is wrong in some capacity.  I am surprised this location is able to stay in business. I would recommend to dine elsewhere.

Reserved a table a week ahead for my father in laws 82nd birthday so that my in laws and friends could watch the dancers. We were notified an hour previous that our reservation was awaiting.  When we arrived with our party we were told they were not honoring reservations because others were staying to watch the dancers. No apology.  Our party was very disappointed.  My wife and I will probably never return to this establishment. Poor business ethics.
Customer service was great and food was great too

This place has a really nice aesthetic and the food quality is excellent. They only serve their own beer (plus a limited selection of liquor) but you will most likely be happy with something.. just tell them what beer you're looking for and they'll get you an equivalent. Happy hour pricing is pathetic ($1 off beer) and the rest of the menu is typical, $15-$20 entrees. Overall, a nice meal but not a super great value.

I loved my experience here. I went for a paint night hosted at this location. I highly enjoyed the espresso stout. I ordered the brussel sprouts, they tasted great however they were incredibly salty. I was only able to eat a couple pieces before it got to much for me to handle which was disappointing. My friend got the pretzel bites and they were fantastic. The server was very pleasant and attentive. My overall experience was great and I will return to try a full dinner and more of their beer selection.

Can't give a great review for the food because the interior is always so dark and cold.  My wife and I only eat here when we can get outdoor seating.  Tonight at 9PM on the nicest night I could hope for I was informed they would not seat us outside.  The hostess informed us it was an inconvenience for management and a limited staff.  There were parties outside enjoying meals, and lots of empty tables inside. I hope the next time we come back they will treat us like their other patrons.
Good beer.  Very good food.  Super bartender.  BUT the high school cafeteria silverware.. .Really? Cheap cheap cheap.  Makes me wonder what other corners they are cutting...
Good food with friends. I didn't really step out of my comfort zone with the food selection. Had a burger and fries which was very good. Beer was great as well.
Great food, beer and facility - have had better service.

Food was good..service was WONDERFUL!  We were a party of 12 and all of our food came out at the same time and everything was good and hot. I am recovering from a concussion and the loud talking and music etc was not good for me at this time of my recovery.

Great food and service.  Excellent menu and large choice if beers on tap.  I'd have given 5 stars except they took a  ery long time to bring my salmon.  The people around me ordered 10-15 minutes after I did and they got their hot food before I got mine.

While I am usually a fan of local breweries this place continues to produce mediocre results. The food and beer never surprise in any way, though it is always clean and service is good. True rating is around 2.5 stars.
The burger was excellent. The mac and cheese was not heavy or overly cheesed up, very good.  Atmosphere was fun and laid back. Service was good, friendly staff.

I got gift cards before Christmas. In fact 4 $50.00 gift cards and no incentive cards back.   (I.E.: 10.00 etc. )  Your managers in two pubs explained you only do that the first two weeks from Black Friday. I was there closer to Christmas.  You might want to think of doing the incentive cards for the month of December.  Just a thought. Happy New Year

Beer is average to good, hefeweizen and double IPA are good. Occasionally, they come up with a really good one, such as their barrel aged imperial stout.  Good burgers, fish, and specialty sandwiches - but pricey.  Go between 4 and 6 on weekdays for happy hour beer discount.

They need to add an online tap list like the Harrisburg location has.

Beer prices are increasing and Happy Hour discount is shrinking.  Actually, all of the prices have been increasing, thus I'm lowering my rating and I will be stopping in much less frequently.
Good food and good brews. We had a huge party of adults and kids. They went out of there way to take care of us. Would recommend to everyone.

Reserved a table that wasn't available when we got there.  Asked for items on the side and never got them. Beer took about 15 minutes. Place was 1/4 full. Food and drinks are good. Not happy with service.
Great beer...knowledgeable and friendly staff. They also have liquor offerings including there own bourbon, ABC. Try it, you still like it if you like bourbon.
Enjoyed this visit very much. One of the managers spent some time with us to explore different beers.  Embers Remains imperial stout was excellent. Burger and fries were delicious.

This is the second time we've visited the new location on the Carlisle Pike. We first attempted Cameron St. but the hostess clearly had the flu, was wiping sweat off her forehead with a paper towel and coughing. We promptly walked out. We then tried the Carlisle Pike location. The atmosphere is that of a funeral. The food was mediocre (the chicken that I ordered was rubbery.) Most of the food prices are gouging ($16 for a small chicken breast, some broccoli and some rice .) The only reason I gave two stars is due to the service being fairly good. I don't believe we will be going back.
Good service, I like all the different types of food I have eaten, hamburgers, chicken sandwich and the zommrilla quesadilla.

You can pass on the prime rib sandwich.   Beef on wick
Prime rib, by definition should be medium rare and well marbled.
The bun.. awesome. the cheese was nice.  the fresh grated horseradish was very good. the rosemary au jus was perfect.
The meat. was arbys.
well done roast beef.
thats NOT prime rib.
was no fat in it either, as id expect from an actual shaved prime rib.
and no, I dont want to hear "well not everyone likes medium rare meat".  then dont order prime rib, because thats what it IS.
should be listed as a roast beef sandwich.
Paying $14 for an arbys roast beef. no thanks.

I've been here (and the other ABCs) too numerous times to count over the years. The food and atmosphere is always great, however... this time... they had a 35 minute wait because 'the kitchen was busy and couldn't take more orders.' They had more than 10 open tables, and probably 6-7 parties waiting.  I know this might be a staffing or management issue, but it's still a negative experience. We went to Aroogas instead, who sat us immediately and had more customers. Ironically, the couple behind us at ABC also went to aroogas, and we chatted about it.

We'll probably go back, in a few months if they fix whatever is going on at this location.
Perogies were excellent and the staff was helpful in suggesting different beers.
The atmosphere here is great. The food is also very good. The beer, unfortunately, leaves much to be desired.

Stopped in at 4 pm for an early dinner. We ordered a burger n fries and a chicken quesadilla. Took over a half hour for food to come out. Burger was ordered well done and it came out rare. Waitress came over showed it to her n she agreed it was rare. Waited for another burger to be made. Food was ok, service from kitchen was very slow. Waitress was very nice. Doubt we will ever return again.
Good food Great beer but the service could use some attention.. Had to chase down the wait staff to get more drinks..
Tonight the food was good but the service very slow. Good atmosphere vibe.
The fish and chips were awesome as well as the root beer and that Irish cheesecake. Yum!!!!

We came here with a group from work when in Mechanicsburg. I have never been treated so horribly. When we came in at 8:20pm we were told they were closing even though the hours on the door said that they closed at 10pm. The waitress Heather came over and slammed menus on the table after telling us that the kitchen closed in 20 minutes. She also threw coasters at us. She was horrible and started putting up chairs around us.  We asked to speak to a manager and the manager was just as rude. Food and beer were good. Service was beyond terrible.
Bad experience, the salad seem to be on the plate since this morning... 🤮
Good beer, sub-par service, okay food.
Leah did a great job! Prompt service and food was hot on arrival!

Food was okay.   I ordered the Beef on Weck ( a Buffalo, NY classic) and for $13 I got a sandwich that was on way too airy of a roll, with fine salt (not Kosher like it should be) and the roast beef itself was over-cooked and tasteless.  Glad the au jou was good.  Had to ask 3x for horseradish as well.

To start, the beer menu is only on the wall... they don't have printed versions to show you so if you can't see far away you're screwed. And it's on a slideshow so it keeps disappearing. One of the dumbest decisions I've seen.

Second the wings were terrible. They were over cooked and so greasy...they are in a butter oil mess with no flavor. Terrible.

When you get to the main meal it's nothing special. I got fish tacos. Needless to say there was nothing special or great about them. Over cooked oily fish with way too many onion.

Definitely would not recommend other than there beer wasn't bad. Don't come here for food. It will disappoint.

Some of the people at our table ordered salads with steak. They literally cut the bottom off the heads of lettuce, sprinkled cheese on them and served them as is. Just a pile of lettuce on a plate. Our waitress was rude and was pretty much was abruptly serving our drinks and food to us. I also felt the food was way over priced compared to what you got. I won't visit this place again.
Great service and food and many options of custom beers! A definate must try!
Very slow service and the food was average at best. Food was served cold and the beer was warm.

Order the sweet potato fries....VERY overdone...
You should remove the Bay leaves from the Gumbo before serving.... Service was ok.
Good service but food wasn't great.
Good beer and nice sized menu. Service is slow and food is middle of the road - still a good choice to meet for drinks and shoot the breeze.
Great food, great service, great house made beverages but offers a limited menu.
We waited 1. 5 hours for our food . Spoke to the manager she was rude. We will never come back.
Service not great, food not great. Visit the Harrisburg location if you're looking to try their beer.
Server wasnt attentive. Not great service for our first time in PA
Great beers; Beef on Weck is fantastic
I like the food, and the atmosphere is good too. However, it always takes a very long time for the food to come after ordering.
Great food, good service.
Food, beer, staff were good

Good beer, nice decide, will come
Back again
Beer, service were good, place is noisy, food is average. If u want wine with ur food, go elsewhere. No interest in wine here.
Ok food, great beer.
Food is pretty good, service is extremely slow. Plan on being here an hour for lunch.
Good beers and food, the place was spacious as well.
Great burgers and fries!
Brew pub with typical bar type food, done well. Service was prompt and friendly
Great beer, good food
Great beer!
Was good....yup.
15 minutes got our drinks and never got to place our order and they were not busy.
Food was not very good. French fries were cold, cheese on the sandwich was old and hard, not up to the usual high standard. Disappointy.
Food was overpriced and mediocre the service was absolutely terrible won't go back again
Average
It was ok
Tonight was my family and I first time going here. The food was awesome but the service was very crappy.

The worst French Onion Soup I've ever had in MY LIFE! It tasted so watered down. There was way too much cheese and I actually love a lot of cheese. Wouldn't have minded it so much if it was the correct type of cheese that goes with French onion soup and if it were correctly made and not watered down. If we weren't in such a hurry for an appointment, I would have definitely taken the time to complain. Not that any server asked how it was.

So I called Miller's in Media on  Friday evening to place a to go order. Phone rang and rang-- no one answered.
Thought I would try again on Saturday @ 4:30 pm before they got busy for dinner time. When the girl answered the phone I asked to place an order for pick up. She informed me that their "to go" phone line wasn't working and that I could go online to place the order.
I told her that I don't place orders online as I am not a computer person and was actually in  my car. She said "oh, I am sorry." I said "so you are not able to take my order?" Her response was "No--I am sorry."
I too work in the restaurant business and I would never tell a customer that I couldn't take their order or try to accommodate them.

Had to wait over an hour to be seated as we were informed the cooking staff was overwhelmed with online/curbside orders but otherwise once we were seated there was no reason to be disappointed.... Our waitress was pleasant, we were seated far away from any other patrons and the food was great. I plan to return once I have my 👶🏾 to give the bar a go 😃

Cheese Curds are the bomb!
Good burgers, cheese steaks, and French Dip Sandwich. Love the seasoned fries. We enjoy eating here.

Staff was very nice but the cooks can't cook. How do you order a medium well and thing comes out well? The Mac n cheese I couldn't even finish. It was greasy and the taste just, wasn't good. Crunchy stuff was in it too. Weird. The appetizers and desserts were good. Management did have them make me a new baked potato and filet mignon. She was very nice as well.
Stop for a late lunch.... Drinks and a sandwich.  Our server was friendly and efficient.   Sandwich's were a good size and tasty!

There were five of us today the food was very good but the service was not good we had the bartender Tifaney  who acted like she no more wanted to be there than the man in the moon had an attitude and it one point we said Miss and she said Tiffany and we went oh sorry Tiffany I don’t think that was rude of us we couldn’t remember her name that we said miss but anyway we don’t need to be treated like that and then towards the end of the meal someone ask her for takeout they said  miss and again she said Tiffany !!! We’re not there to be her girlfriend the five of us were talking we just wanted some more takeout containers she needs an attitude adjustment and if if we ever come back again we’re definitely not having her wait on us she only had three other people at the bar she was waiting on it wasn’t like she was being rushed or anything

There last night, I previously read the reviews and was very unsure about ordering food. Sat at bar and ordered the Zinger loaded fries!! COLD!! Grated cheese wasnt even melted on half the platter. Notified barmaid, Jorgeta, she said she would tell the cook. Never sent manager over to rectify problem.
Noticed several tables waited an extended time for their meals also. Plenty of staff so no idea why they so inefficient....and no sign of a manager.
Also was quite cool inside, if you dare go bring a sweater!

****Response to owners comments below****
No I am not interested in going on your website. If you truly cared about your customers you would have manager in clear view. And advise staff all complaints need to go to management! You didnt care when I was there...no point now. I wont be back!!
Friendly with good service.  Burger a little over the top ..... Good taste but hard to handle re: size.  Needed a lot of napkins.
Had meatloaf platter.  Extra for salad or soup.  Extra for rolls.  Could hardly see the mashed potatoes and gravy on the plate.  Asked if there was a charge for the salt and pepper??

Lackluster meal the first time we visited. Gave it a couole months. Visted tonight, gave our name and number and were tild about 10-15 minutes. 40 minutes went by. When my husband went in to ask what was going on.. They claimed to have texted and called us. WHICH THEY MOST CERTAINLY DID NOT. And since we" had not answered" theyd removed us from the list. And instead of APOLOGISING and seatinf us promptly. Told my husband we'd have to wait another 45+ minutes.  Also our phone number was not on the phone they use to tect and call.  If I could give zero stars I would.

Ordered here for curbside pickup, person who took the order over the phone was extremely helpful and was awesome. Always a plus when kids eat free on Tuesday's. Food was very well prepared and very good!

It was a very good experience.  I had the filet mignon dinner and to my surprise it was very good.  The wait for dinner was longer than usual because they only had two chefs on because of Covid-19 but the food was worth it.

Worst service ever. Takes forever to get food then it's wrong. Second time in a month I say here waiting for my food while my husband is done before I even get mine. Used to be good food and good service I don't know what happened.
I had the black and blue burger and it was delicious. I would definitely recommend visiting there to anyone. Service was on point
One beer cost 8.50. Too much way too much for a middle of the road restaurant
It was clean...our server was GREAT...food was good...my cocktail was weak.
A real fun place ...food was surprisingly good ...usually you get "processed "..but there's the feeling your meal was cooked just for you
Shotti service food not to good over or under cooked zingers(chicken strips) Burger no taste mac and cheese dried out need I say more

This is much better than the one in Springfield, however the wait times are generally long. *But tonight we had an excellent experience. Food was great, service was good and they have really upped there game since my last review. We sat at the bar and Lexie was our server. Pleasant, engaging and a pleasure in which to be served. I’ve increased our rating from 3 to 4 and edited my review. A manager called me about my review and really impressed me with his response. Will definitely be back next week!

Customer service needs work . Waitress took are order brought drinks and never came back . Waited forty minutes , finally we went to speak to the manager and explained that are waitress never came back and explained if they were short handed or anything. Sorry to say I will never go back . Mehhhh..... It was just OK. Servers were pleasant, martini wasn't good, calamari was pale like it hadn't been fried long enough. The rest of the dinner was good. Just needed some pepper. We'll still give it another try.
I had the andouille mac & cheese, loved every bit of it and would recommend to anyone who loves to dress up their mac & cheese from time to time 👍

There was about a 15 min wait. They will text when ready and you must wait outside. Slightly annoying only because guests tend to crowd around the door instead of spacing out. Food was absolutely delicious and the service was good. Garlic parm wings are so good. Table was clean and everything was appropriately spaced and staff was wearing masks and sanitizing stations. My only suggestion is setting up an online waitlist situation similar to Texas roadhouse. Then you only get out of your car when your table is ready and you get a text.

The wings were ok. There was a big chalk board in the waiting area with different beers available. I asked the server for a beer menu because I couldn't see the board from my seat. She directed me to the back of the menu which listed the usual beers but didn't have the ones from the board. Might be a limited selection due to covid. The bottle of beer (Corona light) that I ordered had a funky taste. The atmosphere in the bar area is great and I'll definitely be back. Just hoping that this visit was an outlier.
Very good burgers and apps, fair prices. Steaks not great, texas roadhouse is a couple miles up the road....

Wait time not bad for a Saturday night with covid19 restrictions in place. Restaurant was clean outside and inside.  Bathroom in good condition. Server approached the table in a timely manner and returned with our drinks quickly. Soggy straws but it happens.
Server answered our questions about the menu which we appreciated being our first visit to this restaurant.
My wife had the pepperoni Flatbread, very tasty and she enjoyed it. Like pizza on a cracker but in a good way.
My daughter had the mac n cheese with the chicken zinger sauce on the side. She loved the meal and took home what she couldn't finish.
I opted for the filet, medium well, with baked potato and broccoli.  My steak wasn't seasoned well with the exception of too much salt. Plus when I cut into it, it was bleeding with each cut. Certainly not medium well as requested. Baked potato was average. I did enjoy the broccoli, very tasty with good flavor.
Coke refill was slow. I was halfway through my meal before the refill arrived.
We opted out on desserts.
Based on the steak, I would not return. But my wife and daughter enjoyed their meals so maybe we go back and I try something else if we find ourselves in the Granite Run area again looking for dinner.

I was there with my family the service is great but our food lacked flavor  and presentation my sons Flatbread looked  and tasted  like a cracker my husband's and I mashed potatoes  were swimming  in gravy and my sons loaded French fries were greasy soggy and cold not going  back
Food is delicious there  but mixed Drinks are way too sweet seems more syrup than Alchohol..atmosphere is pleasant
It's all good. We had an hour wait but that's due to covid. Food was good.
Food was good, only problem was it was understaffed.
Good service, good food, good time.

Unfortunately our experience with the food was not the best. Ordered steak med well, came out burned, waitress had kitchen prepare a new one, that was burned too. Chicken fajitas, the wraps were stale and an attempted masking it by heating them up. Zinger app was good. Service was a smidge slow . They seemed busy with to go orders. I will chalk this up to COVID, seems many places are struggling with producing  quality. Waitress did remove steak from bill.
Very nice place. Clean environment where the food and drink were plentiful and very tasty.
I got the blackened shrimp.and chicken cobb salad and it was really good.

The wait to get our food was way too long, and the wait staff kept on interrupting us (not to ask if everything was okay, in fact they forgot to bring us what we asked for a few times), to remind us to provide them with our emails and to fill out a survey. I understand that they need feedback, and that they want to retain customers, but to ask a customer to provide their email address not once, not twice, but five times during a meal? That's absolutely ridiculous. It was down right rude. I did not appreciate being harassed to give them my email address when they couldn't even get our drink order right.
Burgers are amazing! Good service.

My burger was great. My son got a kids chicken zinger and fries with honey Bbq sauce the sauce was liquid and he didn't eat any of the chicken said they were dry and hard.

The food was just okay. My server was not very attentive. Had to ask for straws, napkins, drink refills, etc gave my table someone else's bill. No the restaurant was not crowded or busy.
We were big fans of the restaurant since the opening.... but the last couple times the food was really salty and over seasoned. Service at the bar was not very good.
It was ok nothing to get excited about. The fries were lukewarm & limp & they forgot my bacon on my chicken BLT. Go to Chaps across the street
Covid operations are not doing well for these guys. Food was okay but it would have been nice to get my order correct in the 3 times they came to the car for curbside.

The service was mediocre. We sat at the inside bar and I had to remind the sever for extra napkins and state a few times I wanted another drink . Also my card was charged twice for an error on part of the server . The charge and return was never explained and then I got charged again without explanation. I will never return to this location again .

This restaurant is extremely busy! Food quality and delivery of your food makes haste and waste. Service was great and Management made accomidations for the waste, quickly. However, the food was greasey when we got home.  There was a rush to use the Nessarary room all night long. This was our second time there. The first time, it went real well. Everything was great. There was no management interventions. Just good food, service, and delivery. BUT THIS LAST TIME?

When we were seated at  our table was dirty. Had to ask the server to clean the table. Food was okay I ordered French oinion soup and house salad. The salad have frozen lettuce and the soup was extreemly salty. The server was not attentive at all the manager acually gave us a refil on water and napkins. The server wasnt very good at all. The food everyone had was good except for my frozen lettuce and salty soup.

It was my first visit to this particular Millers. I go to the springfield Millers frequently. I live closer to the Media restaurant however I will continue to go to the one in Springfield and heres why. When we arrived the hostess was short with us. There was grease smudges on the booth seats that we wiped up. Our server was nice. We ordered Prime rib one medium rare and one medium well. We asked for the Ribs to be placed on the grill to cook because we order it that way in Springfield. The medium rare came out rare and the medium well came out medium rare, and not cooked on the grill. The manager did come to our table and we talked about the issue and I stated that the rare steak wasn't what we orderd. He proceeded to tell me that the rare steak was a perfect medium rare. I'm actually shocked that the manager said that and I stated that I am a chef (with a culinary degree from 1 of the top culinary arts universities in NE) so I know the temps. Not to mention the fat on the prime rib was raw. We also mentioned that the aujus was salty. It wouldn't have been an issue if I ate rare steak we could have switched. So we send it back and the manager brings the new prime ribs back and my medium well was medium and the other came out perfect and to our surprise with grill marks.
The atmosphere isn't like Springfield and thats just my opinion, so i will continue to eat there.  Oh and I am aware its more of a sports bar type of place and im sure you are thinking this is not lone star or Ruth Chris LOL. I order prime rib in Springfield and haven't run into this.
Media is new and I hope that they do great, however I prefer Springfield. If management is reading this My server was GREAT.
Nice place. A lot of tv's. Food was just average.
Nice atmosphere and presentable food.
I answered very disappointing when the girl at the desk tells you she doesn't know how long the wait will be.

Nate the host didn’t put in our correct phone number. We are told a 45 min wait. After not hearing anything we inquired and were told they tried to contact us. Never got a text or a call. No apology or compensation was given for the error.
Food and service is really good.
Bad bar design- my beer slipped off the ba and the bartender really copped an attitude. She was really rude - from the beginning. Honestly- the bar staff was downright terrible.
Solid food and brew on tap
Great meal, and service.
Miller's is just OK!!
Fare was fair......
It was okay,
Hours must have changed. Door was open but employee let me know quivk they were closed. I'll try again tomorrow. I'm currently at Brick and Brew. I could not reply to your reply.

We enjoyed our dinner, after having this place recommended by food-loving friends. Appetizers and main courses (London Broil and Lamb Chops) were very good. This is a fun and festive place. The music can get a bit loud, and on a Friday night it is a challenge to have a quiet conversation over dinner.

Who's down for some good ole' fashioned pub food?  The Food Hounds were uber-excited to kick back and enjoy a GREAT little treasure in Reading, Pennsylvania called The Speckled Hen Cottage Pub and Ale House.  The overall vibe in the 'Hen' is laid back; perfect spot to go for a drink or a bite to eat.  If you are a beer drinker, they actually change up their taps on a weekly basis - so it's always a bevy of fresh new flavor for their clientele.  Now, if you are drawn to the sound of music like Lush and I... you'll be ecstatic to know that they have open mic night on Thursday's!  It happened to be Thursday when we stopped in; but since the entertainment normally starts later in the evening... we missed out on getting in on the fun.  The ambiance of the place is dark and romantic, perfect for college kids seeking an inexpensive spot to hang out, a first date or an older couple looking to head out for a night cap.

On to the food... Starters, we tried the Classic Scotch Egg - WHOAH... first time ever sampling the taste of this bad boy!  This sucker is just so ridiculous that it works... a hardboiled egg, enclosed in a pocket of seasoned ground sausage and then FRIED!!!  This egg is slammin' and served on a spicy grain mustard sauce.  The Char Grilled Pub Burger sported a 6oz slab of ground sirloin doused with cheddar cheese (Mmmm CHEESE!!!) and bacon... Bacon... BACON!  Add the delectable fries and POW... a great meal for the guy on the go looking for a quick bite.  The Baked Braided Pretzel with Crab Cheddar Au Gratin was a VERY unique creation.  A large homemade pretzel drowning in cheddar and huge chunks of tender crab...  Mmm CHEESE!  The WIN for me was quite simply the Cottage Sheppard's Pie.  A hefty portion entombed in a small crock-pot of love.  This is comfort food at its finest; seasoned ground sirloin topped off with buttery mashed potatoes... Off the hook!  I could eat that all night long... it was like buttery heaven on my tastebuds!  They have a lot to choose from at Speckled Hen, make sure you stop on in and try them on for size, you certainly won't be disappointed.

This place is never open. I had a gift card to use from judys on cherry when they gave us awful service and tried to make up for it. They're never open. I came here with a few people and they were closed by 930. They also weren't open the whole first half of a week. I never saw a pub closed so much or so early. I can't tell you about the food because there never open or they close at stupid hours.

I lived in the neighborhood my entire life. I never knew this place had good food. They have some classics and they also have some items you will not find anywhere else. Quiet friendly atmosphere and very clean.
For a while it just wasn't appealing to me. Not sure why. I gave it a try, and I loved the place. Excellent food and great scenery. Good staff. Good place. I'll be returning.

A craft beer bar gem surprisingly found in the heart of Mayfair. If you enjoy good beer this is a bar you should be stopping at. It has that dive bar feel, but in a good way. They have great bar food. Personally, I usually go with the lodge steak. The inside has some interesting art work on the walls. The tile beer quote wall in the men's bathroom is a must see/read. There are frequent beer related events. Firkin the 13th and ground hog day are events craft beer lovers should not miss. The selection of beer is massive and the taps are frequently rotated.

A converted local taproom, now one of the off-the-beaten path beer destinations in the city. Still has a lot of the local tappy trappings--loud (but not obnoxious) regulars, sports on TV, a mix of beer lovers and folks from the neighborhood. Solid bar menu. Upstairs a good option if the game doesn't suit you.
Clean, quite well maintained placed.
Doing the best they can at the start of the yellow phase.

Went in to have a beer or two.  After ordering the beer they stated we had to order food as well to drink.  Assume this is a PA government thing due to Covid.  Unfortunately we just ate before entering so they wasted 2 pours of beer because we left when they told us this.  If they had said something about this when we entered we would have left.  Not everyone is from PA so we had no idea about this.
Terrible website!!!!

I visited here twice over the last week - the first time was to check out the beers on tap (most of them from the local ShawneeCraft Brewing Company) and the beers were good and the staff was great.  They offered tastings of the local beer and the glass sizes were generous for the $2-$3 brews.  I wound up trying them all and not a bad beer in the lot!  Stop in any time for a drink, 5 stars there.

I came back later in the week for dinner and brought my extended family. We had the open faced roast beef sandwich, BBQ ribs, wings, and the barbecue quesidillas.

The food was a mixed lot tending toward meh.  The roast beef sandwich was OK, but was just plopped on two big peices of white bread.  Could have been toasted or changed up a bit.  Mashed potatoes OK.  Both me and the wife tried the ribs and got a very heavy alcohol-like aftertaste, similar to when you add in some red wine and don't reduce it enough.  It was bad enough for me not to want to steal any ribs off her plate which is always a bad sign.  The wings were the high point of the meal for me, a mix of BBQ and spicy that was extremely good - everyone liked them. You might not if you have trouble with spice, but they were great.  The quesedilla - mistakes were made.  Both me and the family member that tried it thought it was pretty horrible. The BBQ taste was cloying and it was not good, most of it got left on the plate.  Kid's spaghetti and french fries were good, per my 5 year old.

The ambiance and decor were nice, staff was pleasant, no problems with the service but for the price you paid for the food ($90 with 3 beers) and the taste of the food, a solid 3 stars.  Maybe if you know what is good you could do 4 stars, but I wouldn't roll the dice and pay the price.  Stick with the bar and the wings!
The atmosphere is very pleasant. There drinks are awesome. Big fan of the in house Ale's.
A very hip venue with good food and a nice local beer selection

Close to empty on a Friday night at 6:30. Order the teriyaki wings as an appetizer. The wings were the meat was dry. The sauce did have decent flavor. The wife ordered the deconstructed meatball sandwich. The sauce was brown and tasted more like a chili. The meatballs were not that flavorful. I ordered the chicken cheese steak which more resembled chicken from a can thrown through a blender. The chicken was very dry even though I requested with barbecue sauce. The burger from the kids meal for my son was the only good thing all night. They were nice enough to refund me for the chicken cheese steak when I asked them to. The menu was full of items that sounded good but I will not be going back to try anything else.

Basic cuisine that is decent. Setting is a little more upscale for the area. Best to have a reservation.  Staff is usually well trained, but holiday staff not as much.  Order was wrong twice in same night.  Do not let them put you down in the dungeon downstairs.  Wait for an upper floor table. Way too much lighting down there.  Upstairs has ambiance. Fish'n Chips is probably best you will find in all of the Poconos.

Fun little place in a cool little town. They have their own beers on tap which are pretty tasty. My minor quip is that the food is not interesting enough for the prices they charge. It is all slightly bland and boring

First thing I noticed was the smell.  We decided to give it a go since it is the only place around that serves vegan options.  The impossible burger may not have been impossible but real beef.  The chili had a distinct taste of beef.  The service was very good though and so was the beer.  The whole restaurant needs a good scrubbing.  Our table looked like it hadn't been cleaned since 5 servings ago  But the smell.  I couldn't wait to get to fresh air.

Went to the bar first downstairs - best beer and apps. Bartender was great. Went up for a burger around 5 on a saturday- waited a half an hour.  Make a reservation! There are not many tables unless you are willing to sit downstairs at the bar. Hostesses did not check in with us, did not take my name. People were asking where the brewery was and they turned them away saying it was just down the road. Not really very professional. Our server was amazing - quick and courteous; and the burgers we had were amazing. Mine was cooked perfectly and the topping was just right. Next time... we will make a reservation! Even coming early for upstairs dinner does not cut it .

I had the vegetarian chili which was decent My sister had the impossible burger which she really enjoyed. The falafel  in the Mediterranean wrap was green; it tasted stale and horrible, so we had them take it back.
Food was good. Service was really good for the amount of people we had. Would have been 5 star but the CC machine was down.

The food was great; however, not being from the area, I walked in the main entrance to inquire how to proceed to the patio, which had been booked for a company function. The hostess was quite rude and told me that i should've gone directly downstairs instead of coming in the main entrance. I told her this was my first time visiting and that I was not familiar with the restaurant, so I needed some direction. She then commented that my company contact should've been the one to tell us all that. Aside from her lack of professionalism and common courtesy, the wait staff was very helpful and accommodating to our large party. We ordered a variety of appetizers and everything was delicious. The salads are large enough to share and come with plenty of toppings. All of the burgers were cooked to temperature. I would go back for the food, and hope that there is a different hostess to greet me!

Just meh. The service was great and prices good but overall the food was just okay. This is the impossible bolognese soupy sauce instead of thick, lacked flavor in general. Also they have a vegetarian menu online but when we got there they said they no longer have that. So just ask if you’d like something substituted.

Excellent service. Very friendly! The Gem burger was delicious and the Shawnee Craft Beer IPAs were outstanding. I would definitely go back. I would have given 5 starts, but the cheese steak sandwich was not good and its presence on the menu should be reconsidered.

The atmosphere was nice and cosy. The beer menu is good. Service was very nice.

Food was mediocre, we tried the onion soup, gem burger, grilled cheese and plain pasta (for kids) - which came out cold.

Great venue. Nice service. Great local beers. Hit or miss on the food. The oxtail sliders were very good; the steak was good; the grilled tuna special was fine; the fish tacos were terrible. The dessert portions were generous. I think I would go back to sit outside and enjoy a beer.
Hostess & waitress were friendly & helpful. I ordered a Gem Burger;  it was tasty. Atmosphere was warm & full of friendly conversations.

Good waiter. Food was average. Fast service. The French onion soup was cool upon arrival. The Caesar salad had some kind of pickled onions which I didn’t expect. Not so good but I liked they put dressing on side because I forgot. In general everything was not hot including our main entrees. Tasty but not right temp.
Never thought I would enjoy the oxtail Sliders that much. Staff was friendly and attentative. A most-visit if you're in the area.

Went in and was seated and that was it. Sat and waited for 20 minutes with NO service. Asked the hostess twice and was told we would be waited on shortly. She didn't even just offer us water. It's 2 o'clock in the afternoon , it's not busy at all. So we got up and left. Very disappointed !!

We were looking for our place in the Shawnee area! The first two times the food was good. Then I got sick on the worst meatballs I have ever tried! We tried one more time and it was NOT good. It's sad we couldn't make this place our new hometown restaurant!!!

We saw a show on the Pocono tv channel and it looked like a pretty decent place.  Well when we walked in the door and my shoes stuck to the floor I knew it wasn't going to be good.  We were seated at a table that was so wobbly we had to ask to be moved.  Sat at another table and it was sticky.  We had to have waitress come and clean it.  She said it was sticky because the cleaning solution ruined the top of the table and that the tables are old.  Next ordered food.  It wasn't bad but a little pricey.  As we were leaving, I noticed the vent on the floor.  It looked as if they NEVER cleaned it!  I wont be going back.

My food was way to salty & didn't taste that good either. My husband asked for the beer flight and asked for certain ones and was brought beers he didn't ask for. Other people might like it but I will not good back.

Nice place. Somewhat pricey. Very good craft beer. No wine.

If I could leave no stars, I certainly would. My family and I went in this past Saturday for dinner, and had one of the worst restaurant/customer service experiences we have ever had. We sat downstairs in the Tavern (which was basically empty) and waited for just about an hour and still had not received any of the food we had ordered. When one of my family members got up to ask the bartender about where our food was, considering it was nachos, soups, and salads, she magically appears two minutes later with the nachos and one of the soups we ordered. When we finally got the nachos the top plate was so hot (from sitting under a heat lamp for god knows how long) that the chips were burnt, the chicken was beyond dry, and the cheese was so burnt it was breaking the chips. We called the inattentive waitress back over to say something about our nachos and how burnt they were and she responded with, “Well your order was a little complicated.” Our nacho order was simply half of the nachos came with all of the fixings while the other half did not (we certainly didn’t realize that would be considered “complicated”). The manager also got involved who was just as unpleasant and rude to us and we ended up leaving the restaurant after over an hour with no food and a horrible customer service experience. Having lived in a resort beach town my whole life, and having a lot of experience in the service industry, this restaurant should be embarrassed. Not only would I never recommend this establishment, I truly can’t believe that they are still in business after experiencing the way they handle issues.
Dining room floor was sticky, table was sticky, service was slow (I think they were understaffed) and the food was bland.

Nice spot for lunch...but it looks like it needs a facelift. We went for lunch the day before Valentine's day, and it was a very sparse crowd. The service was very attentive and accomdating. Our food arrived promptly and efficiently. My chicken Caesar salad had some lovely moist chicken breast, but the croutons seemed to have come from a box, very uninspired. The shared starter of veggie lettuce wraps were spicy and delicious. Other menu items ordered were a Brewbin, hamburger salad, crab cake sandwich and fries. All of the dishes were tasty and well executed. It is pricey for lunch, with apparently the same dinner menu.
What I noticed during a trip to the Ladies Room was how shabby it looked. There were slate floor tiles which were cracked and loose near the servers station, the carpet (yuck) in the dining room was dirty, worn, and in need of replacing. The interior looks dated and the woodwork is nicked and worn. This has been such a solid spot in West Chester for so long. It looks like a refresh of the interior is a bit overdue.
The food is still very good, so just go at night when the lights are low.

Awesome wings, I recommend the Asian ones. Great beers on tap that they make right here. Friendly service. Haven't gone on a weekend yet so I imagine it gets busy. This place is a major disappointment. The last thing this city needs is a generic sports centric restaurant with bland food and with Millers that is exactly what you get. We were a party of four and they sat us at a table that barely had any room for the menus let alone plates. Where as they sat a party of two at a huge booth. Our waitress seemed inconvenienced by our presence. She didn't ask or attempt to refill our drinks our entire meal. She got really nice at the end when it was time for us to fill out the tip section. Now let's get to the food, their famous "zingers" claim to be flavorful and tender. When in reality they are the blandest breaded chicken I've ever tasted in my life. The same goes for their burgers and chicken sandwiches. Wildly under seasoned. This restaurant needs to do research on things called salt, pepper and garlic or literally any seasoning. The only good thing was the crispy asian inspired cauliflower. Save your money and go somewhere exciting instead of this bland, boring, repetitive establishment.

The sport bar atmosphere was great. Our waitress was on point and amazing. We visited on the evening of January 23rd. I ordered the Wednesday special, Seafood Pasta and it was very salty. My waitress offered me more of their cream sauce, it made it tolerable. Now don't get me wrong, I like salty dishes, but not this salty. I asked to speak to the manager and told her how I felt about the saltiness of my dish. I even recommend for her to order it and try it out for herself.  Her reply astonished me, she responded with an attitude "next time order it with less seasoning" and walked away. My friend looked at me laughing and said, "I guess it was your fault!" It was our 1st time there. The bottom line; the atmosphere and waitress were great, the food needs improvement and in my opinion the manager needs customer service etiquette training.

Update February 19, 2019.
Originally someone from Miller's Ale House had commented on January 31. That post has now be deleted, stating that someone from management would be contacting me. Just wondering if this is the type of customer service you provide? Still waiting.

Would've been 5 stars, but the servers/bartenders aren't very personable. They'd rather talk to the locals that they know, instead of showing some out-of-towners some hospitality. Tried 2 IPAs, enjoyed one of them, but that's the brewery experience that you should expect anywhere. They make beer for everyone, not just you. Caesar side salad was a steal for just $4. The employees weren't rude, just not engaging, slightly standoffish. But otherwise, a great environment with even better food. Overall, the great food and good beer easily makes up for a lack luster performance by the staff. If you're staying in one of the many local hotels, this should easily be your first choice to try out.

Fifteen more minutes go by and she comes back again to ask us if anyone had taken our drink order yet. We had told her yeah you did and she said that it wasn't her it was probably the other lady. After she walked away me and my girlfriend both looked at each other and said that it was a woman with pigtails who took our drink order, and she was the only woman working wearing her hair in pigtails. After ten more minutes go by, we had finally had enough and walked out. Waiting that long for a simple drink order was unacceptable. I can't say anything bad about the food considering we didn't get that far, but I can surely say that the service was terrible, and we will not be returning

I was driving through Pennsylvania with my parents on our way to NYC.. I found this place on Google and it sounded pretty cool.. I wish I would've read some of the reviews before we decided to stop..

It was 8:30pm on a Saturday night, but we were told after we sat down that the kitchen was already closed aside from making pizzas.. We were pretty hungry/thirsty so we said no worries, pizza it is..

I ordered a frozen sangria off the menu, but was told they were out of it/the blender was broken.. I again said no worries, I'll take a vodka soda, and ordered one of the two vodkas they had on the menu.. Well, they were also out of that vodka, so for the third time in under a minute of being in this place I said no worries, I guess I'll take the only vodka you have..

My mom ordered a vodka cranberry, and my stepdad ordered one of their many beers on tap.. Well, you guessed it, they were also out of the beer he ordered, so he ordered another beer instead..

When the waitress came back, she had a beer and our 2 vodka drinks.. My drink tasted pretty standard, my mom said her's tasted like pure cranberry juice, and my stepdad took 1 sip of his beer and cringed..

We called our server back over, and she told us that they were also out of the second beer that he had ordered, so the bartender just poured him a seasonal pumpkin ale..

My stepdad then ordered 3 other beers, and they were out of all of them.. He then said that he would take any IPA or Pale Ale that they had on tap, but yet again, they were out of all IPA's..

My stepdad finally said he gave up, and he would just take a glass of wine.. The server, who couldn't have been more apologetic, said that she would be right back with it..

She returned a minute later, but only to again apologize and say that they were also out of white wine..

At this point, we just wanted to go across the street to a drive thru, grab a burger, and continue on our way.. But the pizzas were already in the oven, so my stepdad finally said just bring me a vodka cranberry, at least we know you have that..

Well, wouldn't you know it, the waitress came back to say that they used the last of the vodka to make my mom and I's drinks.. I guess this explains why my mom's drink tasted like pure cranberry juice..

The pizza is now on the table, and my stepdad says he'll just take an ice water..

I ordered the buffalo chicken pizza, which didn't have buffalo sauce on it because, THEY WERE OUT OF IT, so instead, they decided to just put jalapeños on it.. My stepdad ordered a make your own pizza with chicken and BBQ sauce, which didn't have BBQ sauce, not because they were out of it, but because they forgot to put it on, so that came in a cup on the side..

I asked for the check before we were even finished eating because at this point, we all just wanted to get out of there.. As I handed over my card to our server, who again, couldn't have been more apologetic, the bartender comes up to our table with a vodka cranberry, and says that he found a last bottle in a box under the bar..

So in conclusion, this place sounds incredible, a brew pub set in an old church, but I'm just bummed that I wasted $55..

3 stars is generous but since they've only been open a week and we visited on a Friday night when they didn't know how many people would come I'm giving them the benefit of doubt. The good: this area needed a place like this - an independent brew pub; they actually brew on site; the menu is straightforward - they're not trying to be more than a brew pub. But, the bad: acoustics - it's atrocious - you can't hear people next to you without screaming. The bathrooms - a single-person bathroom for each gender is not sufficient for the number of seats and drinking beer!  The service - an hour from order to food - for their sake I hope that's first week issues that need to be figured out fast. The portions are a bit small. Order of wings is only 6, but nice smoke flavor. Medium-rare burgers had no pink - brown throughout. They were out of our first 2 beer choices at 7:30 pm. Brisket could have had more flavor. I'll give them some time and then see if they were able to fix things...

Lots of seating options - bar, high tops, tables and outdoor gathering area. Come check out Glenside's newest gem.

The bartenders were very nice, but I'm sorry to report that the beers were terrible. You could taste only wort. I wish breweries would stop opening before fine-tuning their beers. We will not be back.

To be honest,  I didn't like every beer I tried. We ordered set of 4 different beer (2 dark and 2 light). Light beer (bada bing and two coins) tested like water (not much taste) and dark (drunken  philosopher and dark matter) may be wasn't so bad, but was very sweet and specific. So, we ordered ipa (casey jones) on top, and it was ok. I ordered arugula and beets salad. Everything was fresh, but arugula doesn't go well with parmesan.  It should be with a goat cheese.  Also they didn't add any dressing. French fries were great, I even would say the best I ate in Philadelphia area.

The only negative was the noise level. The noise was from the crowd not media. It was difficult to have a conversation between more than two people. I really hope they can get this issue resolved.

Brand new brewery (summer '19) in the heart of Glenside.  Fantastic location, deceptively large with multiple dining areas and a nice patio.  Staff was friendly and plentiful.  Two thumbs up for number of staff - even though it was busy, we never waited!  Other reviews mentioned the noise level being an issue, but I didn't notice it being any worse than your average bar.

Beer has potential and it seems like the owners are interested in varying the styles the brew and improving.  They don't quite have IPAs "down" yet - each of the three we ordered were a darker and heavier than expected, but the taste was fine.  My party thought the Helles lager was an A+, which was a little surprising since that style is traditionally more difficult to brew.  Clean, crisp, and refreshing.

Our food was overall tasty (love the pickled veggies on the nachos) but portion sizes were a little small.  (Sandwich was on your standard-size backyard BBQ burger bun, for instance, and definitely far from overflowing with pork.)  Will the brewery be offering discounts on appetizers and/or pints for happy hour?  I think it'd be a GREAT way to "snag" people getting off the train before they head home for the evening.  (The bars nearby do this.)

In summary - huge amount of potential; impressive space and location; food and beer are fine but could use a few tweaks.  I will certainly return to try a few more beers.
The new brewery in town has very good beer,  nice food selection, and very good service.

My husband and I have been looking forward to this brewpub opening for months and went last night (June 6) for the first time since their opening. We arrived around 8:15p and we were seated (sooner than expected) a few minutes later. That is where the prompt service ended. It took our server over 5 minutes to greet us and bring us a beer menu. We ordered both our beer and a few appetizers to share (smoked wings, veggie burger bites, and nachos) around 8:30p. Our beer came out shortly, but just after 9pm I flagged down our waiter to ask if our food would be coming out shortly. I know they just opened and when we first arrived they were pretty busy, but at the same time we only ordered appetizers which should be pretty easy to get out quickly. The waiter did not apologize for the wait, but instead said matter-of-factly that we are behind the 10-top so as soon as their order goes through then ours will come up, and then he walked away without asking if he can get us anything else. I understand that the 10-top ordered first and that their food should come out together, however, it was only two of us and we ordered appetizers. A well-functioning kitchen should be able to time food and serve people in a timely manner, and a well-trained server should be communicating with his tables. Shortly after this unapologetic explanation, we watched half of the 10-top walk out before their food arrived . Then, we saw all their food come out piecemeal, not altogether, and the remaining customers boxed up most of the food. The owner came out to their table and talked to them for a while and offered to do something to their bill. Meanwhile, we are still waiting without a word from our server. Just after 9:30p two of our three appetizers came out (six TINY wings at $11, and nachos). The server just dropped them at our table and rushed away. He didn’t say anything (e.g. hope you enjoy, sorry for the wait, do you need anything else) except that the burger bites are coming out shortly. He didn’t bring us small plates to eat off of, so we waited for several minutes until we saw another server near-by and asked for plates so we weren’t hovering over the dishes trying to share. When the burger bites came out around 9:40p, he said, “sorry for the wait, but hope it’s worth it” and rushes away again. Who says that? The whole experience was terrible, and although the food was good, and the beer was okay, it was not “worth it”. Eating appetizers at 9:40p, an hour and 25 minutes after we arrived, is not worth our time and the treatment we received.

To an extent, I can over-look a slow kitchen. I understand they just opened and clearly have kinks to work out to get people served in a timely manner and have food come out together. What I can’t over-look is the lack of communication by our server and also the owner who didn’t even care that there are other people waiting to be served as well. You can go anywhere (including several places in Glenside) to get good, unique food and craft or local beer; you choose to continue to go somewhere because of the service, and this was some of the worst I have ever experienced.

We will try to go again in a few months, hoping the process is more stream-lined and the staff is more attentive, and would love to write an updated, better review. As I said, we had been looking forward to coming here for months before they even opened. Hopefully all this gets worked out as they’re open longer and get more kitchen staff or server training or whatever they need to do in the meantime so other customers don’t have the same experience.

Onion rings were good but do they have to be a quarter pound a piece,  I love dipping oinion rings in a tasty sauce, but where was the sauce.  Finally what was really disappointing was what they called Pulled Pork Nachos. A large plate of plain nachos, with dry pulled pork thrown on top of them. I didn't have any food so can't in good conscience give higher than 4 stars.  I did have a few beers though and they were excellent.  The Iron Master's stout was a solid stout.  Smooth, dark, and sweet, with just the right amount of bitter.  Nice decor with ample seating at the bar and even a lounge area for a relaxed atmosphere.  Staff was very friendly, and very knowledgeable about their products.  I will be back.

So this was my first time here.  Very neat establishment.  I'm not a big drinker but I did find a good ale to enjoy. We ordered our food and the wings were done to our liking.  Nicely done. But when our burgers and pizza came that was a different story.  The white pizza would have been good I'm sure if it didn't have hot pepper seeds through out the cheese.  Which some may enjoy but when your not expecting it,  its not a sweet find.  The burger I ordered I asked for medium on the way I would like it done, but well is how it came out.  That being said it wasn't dry but its not what I asked for.  The pulled pork was good.
Now I know I didn't really go there for the food but it does count towards ones perspective.  They did take the pizza off the bill as the pepper seeds were not on the menu as being part of the ingredients.and we didn't eat it.
Thank you.
Great local brewery.  Food is very good but ordering process could be better.  Live entertainment is usually unique and often pretty good.
Great food and service! Try the wings and the Jolene pizza.

Cold fries, burned wings, I mean really? Also it took them forever to bring it out. We ordered a side of fries for our sons burned chicken wings and that didn't come out.... at all until after I complained.  I was really excited to go there and drove over 30 minutes to get there and planned our hike for the day so we could end up here. Not a pleasure experience.  Not going back. Perhaps from the time we ordered and the time it got to the chef was somehow obscured, we waited for hot food for 30 minutes we did get our "snack" within 15 minutes. And when we were offered a dessert it was not made clear what was happening, if it was coming out, if a menu was coming to us, or what was going on. So yes, we left after waiting yet again, with the bad taste of hastily made fries  and burnt wings on our unhappy tongues. I understand you tried to make it right, but it was just an all around unpleasant experience.
Great food. Nice open restaurant. Definitely some different craft beers available - one was a cucumber cream ale.
Great variety of their own beers. I tried the Irish Red. I enjoyed it very much. Also had the Western Burger. Cooked perfectly. Great happy hour prices.

EDIT: The food poisoning was confirmed in the hospital. My father is now home with meds recovering. Thanks for asking.

FOOD POISONING!? The atmosphere is fantastic, jazz for Father's Day Brunch was lovely; yet, my husband and I took my 78 year old father here to celebrate the day and he is now horribly sick. None of us really thought the food tasted very good at the time: with the exception of the great tasting chili and husband's beer, the Gretna Fries and side fries were seriously limp and greasy, the wings were burnt and dry, the few requests we made - salad dressing choice, no onions in fries came out wrong AND sadly, my father's pulled pork sandwich with pineapple has possibly caused him food poisoning. He has been ill with vomiting and diarrhea for the past 4 hours and was just taken to the hospital. If you go, I wouldn't suggest eating the food if something looks or tastes off. It's a shame... great atmosphere and beer, but even just based on the taste of the food, they really need to get their kitchen together. We won't be back.
Great ambience, the food is good, eapecially their brisket! The only think I would change is the service. You have to order in the bar, in order to get food. They also have a nice coffee shop.

I love going here! The staff is always friendly and the coffee and beer are always great. My only complaint is that it's always so cold inside, especially in the restrooms. Definitely bring a jacket to stay warm.

I hate to give only two stars because the beer selection has been pretty decent the 3-4 times I’ve been in. The only word for the service from the bar staff, however, is abysmal. Gotten the vibe a few times that the 30 seconds it takes to pour my beer is a total chore. Every time we go bar staff has been completely inattentive to us and pretty much everyone else there who isn’t another staff member. Even got to see a nice big eye-roll from a bartender after taking another guests order last time.  I’d love to say I’d give it another shot for the beer’s sake, but after 4 visits I know what kind of service to expect.

Father's day brunch turned into father's day dinner. Bar service only. We waited an hour and a half for 4 burgers and fries. There were several other families also waiting long periods of time for their food. Also they offer no diet drink options (my father is diabetic). When the burgers finally arrived they were thick burnt meatballs in oversized buns with burnt cheese and cold fries. The description of toppings did not match what we received. The server removed one of the burgers from our check and was very apologetic and nice. Oh and the bill was 120$ including 3 beers and a soda and also minus 1 of the burgers. It seems as thought they were not prepared for father's day weekend.

All in all the food was fair and we enjoyed ourselves.

I asked if my server was aware that we are waiting and they said they would send someone over.
