You could have the greatest food in the world but if the service is bad you are done. Unfortunately this place has poor service and the food is just as bad. As an appetizer I ordered the fried pickle spears. The batter fell off all of them and as I ate them I felt as if I was pouring salt in my mouth straight from the salt shaker. We also ordered the Brussel sprouts  and it was swimming in balsamic dressing.  I was very excited to see meatloaf on the menu. It was described as smoked and lathered in their BBQ sauce. The BBQ sauce looked like someone just plopped a tablespoon on sauce on tap as an afterthought and as I bit into the meatloaf it was so dry it was like eating cardboard both in texture and taste. My wife ordered the shrimp taco. It was described as “grilled shrimp”. The shrimp was not grilled and from my experience in a kitchen it was most definitely frozen shrimp and defrosted by running cold water over them and heated up in a microwave. The taco also had melted cheese and warm lettuce. So the shrimp was cold and the lettuce was warm. One bright spot is that their French fries were decent. I should have just ordered 3 large orders of fries put them in the middle of the table and squirt a mountain of ketchup in the center. I would have enjoyed that.

Not sure what all the good reviews are about on this one honestly. Beer was meh. Food was delivered to our table literally about 3 minutes after we ordered. I had the brisket sandwich and it was pretty small for $16.50 and the sauce tasted very very sweet and generic. I have no problem with the price, it was just very underwhelming. My wife ordered a well done burger and it came out medium rare but the staff corrected it quickly and she said it was good.

It was good food and the customer service was great. Piper was my server and she was awesome so shout out to her.    My bf got chicken n waffles and he was satisfied lol.  I’ll be back it was a chill vibe

Good pub food. Always crowded. Skiers, outlet shoppers, hipsters.

Response to owner.
Your right. Not always. 90% of the time. I am local and have been at your establishment many times. I simply made a review for others to judge. Not for you to judge me. It doesn't matter what people do for a living. By reading your other responses. Wicked busy is used a lot. Try saying something nice or not at all. That might be better.
Love this place. The only thing is, the wait time to get in was a little long, but, it was worth the wait. We will definitely go back again.

Great cozy brew pub. Very friendly staff. Felt real safe eating they followed really good safety and distance practice's. Nice selection of there own brewed beers and spirit's. Would definitely go back.
Great beer. A lively place. But it was too crowded under COVID-19, we left with our take out food and four pack of beer. Hope they keep social distancing

I took my 2 friends to dinner here 2  nights ago.super stoked to try their food (I’m a door dash driver and they usually smell amazing). We ordered a couple drinks and the fried pickles.. drinks were okay we sent one back and the fried pickles were great... then our food came, big daddy Mac and cheese, waffles and chicken and the chicken pretzel sandwich.... the noodles were PASTE and Wendy’s did a better job at the pretzel sandwich! The chicken and waffles looked frozen... nothing special here don’t waste your money... oh and they still made us pay.... on the bright side our waitress was great but we will not return

We ate in the restaurant yesterday for an early dinner and it was wonderful - 5 stars! My husband and I got the stuffed tots and beer cheese soup/french onion to start. Then he ordered wings and I had a burger. Everything was hot and tasty. Service was decent. They went all out on the Halloween decorations which was an experience in itself for my children!

Then today we decide to place an order to go since we enjoyed their food so much. We ordered fish and chips, a cheese steak, a cheese quesadilla, and a few drinks to go. Since we are staying at a hotel we specifically requested ketchup for our fries and tarter sauce for our fish. We made sure to tip well because we know packaging the food to-go is work too, so we were really disappointed to return the hotel to eat and find no ketchup, tarter, or plastic silverware. Food seemed completely different quality than the day before - more like 3 stars.

I'd definitely recommend this place if you're going to eat in because it's well worth it, but if you want to do carry-out you're best bet is to go elsewhere.

good beer, meh food. We loved the beer we ordered, but were very disappointed by food served almost cold. Tater tots were cold, even the hot chocolate was cold. The burger looked amazing on the menu, but was not as yummy as it sounded. It also tasted of lighter fluid or some other chemical from the barbecue. It was such a strong chemical flavor that I couldn’t finish the dish. The best thing on my plate was the pickle...The mac and cheese plate with shredded pork and chili was tasty, but again not served hot. Was very disappointed. If I were only rating the beer I would have given 5 stars, but the food was honestly such a huge disappointment. Would not recommend for dinner. Could be due to the pandemic situation, as we ate outside at night with some heaters. We didn’t mind, and even though we were eating outside all of our food came in containers so they shouldnt have gotten cold.

I'd pass on this place.

Multiple negatives, which by themselves can easily be easily overlooked. However, at some point you have enough and the experience is no longer enjoyable.

So many issues... I'll just bullet them:

-Taken to table then had to wait for it to be bussed.

-Waited ~20 minutes for server to acknowledge us.

-Ordered appetizers, only half the appetizers came out.

-One of our party's soup came out cold.

-Multiple signature drinks were missing ingredients listed on menu.

-Burger was soggy and inedible.

-Poboy was just buffalo sauce on a roll.

-Requested BBQ on mac&cheese and came without.
This place is very busy but worth it . The drinks were awesome. Food is delicious.

My friends and I decided to have lunch and we ended up having a horrible experience. Our food did not come out at the same time. My fiance found a hair on his plate when we sent it back they brought the steak out raw, they took the food and recooked the same steak when the steak was brought out the 3rd time it was cooked properly but we found another hair on his plate.  We informed the manager who said something to the wait staff who kept coming out of the kitchen to stare, laugh, and complain about us. I informed the manager Amy that we felt uncomfortable and she said "well what do you want me to do about it". I would not recommend this restaurant just because of hygiene and the manager being very unprofessional.

I wouldn’t even give it a 1 star but they are requiring me to choose!!

We visited here on Saturday of Columbus day weekend. It was just okay.

They were extremely busy and we had a reservation - without it we would have waited hours. The waitress and staff did a good job in trying to keep our party of 7 plus an infant happy, but the food was only average.

In my opinion, there is NO way this restaurant was complying with Pennsylvania's COVID restrictions of no more than 25 or 50% seating capacity - I saw about 4 booths/table that were empty out of about 20. That mean's they were about 75% full. We didn't feel unsafe, but it was the most crowded place we have been in about 6 months.

If you choose to go here, be sure you have a reservation and eat the pulled pork - everyone who got that was very happy.

I ate the meatloaf and it was disappointing: yellow/brown in color (looked more like toast, not meat?), it was not hot, so I asked for it to be re-heated and then it was piping hot but dry-as-a-bone. When I was asked, I told them as much and the waitress said, "ohh sorry." Everything else was pretty much fried/bar-food variety.

The beers were fine - nothing that was worth ordering a second or that I liked so mush that wanted I to take one home with me.
Everything bland. LITTERALLY no flavour. Steak is disgusting

Place is great. Service not so much. Unfortunately only game in "town". Waited 30 min for drink order and 1 solid hour for our apps. Dinner and apps arrived at same time and we still did not have silverware.  Had to walk around the place looking for clean knives and forks for our party of 6. Great location, but they NEED  to do better. Probably no local pressure from competition...creates sense of complacency.

Came here on a double date for Sunset Hill Shooting Range and came here for dinner and drinks afterwards. Neat restaurant/bar located near great wolf lodge and camelback resort. Food is mainly bar food and if you get a full course meal such as steak or chicken, it won’t even be like eating at a 3 star restaurant. As you can see in my photos, the meatloaf looks like it was cooked from frozen and simply thrown on the plate. The burgers, however, are above average with an abundance of options to choose from. Great beer selections, too!
Well the waiter was a little too serious but other than that the food was good and the beers were on point!! Great customer service orientation from beginning to end.

GenerAlly bad experience:
- food took a long time
- dirty cutlery
- most importantly the food was generally cold and tasted ok. Avocado toast seems to be from frozen paste.

We stopped by for breakfast this morning after viewing the menu and reviews online. We wanted a break from the resort breakfast, and the menu here seemed perfect for my wife Nd kids.

Now that we have eaten here, I must say I’m extremely disappointed. While the menu is creative and fun, it was so poorly executed it detracted from any positives the creativity may have added.

I ordered the fruit crepe with Nutella. The bananas were dry and browned, as if they had been cut last night and left in the walk-in overnight. The strawberries were mush, and there was barely any Nutella. The crepe was very dry, leading me to believe that like the bananas, it was made ahead of time and reheated.

One of my sons ordered French toast along with an egg plate, so I figured I would eat his French toast instead. The brioche was at least 4/5 days old. It had become crusty and stale, yet still used for French toast.

Overall - the execution was simply lazy. The ingredients weren’t fresh and it all felt like they just phoned it in.

The server wasn’t bad - but she too was just going through the motions, and seemed as if she really didn’t want to be there. Huge missed opportunity for her - my wife and I have both been servers in the past. If I leave 15% it’s only because the service was pretty bad. That’s what we left, normally it’s 40% or more, if they are really good.

Cliff notes version: The food is lazy, find somewhere else for breakfast.
Too loud.  Couldn't hear my friend when she spoke.  Music is one thing....blasting it is another.  Food was good.
Would recommend  the chicken parm pizza, had delicious sauce and motz. Their chocolate porter is also delicious!

A late Saturday lunch at the end of ski season meant we had a 30 minute wait (the estimate was accurate). Service started out quick, but slowed down towards the end of our meal (took quite a while for the check to come), but, as noted, they were busy. We ordered a flight of house beers - they were good, but with some room for improvement (a slight wateriness to some instead of a full-body), but we found ones that we liked. We ordered pub-style fair and thought it was quite good. I'd recommend the pub fair here - they do it well. The prices are resort-town prices - higher than average, but not drastically so for a brewpub. Overall I enjoyed the meal and would recommend.

Couldn’t have been more underwhelmed by the entire experience. The food was meh, the beer was barely meh and the service was not worth mentioning. My wife and I love to try new breweries when we travel and this specific experience was easily the worst we have had. It pained me to pay 76 dollars for 2 meals and 3 drinks.

Posted day of reply by Barley Creek:

Thank you for the opportunity to expand my vocabulary about the experience my wife and I had at your restaurant. Since a simple "meh" didn't suffice, let me expound further to explain our experience. The beer was subpar and basic, the meal was disappointing and regrettable not to mention extremely overpriced and the service was slow and frustrating. Our waitress seemed as if she was inconvenienced by our presence. I'm happy you've won so many awards. Maybe your next one will be on bragging about your success to a customer when they are simply making you and others who may have the similar tastes, aware of their negative experience at your establishment, after all that’s what reviews are for.
If you'd like even bigger words to describe the uncharacteristically poor quality of your food, service and beer, I suggest thesaurus.com under "bad".

Food is good and we had a great server.  Portions on some of the appetizers were a little small but other than that, the food was great and well worth the price.  A few in our party got desserts which were also delicious.

Horrible experience!!!! Reservations and call ahead seating was very unorganized and service was horrible. Im usually very patient and understanding with restaurants and wait staff, especially on a busy night. However,  the service was ridiculous!!!! 45 minutes to get a glass of water, over an hour for our food, which was cold, and not that great.
Excellent quality of CBs. Even canned beers are distinguished. So many people were there with little distancing.

Food was great the service was even better  if you are a draft beer fan they got you covered and have plenty of options of mixed drinks as well don't visit this area very often but I think this just became my go to spot when I do

Meh. Brisket was very good. Everything else just so so.  Kielbasa and kraut was overly salty.  Fried pickles, where to begin as I will consider myself a connoisseur, were odd in both their small size and taste.  Whatever the batter / seasoning, it overpowered the pickle and the pickle lacked that deep deli flavor.  Burger was average.  Overall a bit overpriced for the quality of the food.

As for the location, it's right in the middle of a busy intersection.  While some of the views are ok, it's really not anything to go out of your way for.  The outside area is large though and that was a plus.  The inside reminds me of a PA ski area with large signs hanging from the ceiling, almost like an amusement park.  Dust and webs on most of the overhead items.
Overall decor a bit dated and in need of some TLC.
Food is delicious. Came for dinner and breakfast, wasn't too impressed with their beers and bloody Mary's. A tad pricey.

Positive: Best wings we have had for a LONG time. Heavy steak and cheese sub, Caesar salad was coated just right. French onion soup was wonderful. Ate at the bar, the bartenders worked together to help all the customers.
Negative:   1) Was concerned one waitress had a face shield on, without a mask...as a nurse, we know that doesn't help protect others. Shields should be worn with mask, to protect the one wearing it. Put a mask on.. 2) The bathrooms were filthy, and 2 of 3 stalls were not functioning.

Our experience was really pretty meh. The service was great, particularly considering COVID but the ambience was so bad. We sat outside and they put us in a covered tent with walls rather than the uncovered patio where seats were available. I explained that the covered tent didn’t make me feel comfortable with the current situation and the hostess said she could let us know if something opened up. She didn’t and we saw several people be seated there right after us (before they’d even taken our order). It would have been a much better experience and made me feel better about eating out during our vacation.

My husband and I were staying at Mt Airy and asked for suggestions for the best local brewery, and were told Barley Creek by far.However, the food and the service were top notch, it was overshadowed by the Chuck E Cheese atmospere. There were a few kids at a table that were at the highest volume possible and the adults at their table didn't seem to care. Nor did Barley Creek. Then, just as our food arrived the wait staff were scrambling to set up a table for 10 with mostly kids. As the hostess slammed the extra chairs down, we knew it was time to get the check and a to go container for our food. What could have been a nice afternoon with some drinks and food turned into a stressful listen to unruly kids experience.  While I understand that everyone enjoys good food, it was disappointing that a brewing company had that type of atmosphere.
Food and service was very good.Premises need a good cleaning and refresh

I would not recommend getting carry out from here (perhaps eating in is better?). As we drove away, we realized they didn’t put in the beer we purchased. We got home with the food and realized we were missing the sides to our entrees as well. And the meatloaf and ribs were both very dry. While the staff seems very helpful, we found our experience to be disappointing
U can sit and have a  quick bite across the way from the mall food court

Bad experience. We waited 20 mins to be seated at the back area which had a very heavy odor. It was close to the kitchen door. I could see the kitchen situation from that door and i have never seen something so dirty. I couldn’t stay any longer especially after having seen that and decided to leave.
The women that greeted us was amazing, very personable. The burger my husband ordered was great but the mango salmon that i order was bland, it was NOT good at all, I do not recommend it.
Our first time, food was ok.

My wife and I attended this spot for our 4th anniversary and while appearance tended to show improvements from years past dining out, there were still some disappointments.

Upon entering, we waited several minutes to be seated, which wasn’t bad. However it appeared someone may have been sitting at the hostess stand the entire time from when we arrived, as after someone came over to help us they seemed a bit surprised we were there.

Seating was fine with no issues. We ordered the Dill Pickle Fries as an appetizer and would rate such as very good.

We did seem to wait a bit in between placing the appetizer and having our main order taken. Our initial impression when ordering the entree made it seem we were only there for the appetizer.

For entrees my wife ordered the Pulled Pork sandwich with fries and such was rated very good. However my course came with some hurdles. The house salad was good, along with the one side of baked beans I ordered. The main dish was the Flat Iron steak and was requested medium rare. However, when brought out and cut in half there were no juices and the meat appeared more dark resembling raw meat vs. rare meat. The waitress was good in requesting a replacement which was better, having juices immediately when cut into, but still had several small center pieces that appeared undercooked. The vegetable side of broccoli was also not hot.

Upon requesting the check I had inquired about purchasing beer for take home and the waitress had to check on availability. Upon return she had passed our table to go to another and then almost went back to the host stand before turning around to say by the way we do have them.

All in all the environment was good and majority of the food decent for the pricing. However small mistakes often lead to mixed results when leaving a review.
A big crowded but nice Guess that's what you have when the place is good. Food was good also

The hostess at the door was telling me to go to the next restaurant because I didn’t want to sit by the sun.... shame I didn’t get to try the food..

Reply to the responder:

Clearly this is not a governor’s problem. This is a restaurant’s and management’s problem. There were lots of empty tables by the tent, mostly booths, which “weren’t available”. And if it was spacing problems, the tables were packed up close to each other... don’t know what was the deal with the booths. Thank you for your amazing response; it clearly shows how valuable are customers acquisition to this restaurant.
Enjoyed their whiskey! Also, great brews & food. Wonderful atmosphere
So great. Good food. Fairly priced. Wonderful staff.

Never been to this spot til today. It's like 3mins down the road from the outlets. It is a very busy restaurant. It was very packed andthe food was really good. Would like to go here again in the summer to do the beer tasting. The hard cider was really good. The food prices are decent but the drinks are a little pricey but I guess because of the area. Seems to be like a tourist kind of area as well. I would definitely go again with my family. Only thing I could say I didnt like was waiting for a drink at the bar the service wasnt so good. Took them a while to acknowledge me while I was waiting to be seated. I did like that waitresses did come and keep checking on to see how everything was going and to see if we needed any refills on our drinks. Also that there was no soap in the bathrooms that I had to let one of the workers know in both men and womens bathroom.
Great food! Awesome staff!

Okay let’s do this. Love the food an atmosphere at this place. However service and management were severely lacking last night. Sides came out cold, had to send back, didn’t see new sides till my girlfriends entree was basically finished, disappointing for the money we spent. No one came to check on how things were for a good while. Love barley creek even bought a hoodie, however not a great experience.
A bit pricey place. Portions are medium. Customer service is nice.

Very disappointed.
We made reservations 3 weeks ago for 12 people. Well, when we got there 30 minutes early they told us we had to split our group because we were too many to sit together. Okay, we thought its covid time. Well, when we got seated after waiting 30 min (which is totally ok) they sat us at two complete different tables across the room. In front of us was a group of 15 kids celebrating a birthday.
After asking the manager how come these people sat together and are 15 people and we had to slipt and are only 12 people, he couldn't come up with an explanation.  Instead of apologizing and perhaps bring us an appetizers for free, he argued with us like a 3 year old. Not how you treat costumers! Admit you made a mistake. Never, not even in covid times, was a group that comes together to a restaurant separated. We made this reservations 3 weeks ago and ended up very disappointed.  We won't be back!

My first time here, Sat down in a booth with my family by the window, ordered my food and look over to see an inch of dust on the ledge, the wall is covered with yuck! Spider webs from the ledge to the window And the booth I'm sitting in has dust all over the back where my hair is. I bring it to the managers attention  And he said I cant clean it now it will make it worse and left it at that. No I'm sorry nothing. Absolutely disappointed in the cleanliness of this restaurant! And there customer service  I wouldnt recommend it

Great atmosphere and friendly servers. I had the porter which was good. The French onion soup was awesome. The ribs.... not so much. They were very greasy and tasted more like pork chops then ribs. Otherwise, other food that I've had there in the past was really good.

Great brew pub with their own beers and guest taps. The food is very good and offers something for everyone. The beer is OK but plenty of styles to select from. The atmosphere is great even a game room downstairs for kids. A great place for family.
Food n service was good.

Waiter was slow didn’t get my drink until after my food. Mashed potatoes and corn bread cold with my meal. No one checked in to make sure everything was okay when I got my new order. Waiter would disappear and would t come back for a long time. Not the best, there are other places around that are better by far . Horrible service
What a great place. Service was amazing and our waiter Mike was amazing .highly recommended

Was told food was ready. Waited 20 more minutes for them to bring it. Then ate a cold hamburger. Fries were good. But we asked for napkins and sauce and never got either. It was super busy thou. Over 50 cars so I'll try again.

The dumplings and pretzel nuggz were great. However, the blatant lackadaisical mask policy was horrible. We watching not 1, not 2 but 5 adults walk around and talk to staff without masks. No one batted an eye. Heads up for anyone trying to avoid potential COVID exposure.
Nice outdoor area to eat. Food and service where ok nothing great.
Went in around 2 in the afternoon, the wait was really long and service was poor. Also dirty silverware. A shame because the food was actually good.

1 hour wait when there was 7 people in there.. don’t worry I’ll make sure myself and all our friends never support your restaurant again. Absolutely absurd yet there wasn’t even 50% of your max capacity let alone maybe 25%. Oh well I vented and I’ll spread the word to never visit this establishment.

Foods a bit overpriced. The penne was recommended and it didn’t have much taste. A large group of us ordered flights of beers and after tasting most of them they all tasted similar. Beer was not good at all.
The atmosphere is great and fits the area.
Service was good but food and beer were definitely lacking.

When I'm in the area, I make sure I visit Barley Creek. They have some good beers of multiple varieties so you can find something to your taste. They also have some pretty good specials depending on the day of the week. Check their website to see which night is which. I usually go for $5 pizza and $5 off growlers on Wednesday. (The $1 each toppings can pile up though) Their other food I've also tried is also good. Service is friendly, and if you enjoy decorations, make sure you head there in October! I'd recommend checking it out.
Great that they're open with outside seating.   Had to wait for my wife's meal until we left.  By then my food was cold and it was the wind order.   No help from management.

The food was tasty and the service is polite. Took a bit of time to get served our orders, but food was worth the wait. My only complaint is the menus were dirty. Just takes a rag, some water and disinfectant/cleaner. Just makes you think of the cleanliness of the entire establishment if the menus are dirty. The price is fair, especially on the breakfast specials.The waitress saved the day with her happy morning attitude. 3 stars for taste, waitress & price. Clean the place up and get rid of the Christmas decorations already.
Very poor service long waiting time and when I asked the manager why I had to wait over a hour for my food he says he don’t know he can’t do anything about that the service and management is horrible
Food is great, and they have a kids menu, great service too!
Great local brewery with good pub menu and fantastic outdoor venue; we go early for quick service, but always good even when more crowded later in the day
We were here a year ago and it was much better. They’re doing a good job during this pandemic but the service was very very slow.
Very poor service long waiting time and when I asked the manager why I had to wait over a hour for my food he says he don’t know he can’t do anything about that the service and management is horrible
Food is great, and they have a kids menu, great service too!
Great local brewery with good pub menu and fantastic outdoor venue; we go early for quick service, but always good even when more crowded later in the day
We were here a year ago and it was much better. They’re doing a good job during this pandemic but the service was very very slow.

Great service as always ive been there dozens of times and have always loved the place. But I won't be coming back. I had the misfortune of sitting across from the pizza station and what I witnessed was horrendous. The person making the pizza traveling back and forth from the kitchen to the pizza station hugging other workers and shaking hands with people all while wearing the same black gloves he kept on while he made numerous pizzas. Even witnesses a supervisor approach him to admonish him about a mess he left the day before but never training him on the proper use of gloves during this Covid-19 pandemic. Im mortified and hope this callous disregard for safety does not cause any one to fall ill or worse .

I'm not sure. Chicken wings with the buffalo sauce were good. French fries were good, but the signature Mac n cheese dish my husband got was so bland it was a big disappointment. It had zero flavor. Maybe they forgot the cheese sauce because it was also really dry. French onion soup was nothing crazy. Had a strange sweet oregano flavor. Seemed like an overpriced lunch. Nothing special.

Unfortunately we invited out of town guests to experience the barbecue which was sold out at 5:00 pm. The gyro, salad & burgers were average at best & the ribs had to be returned. They were out of their triple IPA & their other mixed drinks were flavorless with minimal if any alcohol. A beige colored orange creamsicle, a banana flavored pina colada were just bad. The waitress was very kind and very apologetic but it was an embarrassing Father’s Day to say the least.
Beautiful day and the service was terrible. Our appetizer and food was delivered before receiving a beer flight or any type of drink. Very disappointing.

It was just ok. Not sure why the reviews are so great. I think it's because the place itself is very nice looking - warm and cozy and well-decorated. The beer is mostly ok to good. Specifically, we found the two regular IPAs only ok. The Double IPA, High Tide Lager, and the brown ale are good. Winter Fest Lager was excellent. The service and the food, on the other hand, was sub-par. Most of the food was lacking flavor, and it took forever to get to the table. When it did, most was only room temperature. Often we had to ask for things twice before we got them. Overall, wouldn't go back unless I ran out of other options to try. (Note: we are first-time, out-of-towners)
Been here a couple times with the family. In summary...good food, friendly service and great selection of brews! Looking forward to my next visit!

Extremely below average, pulled pork was so underwhelming. Zero flavor so I’m guessing they must use a store-bought bbq sauce; if they make their own then it’s just not my style and maybe you’d like it, but their menu claimed they had the best in many different states. Maybe they had a bad batch that day. Fries were soggy and had no seasoning, not even salt. They put my girlfriends French onion soup in a to-go coffee cup and didn’t provide a spoon, it was really great for her to eat her soup with a fork since we drove twenty minutes to get there. Half of her crab cake sandwich was lettuce. Even the pickles were terrible. If this was less expensive I would be inclined to give it 3 stars but it costs $40 for two sandwiches. I’m not saying you shouldn’t check this place out if you’re hungry, just don’t expect much in terms of quality of food. Interior was nice and welcoming and I’m sure a great place to grab a drink. Prices are ridiculous though

Our experience here was ok. For the prices, the food wasn't amazing but the greater issue was that things were forgotten (sides, sauces, toppings) on 4/5 of our meals. The staff fixed everything but it was a bit disappointing. The staff and manager were super nice and helpful!

Probably will NEVER go back to this restaurant!! This was our 2nd visit there and we're so disgusted!! The restrooms looked like they hadn't been cleaned in months!!!! They were FILTHY!!!!!! We can only imagine what the kitchen looks like!! Such a shame because this has the potential to be a nice place... GET YOUR ACT TOGETHER!!!

This place does not deserve even one star. We waited 45 mns for a table for them to put us somewhere filled with bugs all over the chairs and tables! When i finally said i wanna to be moved, they refused to sit us anywhere else and said we have to wait in line again. They were rude and not cooperating!!!
Great service, great beer.....
The food is overpriced, and not good. The service is decent, and the establishment looks very nice.
Food was decent, would visit again.

Great beers but totally mediocre food and service. Even the kids left food on their plates. Probably would not return. Most of our food showed up a half an hour after ordering and was obviously left under a heat lamp (even the salad was warm).

We went last Friday Feb. 21st it was crowded but we were seated right away. Our Waiter was awesome and took great care of us. There were 3 in our party 2 of us had burgers and the other had shrimp tacos. Our food was hot cooked as ordered, the only complaint we had was the sweet potato fries were cold , our waiter got us a second order.  Those were hot but well over cooked and greasy and un edible  that is the only reason I gave 4 stars. I would recommend to others and would also return for another meal. But I think I would do so on another night other then Friday or Saturday because of the crowd and it being very loude.

4.24.20
Just an update to my review with this world the waynitnis right now the family wanted some restaurant food/wings. We ordered wings last week with door dash and it was awesome . We also ordered a full meal tonight burgers, pulled pork,chicken sandwich and wings . The meal was awesome and deliveru was prompt even for a Friday night. Thank you for being open and serving !!! Please support your local restaurants they need us so they survive !!!

great environment and always a good selection of beer. The last few times i've eaten here I wasn't too happy with the burgers and steaks hence the 4 star rating but the wings are always perfect. I'd love to explore more food items but I'm a little nervous about that given the quality of the red meats. But i've been a customer for over 10 years and I'll continue to be a customer. Thanks

First time here.  We were told a half hour wait, but it was a 50 minute wait. The place was packed. When we were seated we had to share a menu as the hostess stand was out. Our waiter waited on the table sat after us before finally taking our order. My husband wanted the meatloaf sandwich but they were out of meatloaf, he asked if he could get Cole slaw with the pulled pork sandwich,  that was a no, he finally ended up ordering the bratwurst which was good but too much roll. I wanted the empanada but that's only available after 4, it was 3:30. I ended up with the mac and cheese , it was dry noodles with shredded cheese tossed on top, definitely missing the 5 cheese sauce and the breadcrumb topping. Apparently the mac and cheese came with a salad which was never came. When I mentioned it to the waiter he asked it I wanted it to go, I did, but forgot it there anyway. Maybe we'll try again during a slower time? The app was good (pretzel) the drinks were good and the dessert was great, beyond that it was very disappointing.

Spent 3 hours here for a group of 8, half kids half adults. The service was nice but very slow. The Harvest salad is healthy and vegan or added protein, but pretty much just a Trader Joe’s bag of shredded veggie mix with some apple slices. Sweet potatoe fries were not good. Meatloaf was good as were the beef tacos.

A local who has been frequenting this place for 5+ years and I am disappointed in the quality lately. Mushy sweet potatoe fries, drinks served in different glasses each order for same drink, waitress with a dont care attitude  ... very disappointing.
Good atmosphere but the food wasnt very good.

Soo disappointed by our experience at the pint sized park today! The lack of service and the inexperience of the bar and wait staff was unbelievable. We waited 15 minutes for a server before walking to the bar ourselves in order to order drinks to only wait another 10+ to be acknowledged let alone served... Mind you the outside area was only a quarter filled at this point. The wait staff seemed to have some type of "your next " type of selection for who your server would be which was obviously extremely flawed. In no world should it take 30 minutes to place a drink order. Our first experience at Barley Creek last year was fantastic, your female bartenders on Saturday were amazing! Unfortunately we will be taking our business elsewhere based on today's service :-/

We enjoyed everything.  Liked the brussel sprouts appetizer and the buffalo wings.  The pizza crust was great but the cheese could have been melted more.  The beer selection was good as well.  Friendly staff and nice atmosphere.

Good citrus IPA.

Good cider.

Good food. We split the veggie pasta dish. Loaded with sundried tomatoes.  Was good and too big for a normal person anyway.

Good service.
Good beer selection.

Nice place. Decent bar and good food. Service was very good and prices are good. Met up with some old friends and we sat for 4 hours talking and the staff was always attentive. I'll be back when in town again.

I went here a year ago with my husband. We came in and waited about 45 minutes and no one even came to get our drink order. So we ended up walking out disappointed and going somewhere else. It was a busy night so we decided to try it out again on my birthday for breakfast. Wasnt nearly as busy and we waited about an hour and a half for our food. You wouldnt even think we had a server because she never checked in on us and reassured us that our food was running behind. Food came out hot and good finally. The beer I had was incredible, probably one of the best pumpkin beers I ever had. Was just really disappointed on the wait time and service.

Do not waste your money or time. This place is extremely overpriced and has horrible food.  I ordered a southwestern salad with grilled chicken. The salad came out, but no chicken. They took my silverware away and I had no knife to cut it.  Took a ridiculously long amount of time for a waitress to come back and forth. Then, there was no fire roasted corn,  which was one of the main ingredients I was looking forward to  Scratch that, there was one kernel.  This was basically lettuce  with a scrawny piece of dried chicken That came after the fact. Simply inedible, very poor service, and extremely overpriced.

Delicius food country breakfast. I stop for breakfast 3 weeks ago. The food was delicious. I tried the South west Fritata which call my attention from the menu. You can go on line and check the menu pictures.
Food is pretty good.  They keep a good mix of things on the menu.  Beer is ok.  They have a pretty good variety for those who want a brewery flight.  Service is great.

Staff forgot about us after being seated. After a server stopped by and took our order, I ordered the short rib tacos. They were just shredded short ribs and onions on a bed of lettuce with shredded mild cheddar cheese, served with a side of what I'm 99.9% sure were original Ruffles chips straight from the bag. The tacos were really bland and not worth the price. The beer was good though.

Pretty average tonight. They have a good menu so there's a good variety to choose from. I had the New York strip steak which arrived only lukewarm. I like my dinner hot. Overall the dinner not bad but certainly nothing special. Will my wife and I go here again? Probably but we will take a break for a while. If you go there expect it to be quite noisy as it was tonight. It was a little too much for me at times

Beer was good! Service was so so.  The menu was priced entirely too high.  I have lived in major cities most of my life and have paid major city prices for food......the menu prices here were on par with those of much bigger places and it was not merited.  I also used to be a chef in DC, I know what food cost is....Come on, a burger for almost $20.

Overall not bad but not great
Great food. Meatloaf was soooooo tender and had a great taste.

This really is a great place and quite an experience.  Halloween decorations galore when we went.  We were seated very quickly and our food was very good.  Our waiter was a bit odd.  It seemed that once he realized we weren't there for beer, he was sort of hurrying us along.  We weren't "camping out" and the place wasn't very busy at 4, but it just felt rushed for no reason.  It wouldn't stop us from coming back though.

Decent food and drinks. Nothing to brag about. They go all out to decorate for Halloween and Christmas, if you enjoy a festive atmosphere. I'm never longing to return. I have been to breakfast once and that was delicious. I nice break from the plethora of diners in the area.

We would go mainly for their specials like steak night, burger night, etc which are only advertised on the website. You have to ask about the specials from the waiter. They don't tell you about them otherwise and there is nothing on the menue about them. When we last went, we asked what the special burger was for that day. The waitress told us it was a southwestern burger. We asked her to describe it. She said it had spicy mayo, lettuce, tomato, and other things you'd find in a typical southwestern meal. So me and my party are thinking there could be pico de gallo, guacamole or avocado, etc. The burgers come out and they are literally a patty with bacon, a thin layer of mayo, in between heavily buttered bread. We ask where the tomatoes and lettuce are. The waitress was very nice and accommodating. She brought the things out on a separate plate for us to add. While this helped, it was one of the greasiest burgers  we'd ever had. My stomach was upset after eating half of it. The only southwestern aspect was the spicy mayo which was hard to even taste due to it being so sparsely added to the burger and the overpowering grease. My husband told the waitress he wasn't satisfied and he was able to order something else. Because they only advertise the specials online, I'd guess there isn't much forethought in what kind of burger the cooks will make and relaying that info to the waiters. This experience killed burger night for us. The pros were our accommodating waitress, the fries, and the christmas decorations. The reason we came, our entree, the burger, was a huge con.

Update 15JUN18:
They've updated their menu and the new nachos are AWESOME.

Original:

They give you a heaping pile of nachos for very fair price. It can easily feed 3 people on it's own.

That said, they are rather dry, you definitely need the sauces and such they provide with it.

The first time we went the chicken on top of our nachos was diced up in very small pieces, we realized that was to hide that the chicken was rather burned. The second time we went and got the nachos, we got nice large chunk of grilled chicken breast cut into long strips, which only reinforces the idea they were trying to hide that it was burned.

The staff are inconsistent in wither they will give you replacements of the very small containers of cheese and salsa they give you with the nachos, some will offer it, some will not.

The mac & cheese is overly rich to the point where we didn't want to eat it. Definitely a miss with them.

Drinks are okay, they are deceptively weak tasting due to the volume in the glasses they give.

Nice ambiance,  food was fine , but price of liquor, wine and beer was outrageous!!
I pay $11.00 for a bottle of kendall jackson  and they charge $12 for a glass.  Not fair.
No difference with bottle of domestic beer.
And $10 for a bloody Mary,  ouch!
Gave waiter 10$ cash tip on 57$ bill. Then payed with credit card. They automatically took out another 18% on my card without notice.

All of the burgers were overcooked, the salads were wilted, the Avacado was unripe and, the food was obviously sitting under the heating lamp because the chips were hot. But, the restaurant was nice looking.
Pamela is a good server! Food is okay maybe because it’s the end of the shift and the kitchen is ready to go! But overall Barley Creek is good just go wayyyyy before closing.

This place used to be great. Now I can honestly say the food is sub par. The service is sub par. But the drinks are good and I don’t drink a lot if at all. Hopefully my last few experiences were flukes but that’s not the word around town
Very good fish and chips.

Food was really good, had the turkey avacado blt. Service was very slow, waitress hardly checked on us and we waited a long time to order, as we kept seeing employee after employee walk past us.  Nice atmosphere otherwise.

Our waitress, Kelli, was personable and attentive. Unfortunately, she was the only good thing about our visit. My wife's T-bone was not cooked as ordered and was tough to chew. I ordered the charcuterie platter, which was a totally uninteresting selection of cheese and two really small portions of prosciutto and sausage. My friend's chicken and broccoli was ice cold. Overall, high expectations were dashed!! We left sadly disappointed...

Nice Beer, Awful Food. Came here for a late breakfast. Ordered eggs Benedict florentine. I expected soft poached eggs, but yokes were hard. Guess I should have specified soft yokes but I'm not sure that would have mattered. Came with fried potatoes that were the worst I've ever experienced. 3 other adult meals in our group, all of them very bad.
Wait staff was very nice. Beer is good. Go there to drink, not eat.

This was our first time visiting here and our last.  Service was slow, it wasnt busy. I ordered a salad that was full of things like brussel sprouts, walnuts, etc. It was supposed to be served with some kind pumpkin "goddess" dressing of which they were out of. Turns out, the salad I paid $9 for was the same bagged salad I buy at home at the grocery store. A lot of the salad was browning.  My boyfriend got the appetizer of fries with gravy and meat. I dont recall the name; its french. That was really good and the portion was good sized.
Drinks (water) took a long time to come out. I tried their wheat beer; just a taste.  It was watery and not good.
My bf had a sangria which was basically red wine with muddled blueberries and mint. Not truly a sangria and the flavor was mediocre and overpriced.
If you want slow service and overpriced mediocre food, go here.  Otherwise, I'd find somewhere better.
While part of the responsibility is on the customer to speak up if something isn't right, that would mean a waitress would need to be available.
Great beer, good food, good servive. Their in house brewed beers are all excellent. Great atmosphere.

My favorite restaurant for fueling back up after a day on the slopes at Camelback or shopping at the outlets has gotten bigger and better. The wait can still be a bit long on winter weekends for dinner, but it’s worth it. The beer here is pretty great considering you’ll never find it elsewhere. They brew plenty of styles in house and they are actually pretty great. The black IPA and winter lager are personal favorites. Pro tip: hit the onyx bar in the back to avoid the lines and crowd of the main bar in front. The food is no slouch either. The burgers are very well seasoned and cooked to perfection with lots of choices of toppings. Even the fries and onion rings are good. It’s the place to go after an exhausting day on the slopes.

This place has a very nice atmosphere and the menu is big and food is pretty good.  The  beer and drink options are great.  The service is slow and lacking but if you are not in a hurry and have time to relax it is a great place to try.

The beer selection is great as well as the staff. I love that the place is always decked out for the holidays (The Halloween display was awesome!) The food could be better, it's pretty average. The burgers are probably the safest bet.
Food was really good. Had smoked meatloaf, really enjoyed it. Short Stack Stout was good, but lacked something. Super Hop Triple IPA was really good.

Beer and cocktails were great. Beer Bite Crab Dip was seriously fantastic. Only complaint is that our entrees(Pocono Po Boy and Smoked Prime Rib Melt)were maybe a little blander than we were hoping for after how flavorful the appetizer was, but the service was fast and friendly, which made for a pleasant dining experience overall.
Prices are outrageous for the portion sizes.  Nice place good service just pricey. Don't know if I'll return. A burger is a little under 20 bucks, a burger!!
Excellent brewery/ restaraunt combo. Got 2 flights all beers were good and tasted according to description. Food was good. Prices were a tad on the high side but not unexpected near the ski resort.

Not impressed. Got the meatloaf, tasted like pure wood. My wife got the crab cakes, she said crab was bad. Our friends got the Vodka Pie & Shepards pie. Vodka pie had too much parm cheese on it. Shepards pie no flavor. This is the third time here, the first time was too busy, second we were told 10 mins for a table of 6, 40 mins later we get we don’t know how long it will be.

The menu sounds so good so we tried it. The beer was decent but the food was not good.  Bagged lettuce, ruffle bag chips, flavorless meat, Not impressed.  Also, although the staff is friendly,  they are also flirty... with your husband... I don't think I'll go back
Food was good but the service sucked.
Not the best product for the money. If you stay at camel back they opened up a couple of really nice resturants that aren’t too expensive. An definetly have this place beat on quality
Good food

First off I never write reviews but was totalled turned off. Been here multiple times. Food is great.  Usually good experience.  Went twice in the same week and the 2nd time was for my daughters 8th birthday.  The place had decent amount of people but in no way slammed.  Gave the server a candle with #8 on it, asked to put it in chocolate ice cream and have workers please sing for her.  She comes back with ice cream with candle lit saying sorry we are too busy to sing.  Are you serious? It takes 20 seconds to sing.  I talk to manager and her response is sorry I'll talk to the server.  So my daughter gets a birthday song from us( just family) which we could have done at home or at hotel room and got the same experience and your resolution is I'll talk to server.  How bout heres a $5 gift card at the bare minimum?  I never write reviews but since this ruined the end of her birthday night so I had to.  Sadly will be looking for new restaurant when we visit next.
Pleasant service and good American food.
The food is just ok.
Really good food and excellent beer selection...
Brought a group to the brewery today. Went zipping at camelback and here for lunch. All 13 of us agree our food and drinks were very good!

My drink tasted like Kool aid, my empanada was burned, my friends post roast was tough, the noise level was ridiculous , and the workers making the pizza  tossing the dough in the air hitting the vent awning was disgusting and then using it to make the pizza! How gross!

I was creeped out when I went into the bathroom to wash my hands. The bathroom looked and smelled like it hadn't been cleaned in years.  The toilet seat was beyond disgusting! If the bathrooms (something the public can clearly see) aren't clean, then what is the rest of the place that the public can't see look like? The beer was terrible. The food was ok. Our server was very friendly, but didn't bother to take away any plates before or after dropping off the bill. I'll be steering clear of this place. Please clean your toilet seats top and bottom! ABSOLUTELY DISGUSTING!
Great place! Atmosphere blended with the landscape around, food was excellent and it was only a mile and 1/2 from the outlet stores.
Great food location and service
Food is terrible if you must go stop at any gas station first and eat any food you can find. The beer is average at best. You can get better beer at beer distributor. Tourist Trap!!!!
Great food. Great service. Will come again. Thanks
Great food and Drinks as Usual but Bar Service is Absolutely Horrible. Barely anyone here and takes 20min to even talk to anyone ea time You want something.

Start off with no one serving our table. Ordered a steak and it looked terrible, definitely did not want to eat it. Sent it back to get a burger instead, order medium well and there was a pool of blood on the plate. Would give 0 stars.
Food is alright drinks are very expensive and I only had Miller lite employees very nice and I think the food is a little over priced. I guess us locals shouldn't go there.
Nice atmosphere, have to go back to try Brew s from brewery here breakfast time.

Very friendly wait staff, but the service, beer, and food were mediocre.  I had the Irish red and the brown ale, neither of them were to style, the irish red was very turbid, and looked like it was from the bottom of the keg.  The brown ale was just "meh".  The tuna avocado sandwich was underwhelming.  My wife had the turkey sandwich which tasted more like pastrami, which i suppose could be worse.  The only good thing we ate was the loaded potato poppers (I think thats what they were called).  It took FOREVER for the waitress to take our beer order, and just as long for her to bring it back to us, and there wasn't even alot of people there when we were.
Good beer (love the pumpkin), food over salted.

Went there thinking they serving brunch, it was noon, but they don't. The bartendar, who was tending out table, made sure to let us know they weren't serving breakfast. We left! Ended up at Huddle House, which is a Denny's style restaurant a short distance away

Innovative yet comfort-food like menu choices at reasonable prices and everything we ordered was delicious!Their microbrews are fantastic, they offer a flight and you get a commemorative glass with it.  Great place for a family outing or to just hang out with friends.
Love the food and service here,  usually give 5 stars but there was a little too much fat on my pot roast.

Great: onion soup. Delicious melted cheese on top.

Terrible: Shrimp tacos. As a taco lover and latina, I can assure you that the choice of putting Wasabi sause on top is terrible. Sadly this is not clear in the menu so you might get a horrible surprise. I didn't eat even one and I had to pay $14 🤮🤮🤮

We went for lunch today - 5 adults and 5 kids. I was really looking forward to the visit after reading the other reviews but, sadly, we were very disappointed. All 5 of the adults’ beers were flat and they were 3 different kinds of beers. The waitress said the one keg was just tapped, which couldn’t have been the case. Thinking it may be the BC beer that was the issue, we ordered a couple of the guest beers. Also flat - almost no carbonation. We mentioned to the waitress that the beer didn’t seem right. Her response was “those are the beers you ordered”. It took over 30 mins for our food order to be taken so the little kids were getting hangry. The kids’ mac and cheese looked like it was sitting out for hours - dry and stuck together. The pulled pork was dry, the steak on the salad and on my sandwich was overcooked. On a positive note, the house made tator tots and shrimp tacos were good.
Good options for vegetarians and a great gluten free menu!
very clean, very friendly environment great service good food highly recommend

Beers tasted thin to me, not impressed. But the reason I wont go back is because this is a brewery with no beer menu. I had to stand up in the middle of the restaurant hovering over another table to look at the TVs.  I drink a lot of beer and a chalkboard at the bar is one thing,  but in a restaurant environment I want to sit,  relax,  and read about the beers as I choose.  It's it really that expensive to print out a couple beer menus, lol.  The food was also a 3 out of five.

Beers tasted thin to me, not impressed. But the reason I wont go back is because this is a brewery with no beer menu. I had to stand up in the middle of the restaurant hovering over another table to look at the TVs.  I drink a lot of beer and a chalkboard at the bar is one thing,  but in a restaurant environment I want to sit,  relax,  and read about the beers as I choose.  It's it really that expensive to print out a couple beer menus, lol.  The food was also a 3 out of five.
Great food and reasonably priced.  Family friendly.  A bit loud, but a really fun place
Good options for vegetarians and a great gluten free menu!
Good beer (love the pumpkin), food over salted.
Had the bang bang shrimp taco daily special. Definitely enjoyed it.  Side note, no bathroom inside only a Porta Potty and it's cash only.

Went there thinking they serving brunch, it was noon, but they don't. The bartendar, who was tending out table, made sure to let us know they weren't serving breakfast. We left! Ended up at Huddle House, which is a Denny's style restaurant a short distance away

Used to be good but not anymore. I used visit approx once a month. Over the last 3 or so months the service and the quality of food isn’t what it used to be. Let me break down my last visit. My wife and I and her parents ordered 20 wings and nachos for apps. Out of the 20 wings there was 18 legs and 2 wings. The nachos arrived with a hair in the sour cream (a long straight brown hair) which I noticed after right away and the server apologized and said she would have them put in another one. Fast forward 10-15minutes; the server brought out our new nachos and apologized again.  The nachos were burnt and not all but some of the cheese was burnt brown. Ok so we picked around the burnt nachos. The hostess came to apologize about the hair in the sour cream. She said “I’m so sorry about that. I gave you a new order of nachos. We are good now right” I just said yes because we were in the middle of eating our entrees. (Perfect timing to come when we are eating our dinner 🤦‍♂️) as for our entrees. My cali chopped salad came in soggy so I just ate the chicken.  My wife ordered the salmon wrap and only ate one bite and whatever her parents ordered ended up half eaten and nothing was taken to go. Now I know why all of the people in my community have been bad mouthing the place. I’m done defending barley creek. In fact I’m done visiting them. It’s good to know that a new resteraunt just opened on top of the mountain and another 2 are to follow.

Nice pavilion.  Good food. Great bear.
Nice place great food. Don't drink beer but im sure it's probably good.
Pricey but filling fare.  Typical for resort area.  Beer is drinkable - golden ale was our favorite.  Service was above average.

If you are in the area to Barley Creek breakfast restaurant is amazing the waiters and waitresses are lovely and kind , the food was amazing my wife and I had the French toast with banana sauce with cream truly delightful.
The beer was not worth coming for, the stuff attitude was rude, overall unpleasant experience
Food was excellent.  Was a little pricey but all in all the atmosphere was nice.

After the loss of some favorite menu items as well as failing standards, we are sad to lose our favorite breakfast spot. We had gone almost every weekend for about two years.  Once some of the items became literally inedible and were told "it's not like they are from yesterday" we had to give up hope that they would eventually recover. Very sad

My wife called from the parking lot of Camelback and asked about how long of a wait they had.Girl who answered the phone said no wait,come on in.We arrived 10 minutes later to find a 50 min. to 1 hour wait.They played dumb and were very good at it.We actually decided to wait and took a beeper.As I was getting out of the way of the front area,a hostess told me I couldn't stand were I was ,I had to move 1 foot to the right.
I smiled and handed her our beeper.SEE YA!
Nice please. They have vegan and vegetarian options.
Cool place. Good food.
Great quality food. Tad bit expensive.
Great food, great drinks and absolutely great atmosphere.
Tasty food, great beer choices, and friendly employees.
the beer bite crab dip was delicious, the steak was so, so but over all is a Nice spot to go to.

Drove 30 mins to come for the breakfast that everyone is raving about. No way are the five star reviews valid.

The scrambled eggs were terrible. How could you mess up scrambled eggs, you ask? I have no idea.

Friendly staff and solid beer (3 stars). Just come for the beer and have breakfast at home.
Fast service, nice staff and good food. I like the selection of beers and the waitresses were great.

We stopped here for breakfast twice.  Disappointed each time.  Food was so so.  Services were down right bad.  Could not find a server once food brought to the table.
Will not come back..
Great food & service, but a noisy restaurant
Usually good but get pricey and service varies.  Some times it's excellent other times we have been forgotten about.
Great food and service. I would definitely recommend it.
It was ok. Food was good.

Some of the worst beers I’ve ever tasted. Nope.

Update to the barley creek reply:
bartender let us know he was out of flight glasses. Only recommendation we got. No service. Not one server came to ask us if we were ok. Also between the party, we tried 10 of your beers. Flat, tasteless, and your lager has spoiled stressed hops....diacetyl... I don’t like my lagers tasting like buttered popcorn. Have your brewmaster have a taste before serving. I guess it’s good your tourist population knows nothing about beer.
Didn't like the chicken and waffles. Didn't taste great. Also, the beer cheese soup is one to skip... Tasted like badly mixed chicken broth and cheese.
Food was good wasn't a fan of the Corey Halloween decorations

Maybe it's because I'm from Michigan, but this place was mediocre at best. The server Dustie had no personality and never did a two bite check, so when my fries were cold and obviously old, I couldn't get fresh ones. Beer was alright.
love the place.
Very good meal very good service fun place with the Halloween decorations only bad thing is they need to have the Brick Oven meals available before 4 p.m.

I really like Barley Creek, but there are too many flaws.

Cons:
The food is way overpriced and portions are often small. The vegetarian and vegan options are not great. For example, the salads are nothing special, I'd rather get a salad at the local grocery store. Most of the veggie options are covered in oil. The appetizers seem like they are premade, as if they are pulled out of a package and heated up. Barley Creek is always crowded and hard to find tables and/or a seat at the bar. Part of always being crowded, the place is always super loud. I often find myself shouting across the table, sometimes shouting is not enough. If you care about talking while eating out it is best to avoid this place.

Pros:
Good food, they have a large selection food options including vegetarian and vegan options. If you don't mind your veggie options being mediocre and over priced.

Great beer, they brew their own beer and each type is really good.

Big eating area, there are two bars and a lot of other seating options. That's if the place is not over crowded.
Wait times were long but food was good
Food and beer were very good. Service was lacking. Seating is uncomfortable, especially in booths for taller patrons. Overall, wasn't even worth taking a pic.
Food was good.  Specials had great prices.
Thoroughly enjoyed lunch  $$ but the quality and service were on point
Cool atmosphere, however, ribs were not good at all. Chewy and not much flavor.

Was somewhat busy when I went. Beer was average, they offer flights. Only had the Charcuterie board, tomatoes were too thickly sliced as well as the apples and prosciutto, hard cheeses were ok. Overall the board was ok, just bad presentation, especially for the price. Was on my way home and would've gotten food but decided it would be a safer bet to get closer to home. Seems to cater to the ski resort crowd, overpriced but decent. Mediocre service, average beer.
Good beers.  Food is good a bit pricey.  Ok for family. Near camelback resort and thw crossings outlets
Place was dirty, food was cold and awful, service was bleh.
Food was not good. No season
Excellent beer, food, and atmosphere.
Jeff and mike 2/10/2017 abousultly the worst i ever had as servers in the 15 years of coming to this establishment.  Dont ever cut off old timers.. thats why i gave you 5 cent tip. Learn!!!!
Food was tasty but we feel robbed because prices are high and the serving sizes are very small and will not be coming back.
Horrible experience. Very loud atmosphere that left us with a headache. We also left with empty stomachs since there was a large hair in the meatloaf we both ordered
They only show brewing @12:30. It's more of a fancy pants bar with many decorations (not machines)
Staff was friendly and well organized. Food was ok.
Everything was great! I'm stuffed!
Very friendly atmosphere. Very friendly staff (hey, Kerri 😊).
Food good, beer meh
Lousy Entree. Get a Burger.
Good food but way too noisy. Can not have a conversation with friends in the same booth.
Baked beans and smoked pig outstanding
Food was tasty, a tad on the expensive side. The daily specials listed on the website are only good for dine in service--not for take out, which was disappointing.
Travel from nyc to a circus ! Never again 0
Dirty and expensive
Great food, real food.
Great food,  TERRIBLE sangria
Overrated

Poorly ran operating I ordered 12 drumsticks and only received 3 ...and the beer took 10 minutes to get to the table on top of this I had a gift certificate that they would not honor because its wing night ...I work in the bar industry as well this is poorly  managed and would never go back...STAY AWAY at ALL costs !!!
Excellent local brews

We sat at a high top in the bar the food was good the service was good except for the screaming child at the high top behind us who was mad or upset about something and who kept hitting my mom's back when he keep getting down from the chair which happen at least 4 or 5 times.

This was our first time at Iron Hill Brewery.  It's a nice looking place.  The regular menu is only available via QR code.

We sat at a high top table "near" the bar.  We aren't big drinkers but are usually good for at least one each.  I'm always interested in what special cocktails are available as I and a sweeter palate.  I chose the Dublin Cider.  A warm simple cocktail of apple cider and Jameson Irish whiskey.  Boy was that delicious.  I found out later that they offer just the warm cider one it's own.  Nothing feels like fall better than warm apple cider.  Both were amazing.  My lady chose the pumpkin spice martini.  She enjoyed it but we both agree it could have used more pumpkin and spice.

For our meal we chose to share two entrees.  The chicken and ribs were good.  The ribs were tender fall off the bone and the chicken was chicken wings.  We got a soup as our one side.  Chicken corn chowder was perfect for the cold windy evening that it was.  Plus our other entree was a bacon grilled cheese sandwich that included smoked gouda cheese.  All in all it was nice experience.

Please support your restaurants.

The chicken pot pie was not anything like described in menu. Kenneth square mushroom soup was gritty ground up mushroom puree. Ok but not to my likings. Fish, fries, steak and salads ordered were good. Even with calling ahead an hour before arriving we had an hour wait. Very noisy place. Waitress was good.

I never expect much when it comes to chain restaurant breweries, but Iron Hill comes through. I had the half rack of ribs with the chicken wings. The ribs were fall-off-the-bone. The wings were so-so. The beers were quality too.
Nice atmosphere, good food, great wait staff. Would return.
Great food! Gets busy and service was a little slow but it was a great atmosphere. Was there for Girl Scout cookie pairing. So fun!
Nice venue. Bit overpriced and somehow drinks were warm with ice.
Cheesesteak rolls are the bomb
Beers were pretty good. Brought us the breadsticks instead of pretzel sticks though, and the chopped salad was ALL iceberg (I didnt think anyone was doing that anymore?). It said it had romaine also.
Good beers nice atmosphere.

Was one of 3 tables in the entire restaurant at 330 in the afternoon we were sat down in the corner booth and waited 20min for service to not even get approached once by a server. Two employees at the front door were staring at their phones laughing as we all walked out they lost our business today due to the lack of service.
Very long wait, but well worth the wait. Very good food and great bar selection.

The only not great thing about eating here was that I reserved a table and they said 40mins. After An hour. They said it would be another half hour or more. I was not amused... But the food was really good and the staff was friendly.
Found hair in my takeout meal. Avoid eating here during a pandemic if they can't keep hair out of their food.
The food is just okay. They brew their own beer but they have a good selection of other craft need as well.
Santa feel Turkey burger was really good! Service was good as well.
Fairly average. Decent but not great food and beer. Definitely better options in Hershey.

Good food friendly service
Pub burger was delicious!
Great food, better beer, meh server
Good food, great service and atmosphere
Love this place
This was amazing. Chorizo Chimichanga. Huge and delicious!

Not overly impressed with the food. Chipotle chicken quesadillas were surprisingly pretty bland given it being "chipotle". The sour cream was crema consistency,  which is fine- just mentioning it in case others wanted to know. We didn't try any Italian dishes,  so I can't comment on those.
One of the best Pittsburgh Steak Salads! Four stars only because it took a very long time to get to me when it was not particularly busy. Would have given it 4.5 stars because it is all fresh food.
Love this place, except for the COVID restrictions.

I came into Mexitaly for the first time on a slow Wednesday afternoon, there was no greeting at the door and I had to seat myself after 10 minutes of waiting to be greeted. I saw what seemed to be a manager/owner on his phone and not even remotely paying attention to his customers or his employees. No one acknowledged my presence until it was finally time for me and my family to order our dinner. Once we were finished with our dinner me
and my 6 year old son went back to use the bathroom. While in the bathroom my son noticed something crawling across the floor. Upon further inspection I discovered that it was a large sized cockroach of some kind. Truly disgusting and unprofessional, will definitely not be returning to Mexitaly any time soon😡😷
I had pork BBQ tacos that tasted okay, but they did a MSG sort of thing to me and I didn't like that. Probably won't hustle back, though a pizza someone was eating looked good. I was in for lunch.

Just to clarify Greg , you were seen spraying for roaches. In fact , one almost crawled right on a pizza ! You are very rude and unprofessional. You told my daughter she is a failure because she made mistakes. It is obvious you are lacking managerial and people skills . Does it make you feel better to belittle the young girls you hire?

Great food,but the owner Greg likes to talk to customers to.much ,like trys to sit down at guest tables and tell the story of how he opened his restaurant, very condescending and rude owner, everyone else was great
I saw a roach on the floor, I’m not going back. The food is good but I’m not going to eat at a place that isn’t properly clean.
What a hidden gem! Beer is really good! Food on point! If I were a local I would eat here a lot !

Been here a couple times now, service continues to be slow. The food (what I've tried) is good, but seriously there's 2 servers on the floor and its super slow and seems disorganized. Don't expect anything quickly.

Decent food and drinks. I’d recommend going here for the drinks more than the food. They do have a bathroom if you ask too! Don’t go here on a date. Overall this is a decent place and I’ll probably come back here to give it a shot again. We will have to see! I’ll just have to find some time to travel back to the area and see family once again!

Let me start by saying I have been a customer of Mexitaly for roughly 4 years. I have even had a surprise birthday party there. Went in today, like most Tuesdays for taco Tuesday. I was told by owner that he is switching up happy hour to require that you buy alcohol.  What this means is at a family friendly restaurant my 9 year old son can not enjoy taco Tuesday.  I am a single father so managing money is a priority. I am also a business owner that recommend Mexitaly to just about every person that I painted for in past few years. Needless to say I will not be recommending it ever again......

Don't bother looking at the hours shown online or on the door because they will close whenever they are slow. Our party of 5 and a couple behind us got there at 9pm on a Saturday night and were told by the manager they were closing because they "are slow this time of here, but in the summer we will definitely stay open later". I won't be coming back to find out if that's the case. 9pm on a Saturday night at a place where the door says they close at midnight and the manager turns away 7 customers...hope the servers have something else lined up.

I normally rave about this place, but after this experience I doubt I'll return. I wasn't aware that they sprinkle "Romano cheese" on all the pizzas. As soon as I got it I couldn't stand the smell or taste. Took it back to the counter and staff just looked at me stupidly. Terrible customer service.

Food was good but a bit disappointed. I went their with with the expectations of getting a roasted root veggie burrito but apparently they keep all the ingredients mixed together and couldn't make me one without the item im allergic to and being i was with a group whom already ordered i had to settle for a veggie pizza when i really didn't want pizza nor did I wanna be the weirdo that sat in a group and ate nothing while they did. I think it would be a lot more beneficial to the restaurant and patrons if they made their burritos piece by piece like most other good burrito establishments in the area.

We ordered and sat there 35 minutes waiting for our food. I questioned the them about it and was told we would be next. Then i watched her rummage through the trash looking for our order. She told the manager that we were waiting a long time. He said it would be 5 minutes longer, but i said i wanted my money back. He handed it to me and said a barley audible "sorry". What a pathetic way to try to right a wrong. They lost any business from me in the future.
The food and drinks were good, but the salsa tasted like marinara sauce.

I had the quesadilla and my wife ordered the burrito. The food was good, but the reason for only getting a 3 star rating was the lack of staff at the bar. I won’t bother trying to call them again ever.

I loved the concept but I don't like craft beers, rather have domestic at least offer bottle domestic.
Went to mexitaly today got spicy fries and found a bolt in the basket no sorry no refund sorry sad place don't go they don't care

ORIGINAL REVIEW:: Great every time I go..
UPDATE 12/7/19:  I was going to email my complaint instead of posting because I did not want to bash you guys. But there is no email listed to contact on your website. I've come for years, and have recommended dozens of friends. But I've recently had 2 poor experiences at your business. The first one, was with the actual owner. My wife and I come out almost once a week, (we love the food and music) except this time she forgot her purse and only had her AMEX card in the car. A simple oops, So I explained the issue to the owner. The owner held up the line and replied "There is an ATM across the street, we don't accept that form of payment.  Great way to embarrass a long time costumer.  I work for a local business (and yes you get charged an extra few percent for using AMEX) Us local business rely on our customers, especially word of mouth and reviews. But we can't treat our customer as disposable,, not over 50 cents. So I'm docking my original review of 5 stars from over a year ago to 2 stars. From what I've heard, many other people are having similar issues with your restaurant. No time to type out the second terrible instance.

I understand that they were busy, but the staff at the counter was very rude and "snippy" . Half of our order sat there for an extended period of time ,when our buzzer finally went off half of the first plate was cold and the other half warm at best. Then when we asked for a second plate (because we were splitting) we were handed a 6 inch round metal plate . This was once of my favorite places .
Menu is different from the one posted on their website. No low carb options at all.

Everything here us awesome for the most part. But it's gets crowded because its so good. Its a small place so the put seats close together in some areas to add more seating. This makes the bar area very tight during busy times. Thats my only gripe about this great establishment.

I can't comment on the food, I did not get a chance to order anything. I gave it 2 stars because it looked clean.

Response to owner.  When we walked in we did not feel like we were welcomed as new customers regular customers had no problem ordering, so when no one approached us we just left.
The food is good, they could have a bit healthy options! No complaints about the adult beverages they do have.
Not a fan of this place in the day time.  Still good food
My seafood burrito was awesome!!
Food was  soso ! Prices were outrageous! Only thing good was the beer!! Would go back for beer not the food!
Food is good just need the kitchen open longer on Fridays. 11-11:30. Please consider.

Beers and pizza, need I say anything else?

The cooks do a great job, the speed is something that will come eventually.  They need to speed it up though.  A 1 hr. Wait for food is too much though.

Rusty Rail has an amazing atmosphere & food. They DO NOT take reservations!
I have been there 3 times. The 2nd time i went, I went with my husband, we sat at the bar.  It was busy!   However when we got our appetizer which was the Rosemary Asiago House Cut Fries, it was cold -we had to send it back!

great beers, across the types, and good, hearty, tasty food options! great adherence to health precautions, too, by staff and customers.
and a local spot for safe live music.
