Very good beer. Great atmosphere. Very dog friendly, there was a diving dogs pool. Definitely would go back.

Nice newer craft pub. Beer for everyone taste. They did serve food however I did not order any while I was there. Plenty of of parking. Nice deck for outdoor seating. Comfortable bathrooms. Had a few TV's hanging for sports and what not. Worth a stop. It's right across the street from the historical Round Barn.

I love the beer, service, and live music. This isn't a 5 this time because there was a huge group of motorcyclists that visited Saturday afternoon. Most of whom were not wearing masks. Masks are required in restaurants, it was posted, and Thirsty Farmer should have enforced it.

Great outdoor area, plenty of seating on the deck or picnic tables and a lot of room to spread out with lawn chairs. Vienna lager was very good. Food was OK, I would prefer something besides sandwiches and fries but that is just my preference.

Have more than one cashier... beer is okay but super standard.

Good beer and food. Nice views outside. It was cool to see all the hops growing in the field.

Excellent beer at this place.  Hard to decide what to get!  Of course, a flight is a good place to start, but choosing your next one is difficult.  Food menu is fairly limited, but the food itself is delicious.  Easy ordering too with filling out a paper and handing it in.

Prices are very reasonable and nothing is outrageous.

This place is fairly small, so when it is crowded, it is a little lacking in seating.  Covered deck outside is great in nice weather.

Parking is decent, a gravel lot but easy to maneuver.

In growing season, you see hops growing outside which is awesome.  They source locally when they can as well.

Service is great, pleasant, easy-going staff eager to help you find the right beer for you.  Want a little taste?  No problem!  They'll help you choose if you're just not sure.
Food was decent. Fresh for sure but seemed underseasoned. Def needed salt. Super friendly staff.
The beer and cider are really good, and the apple cider donuts are the best!
Nice visit and good beer.
Not enough staff for the number of customers. None of the staff seemed happy. Other than that, atmosphere was nice. I liked the complimentary ice water on the condiment table.
Felt like I was in church hall! Bleak atmosphere! Pick up food service, tiny menu and overpriced!
Nice place. The dog competition was really cool. Staff seemed preoccupied, possibly over worked?? Not very attentive or helpful. Nice seating indoors and outside

Nice place to hang out as a group. Probably better when the weather is nice!

They are overpriced. So, I wouldn't say it is for locals.

Nice setting. Real nice wood bar. Very good beer. Really like the Vienna Lager

Service was slow and looked like they were irritated that you were there.  I take no joy in begging the staff to take my money.

Setting was cute and had an outside seating area.  Menu looked promising but our party did not order food but was on the #getpoured tour.  I am gluten free (no gf beer options) but four in our party ordered beers and enjoyed them.  Have plans  to go back for food in the future.

Good food, interesting but limited menu.
Maybe an off day, but service was slow and and disorganized.
Pretty cool little spot. Not the best beer or food but works for what it is!
Beer was very good. Loved hazelnut brown ale.  Atmosphere fun, clean and picturesque.

good food and awesome beers.

Three of us went out to eat. I got the nachos. It was a huge dishes considering it was under appetizer. I think the host should of told me how big it really was . It was made for three. And it was just for me. I hated to waste and throw away food. And $17 for nachos UGH !!. My wife had the cheese steak plus soda . The bread was soggy and wet. And when she picked it up the plate just filled with grease. The French fries were cold and limp. My mom order sunrise grill cheese I believe and got some kind of other sandwich. She had a rum and coke. That was a joke you hardly tasted a hint of rum. All soda!  Tried a beer after that. It was ok nothing special
This experience end up costing us $65 plus

Absolutely disgusting. Ordered take out through Uber Eats. Ordered 2 burgers, bit into them and they were blood red gooey meat. Called the restaurant 4 times and got recorded message saying no one can take my call, please leave a message.

Nice established spot downtown with good food and a great selection of beer.  Great Cobb salad with fresh ingredients.  Service is always top notch and welcoming.  We had a party upstairs and they handled everything very well.
Pretty good food. Decent service. Overall a nice place to eat in downtown Bethlehem. Sometimes the wait is a bit long for what you actually get. It's just pub food, people.

Was very happy with our most recent visit. I don't know why I've never gotten one of their burgers in the past. I was actually really pleased with how good it was, and they didn't over cook the meat. Our waiter was a very nice gentleman.

The food was the only thing that really saved my experience here. When we walked in we were greeted and the hostess seemed confused about seating two people as she took us to two tables that had yet to be bussed.  After getting seated in a booth where I noticed that there was some garbage on the floor.  Our waitress was inattentive as soon as we didn't order alcohol and my order was made wrong.  When we finally got our food it was fantastic and that is the only reason I gave this place three stars.

Really rude and unaccommodating!! We waited for a table for a long time, once called, we were put upstairs where it smelled like a dirty dish rag, asked to move and were told we would have to be put back on waiting list which was at least 45 minutes! Told both girls at front desk that we already waited and should not have to be put back on list and they both basically said we can leave.  Will never be back.
I order wings and they gave me all drums... bad service and it took 1 hour to come.. wont ever come here...

We have been coming here for 18 years for the beers, and the last 10 for food and beers.  Typical pub food with flair.  They have decent burgers, good wings, good brats and decent nachos.   Beer line up changes with season,  but the regular wheat, golden ale are reliable and can be enjoyed all year round.  The service is always good.  Staff is friendly and attentive.

Not a place I'd be interested in going back to but if I did I wouldn't order anything but the wings which are pretty good but not something you can't get better somewhere else. Had their house salad with grilled chicken and goat cheese dressing and only picked at it. It was very bland and served on a plate so you couldn't really mix it together.

I do not recommend this place for large gatherings. There is a policy in effect where all members of a table must be 21+ for any individual to order an alcoholic beverage.
Edit: Management: Thank you for the clarification on this issue. I however will go on to recommend that you better train your staff regarding this law, since I was not even permitted to show identification before being refused service.

Good beer but literally everything else was lacking. The food all came out of a freezer, including the "Belgian Pomme Frites" which were disappointing cold and soggy. There was very little cheese on the brisket grilled cheese and the brisket tasted like it had been "on the line" for many hours if not days.
Good beer, large selection of different types of food, good service
This place used to be great but now it’s just another mediocre bar food restaurant. Beer and cheese soup is really amazing but other than that it’s just not worth it.
Great beer variety. Fairly standard brewpub menu. Friendly staff. Easy to find a beer to suit your taste.

My experience started off with the waitress telling me they are out of one item on their specials menu, as well as a couple things on the regular menu that she would let me know about upon ordering. First item I tried to order as an appetizer was some wings, they were out of those. The appetizers I did end up with were the soft pretzel and the nachos, both of which were amazing and I wish my family and I stopped there. Main courses came, they forgot the toppings on my mother-in-laws burger, put croutons on the salad she asked for without, my burger was burnt so bad that when I took a bite it crumbled apart, and the burger/sandwich sizes are half of what you would get some place else for cheaper. If the main course was good I would have given 3 stars because no matter what you would get a better deal and better food else where.

The wait was not long, the waitress was nice but the food was mediocre. My friend and I ordered the Philly cheese steak, it was suppose to come with sauteed onions on them. I took two bites and notices I didn't have any onions on mine and hers as well. I politely told the waitress and she said "Maybe they are in the cracks of the sandwich." Then another worker comes over and tells me they are minced into the sandwich. I'm not stupid, there were no onions on the sandwich. I didn't get nasty and I didn't even want a refund but I was offended that they didn't just admit they forgot to put the onions on..the alcohol was just ok this place is nothing special. I will not be going back again.

The service was excellent. Our orders were delivered in a timely manner and the server checked back within a couple of minutes.

I had a hamburger and fries.  About as basic as you can get. I asked for medium rare and it came out medium rare. Good start. The bread was greasy. It almost seemed that they wiped butter on the outside of the bun. Still it was a burger.  I was eating greasy meat.

I was also a little unhappy with the fries.  They were not crisp and the coloration made me suspect their grease should be changed.

Still, the food was better than you can get at most restaurents. A good experience overall.
In a crazy beer world, Fegley’s brew works remains consistent.  No glitter IPA’s or cake stouts (yet) but the food is delicious and the beer is solid.

We weren’t even properly greeted just basically told to get out. They don’t close for another 4 hours. I’ve heard bad things about this place before but still tried giving it a shot - disappointed but not surprised.

I can't be too mad. I love this place, we had a great time our server was awesome and we didnt have to wait for anything. However my friend has celiac disease so we bought a bottle off their wine list to take home and continue our girls night, low and behold the bottle we ordered off the said "wine"list was not wine which we found out unfortunately when we arrived home. Perhaps separate your large bottles of beer from your wine. Thankfully I noticed before she drank too much but she could have gotten severely sick. It's pretty important to separate those things. Unless of course said beer is gluten free and can be included on your wine list. I hope you seriously look into this, we do enjoy coming here, I would however hate to see something labeled incorrectly and see someone get sick. Hopefully its corrected, and just voicing a concern for a good friend. Thank you for listening!

Sorry dont know how to respond to responses, I really appreciate you looking into it, we just need to look at it more carefully next time I suppose,  like I said we love the place and my friend loves that you always have gluten free options for food and drinks! Would never try and add a bad rep! Thanks for your care and understanding!

Ordered buffalo chicken flatbread via uber eats. Showed up with 7lbs. of purple/green/blue cheese drenched on top of the entire flatbread. Couldn't eat it even after scraping off all the blue cheese I could. Would give 0 stars if possible. Will never order from here again!

Beer was good, but food was disappointing. Buffalo chicken sandwich was supposedly 'grilled' but tasted microwaved. The pork rib tips flat bread was measly, few shreds of meat on bread and cheese for $12. Brussel sprouts was probably fried frozen, and had that bitter taste with soggy texture inside the crust. Probably will not come again.
Service is so inconsistent. Today was bad bartender day. Sorry to bother you for a beer........ I'd go more often if they would only hire bartenders that didn't hate their job.

Good atmosphere and really like how they rotate in a few new beer selections every time we have come. Staple beers are good as well. Unfortunately the menu has changed to the point where we might not be coming back as often as usual. They use to have a larger menu and a lot of good selections, but they cut out a good chunk of those in place of a new, smaller menu. This includes my wife's favorite chicken hummus wrap. When we were there this past week, they had their new menu setup. While I was ready to make a selection, my wife had yet to make up her mind. This boggled my mind since I'm always last to figure out what I want. This just proves the limited menu doesn't have anything that pops out at you. While the new menu changes won't deter me from going (beer is still good and decent sanwhiches/burgers), my wife will have a tough time wanting to come back.

Well to start off, it took 30 minuets for my mother to get her drink.And once she found out it was out of stock after 30 minuets thats when it got worse. I found hairs on my Mac' N Cheese. And portions of my mother's food were cold. I am glad they took it off the bill.
Good Service, good food, good beer
Great local beers and food...I would come back when in the area again...
Bad menu, bad service. Food was not horrendous. Used to be a packed establishment now it's empty.
Place was great friendly atmosphere.  Can get a little noisy on weekends.  Food is excellent.
Food good but service was not. Server never smiled and looked like someone shot her puppy.
No silverware or plates for appetizers til we asked for them. No napkins or refills til we asked. Poor all around service. Haven't been here in a while. Now I remember why 👎👎👎
Good food and friendly staff
Cool atmosphere. Great food and service.

As a 11 year customer (and recent member) I have to say I'm disappointed with the new menu and how the food is cooked/prepared lately. I hate to say it but in my last few visits in 2016 the food went from being great to just okay.  Sorry Brew Works but I'm going to have to lower my rating.

Super loud on a Friday night in the main section seated on an aisle just above the downstairs lounge. My husband had a good quality burger with tasty bbq sauce, but it could have benefitted from tomatoes and lettuce, and was ultimately forgettable; my coleslaw could be procured at any other establishment. However, my husband upgraded his side to the mac and cheese, which proved to be the perfect pairing to my pulled pork and broccoli rabe sandwich (a chef special that day). We shared bites of my (much larger) sandwich and even went so far as to swipe up the cheese sauce left in his bowl. Of the six beers I sampled, the one that stood out was the Steelgaarden Wit, with notes of "chamomile" and "coriander", although the addition of a slice of orange (as suggested by the menu) did not contribute anything. The Wit would be a great summer sipper.

Service was not that great not really sure what the problem was however we were there for a birthday event however food did not come out on time some people's food came out cold however manager tried best to make it right so that was the positive side will probably try to go there again just for date night
Cold food warm beer! Very slow service

This use to be my favorite place to eat the food was amazing! A few weeks ago they changed the menu and got rid of basically everything they were known for.. And to make matters worse I went back twice and both times the food was horrible.. I can't believe this place is turning into this.. Unfortunately I will not be going back unless they bring there old menu back. So disappointed.
Good beer and nice vibe to the place 👍
Food is fine, nothing spectacular. It's extremely loud inside. You can barely hear the person sitting next to you.

We use to love this place until they changed their menu, gave it a 2nd chance after the menu change, now it seems the service is awful and the menu selection stinks, very disappointed after coming here for years, bringing out of state family, it's sad to see. My first time their,it's a nice place ,the food was good,our food came out not hot it was warm,but we ate it ..

My friend and I went for Brunch this morning, the Bloody Mary's and Irish Coffee were delicious. My only suggestion would be, is to have music outside.  Doesn't have to be loud, but music would make the visit a good time.

My wife and I went on Friday night for drinks and dinner. The place was crowded but we got seated very quickly. Had a few drinks and our dinners came out very quickly. Everything was great and our waitress was excellent. Will definitely go back again.

Last nights Dinner consisted steamed broccoli, old bay jumbo shrimp 🦐, Lemon butter garlic mussels, and Hot and spicy garlic Snow crab legs 😋🦀. Our server was very nice and very attentive. I had a bad experience at Maggie's. This was my second time coming here and I left highly disappointed. The wait time was reasonable considering they were packed and we were walk ins (we waited approx. 30 minutes give or take). However the rest of my time there was not at all what I expected. First there were bugs, flies and bees which made me super uncomfortable. I understand because its outside dining certain outside pests are expected however I've dined outside plenty of restaurants and never had this many bugs outside. I kept having to stand and move until the bees and other bugs flew away so I spent most of the time standing next to my table. The waitress did not provide us with any utensils or napkins nor water. The table was wobbly and kept moving. My partner ordered the shrimp appetizer and she said it tasted rubbery and didn't eat it. Our drinks were probably the worst I've ever had. They had almost no alcohol in them. I had the buffalo wings appetizer and it was okay. We both ordered the steak entree and at that point I had enough of the bugs so we decided to take it to go. I also got a bowl alcohol type of drink and when I tell you this drink tasted like nothing but cool aid and water I am extremely serious. When we got home and opened the container with our food I want to say the food was okay for me nothing special, my partner on the other hand was extremely disappointed and didn't do anything but taste it   and close the container. We paid $97 total and the restaurant actually charged my partner $120 even though we paid the tip in cash. Needless to say I will not be going back to Maggies and I don't recommend it especially if you want good quality seafood and great service. They obviously don't have either.
Great menu selection and the special fries with cheese rival Chickie's & Pete's! But the mixed drinks were somewhat watery. Will update after next visit.

Truly amazing restaurant in our neighborhood on the Delaware. My wife and I frequently walk over for a delicious lunch there. Burgers are my favourite with seasoned fries and draft beer. Only reason for me to limit to 4 stars is due to the

First time going to  Maggie's.  Had a great time with friends.  Had the "honey hot" wings and they were very good. I love the scenery, the staff is great, the food is pretty good, im still working through the menu rhe BBQ jack dainels chicken is a double no tho🤷🏾‍♂️

The food is consistently averaged to good. I've never had anything that I would call great. Good bar food good comfort food. I don't like having my main course sitting there getting cold and I don't like having to rush right into my dinner without having enjoyed the appetizers first. Very good. We both had the Maryland crab chowder and I had the onion soup burger, which comes with onion soup au jus and seasoned fries and my husband had the mini pizza with fries. Very upset with my recent visit at Maggie’s. My debit card was overcharged by my server. When I signed the credit card slip it had the right amount printed on it. I left my tip in cash so the server must have purposely added the wrong tip on my card after I left. When I called about this problem I was told “he accidentally double charged you”. A server can not do this by accident and the amount I was overcharged by wasn’t even double. It wasn’t a large amount of money but my biggest problem is they didn’t even put it back on the card. I was told I had to come to them to pick it up in person. This was a big inconvenience because I do not live close to the restaurant.
Nice place, outside seating wasn't open, not sure why, weather was good. A little pricey but I'll go back for sure.
Ok for bar food & prices best at happy hour

Upon entering this spot on the waterfront I was pleasantly greeted by the hostess. Another person sat us at our table, and we were pleasantly greeted by our waitress. She took our order and was very kind. Food was good and priced well.

Now the not so good. Upon entering the restaurant ahead of my son and I were an older couple. We were led away at the same time as the couple. We never saw that couple the rest of our time there. We were led to the back of the restaurant where there wasn’t another patron, only staff walking in and out of some back room. The restaurant wasn’t empty and surely wasn’t crowded. Another server served me the soup I ordered and with an off putting smile and not so sincere demeanor asked if anyone told me to turn my hat around. I replied “no”, and she instructed s to turn it around because those were the “rules”. My hat can be described as a “dad” hat, no logos or designs. My clothes weren’t baggy and very plain and simple. I then wondered where the couple ahead of us were seated. So there we were just the two of us waiting for our lunch when 2 Hispanic (they were speaking Spanish) women came into the back and appeared to be looking for something they had left at a table earlier. Our food came and we began to eat it. I was a bit uncomfortable as I tried to not over think the situation. Then a 5 year old darker skinned, very curly haired boy (my son) said something unprovoked that chilled me. He said, “daddy why are we the only people back here, I want to go to where the people are”. For a little boy of 5 to notice something was off should speak volumes. I’m not one who plays the “race” card but today I certainly felt prejudged... perhaps it was nothing, perhaps it was more. You be the judge. And I would gladly be open to a discussion, I’m not one to hide behind a keyboard.

Asked for ketchup and mayo on the side for all, only got ketchup. Didnt get any dipping sauce(ranch / blue cheese) for the mountain melt OR regular zingers. All of these were items specifically asked and noted on order).

UPDATED REVIEW: Came in last night for an outing with my family. Previously we visited and did NOT have a good experience, so much so, I felt compelled to write a review about it and after speaking with the GM Stephen who reached out to gain clarity on the situation, we were assured that our next experience would be much better. As a courtesy, the general manager did send a $25 gift card via postal mail for our trouble. So we came in for the exact same meal. Not only was service better, the food was also. Our waiter last night was Shawn and he was very pleasant, courteous and efficient. We could all tell Shawn had many tasks at hand as there were numerous customers dining in, but that did not stop him from delivering EXCELLENT service! Shawn completely turned around our experience and made it even more clear that the previous waitress we had (who shall remain nameless) on our last visit really did treat us poorly. Shawn not only delivered prompt service, he had a smile throughout our visit and asked if we needed anything, he asked how was the food, and thanked us for dining in. I hope Shawn really excels at whatever he wants to do in life because he is an exceptional gentleman and has a great spirit. Thanks so much Shawn for your hospitality and making Miller’s our favorite spot again!

They call this place a Sports Bar, but they don't get Comcast Sports Net in Philly, which is the home of the Phillies and Flyers and all of the important local college basketball.
So let me get this straight:
You advertise yourselves as a "sports bar" located in one of the most rabid sports towns in America and you don't get Comcast Sports Net?
That's like being in New York City and not getting The New York Yankee network.
I walked into that place and specifically asked if I could get the Temple basketball game on the other night.
She asked what channel it was on?
I said Comcast Sports Net.
She said, "I'm sure we do. I'll get the manager to put it on. Can I get you anything?"
I asked for a bucket of Miller Lites.
Then the manager comes out and said, "We don't get Comcast Sports Net?"
"You don't get Comcast Sports Net?" I asked incredulously.
"No, all of our stations come out of New York," he said.
"Well, this is Philly," I said.
I went down the street to Hooters.
They had Comcast Sports Net.
